symptom: Linux VMs connected to Log Analytics workspace but not showing in Update Management Dashboard. Only ~17 of 70+ VMs visible. Heartbeat and Perf logs collecting normally.
rootCause: Linux VMs require explicit addition to Automation Account for UpdatesEnabled=True. Unlike Windows (which uses MMA agent rules), Linux uses hybrid worker component requiring Automation. VMs only connected to Log Analytics without being added to Automation Account will have UpdatesEnabled=False.
solution: Add Linux VMs in Automation Account > Update Management page. After adding and waiting, VMs will appear in Update Solution Dashboard. Windows VMs show up automatically when connected to LAW.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======16. Automation======/16.6 Update Management Dashboard显示不全.md

symptom: Customer wants to use Python SDK to list all alert rules configured in subscriptions but cannot find the right MonitorManagementClient function.
rootCause: Azure Monitor has multiple alert rule types (metric, log, activity log, classic), each requiring a different Python SDK method. No single API lists all alert types.
solution: Use MonitorManagementClient with different methods per alert type: metric_alerts.list_by_subscription() for metric alerts, scheduled_query_rules.list_by_subscription() for log alerts, activity_log_alerts.list_by_subscription_id() for activity log alerts, alert_rules.list_by_subscription() for classic alerts. Use F12 browser trace to discover API method names, then map to SDK functions. For Mooncake, set base_url=CLOUD.endpoints.resource_manager and credential_scopes accordingly.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======16. Automation======/16.8[MCVKB][Python SDK]Manage Azure Resources via.md

symptom: OMS Agent error: Couldn't open AzSecID file: open /etc/azsec/AzSecID: no such file or directory. Hash mismatch in oms_audits.xml validation. SecurityBaseline plugin not working.
rootCause: Current.mof file (/etc/opt/omi/conf/omsconfig/configuration/Current.mof) does not exist, indicating OMS agent installation was corrupted from the start. The oms_audits.xml file is out of sync with omsremediate file. DSC Engine reports 'Current configuration does not exist' (error code 6).
solution: 1) Verify Current.mof exists. 2) If missing, run: sudo -u omsagent python /opt/microsoft/omsconfig/Scripts/OMS_MetaConfigHelper.py -enable. 3) If still missing, perform complete OMS agent purge: uninstall LinuxDiagnostics and OMSAgentForLinux extensions, remove /var/opt/microsoft/omsagent, /var/opt/microsoft/omsconfig, /var/opt/microsoft/scx folders, run onboard_agent.sh --purge, then reinstall. 4) Check solution config with: sudo su omsagent -c 'python /opt/microsoft/omsconfig/Scripts/GetDscConfiguration.py'
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======10. Monitor=======/10.10 [OMS Agent]Couldn't open AzSecID file_ open.md

symptom: Fired alerts not showing in Log Analytics workspace Alert portal page, but visible when querying Alert table directly via KQL. Alert rules confirmed to be triggering successfully in ASC.
rootCause: LAW Alert page uses Resource Graph query filtering by targetResource matching the specific LAW resource ID. Alerts whose targetResource is not defined as this specific LAW will not appear in that view.
solution: Use Azure Monitor → Alerts for a centralized/comprehensive view of all alerts across all resources, instead of viewing per-resource Alert pages. Per-resource Alert page only shows alerts whose targetResource matches that specific resource.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======10. Monitor=======/10.11 [Alert]Fired alert couldn't be found.md

symptom: VM metric alert triggered but portal metric chart shows value did not exceed the configured threshold. Customer believes alert is false positive.
rootCause: 1) Time granularity in portal metric chart is too large, causing peak values to be averaged out (e.g., 1-min peaks of 476 averaged to ~300 at 5-min granularity). 2) Missing metric data points can cause fewer samples in average calculation, inflating the average above threshold (e.g., 4 data points instead of 5 minutes due to missing data).
solution: 1) Set metric chart time granularity to 1 minute when comparing with alert rules to see actual peak values. 2) Check for missing metric data points in the evaluation window. 3) Use Portal's fired alert details to see the exact calculated metric value and timestamp. 4) Jarvis dashboard can query VM metrics for validation.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======10. Monitor=======/10.5 Case triage summary about Metrics alert - SR_.md

symptom: Log search alert with metric measurement triggers multiple/unexpected alert emails per evaluation. Only one VM exists but two alerts fire each time.
rootCause: When KQL 'summarize by' only contains bin(TimeGenerated, 5m), the 'aggregate on' field defaults to timestamp. Each time bin becomes a separate entity evaluated against the trigger condition independently, causing multiple alert firings. Setting 'total breaches greater than' to non-zero may cause alerts to never fire since each timestamp entity has only one entry.
solution: Add a dimension variable (e.g., Computer) to the 'summarize ... by' clause, then select that variable under 'aggregate on' instead of timestamp. This groups results by the meaningful dimension so each Computer is treated as one entity across all time bins.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======10. Monitor=======/10.6 _aggregate on_ field in Alert for custom log.md

symptom: Cannot modify Data Retention of Log Analytics workspace on Azure portal. Changing retention days (e.g., 90 to 30 or 100) returns GatewayTimeout error: 'The gateway did not receive a response from Microsoft.OperationalInsights within the specified time period.'
rootCause: Backend bug in Log Analytics service — workspace write requests are redirected from DAS to AMS sub-provider, where they fail with 'ClusterResourceId is a read only property and cannot be changed'. Confirmed as known bug by PG via IcM 211484608.
solution: 1) Collect F12 HAR file to identify the failing PUT request. 2) Use ARM Kusto (armmcadx cluster) tables: EventServiceEntries for correlationId/status, HttpIncomingRequests for 504 errors, HttpOutgoingRequests to trace redirection to sub-providers (DAS→AMS). 3) Check Jarvis LogsDAS and LogsAMS namespaces for exact error. 4) Raise IcM to PG with the error evidence.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======10. Monitor=======/10.7[Log Analytics] How to track and troubleshoot.md

symptom: Azure Policy deployIfNotExists for subscription-level Activity Log diagnostic settings fails with LocationNotAvailableForDeployment error in Mooncake.
rootCause: Policy definition missing deploymentScope='subscription' or using invalid/missing location value. Mooncake requires explicit deployment location from available regions (chinaeast2, chinaeast, chinanorth3, chinanorth, chinanorth2). Using 'global' or omitting location causes the error.
solution: 1) Set resource type to Microsoft.Resources/subscriptions. 2) Set deploymentScope to 'subscription'. 3) Set deployment location to valid Mooncake region (e.g., chinaeast2). 4) Configure existenceCondition to match desired log categories (Administrative, Security, Alert, Recommendation, ResourceHealth) with count=5. 5) For auto-remediation, update subscription properties to trigger policy scan. Full policy example in MooncakeCodeRepo ADO.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Dev=======/7.1 Use Azure Policy to enabled subscription level.md

symptom: Azure JS SDK monitor-query for Log Analytics in Mooncake throws AADSTS500011 error: 'The resource principal named https://api.loganalytics.azure.cn/.default was not found in the tenant'.
rootCause: The audience parameter in LogsQueryClient constructor includes '/.default' suffix which is incorrect for China cloud. The audience should be the bare resource URI without the scope suffix.
solution: Change audience from 'https://api.loganalytics.azure.cn/.default' to 'https://api.loganalytics.azure.cn' (remove /.default). Keep endpoint as 'https://api.loganalytics.azure.cn/v1'.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Dev=======/9.1 Azure JS SDK AuthenticationError_  invalid_res.md

symptom: Application Insights in Mooncake can record trace but not request telemetry. Customer manually creates TelemetryClient without proper DI setup.
rootCause: In Mooncake, Application Insights requires Code Base approach (Codeless not supported for PaaS). Customer bypassed dependency injection and created TelemetryClient manually with only the telemetry channel endpoint, missing QuickPulse and ProfileQuery endpoint configurations. Without proper DI, request telemetry pipeline is not initialized.
solution: Use ASP.NET Core dependency injection in Startup.ConfigureServices: 1) Configure QuickPulseTelemetryModule endpoint: https://live.applicationinsights.azure.cn/QuickPulseService.svc 2) Configure ApplicationInsightsApplicationIdProvider: https://dc.applicationinsights.azure.cn/api/profiles/{0}/appId 3) Configure ServerTelemetryChannel: https://dc.applicationinsights.azure.cn/v2/track 4) Call services.AddApplicationInsightsTelemetry() 5) Use constructor injection to get TelemetryClient in controllers.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Web=======/3.1 Application insights不能监控到request.md

symptom: Azure Function integrated with Application Insights via custom Startup class, but host.json log level settings (e.g., default=Warning) do not take effect. Information-level logs still being recorded.
rootCause: When using a custom Startup class (FunctionsStartup) for DI, Azure Function runtime uses the custom Startup instead of default. ASP.NET Core requires explicit logging configuration in custom Startup classes — host.json logging settings alone are insufficient.
solution: In custom Startup.Configure method, add builder.Services.AddLogging() with proper filter configuration. Define log factory with builder.AddFilter() to control which categories and levels are logged. Example: builder.AddFilter((category, level) => category.StartsWith('YourNamespace')). Refer to Azure Functions DI docs and ASP.NET Core logging docs for detailed patterns.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Web=======/6.1 Azure Function使用Application Insight后发现在host.js.md

symptom: Azure Function 2.x in Mooncake cannot fully support Application Insights (Live Metrics, Profile Query).
rootCause: Azure Function 2.x runtime requires custom Startup class (FunctionsStartup) to override default QuickPulse, ApplicationIdProvider, and TelemetryChannel services. Default endpoints point to Global Azure.
solution: Create custom Startup class inheriting FunctionsStartup. Remove and re-register services with Mooncake endpoints: QuickPulseServiceEndpoint=https://live.applicationinsights.azure.cn/QuickPulseService.svc, ProfileQueryEndpoint=https://dc.applicationinsights.azure.cn/api/profiles/{0}/appId, EndpointAddress=https://dc.applicationinsights.azure.cn/v2/track. Install Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.PerfCounterCollector for live metrics.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Web=======/6.3 如何在Azure Function 2.x中使用code base的方式启用Applicat.md

symptom: APIM Self-hosted gateway on AKS in Mooncake cannot send request logs to Application Insights. Same configuration works in Global Azure.
rootCause: APIM Self-gateway container uses hardcoded Global telemetry endpoint (https://dc.services.visualstudio.com/) regardless of Mooncake environment.
solution: Add logs.applicationinsights.endpoint config to Self-gateway ConfigMap YAML: logs.applicationinsights.endpoint: https://dc.applicationinsights.azure.cn/v2/track. Redeploy Self-gateway on AKS.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Web=======/7.2 Application insights 收集APIM Self-gateway的请求日志.md

symptom: Service Health advisory alert not firing for health advisory events, but fires correctly for incident events.
rootCause: Alert rule condition is configured with properties.incidentType equals Incident only. Health advisory events use operationName Microsoft.ServiceHealth/actionrequired/action which does not match Incident type filter.
solution: Re-deploy the Service Health alert rule template with condition: properties.incidentType equals actionrequired (in addition to Incident). Use Kusto on azureinsightsmc cluster: SettingTelemetry to check resourcePayload for rule conditions, EventData to verify activity log events, SettingEvaluation to trace rule evaluation results per eventId.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/[Alert]Health advisory alert not firing.md

symptom: AAD Diagnostic settings configured to send logs to EventHub fail with UnauthorizedAccess error. Storage Account destination works fine for same diagnostic setting.
rootCause: EventHub namespace has virtual network/firewall enabled, blocking Azure Monitor diagnostic settings service from accessing EventHub resources.
solution: Enable Allow trusted Microsoft services to bypass this firewall setting in EventHub namespace. Troubleshooting Kusto queries on azureinsightsmc: 1) RegistrationTelemetry to check diagnostic setting configuration. 2) EventHubSendBatchTelemetry filtered by tenantId and eventHubName to see isFailed/failureReason. 3) Jarvis LogsDAS namespace for exception details.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/[Diagnostic]AAD Diagnostic settings failed to send.md

symptom: Log search alert (LSA) v1 shows confusing value and deviation in alert summary page. Numbers appear incorrect compared to actual query results.
rootCause: LSA v1 (created with API version 2018-04-16) displays the number of rows that passed the threshold in the alert summary, not the actual metric/aggregated value. This is by design for v1.
solution: Migrate to LSA v2: export the alert rule to ARM template, then re-import it. The newly created rule will use v2 API (2021-08-01+) which shows actual values. No official v1 deprecation plan exists currently.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/[Alert]_Incorrect_ value and deviation in the aler.md

symptom: Diagnostic settings remain visible in Azure Portal after the associated resource is deleted or migrated to a different resource group. Old resourceId persists as orphaned diagnostic settings.
rootCause: Known Issue (CSS Wiki 14416): Azure Monitor does not automatically clean up diagnostic settings when the associated resource is deleted or migrated. The diagnostic setting retains the old resourceId path.
solution: Two options: 1) Recreate the Azure resource under the same subscription, resource group, and resource name to make orphaned settings accessible for deletion. 2) Submit request to Azure Monitor PG via Product Group Escalation (https://aka.ms/azmonpgescalation) to remove orphaned settings. Use Kusto RegistrationTelemetry on azureinsightsmc to verify diagnostic setting configuration.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/[Diagnostic]Diagnostic settings are not deleted wh.md

symptom: Jarvis action get events for Activity Log query is locked/deprecated due to SFI enhancement rollout. Cannot query Activity Log via Jarvis.
rootCause: SFI enhancement removed the default EventService-PlatformServiceViewer claim from Jarvis, deprecating the get events action.
solution: Use the new Activity Log query feature in Azure Support Center (ASC). Verified working for Mooncake ASC at azuresupportcenter.chinacloudapi.cn. Navigate to Resource Explorer > subscription > Azure Activity Logs - New tab.
sourceRef: MCVKB/General/[MCVKB]How to query the activity logs with the new.md

symptom: Application Insights availability test (URL ping, multi-step test) not available in Mooncake. Customer needs custom URL monitoring solution.
rootCause: Availability test feature is not supported in Mooncake (Azure China). Only Code Base approach is supported for PaaS monitoring.
solution: Implement custom availability test using TimerTrigger Azure Function with Application Insights SDK: 1) Create TimerTrigger Function (e.g. every 5 min). 2) Configure TelemetryClient with Mooncake endpoint (ServerTelemetryChannel EndpointAddress). 3) Use HttpClient to ping target URL. 4) Track results via TelemetryClient.TrackAvailability(AvailabilityTelemetry). 5) Call TelemetryClient.Flush(). This replicates URL ping test functionality. Can be extended to monitor any custom resource.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Web/======3.Application Insights=================/3.2 如何定制Application Insights监控自定义得资源.md

symptom: Host metrics showing dotted line on Azure portal. PG requests monitoring agent logs from host node for investigation/escalation.
rootCause: Host monitoring agent logs (MaMetricsExtensionEtw*.tsf files) are stored at C:\Resources\MonitoringStore\Tables\ on host nodes and are overwritten within ~24 hours. Logs must be collected promptly after issue is reported.
solution: Method 1 (Jarvis - preferred): Browse Jarvis link (jarvis-west.dc.ad.msft.net/881A73F6), filter by host node IP (obtainable from ASC), download MaMetricsExtensionEtw*.tsf from C:\Resources\MonitoringStore\Tables\. Then enable diagnostics on a Windows VM, copy table2csv.exe from C:\Packages\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Diagnostics.IaaSDiagnostics\<version>\monitor\x64\, run table2csv.exe against .tsf file to generate .csv. Method 2 (Escort session): Apply for escort session to host node, copy .tsf files, convert on node using D:\Packages\<AgentPackage>\MonitoringAgent\table2csv.exe. Share resulting .csv with PG via IcM.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======VM&SCIM======/7.6 [Host Metrics] How to get monitoring agent log.md

symptom: Need to retrieve action group configuration details for troubleshooting alert notification issues (e.g. notifications not received, wrong recipients).
solution: Use Jarvis "Get Resource" query with resource provider namespace "MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/ACTIONGROUPS" and API version "2017-04-01". This returns the full configuration of all action groups in the subscription including email/SMS/webhook receivers and their enabled status.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Alert_action groups/How to get action group details.md

symptom: Need to verify whether an alert rule actually fired and search historical alert trigger records for a subscription.
solution: Use Jarvis link (jarvis-west-int.cloudapp.net/A6F1405E) with ScopeID set to the subscription ID. Filter by ruleTriggered to find triggered rules. Alternatively, use Azure Support Center (ASC) alert diagnostics for trigger verification.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Alert_action groups/How to search alert trigger records.md

symptom: Deleting the Log Analytics workspace linked to AKS Defender sensor, then restarting the AKS cluster causes the cluster to report ARM resource not found error, unable to connect to API server. Cluster appears unrecoverable from portal or kubectl.
rootCause: AKS has two types of linked Log Analytics workspaces: Monitor addon and Defender. Defender sensor has a hard dependency on its linked workspace. Deleting the workspace breaks the ARM resource reference; upon cluster restart the dependency cannot be resolved.
solution: If AKS has NOT been restarted after workspace deletion: (1) For Monitor addon: disable monitoring addon or recreate the exact same Log Analytics workspace. (2) For Defender: run 'az aks update --disable-defender' then 'az aks update --enable-defender'. To change Defender workspace: use --defender-config with JSON {"logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceId": "<id>"}. If AKS HAS been restarted: engage PG for cluster recovery (follow-up needed).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Case Study###/[AKS]Log Analytics workspace related questions.md

symptom: Container Insights logs incomplete with high ingestion latency. fluent-bit.log shows '[warn] [input] tail.0 paused(mem buf overlimit)' warnings repeatedly. E2E ingestion delay can reach hours.
rootCause: Default fluent-bit configuration in ama-logs agent has conservative memory buffer limits (tail_mem_buf_limit=10MB, tail_buf_chunksize/maxsize=32KB). High log volume causes buffer overflow, pausing log tail collection.
solution: Deploy container-azm-ms-agentconfig ConfigMap with adjusted fluent-bit parameters: log_flush_interval_secs=1 (default 15), tail_mem_buf_limit_megabytes=10, tail_buf_chunksize_megabytes=1, tail_buf_maxsize_megabytes=1, tail_ignore_older=5m. Warning: increasing limits raises ama pod CPU/memory usage; default pod limit is cpu:500m memory:750Mi. If OOM occurs, PG can raise resource limits via IcM backend. Use KQL to measure latency: extend E2EIngestionLatency = ingestion_time() - TimeGenerated, AgentLatency = _TimeReceived - TimeGenerated.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Case Study###/[AKS][Container Insights] Missing logs.md

symptom: ama-logs daemonset/replicaset pods experiencing OOMKill and frequent restarts in AKS Container Insights.
rootCause: Default ama-logs pod resource limits are insufficient for high-volume log collection. Previous PG backend adjustment workflow for resource limits has been disabled.
solution: Apply container-azm-ms-agentconfig ConfigMap with tuned mdsd_config settings: monitoring_max_event_rate=50000 (default 20K eps), backpressure_memory_threshold_in_mb=1500 (default 3500MB), upload_max_size_in_mb=10 (default 2MB), upload_frequency_seconds=1 (default 60), compression_level=0. Steps: (1) Check if configmap exists: kubectl get cm -n kube-system | grep container-azm-ms-agentconfig. (2) If not, download from raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/Docker-Provider/ci_prod/kubernetes/container-azm-ms-agentconfig.yaml. (3) Uncomment and modify the agent_settings.mdsd_config section. (4) Apply configmap.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/#Container Insights/##Insight addon ama pod OOM.md

symptom: Need guidance on proper alerting thresholds for AKS ETCD database size to prevent control plane performance degradation.
solution: Set alert when ETCD DB size exceeds 2GiB (indicates room for optimization and potential performance degradation). Limits: paid clusters = 8GiB, free clusters = 4GiB. Use percentage-based thresholds: 25% for paid clusters, 50% for free clusters. Reference: AKS best practices monitoring docs and eng.ms Etcd Customer Communication wiki.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/#Container Insights/##Monitor CCP.md

symptom: Need to collect Container Insights agent logs for troubleshooting ama-logs issues (missing data, high latency, pod crashes).
solution: Use official log collection tool: wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/Docker-Provider/ci_prod/scripts/troubleshoot/LogCollection/AgentLogCollection.sh && bash ./AgentLogCollection.sh. Prerequisites: Linux/bash environment connected to AKS cluster with kube-system namespace permissions. Install kubectl via 'az aks install-cli' if needed. Tool collects: ama-logs-daemonset logs, ama-logs-replicaset logs, DCR configs, mdsd configs, prometheus configs, configmap, node info, pod descriptions, process listings.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/#Container Insights/How to collect logs for Container Insights.md

symptom: Azure Monitor for AVD ARM template deployment fails with Deployment template validation failed: Template parameter JToken type is not valid. Expected String, Uri. Actual Object.
rootCause: ARM template parameter expects a string (workspace resource ID) but receives an object due to incorrect parameter binding in the Azure Monitor for AVD workbook deployment.
solution: Configure data collection directly in Log Analytics workspace: (1) Go to LAW Agents Configuration, (2) Add Windows Performance Counters and Windows Event Logs manually, (3) Set up log alert rules separately via Azure Monitor Alerts. Ensure workspace is properly linked to AVD host pool before deployment.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 4 - 22th Nov 2021/[Philip] Lab verification - Use Azure Monitor for.md

symptom: Log search alert with metric measurement type fails to trigger or returns unexpected results. KQL query output missing required AggregatedValue column.
rootCause: Metric measurement alert rules require the KQL query to output an AggregatedValue column via summarize. Without this specific column name, the alert engine cannot evaluate the metric threshold.
solution: Ensure KQL query includes summarize AggregatedValue=<aggregation>(field) by bin(TimeGenerated, period). Example: Perf | where CounterName == 'Free Megabytes' | summarize AggregatedValue=min(CounterValue/1024) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d). The alert Period setting controls query time range, NOT the time filter in KQL.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Log Analytics/Sample log query/Export Log A data with log alerts and automation r.md

symptom: Azure Support Center (ASC) for OperationalInsights in Mooncake has limited troubleshooting features compared to Global Azure.
rootCause: Feature gap between Global Azure and Mooncake ASC for OperationalInsights/Log Analytics. Not all diagnostic features ported to sovereign cloud.
solution: Fall back to Jarvis for diagnostics: (1) Check inmem worker role processing events via Jarvis link C2A69DC6, (2) Check if logs sent to OMS/ODS via Jarvis link DDF11900. Add workspaceId filter. Check numberOfRecordsWritten for non-zero values. If zero, escalate to Azure Monitor Team.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Log Analytics/Troubleshooting/Azure Support Center.md

symptom: Log Analytics workspace reached daily cap (daily data volume limit). All additional data sent to the ingestion pipeline is dropped for the remainder of the day.
rootCause: Workspace configured with a daily cap on billable data. Once the cap is reached, ODS stops ingesting new data until the next day reset.
solution: 1) Verify daily cap hit via Operation table: Operation | where OperationStatus in ('Error','Failed','Warning') | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by Detail, OperationStatus. 2) Check daily cap setting in workspace properties. 3) If business-critical, increase or remove the daily cap. 4) ADO Wiki ref: How-to: Check if the daily cap was reached.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Log Analytics/Troubleshooting/Log A data ingestion flow.md

symptom: Data dropped in Log Analytics with error: 'The number of custom fields 511 is above the limit of 510 fields per data type'. Custom log or AzureDiagnostics table exceeds custom field limit.
rootCause: Each Log Analytics table has a maximum of 500-510 custom fields. When a resource sends data with more unique columns than the limit allows, ingestion drops the data.
solution: 1) Validate custom fields limit: ADO Wiki 'How-to: Validate if custom fields limit is being reached'. 2) If limit is hit, request PG to increase limit: ADO Wiki 'How-to: Escalate AzureDiagnostic table exception'. 3) Consider switching from AzureDiagnostics mode to resource-specific tables to reduce field count per table.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Log Analytics/Troubleshooting/Log A data ingestion flow.md

symptom: Diagnostics data missing or incomplete in Log Analytics workspace. Azure resource logs not appearing in expected tables.
rootCause: Data pipeline issue in ODS or InMem components of the Log Analytics ingestion pipeline. Possible causes: data not reaching InMem, custom field limit, invalid data format from resource provider, or service-side outage.
solution: Follow escalation flow: 1) Confirm data reached InMem (ADO Wiki: How-to Check if Diagnostics data arrived in InMem). 2) If not in InMem, check ODS quota. 3) Check custom field limit for AzureDiagnostics table. 4) Check for ingestion errors (ADO Wiki: HT How to check for ingestion errors). 5) Check for invalid data format. 6) Before IcM, check Azure Monitor Status Blog and Outages channel for known issues. 7) If confirmed ODS/InMem issue, raise IcM to Ingestion team.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Log Analytics/Troubleshooting/Log Ingestion Issue.md

symptom: High latency or delay in Azure resource logs appearing in Log Analytics workspace. Diagnostics data takes excessively long to become queryable.
rootCause: Latency can occur in ODS or InMem pipeline components. Need to determine which component is causing delay.
solution: 1) Review ingestion time concepts: docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/data-ingestion-time. 2) Follow ADO Wiki: How-to Check the latency or delay. 3) For OBO latency, query azureinsights.kusto.windows.net Insights database. 4) For InMem latency, query omsgenevatlm.kusto.windows.net OperationInsights_InMem_PROD or use Jarvis portal.microsoftgeneva.com/s/9FD9C196. 5) Before IcM, check Azure Monitor Status Blog and Outages Teams channel. 6) If confirmed, raise IcM to Ingestion team.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Log Analytics/Troubleshooting/Log Ingestion Issue.md

symptom: Failed to update diagnostics for resource with error: 'Please register the subscription with Microsoft.Insights'. Diagnostic settings configuration fails.
rootCause: The Microsoft.Insights resource provider is not registered on the subscription. This is required for Azure diagnostics to work.
solution: Register the resource provider: 1) Azure Portal → Subscriptions → select subscription → Resource Providers → find Microsoft.insights → click Register. 2) For PowerShell errors about WorkspaceId parameter not found, update Azure PowerShell to v2.3.0 or later.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Metrics/Microsoft.Insights not registered.md

symptom: Customer asks for detailed calculation mechanism of Azure Advisor Cost Optimization recommendations (VM right-size/shutdown). Unclear how P95 CPU, memory, network thresholds work.
rootCause: Azure Advisor uses P95th percentile of max CPU/memory/network utilization over 7-day period. CPU sampled every 30s → aggregated to 5min → 30min → 48 values/day × 7 days = 336 values. Shutdown: CPU P95 <3% AND network <2%. Resize: CPU <20% (configurable), target <80% non-user-facing / <40% user-facing. Memory threshold: 80% user-facing / 60% non-user-facing.
solution: Explain P95 calculation: 336 sample values over 7 days. Shutdown criteria: CPU P95 <3% + network <2% (memory not considered). Resize criteria: CPU + memory + network analyzed, bottleneck metric determines target SKU. Portal CPU filter (5-20%) only filters displayed recommendations, doesn't change target. User-facing vs non-user-facing classification is automatic via MSR algorithm.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======VM&SCIM======/19.1 How Cost Optimization works in Azure Advisor.md

symptom: Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) installed on Windows client device (non-Azure VM) for Azure China fails to send data to Log Analytics workspace. Registry shows Cloud_Env=Unspecified despite using cloudenvironment=azurechina parameter.
rootCause: Incorrect MSI parameter name used during installation. The parameter 'cloudenvironment' is not recognized by AMA MSI installer. The correct parameter is 'CLOUDENV' (case-sensitive MSI property).
solution: Uninstall existing AMA via Control Panel. Reinstall using correct command: msiexec /i AzureMonitorAgentClientSetup.msi /qf CLOUDENV="Azure China". Must use Command Prompt (not PowerShell). Use /qf for verbose logging. Verify Cloud_Env registry value changes from Unspecified to correct value after reinstall.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Azure Monitoring Agent/AMA on Windows client devices.md

symptom: Need to collect Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) logs on Linux VM for troubleshooting data collection issues.
solution: Collect AMA logs from: extension /var/log/azure/Microsoft.Azure.Monitor.AzureMonitorLinuxAgent/; waagent.log; AMA extension /var/lib/waagent/Microsoft.Azure.Monitor.AzureMonitorLinuxAgent-ver/; mdsd logs /var/opt/microsoft/azuremonitoragent/log/; events /var/opt/microsoft/azuremonitoragent/events/; config /etc/opt/microsoft/azuremonitoragent/mdsd.xml. tar+gzip for PG.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Azure Monitoring Agent/Log collection.md

symptom: AMA Send data to Storage Account/Event Hub preview feature not working or being deprecated.
rootCause: PG confirmed AMA direct-upload-to-Storage/EventHub preview will be entirely removed. For Storage, Log Analytics Auxiliary tier is replacement. For Event Hub, future cloud ingestion pathway may be revisited.
solution: Inform customer feature is deprecated. For Storage: migrate to Log Analytics Auxiliary tier. For Event Hub: use ADX as destination. Troubleshooting: check AMA running, UAI with Storage Blob Data Contributor, DCR association, syslog path, network.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Azure Monitoring Agent/[Feature Verification] Send data to Storage Accoun.md

symptom: Customer needs to query Log Analytics data via REST API in Mooncake to export >30000 rows. Confused about API permission vs RBAC.
rootCause: Log Analytics REST API requires Azure RBAC on workspace, NOT AAD API permissions. API permissions alone insufficient for client_credentials. Mooncake token endpoint: login.partner.microsoftonline.cn, resource: https://api.loganalytics.azure.cn/.
solution: Register App, assign RBAC Log Analytics Reader on workspace. Get token: POST login.partner.microsoftonline.cn/<tenant>/oauth2/token. Query: POST api.loganalytics.azure.cn/v1/workspaces/<id>/query. API supports up to 500K rows.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/KB - Log A Query Data using REST API.md

symptom: VMs sending AzureMetrics to LA workspace but no DCR or VM data source visible. Hidden diagnostic settings causing unwanted ingestion.
rootCause: Hidden diagnostic settings named setbypolicy created by Azure Policy remediation on VMs. Not visible in Monitor Diagnostic Settings UI, only via ASC Diagnostic blade or REST API.
solution: Check AzureMetrics table by _ResourceId. Check ASC Diagnostic blade for hidden diag settings. Delete via Invoke-AzRestMethod DELETE on diagnosticSettings/setbypolicy. Verify policy no longer applies. Known Issue ADO#96148.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/Look for VM data source of AzureMetrics in workspa.md

symptom: Container Insights shows no data or incorrect metrics in AKS Insights blade. ama-logs pods show connection errors in fluentd.log.
rootCause: Firewall or private DNS blocking AMA/Container Insights required endpoints (global.handler.control.monitor.azure.cn). DNS resolution fails preventing data upload.
solution: Check live data vs kubectl. Login to ama-logs-rs pod, check fluentd.log. Test endpoint: curl -v global.handler.control.monitor.azure.cn. Configure firewall/DNS to allow AMA endpoints. Collect logs via kubectl cp.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/[2507260030000162]Container Insight has no output.md

symptom: Metric alert shows only Resolved notifications without preceding Fired. Alert state incorrect after rule config change.
rootCause: Modifying metric alert rule dimensions/thresholds can corrupt alert state. Removed dimension value causes direct Resolved without Fired transition.
solution: Query Kusto azalertsmcchn2 traces by AlertRuleId. Check Jarvis for state change keywords. Fix: disable/re-enable alert rule to reset. Check recent rule modifications. TSGs on ADO wiki.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/[Alert Status]How to get status change history fro.md

symptom: Cannot cross-query between ADX and Log Analytics workspace in Mooncake. Docs show wrong endpoint.
rootCause: Official docs show Global endpoint ade.loganalytics.io which does not work in Mooncake. Correct Mooncake endpoint: adx.loganalytics.azure.cn.
solution: ADX to LA: use https://adx.loganalytics.azure.cn/subscriptions/<sub>/resourcegroups/<rg>/providers/microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces/<name>. Need LA Reader role. LA to ADX via adx(): need Reader + ADX Database Viewer.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/[LogA]ADX_LA workspace access.md

symptom: Duplicated entries in custom log table in Log Analytics. Same log lines ingested multiple times with different _ItemId.
rootCause: Known platform issue in Log Analytics ingestion pipeline. _ItemId differs between duplicates. PG acknowledged with fix deployment.
solution: Verify duplicates via hash(dynamic_to_json(pack_all())). Calculate duplicate %. Confirm _ItemId differs. Check daily volume. Escalate to PG with ICM. TSG: CSS wiki duplicate records identification.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/[LogA]Duplicated logs.md

symptom: Customer using Log Analytics workspace to monitor AKS wants to know how much billable data (data size) is collected by each AKS service per day.
rootCause: Standard Usage table shows total workspace-level data but does not break down by AKS container service. Need to use find operator to cross-query ContainerLog and correlate by ContainerName.
solution: Use KQL: (1) Usage table for total billable GB: Usage | where IsBillable == true | summarize TotalVolumeGB = sum(Quantity)/1000. (2) find operator for per-AKS-service breakdown: find where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) project _BilledSize, _IsBillable, ContainerName | where _IsBillable | extend CN = tostring(split(ContainerName, '/')[1]) | summarize TotalGB=sum(_BilledSize)/1e9 by CN.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/[LogA][Query] To get the amount of billable data s.md

symptom: Custom metrics on Azure VM/VMSS in Mooncake are viewable in Azure Portal (fetched from storage account), but cannot be queried via az monitor metrics list-namespaces or used for alert rules. Custom namespace not showing.
rootCause: Custom metrics Sinks feature (sends metrics to Azure Monitor backend for CLI/PowerShell query and alert rule creation) is in Preview on Global Azure but NOT available in Azure China (Mooncake).
solution: In Mooncake, use WAD/LAD diagnostics extension to collect VM/VMSS performance metrics to Storage Account. Metrics viewable in portal via storage SAS-based fetch but alert rules on custom metrics not supported. Consider log-based alerts via Log Analytics as alternative.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/[Monitor]Custom metric on Azure VM_VMSS.md

symptom: Customer reports many HTTP 529 errors in Azure Monitor Metrics API calls for their subscription.
rootCause: HTTP 529 is throttling/overloaded response from Metrics API backend. In the analyzed case, 529 errors were only 0.1% (5086 out of ~5.96M total requests), indicating normal transient throttling.
solution: Use Kusto on HttpIncomingRequests: filter by subscriptionId and operationName containing MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/METRICS, summarize count() by httpStatusCode to calculate error percentage. If 529 is less than 1% of total, it is expected. If significantly higher, escalate to Monitor PG.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Case Study/get the sum of curtain return code within a sub in.md

symptom: Metrics connections in Log Analytics workspace get disconnected automatically. Diagnostic settings for metrics data to Log Analytics appear to work initially but then disconnect.
rootCause: Metrics export to Log Analytics workspace is not supported in Mooncake (Azure China). PG confirmed and hid the All metrics setting in Monitor Diagnostic settings for Mooncake portal. IcM 119118816.
solution: Metrics export to Log Analytics not available in Mooncake by design. Do not configure metric diagnostic settings to send to LA workspace. Use platform metrics directly via Azure Monitor Metrics Explorer or REST API instead.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Log Analytics/Known issues/119118816 Metrics connections in workspace get dis.md

symptom: OMS agent for Linux generates Core dump files in /var/opt/omi/run. omiserver.log shows FATAL abnormal termination and WARNING child process terminated abnormally. Kernel log shows segfault in libcontainer.so.
rootCause: Docker container provider in omsagent 1.16 is not compatible with docker-ce 25.x. Confirmed via GitHub issue microsoft/omi#755 and IcM 476872192.
solution: Either (1) reinstall omsagent with skipDockerProviderInstall flag: az vm extension set --settings '{"skipDockerProviderInstall": true}', or (2) upgrade omsagent to version 1.17.2+.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Log Analytics/Known issues/OMS agent for Linux has segfault with docker-ce 25.md

symptom: Log Analytics Agent for Windows versions not supporting SHA-2 code signing will stop communicating with all registered services (including Defender ATP) after deprecation date (August 17, 2020).
rootCause: Azure Log Analytics team deprecated SHA-1 code signing for Windows agent. Agents without SHA-2 support cannot authenticate with backend services after the cutoff date.
solution: Upgrade Log Analytics Agent (MMA) for Windows to a version that supports SHA-2 code signing. Check agent version and upgrade instructions at: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/updates/action-required-log-analytics-agent-for-windows-will-enforce-sha2-signing-on-5182020/
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/MONITOR/## Log Analytics/Known issues/Windows sha-1 code signing deprecated and required.md

symptom: Client resolves Azure Monitor endpoint FQDN to incorrect private IP address when using AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)
rootCause: Multiple AMPLS resources exist on the same VNET or peered VNETs, violating the single-AMPLS-per-network principle, causing DNS override
solution: 1) Check for other AMPLS resources in subscription via ASC Properties > Private Links 2) Validate expected IP from PE DNS zone records matches nslookup result 3) If mismatch, look for competing AMPLS in peered VNETs or other subscriptions 4) Involve Azure Networking team if source of incorrect IP not found
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Investigate incorrect private IP resolution for an AMPLS Azure Monitor resource

symptom: Stale Private DNS Zone preventing proper name resolutions for Application Insights with AMPLS - telemetry ingestion fails due to DNS resolution failure
rootCause: Private DNS Zone records become stale/outdated, causing Azure Monitor endpoint FQDNs to fail DNS resolution or resolve to wrong IPs
solution: 1) Validate DNS connectivity for Application Insights endpoints 2) Check Private DNS Zone records for stale entries 3) Update or recreate Private DNS Zone records to match current Private Endpoint IPs 4) Verify resolution with nslookup after cleanup
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Support Topics/I receive no data or cannot run queries/DNS not resolving to correct IP address

symptom: No telemetry data flowing to Application Insights after configuring AMPLS - ingestion endpoint resolves to public IP instead of private IP
rootCause: Web app/function hosted on App Service without VNET support (e.g., free tier) cannot resolve AMPLS private endpoints - App Service free tier does NOT offer VNET support
solution: 1) Ensure web app/function is on at least a private network with VNET support 2) Upgrade from App Service free tier to a plan that supports VNET Integration (Basic or higher) 3) Configure VNET Integration for the App Service 4) Verify DNS resolution returns private IP after VNET integration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Support Topics/I receive no data or cannot run queries/No data in Log Analytics, Metrics, or Application Insights

symptom: Cannot add Action Group directly to Built-In Azure Monitor Alerts (Azure Backup, Stack Edge, Stack HCI, Data Box Gateway, SCOM MI, Azure Local) - no option to attach Action Group
rootCause: Built-In Azure Monitor Alerts have no resource ID because they skip the Alerts Data Plane; the onboarded service processes alerting logic in its own environment, so there is no alert rule resource to attach an Action Group to
solution: Use Alert Processing Rules instead of directly adding Action Groups. Alert Processing Rules can match Built-In alerts and route them to the desired Action Group. Exception: Event Grid Built-In Alerts have an Event Subscription resource ID, so Action Groups CAN be directly added.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Built-In Azure Monitor Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert (LSA) fires false alerts or misses alerts due to late arriving data - ingestion delay causes inconsistent evaluation results
rootCause: Ingestion delay of logs to Kusto causes data to arrive after the alert evaluation window has passed; retries increase load on Draft/Kusto backend
solution: LSA uses sliding window optimization: the query is automatically modified to cover multiple windows of timespans in a single execution, simulating additional retries. Each result appears in multiple executions but alerts fire only once per bucket (both stateful and stateless). The cutoff time for retries is 30 minutes. Prepended query uses LSA_frequency, LSA_winNumber, LSA_windowTimeSpan variables with bin_at() and mv-expand to create overlapping evaluation windows.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Alerts optimization flow and query manipulation

symptom: Log search alert rule querying across tenants fails or stops working - cross-tenant target resource no longer supported
rootCause: Since March 2024, cross-tenant queries for log search alert rules are no longer supported unless Azure Lighthouse is used to assign RBAC roles across tenants
solution: Use Azure Lighthouse to assign RBAC roles with adequate access across tenants for the alert rule identity (default or managed). Direct cross-tenant target resource is deprecated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Data Access for Log Search Alert Rules

symptom: Log search alert rule evaluation fails with 403 Forbidden 'InsufficientAccessError' / 'NspValidationFailedError' when workspace is in Network Security Perimeter
rootCause: Alert rule is not part of the same Network Security Perimeter (NSP) as the Log Analytics workspace it queries
solution: Add the log search alert rule to the same NSP as the Log Analytics workspace. If resource-centric and not in NSP, evaluation succeeds but data from NSP-protected workspace is silently hidden.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Data Access for Log Search Alert Rules

symptom: Log search alert rule using linked storage account fails after July 2024 or when using system-assigned managed identity with linked storage
rootCause: Since July 2024, linked storage requires managed identity; only user-assigned managed identity is supported (not system-assigned). Also requires Storage Blob Data Contributor role, not just Contributor or Reader.
solution: 1) Configure user-assigned managed identity on the alert rule 2) Assign Storage Blob Data Contributor role on the linked storage account (write operations needed for storing log queries) 3) Assign Reader role on the workspace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Data Access for Log Search Alert Rules

symptom: Log search alert rule evaluation succeeds but returns no data - data is hidden from resource-centric alert rule
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace access control mode is 'Require workspace permissions' and the alert rule identity lacks workspace-level permissions; data from that workspace is silently hidden
solution: Grant the alert rule identity Reader role on the Log Analytics workspace, or change workspace access control mode to 'Use resource or workspace permissions'. Check access control mode in Azure Support Center > Workspace Settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Data Access for Log Search Alert Rules

symptom: Log search alert rule cross-service query to ADX or ARG fails - default identity does not work for cross-service queries
rootCause: Default identity is not supported for cross-service (Azure Data Explorer / Azure Resource Graph) log search alert rules; managed identity is required
solution: Switch to system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity. For ADX: Reader on workspace + Reader on ADX resource + database viewer on all queried databases. For ARG: Reader on workspace + Reader on queried resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Log Data Access for Log Search Alert Rules

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Unavailable with NO_REASON for alert rules with evaluation frequency > 15 minutes (e.g. 30 min, 1 hour). Status appears as unavailable but alert is functioning normally.
rootCause: Alert rules that run less frequently than every 15 minutes will not provide health status updates to Resource Health. This is by design - the health check interval is 15 minutes, so lower-frequency rules naturally appear unavailable between evaluations.
solution: This is expected behavior, not a real issue. Verify the alert rule evaluation frequency. If frequency > 15 min, the unavailable status is normal. Also may appear for newly created alerts until first evaluation completes. Doc: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/log-alert-rule-health
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Degraded - Setting up resource health (RESOURCE_STATE_NOT_IN_DATAMART) after alert rule creation.
rootCause: Newly created alert rules need time for the first evaluation to complete before Resource Health can report status. The health data has not been populated in the datamart yet.
solution: Wait until the first evaluation of the alert rule completes. This is a transient state that resolves automatically after the first successful evaluation cycle.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Degraded - Query validation error (DRAFT_QUERY_VALIDATION). Alert evaluation keeps failing with validation error.
rootCause: Most commonly caused by customer attempting to create an alert rule targeting Basic Logs tables. Log search alerts do not support Basic Logs data plan - only Analytics plan tables are supported.
solution: Check if the target table uses Basic Logs data plan. If so, switch the table to Analytics plan or restructure the alert to query an Analytics plan table. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/basic-logs-configure?tabs=portal-1#compare-the-basic-and-analytics-log-data-plans
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Degraded - Workspace not found (DRAFT_WORKSPACE_NOT_FOUND) or Application Insights resource not found (DRAFT_APPLICATION_NOT_FOUND).
rootCause: The alert scope resource (Log Analytics workspace or Application Insights) was moved, renamed, or deleted after the alert rule was created. The alert rule still references the old resource.
solution: Recreate the alert rule with a valid target Log Analytics workspace or Application Insights resource. The original alert rule cannot be repaired if the scope resource no longer exists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Degraded - Query is throttled (DRAFT_THROTTLING). Alert evaluation keeps failing with HTTP 429 throttling error.
rootCause: The alert rule KQL query exceeds Log Analytics query rate limits or concurrency limits, causing throttling (HTTP 429).
solution: Review and optimize the query to reduce resource consumption. Check query limits at https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits#user-query-throttling. Reduce query complexity, narrow time range, or reduce evaluation frequency.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert evaluation fails with Active alerts limit exceeded (LIMIT_DAILY_ALERT_COUNT_EXCEEDED). Alert stops firing new alerts.
rootCause: A single alert rule can fire up to 12,000 times within 48 hours (documented as 6,000 fired alerts per rule at a time). When this limit is exceeded, further evaluations are blocked.
solution: Reduce the number of fired alerts by: (1) adjusting alert threshold to reduce firing frequency, (2) configuring auto-resolve, (3) reviewing if the alert is too sensitive. Check limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits#alerts
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert evaluation fails with Dimension combinations limit exceeded (LIMIT_DIMENSION_COMBINATION_COUNT_EXCEEDED).
rootCause: The alert query returns too many unique dimension value combinations exceeding the limit. Stateless alerts: 6,000 dimension combinations. Stateful alerts: 300 dimension combinations.
solution: Reduce the number of dimension combinations by: (1) reducing the number of dimensions used, (2) adding filters to narrow results, (3) switching from stateful to stateless if > 300 combinations needed. Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits#alerts
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Log Search Alert Resource Health shows Degraded - NSP validation failed (DRAFT_NSP_VALIDATION). Query evaluation fails due to Network Security Perimeter restrictions.
rootCause: The Log Analytics workspace is configured with Network Security Perimeter (NSP) that only allows query access for resources within the perimeter. The log search alert rule is not included in the NSP or no inbound rules allow Alerts service to query the workspace.
solution: Adjust NSP settings: either include the log search alert rule in the perimeter, or add inbound rules to whitelist the Alerts and Action Groups IP addresses. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/network-security-perimeter
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Concepts/Resource Health for Log Search Alerts

symptom: Alert fired but Logic App action group connector fails with 'connection removed' error in workflow run history output
rootCause: The API connection for the Logic App connector (e.g., Office 365) has been removed or expired, causing the connector step to fail
solution: Navigate to Logic App Designer, find the failed connector, click 'change connection' at the bottom, and follow the wizard to sign in to the proper account to recreate the connection
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How-To Validate Logic App Action

symptom: Alert fired but Logic App workflow did not run at all - no execution appears in Logic App Run History, Action Group test returns 404 error
rootCause: The Logic App HTTP trigger URL (Callback URI) has changed or been regenerated, causing a mismatch between the Action Group's stored callback URL and the current Logic App trigger URL
solution: Compare the Callback URL in ASC (Action Group details) with the HTTP URL in the Logic App Designer's first connector ('When an HTTP request is received'). If mismatched, remove the Logic App action from the Action Group and re-add it to get the updated trigger URL
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How-To Validate Logic App Action

symptom: After May 17/18 2024, users who inherit RBAC roles through Azure AD group membership started receiving unexpected Email Resource Manager Role notification emails from Action Groups or Smart Detection (Failure Anomalies)
rootCause: Design change on May 17-18, 2024: Action Groups with Email Resource Manager Role notification type now send emails to individual users who inherit the specified RBAC role through group-level assignments. Previously, only users with direct role assignments received emails.
solution: This is by-design behavior change. Verify user's RBAC assignment type (direct vs group inheritance) via Azure Support Center → Tenant Explorer → Resource Explorer → Access Control → Check Access. If customer wants to stop emails to group-inherited users, they need to change notification type or adjust RBAC assignments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How to check which users have an ARM role to investigate the notification type Email Resource Manager Role or smart detections emails

symptom: Need to check the delivery status of an Azure Notification (email, SMS, webhook, etc.) - customer reports notification not received or wants to verify notification was sent
rootCause: Notification delivery issues can be caused by rate limiting, invalid recipient, mechanism failure, or upstream alert evaluation issues. NotificationState and FailureReason in AzNS Kusto table reveal the exact cause.
solution: Query AzNSTransmissions_All table in Kusto (cluster: aznscluster.southcentralus.kusto.windows.net, database: AzNSPROD) using NotificationId or SubscriptionId. Check NotificationState (complete=success, failed/skipped=check FailureReason), MechanismType, CompletedTime. KQL: AzNSTransmissions_All | where NotificationId == "<id>" | sort by CreatedTime desc | project-reorder NotificationId, TransmitId, NotificationState, MechanismType, CreatedTime, CompletedTime, FailureReason, AssociatedGroupId, AdditionalInfo
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How to get status of an Azure Notification from Kusto

symptom: Need to check delivery status of an Azure Service Health Notification - customer reports not receiving Service Health alerts or wants to trace a specific Service Health event notification
rootCause: Service Health notifications flow through AzNS with UserIdentity SHR_dialtone@activitylogalertsvc.com. Delivery failure reasons visible in NotificationState and FailureReason fields.
solution: Query AzNSTransmissions in Kusto (cluster: aznscluster.southcentralus.kusto.windows.net, database: AzNSPROD) filtered by action group resource ID and UserIdentity =~ SHR_dialtone@activitylogalertsvc.com. Optionally filter by SHAMetadata contains tracking ID. KQL: AzNSTransmissions | where AssociatedGroupId =~ "<actionGroupResourceId>" | where CreatedTime > ago(7d) and UserIdentity =~ "SHR_dialtone@activitylogalertsvc.com" | where SHAMetadata contains "<trackingId>"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How to get status of an Azure Service Health Notification from Kusto

symptom: Email notification from Azure Monitor alert not received or partially delivered - need to trace email delivery through MEO (Microsoft Email Orchestrator, formerly AEO)
rootCause: Email delivery failure through MEO can be caused by SMTP relay rejection, invalid recipient domain, or message processing errors. MessageStatusHistory shows processing status (Added→Generated→Sent) and MessageDelivery shows SMTP response codes.
solution: 1) Get AEO BatchId from AzNS trace logs (search for AEOBATCH in Message property). 2) Query meoshared.westus.kusto.windows.net / AeoAnalytics: use MessageStatusHistory_All_Shared and MessageDelivery_All_Shared tables filtered by BatchId. 3) Check Status: Added=queued, Generated=email created, Sent=forwarded to SMTP. SmtpCode 250=accepted. 4) If AzNS confirms sent to MEO but email not delivered → escalate to MEO via ICM template in ASC (only after confirming AzNS processed successfully).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How to get trace logs for emails sent via MEO (Formerly AEO) in Kusto

symptom: Azure Monitor alert notification not received - suspected rate limiting on action group notifications (email, SMS, voice, webhook)
rootCause: Azure Notification Service applies rate limits to prevent notification flooding. When rate limit is reached, notification is suppressed with Need Notify = False flag.
solution: 1) Get NotificationId. 2) Trace notification in ASC or Jarvis. 3) Search diagnostic log results where Message contains "need notify". 4) If matched with "Need Notify - False", rate limiting was applied and target receiver was suppressed. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/alerts-rate-limiting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/How to identify if rate limits have been reached for an Azure Notification

symptom: Action Group runbook action may be using legacy built-in runbook (Run As Account) instead of updated V2 runbook (Managed Identities) - need to identify which version is in use
rootCause: Action Groups with built-in runbook actions (e.g. RestartAzureVM) were originally created using Run As Accounts. Updated V2 runbooks use Managed Identities. Legacy runbooks may fail when Run As Account is deprecated.
solution: Three identification methods: 1) Check runbook Jobs in Automation Account - if name contains V2 (e.g. RestartAzureVMAlertGlobalRunbookV2), it is the updated version. 2) Use Get-AzAutomationWebhook to list webhooks and match to Action Group JSON view under automationRunbookReceivers. 3) As support engineer: find webhook name in ASC Action Group details, then query OaaS Kusto cluster (oaasprodeu.northeurope.kusto.windows.net / OaaSProd) - query AzureAutomationAuditLogs + EtwAll by webhook name and correlationId. If RunbookName ends with V2, it is updated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Action Groups and Notifications/Identify if an Action Group runbook action uses a legacy built-in runbook or an updated built-in runbook

symptom: Need to analyze Activity Log Alert event evaluation history in sovereign clouds (Mooncake/Fairfax) where ASC execution history is not yet available
rootCause: Activity log alerts re-platforming is not yet completed in sovereign clouds, so ASC execution history tab is unavailable. Must use Kusto directly.
solution: Query SettingEvaluation table in sovereign Kusto clusters. Mooncake: azureinsightsmc.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn (database: azureinsightsmc). Fairfax: azureinsightsff.usgovtexas.kusto.usgovcloudapi.net (database: azureinsightsff). KQL: SettingEvaluation | where TIMESTAMP between (StartTime..EndTime) | where isnotempty(ruleTriggered) | where scopeId == alertRuleSubscriptionId | where ruleResourceGroup == alertRuleResourceGroup | where ruleName == alertRuleName | join kind=inner (EventData) on $left.eventId == $right.eventDataId | project TIMESTAMP, scopeId, ruleTriggered, eventId, category, eventTimestamp, operationName, properties
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Activity Log Alerts/How to analyze Activity Log Alert event evaluation history in Kusto for sovereign clouds

symptom: Service Health Alert rules do not fire as expected; alerts configured with invalid or outdated service names via REST API or ARM template fail silently
rootCause: A bug allowed customers to create Service Health alert rules with invalid/outdated service names through API or ARM templates. The portal Services dropdown showed valid names, but API/ARM had no validation, so rules with wrong names never matched incoming events.
solution: PG deployed a fix (rollout started Dec 2023, completed ~Feb 2024): (1) Validation now rejects invalid service names on create via API/ARM; (2) Existing rules with invalid names are automatically mapped to correct names behind the scenes (transparent to user). Customer action: run the provided PowerShell script (Azure Automation with System Managed Identity + Monitoring Reader role) to identify misconfigured rules. Valid service names available via API: GET https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.ResourceHealth/metadata?api-version=2022-10-01. Customers may start receiving alerts for services they previously didn't (because previously invalid rules are now valid). Tracking ID: JN_B-HSZ.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Activity Log Alerts/How to identify Service Health Alerts configured with invalid service names using PowerShell

symptom: Alert processing rules with filters on non-common alert schema fields (e.g., resourceName, resourceId, portalLink, Alertrulename, description, Alertcontextid) stop working after bug fix rollout; breaking change for rules using non-standard alert context payload filters
rootCause: Bug in alert processing rules allowed filtering on non-common alert schema fields in alert context (payload) filter; fix enforces only common alert schema fields as documented
solution: Migrate alert processing rule filters to use alternative filter types: Platform/Resource Health alerts → use "Resource" filter instead of resourceName/resourceId; Application Insights/Log Alerts v2 → use "Alert rule name" and "Description" filters; Log Analytics v1 → review filter after rollout as format changes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Alerts Management/How to change alert processing rule filters on alert context (payload) to match those of the common alert schema

symptom: Need to query fired alert instances programmatically at scale across multiple subscriptions
solution: Use Azure Resource Graph (ARG) via Resource Graph Explorer in Azure Portal. Query 'alertsmanagementresources' table with KQL to get alert details (severity, status, state, firedTime, etc.). Preferred over Alerts Management API for cross-subscription queries. Also available via CLI/PS/REST.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Alerts Management/How to get fired alerts using Azure Resource Graph query

symptom: Customer accidentally deleted an alert rule and needs to recover its configuration; or ASC is down and need to retrieve alert rule config
rootCause: Alert rule accidentally deleted; configuration snapshots available in Kusto
solution: Query AlertRuleTelemtry table in azalertsprodweu.westeurope cluster (Insights database) with alert rule ARM ID. Snapshots taken every few hours. For metric alerts use MetricAlertsTelemetry table. Supports log search, activity log, metric, and Prometheus rule types. Note: search query values and description are redacted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/All Alerts/How to get alert rule configuration using Kusto

symptom: Need to investigate fired alert history and notification flow for troubleshooting alert delivery issues
solution: Query AzureAlertsManagement database on azalertsprodweu.westeurope cluster. For metric alerts: traces table filtered by AlertRuleId + 'POST alerts/createorupdate' operation. For activity log/log search alerts: similar traces query filtered by MonitorCondition. Use operation_ParentId for full trace. Filter cloud_RoleName=='Microsoft.AlertsMgmt.Notification' for notification flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/All Alerts/How to get history of fired alerts from Kusto

symptom: ITSM v1 action fails with 'No such host is known' or 'Web App - Unavailable' or 'Error 403 - This web app is stopped'
rootCause: Server error from ServiceNow side - ServiceNow instance is unavailable or stopped
solution: Customer should reach out to ServiceNow support to investigate their instance availability
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/ITSM/How to interpret ITSM v1 action failures in ASC

symptom: ITSM v1 action fails with 'AccessToken and RefreshToken invalid. User needs to authenticate again'
rootCause: ITSM connector token expired or integration rights missing for OMS_App_User
solution: 1. Re-enter credentials in ITSM connector configuration and re-sync. 2. Ensure integration user (OMS_App_User) was created and configured correctly per documentation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/ITSM/How to interpret ITSM v1 action failures in ASC

symptom: ITSM v1 action fails with 'Failed to retrieve connection configuration'
rootCause: Newly created ITSM Connector instance has not finished initial sync or connector was not defined correctly
solution: Re-enter credentials in ITSM connector configuration and re-sync
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/ITSM/How to interpret ITSM v1 action failures in ASC

symptom: ITSM v1 action fails with 'Target record not found. Operation against file incident was aborted by Business Rule'
rootCause: ServiceNow Business Rules (server-side logic) are rejecting/ignoring the incident creation
solution: Customer should reach out to ServiceNow support to review and update the business logic/rules
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/ITSM/How to interpret ITSM v1 action failures in ASC

symptom: ITSM v1 action fails with 'Target record not found. Row transform ignored by onBefore script'
rootCause: Custom script deployed in ServiceNow or modified OMS Integrator app code is causing incidents to be ignored
solution: Customer should reach out to ServiceNow support to disable or adjust the custom script
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/ITSM/How to interpret ITSM v1 action failures in ASC

symptom: Log search alerts fire with delay; customer observes 'delay in mins' value in ASC Execution History
rootCause: Log Search Alerts have built-in retry mechanism (30s, 5min, 30min intervals) that re-queries for late-arriving data. Non-optimized flow retries same period; optimized flow uses sliding window model. Maximum retry cutoff is 30 minutes.
solution: 1. Check Execution History in ASC for the fired alert. 2. Find the Operation ID and check Alert latency in Execution Summary. 3. Review Execution Logs for retry details. 4. Check if alert rule is optimized via Properties > Is Optimized field. 5. Late-arriving data (<30 min) is expected to cause delay due to retry mechanism.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to Validate Log Search Alerts fired with delay - Retry mechanism
Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to interpret log alert evaluation failure messages
symptom: Log alert evaluation fails with 403 Forbidden: Given credentials do not have access to read logs (no NSP details)
solution: Customer edits the alert rule (even a simple enable/disable) with user credentials that have read access to query the logs. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/manage-access
rootCause: For non-managed-identity rules, permissions are based on the last user who edited the rule. That user lost or never had read permissions on the target workspace/table.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to interpret log alert evaluation failure messages
symptom: Log alert evaluation fails with 403 Forbidden and NspValidationFailedError: Access denied due to failed Network Security Perimeter validation
solution: Adjust NSP settings: either include the log search alert rule in the perimeter, or whitelist Alerts and Action Groups IP addresses in NSP inbound rules.
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace is configured with NSP that requires resources to be within the perimeter. The log search alert rule is not part of the NSP or no inbound rules allow Alerts to query the workspace.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to interpret log alert evaluation failure messages
symptom: Log alert evaluation fails with 400 BadRequest: Failed to resolve scalar expression named <column>; or column not found in Measure/Dimensions
solution: Ensure the column referenced in Measure/Dimensions is returned by the query, or change the measure/dimension to a field that the query actually returns.
rootCause: Column selected in Measure or Dimensions is not returned by the alert rule query. Rule was created before validation was deployed, or customer used -skipQueryValidation flag.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to interpret log alert evaluation failure messages
symptom: Log alert evaluation fails with 404 NotFound: The workspace could not be found / The application could not be found
solution: Delete the alert rule and create a new one with the updated scope pointing to the correct workspace/AI component.
rootCause: The Log Analytics workspace or Application Insights component that holds the data was moved or deleted.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to interpret log alert evaluation failure messages
symptom: Log alert with managed identity fails with 403 Forbidden or identity-related errors (unexpected response payload, resource does not have access, identity not found)
solution: 1) Grant the managed identity read access on all queried workspaces/ADX clusters. 2) For inconsistency errors, disable/enable the rule. 3) If identity is missing, add a managed identity to the rule. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview
rootCause: Managed identity associated with the alert rule lacks read access on all workspaces/data sources queried, or identity was deleted/detached, or MSI state inconsistency between ARM and LSA.

solution: Add ingestion_time() filter to reproduce evaluation-time data: append | where TimeGenerated >= datetime(QueryStartTime) and TimeGenerated <= datetime(QueryEndTime) | where ingestion_time() < datetime(FinalSearchExecutionTime) to the customer query. For join/union queries, add the filter to each table separately. Run in ASC Query Customer Data tab with custom time range.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to validate query execution results on false or missed Log alert
rootCause: Ingestion delay of logs to Kusto causes alert query evaluation against partial data. Results differ because more data arrives after evaluation time.
symptom: Log alert fired when it should not have (false alert) or missed firing (missed alert); re-running customer query returns different results than alert evaluation

solution: Replace bin(TimeGenerated, interval) with bin_at(TimeGenerated, interval, datetime(QueryEndTime)) when validating alert query results to match the exact bucketing used during evaluation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Log Alerts/How to validate query execution results on false or missed Log alert
rootCause: LSA service automatically converts bin() to bin_at() at runtime using query end time as fixed reference point. Manual bin() rounds to nearest interval from epoch, causing different bucket boundaries.
symptom: Log alert query using bin() function produces different time bucketing results when re-running manually vs during alert evaluation

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Metric Alerts/How to identify Red-Black mismatch causing false or missed metric alerts
rootCause: Red-Black mismatch in Geneva Metrics: metric data is not identical on both instances (red/black) of the stamp, causing inconsistent threshold evaluation results.
symptom: Metric alert fires falsely or misses firing; metric value differs between red and black instances of Geneva Metrics stamp
solution: In ASC evaluation logs, add the second instance (-red or -black suffix) to the Datacenter filter, then filter by LogTag in (ThresholdViolated,CombinationEvaluation). Compare ThresholdViolated and metric values between instances. If one shows ThresholdViolated and the other does not → confirmed red-black mismatch (escalate to PG).

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Metric Alerts/How to use the Metric Delay dashboard to identify late arriving data
rootCause: Late arriving metric data: metric ingestion latency exceeded the 3-minute buffer that metric alerts enforce before firing. Alert evaluated against incomplete metric values.
symptom: Metric alert fired when it should not have (false alert); metric value at evaluation time differs from resource metric chart value
solution: Check MetricDelay Jarvis dashboard (https://jarvis-west.dc.ad.msft.net/dashboard/MetricTeamInternalMetrics/Sla/MetricDelay). Set Metrics Backend and Environment, filter by evaluation time. For platform metrics check Metric Delay chart; for custom metrics (CUSTOM account or azmon stamp) check Metric Puller Service absolute publication delay chart. If latency > 180 seconds at metric data window end time → confirmed false alert due to late arriving data.

symptom: Prometheus rule group updates not taking effect after modification - changes made to rule group are not being processed by the data plane
rootCause: Synchronizer component (ModifiedRuleGroupIdsSynchronizerRunner / RuleGroupsSynchronizerProcessor) failed to sync rule group updates from ARM control plane to data plane in Azalertsprodweu cluster
solution: 1) Query AlertRuleTelemtry in Azalertsprodweu/Insights with RuleArmId to get RuleUpdateTimes 2) Check synchronizer processing logs in Application Insights (promalerts-ai-privatepreview) for ModifiedRuleGroupIdsSynchronizerRunner + RuleGroupsSynchronizerProcessor entries 3) If processing logs empty, updates were not synced 4) If error message found, open CRI using "Azure Monitor | Prometheus alerts control plane" escalation template in ASC
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/How-To/Prometheus alerts (public preview)/How to check if updates to Prometheus rule group were processed from Kusto

symptom: Customer wants to restore deleted alert rules or action groups
rootCause: Most Azure Monitor alert resource types (Log Search Alert, Activity Log Alert, Service Health Alert, Metric Alerts, Smart Alert Rules) cannot be restored once deleted. Only Action Groups support restoration via ICM.
solution: For deleted Action Groups: request restoration via ICM to PG. For deleted alert rules (all types): PG can only provide last known configuration — customer must recreate the rule manually using that info.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Alerts/Known Issues/Resource Recoverability

symptom: Log alert notification does not contain search results in payload
