symptom: JavaScript SDK telemetry missing or blocked; HAR trace shows track calls with no Response Headers, or POST with no Payload; client-side telemetry stops working or never works
rootCause: CORS blocked by browser (e.g., Edge) due to tracking policy; custom domain not included in AllowTrackingForUrls browser policy list, preventing CORS preflight from completing
solution: Configure Edge browser policy AllowTrackingForUrls to include Application Insights ingestion endpoint and any custom domain names used by the app. Roll out policy change via group policy. Note: this is one possible cause — many other browser/network causes exist.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Additional Reference Material/SDK and Agents References/Validate javascript functionality

symptom: App Service codeless 自动插桩在特定时间窗口停止向 Application Insights 发送遥测数据，随后自行恢复正常
rootCause: 应用程序 bin 目录中存在冲突的 System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource.dll，导致 App Service 自动插桩扩展主动退出（back off）并停止收集遥测
solution: 通过 AppLens 'Application Insights Java Node and Dot Net Auto Instrumentation' detector 的 Data Sources tab → Run in Kusto Web Explorer，查询 WebAppsAppInsightsExtension 表中 msg 字段定位冲突 DLL；从应用 bin 目录删除冲突 DLL。参考: https://learn.microsoft.com/troubleshoot/azure/azure-monitor/app-insights/status-monitor-v2-troubleshoot#conflicting-dlls-in-an-apps-bin-directory
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Inspect App Service autoinstrumentation logs

symptom: .NET Core web app 启用 App Service codeless 自动插桩后，Application Insights 中完全没有遥测数据
rootCause: 应用使用的 .NET Core 2.2 框架版本已不再被 App Service 自动插桩支持，插桩扩展无法成功附加
solution: 升级应用至受支持的 .NET Core 框架版本。可通过 AppLens 自动插桩日志（WebAppsAppInsightsExtension 表，ProviderName 含 AspNetCore-StartupBootstrapper 等）查询 msg 字段确认原因。参考发布说明: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/codeless-app-service?tabs=aspnetcore#release-notes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Inspect App Service autoinstrumentation logs

symptom: .NET Full Framework App Service Auto-Instrumentation backed off because conflicting DLL found (Microsoft.ApplicationInsights, System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource, or Microsoft.AspNet.TelemetryCorrelation) - AppLens detector shows 'instrumentation has backed-off as it found a reference of Application Insights SDK'
rootCause: One of the conflicting DLLs (Microsoft.ApplicationInsights, System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource, Microsoft.AspNet.TelemetryCorrelation) was found in the application binaries, causing the auto-instrumentation agent to back off to avoid runtime conflicts with the existing SDK
solution: Use the troubleshooting article https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/azure-web-apps-net#troubleshooting for applications referencing any Application Insights packages; customer should remove or reconcile the conflicting SDK references to allow codeless auto-instrumentation to work
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Interpret AppLens Application Insights Auto Instrumentation Detector output

symptom: App Insights Auto-Instrumentation on App Service shows 'enabled with old version' message (Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.AzureWebSites extension detected) - this extension version is not supported
rootCause: Old auto-instrumentation extension (Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.AzureWebSites) was previously installed on the App Service and is no longer supported by the current platform
solution: 1) Clean up old Auto-Instrumentation extension on the App Service Web App (follow internal wiki guide for cleaning up old extension) 2) Re-onboard using the new auto-instrumentation experience via the Azure portal Application Insights blade
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Interpret AppLens Application Insights Auto Instrumentation Detector output

symptom: .NET Core App Service Auto-Instrumentation backed off because Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.DLL was found in the application binaries (e.g., at 'D:\home\site\wwwroot\Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.dll')
rootCause: .NET Core auto-instrumentation only backs off when Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.DLL is explicitly present; unlike .NET Framework, conflicts with System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource.DLL are handled internally by .NET Core Framework itself; Microsoft.AspNet.TelemetryCorrelation.dll is not applicable for .NET Core
solution: Use the troubleshooting article https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/azure-web-apps-net-core for .NET Core; customer should remove the explicit Microsoft.ApplicationInsights package reference from their project or use the XDT_MicrosoftApplicationInsights_PreemptSdk=1 Interop setting if they need both SDK and auto-instrumentation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Interpret AppLens Application Insights Auto Instrumentation Detector output

symptom: HTTP 5xx errors (especially 503) are visible in App Service infrastructure but NOT logged in Application Insights requests table - customer reports missing telemetry for specific error timeframe
rootCause: App Service front end handled those requests but never forwarded them to the worker role where the Application Insights SDK runs; AI SDK only captures requests processed by the App Service worker (w3wp.exe); requests terminated at the front-end layer (e.g., site scaling, graceful shutdown, front-end rejection) bypass the worker entirely and are invisible to AI
solution: 1) Obtain 'Antares Kusto Readers' and 'AppLens CSS Access' entitlements 2) In AppLens, use HTTP 5xx detector > Data Sources tab to get auto-generated query for AntaresIISLogFrontEndTable 3) Run query to confirm 5xx requests appear at front end 4) Use same ActivityIds to query AntaresIISLogWorkerTable - if requests appear in FE table but NOT worker table, the App Service never forwarded them to the application, so AI SDK never had visibility
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Trace HTTP requests on App Services platform

symptom: Customer claims Application Insights is logging incorrect or unexpected HTTP status codes for certain requests (e.g., 404 on a URL they believe should return 200)
rootCause: Application Insights reflects the actual HTTP status code returned by the App Service worker role; the SDK does not modify or misrepresent status codes - what AI logs is exactly what the worker's w3wp.exe process returned
solution: 1) Query AntaresIISLogFrontEndTable to confirm the requests and their status codes at the front end 2) Trace the same ActivityIds in AntaresIISLogWorkerTable to see what status code the worker returned 3) If both tables show the same status code as what AI logged, confirm to customer that AI is correctly reflecting the worker response 4) If status code is wrong at the worker level, escalate to App Service team or investigate application code
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Trace HTTP requests on App Services platform

symptom: Application Insights availability tests reporting failures; customer unsure whether the failure is caused by the App Service resource itself or by Application Insights webtest infrastructure
rootCause: Underlying App Service resource experiencing downtime, restarts, high CPU, or slowness at the same time as the availability test failures, causing webtest pings to fail before reaching the application
solution: Review AppLens detectors for the customer's App Service resource to correlate with AI availability test failure timeframes: 1) Web App Down - reports availability as detected by App Services (independent of AI webtest clients) 2) Web App Restarted - identifies if failures align with app restarts or new deployments 3) High CPU Analysis - detects if CPU spikes caused request timeouts 4) Web App Slow - identifies response time degradation. If AppLens-reported failures overlap with AI availability test failure windows, the App Service resource state is the root cause
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Use AppLens to review an App Services resource for failures

symptom: Application Insights telemetry not flowing from VNET-integrated App Service web app; DNS resolution for App Insights endpoints returns CNAMEONLY code or resolves to no IP addresses
rootCause: DNS resolution failure for App Insights endpoints within the VNET — custom DNS server is not forwarding resolution for App Insights FQDNs to Azure DNS (168.63.129.16), resulting in CNAMEONLY responses and no IP returned for App Insights ingestion endpoints
solution: 1. Use AppLens 'VNet Integration DNS Logs' detector to identify which App Insights FQDNs are returning CNAMEONLY or no IP addresses. 2. Check if VNET is using custom DNS servers (private IPs listed instead of Azure-provided 168.63.129.16). 3. Configure custom DNS to forward App Insights endpoint resolution to Azure DNS, or use Azure DNS Private Resolver to ensure successful name resolution within the VNET.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Determine name resolutions for VNET integrated web apps

symptom: Cannot correlate Application Insights cloud_RoleInstance field with the correct App Service worker instance in AppLens detectors or Kusto queries — AI telemetry shows a different host identifier than what App Services platform uses
rootCause: Application Insights SDKs populate cloud_RoleInstance using the host MachineName (e.g., 'RD001...'), while App Services platform and Kusto tables reference a different RoleInstance identifier for the same worker — these are two different naming schemes for the same underlying compute unit
solution: Use AppLens 'RD Host to RoleInstance Mapping' detector for the App Service to get a mapping table between MachineName (as recorded by AI SDK in cloud_RoleInstance) and RoleInstance (as used by App Services/Kusto). Use this mapping to correctly correlate AI telemetry with App Services platform-level diagnostics.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Identify cloud role instances in App Services

symptom: Application Insights auto-instrumentation (codeless) stopped working for a specific time window on App Service then self-resolved — telemetry gap visible in ingestion charts
rootCause: Customer's application deployed with System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource.dll in the app's bin directory, conflicting with the App Insights auto-instrumentation agent and causing it to back off
solution: 1. In AppLens, open 'Application Insights Java Node and Dot Net Auto Instrumentation' detector → Data Sources tab → Run query in Kusto Web Explorer. 2. Filter WebAppsAppInsightsExtension table by the affected timeframe, AppName, and EventPrimaryStampName. 3. Inspect the 'msg' field for log entries indicating DiagnosticSource.dll or other conflicting DLLs caused the agent to back off. 4. Remove the conflicting DLL (System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource.dll) from the app's deployment. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/troubleshoot/azure/azure-monitor/app-insights/status-monitor-v2-troubleshoot#conflicting-dlls-in-an-apps-bin-directory
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Inspect App Service autoinstrumentation logs

symptom: App Insights auto-instrumentation (codeless) enabled on .NET Core App Service web application but no telemetry is appearing in Application Insights
rootCause: .NET Core 2.2 (or other end-of-life .NET framework versions) is no longer supported by Application Insights auto-instrumentation for App Services
solution: 1. Query WebAppsAppInsightsExtension Kusto table via AppLens 'Application Insights Java Node and Dot Net Auto Instrumentation' detector → Data Sources tab to confirm framework version is reported as unsupported. 2. Review supported framework versions in the auto-instrumentation release notes: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/codeless-app-service?tabs=aspnetcore#release-notes. 3. Upgrade the app to a supported .NET version (e.g., .NET 6, .NET 8). 4. If upgrade is not feasible, use the Application Insights SDK manually instead of codeless auto-instrumentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/AppLens/Inspect App Service autoinstrumentation logs

symptom: Application Insights data showing gaps/missing telemetry each day at the same time; ASC Ingestion tab shows no Breeze-level drops (data accepted at AI endpoint); Application Map shows incomplete or missing data
rootCause: Log Analytics Workspace Daily Cap (OverQuota) is being reached independently of the AI component's Daily Cap. The AI component accepts data at its Breeze ingestion endpoint, but the downstream Log Analytics workspace enforces its own separate Daily Cap, causing ingestion to stop when that cap is hit.
solution: Run the following KQL in the linked Log Analytics workspace to confirm: `Operation | where TimeGenerated > startofday(ago(3d)) | where OperationCategory == "Data Collection Status" | project TimeGenerated, Detail | order by TimeGenerated asc`. Look for 'Data collection stopped due to daily limit of free data reached. Ingestion status = OverQuota' entries. The daily cap resets at ~4 PM UTC. Resolution: increase the LA Workspace Daily Cap above actual daily ingestion volume. Note: the AI component Daily Cap and LA Workspace Daily Cap are independent settings—both must be checked.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Use Ingestion tab/Read Aggregate by Dropped By Reason/Missing Application Insights data due to Log Analytics Daily Cap

symptom: Application Insights Component has been accidentally deleted; customer reports the AI resource is missing from their subscription and wants it restored
rootCause: AI Component was deleted by the user. The resource enters a soft-deleted state and is recoverable within 30 days, subject to: the exact same resource (same name + same resource group) has not been recreated, and the original resource group still exists.
solution: Recovery via ASC: 1) Navigate to the subscription in ASC → expand microsoft.insights provider → click any existing AI Component → Actions tab to see recoverable deleted components. 2) If no AI components exist, have customer temporarily create a generically named one (NOT the same name as the deleted one) to unlock the Actions tab. 3) Click the hyperlink for the component to recover → select Restricted CRI → choose Cloud Instance = 'Public' → Submit. 4) Recovery completes in ~30 min; refresh ICM to track status. 5) Verify in ASC that component reappears under microsoft.insights. 6) Note: availability tests within the deleted component are automatically restored. 7) If component is not visible after a few hours, a cache refresh may be needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Recover a deleted AI Component

symptom: Unable to send telemetry or query telemetry data for an Application Insights resource behind Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS); client cannot reach the AI ingestion endpoint despite AMPLS being configured
rootCause: Client is resolving an incorrect IP address for the Application Insights regional ingestion endpoint. The private DNS zone record set for the AMPLS private endpoint does not match what the client resolves, breaking the private connectivity path.
solution: 1) Get the IP the client is currently resolving for the endpoint (see: Validate DNS connectivity guide). 2) In ASC, locate the AI component → find 'Private Links' section → click the associated AMPLS resource (microsft.privatelinkscope) → Private Endpoint Connections → click the private endpoint resource. 3) On the Properties tab, scroll to 'Private DNS Zone Record Sets' → find the row for the regional endpoint (e.g., connection string contains 'uksouth-1.in.applicationinsights.azure.com', look for record 'uksouth-1.in.ai') → expand the row → note the 'IP Addresses' value. 4) This IP must match what the client resolves. If mismatch, fix the private DNS zone configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Check DNS Configuration in Private DNS Zone for AMPLS

symptom: Conversion of Classic Application Insights resource to workspace-based resource is blocked and fails to proceed
rootCause: Continuous Export feature is enabled (Enabled: true) on the Classic AI Component. This feature does not exist in workspace-based AI components, so migration cannot complete while it remains active.
solution: In ASC, open the AI component → Continuous Export tab → check the 'Enabled' field. If it shows 'true', guide the customer to disable Continuous Export on the Classic AI resource before reattempting conversion to workspace-based resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Continuous Export

symptom: Continuous Export is configured and enabled on a Classic Application Insights resource but data is not being exported to Azure Storage (no new files in storage account)
rootCause: The Continuous Export job is failing; Export Status is not 'Success', indicating an error occurred during the last export run (storage access, permissions, or connectivity issue)
solution: In ASC, open the AI component → Continuous Export tab: check 'Export Status' (current job result), 'Error' field (specific error from last failed run), and 'Last Success' timestamp (when data last successfully exported). Use 'Container Name', 'Resource Group', and 'Contain Location' fields to identify and investigate the target storage account for access, permissions, or connectivity problems.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Continuous Export

symptom: Application Insights daily cap not honored — data continues to ingest past the configured daily cap limit
rootCause: Application is using instrumentation key (IKey) only, which routes telemetry to the global ingestion endpoint (dc.services.visualstudio.com). The global endpoint does not properly enforce daily cap. Applications using connection strings use regional endpoints which do enforce the cap.
solution: Migrate the application from instrumentation key to connection string so telemetry is routed to the regional endpoint. To identify the source: in ASC Ingestion tab use 'Aggregate by Ingestion Endpoint' — if global endpoint traffic is present alongside daily cap violations, IKey-based apps are the cause. Cross-reference with 'Aggregate by Parsed SDK Names' to identify which SDK is sending to global endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Use Ingestion tab/Read Aggregate by Ingestion Endpoint

symptom: Some Application Insights telemetry types suddenly stop flowing while other types continue, often following a deployment
rootCause: Partial deployment — not all application binaries/services were updated simultaneously, resulting in SDK version mismatch across components. The components that did not receive the update stopped sending telemetry.
solution: Use 'Aggregate by Parsed SDK Names' or 'Aggregate by Raw SDK Names' in ASC Ingestion tab to identify when a specific SDK version disappeared or changed. Correlate the timing of the SDK version change with the deployment event. Identify which SDK versions are still sending data to determine which services were partially updated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Use Ingestion tab/Read Aggregate by Parsed SDK Names or Raw SDK Names

symptom: No telemetry data visible in Application Insights — ASC Ingestion tab shows nothing in 'Aggregate by Telemetry Names' AND nothing in 'Dropped by Reason'
rootCause: Data is not reaching the ingestion endpoint at all. Most likely causes: (1) networking issue between the app host and the Application Insights ingestion endpoint, or (2) the client SDK/agent is not using TLS 1.2.
solution: Check network connectivity from the app host to the Application Insights ingestion endpoint. Verify TLS 1.2 is enabled and configured on the client. Confirm the 'Dropped by Reason' view shows no data — if both Telemetry Names AND Dropped views are empty, data is not arriving at the endpoint. If Dropped data IS present, the data is arriving but being dropped (different issue).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Use Ingestion tab/Read Aggregate by Telemetry Names

symptom: Customer reports missing telemetry data but ASC ingestion charts show no data drop — data appears to have reached the endpoint but is not visible in logs
rootCause: Ingestion latency — data successfully reached the Application Insights ingestion endpoint but has not yet finished processing and becoming available in the log tables.
solution: Use the 'Query Customer Data' tab in ASC to query the Application Insights tables directly (e.g., union * | where timestamp > ago(90d) | summarize max(timestamp) by cloud_RoleName, itemType). If data is present in tables but customer sees it as missing in the portal, that confirms ingestion latency. Also check if there is a discrepancy between when data was received vs when it became available using: requests | extend TimeRequiredtoIngest = ingestion_time() - _TimeReceived.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Use Query Customer Data tab

symptom: Customer reports they did not receive an expected alert notification when their Application Insights availability test should have triggered a metric alert
rootCause: Two possible causes: (a) the alert rule did not fire because the failure threshold (default: failed locations >= 2) was not met during the reported time window, or (b) the alert rule fired but the notification was not delivered (action group misconfiguration or notification delivery failure).
solution: 1) In ASC, navigate to the metric alert associated with the availability test. 2) Open the 'Fired Alerts' tab and set the date/time range to the period when the notification was expected. 3) Click Run. If no firing is shown during that window → the alert threshold was not met; investigate whether the availability test was actually failing (check test execution results). If alert DID fire → the issue is in the notification/action group pipeline; check action group notification history.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Use metricalerts Fired Alerts tab for Application Insights Availability Test

symptom: Application Insights component shows 'Ingestion Mode: Disabled' in the ASC Properties tab
rootCause: Three possible causes: (1) The underlying Log Analytics workspace has been deleted; (2) No telemetry data has been sent to the component for 90 or more days; (3) Log Analytics-based resource where ASC status display overrides the actual Classic vs LA-based mode — the 'Disabled' status is the ASC representation, not the actual resource state.
solution: (1) If workspace deleted: re-link the Application Insights component to an existing Log Analytics workspace via the portal. (2) If no data in 90+ days: verify SDK/agent is properly configured and sending data; check instrumentation key or connection string is correct. (3) Use the App Insights Resource Finder AppLens detector to confirm the actual operational mode of the component regardless of what ASC shows.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Use the Component's Properties tab for an Application Insights Component

symptom: Application Insights resource (component, webtest, alertrule) not visible in Azure portal despite existing, OR deleted resource still appears in portal with broken page, OR creating a new resource with the same name fails with 'name already exists' error
rootCause: ARM cache inconsistency — tracked resources are cached by ARM Frontdoor for performance. Cache can become stale when resources are auto-provisioned, moved between subscriptions/resource groups, or cleaned up in the backend without proper ARM notification
solution: 1) Check current ARM cache state: Jarvis → 'Azure Resource Manager > Resource Group Management > Get resources from subscription', filter: $filter=resourceType eq 'microsoft.insights/components' (or relevant type). 2) Check resources from RP: Jarvis → 'Get resources from provider', path: /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/microsoft.insights/components API 2015-05-01 (webtests: 2015-05-01, alertrules: 2016-03-01). 3) If resource in RP but not ARM cache → re-hydrate (Create). If in ARM cache but not RP → remove (Delete). Proceed to Jarvis 'Synchronize resource state' action for resolution.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Jarvis/Determine if there is an ARM Cache issue

symptom: Need to migrate an Application Insights component to a different Azure region; direct region-to-region migration is not possible via portal or API
rootCause: Azure does not support native migration of Application Insights components across regions — the resource must be recreated in the target region
solution: Create a new Application Insights component in the target region. Use Jarvis (requires SAW device + AME.GBL account with App-Insights-CSS-TA-EEE membership): navigate to portal.microsoftgeneva.com → Application Insights → 'Swap Instrumentation Key'. Set 'Current Instrumentation Key' = new component's iKey, 'New Instrumentation Key' = original component's iKey. IMPORTANT: iKey swap only updates the iKey and its value in connection strings — it does NOT change ingestion endpoints. Customers must update connection strings in deployed applications to use new ingestion endpoint. Only apps still using legacy global endpoints (dc.services.visualstudio.com) are unaffected. No data is migrated; only new telemetry streams into the new component.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Jarvis/Swap Instrumentation keys

symptom: ARM cache out of sync: resource created but not visible in portal; deleted resource still shows as ghost with broken page; Failure Anomalies alert shows 'sad cloud'; creating same-name resource fails with conflict error
rootCause: ARM internal storage (tracked resource cache) is out of sync with the resource provider. Common triggers: resources auto-provisioned (e.g., NRTPD alert rules) without ARM notification, resources moved to different resource group/subscription, or backend cleanup without ARM notification
solution: Preferred (fastest): Jarvis → 'Azure Resource Manager > Resource Synchronization > Synchronize resource state' (https://portal.microsoftgeneva.com/EEA553D5, requires SAW). Parameters: Endpoint=Azure Resource Manager, Subscription ID, Resource Group, Resource Provider Namespace=microsoft.insights, Id=components/{component-name}, Resource Location (e.g. 'East US'), API Version=2020-02-02, Provisioning State=Create (resource in RP, missing from ARM) or Delete (ghost in ARM, absent from RP). Completes within minutes. Track status via correlation ID in Kusto: https://armprodgbl.eastus.kusto.windows.net/ARMProd. Multiple resources: use 'Synchronize resource group resources'. Subscription-wide (takes up to 24h+): 'Synchronize subscription resources from specific provider'. If sync fails: escalate to armhot@microsoft.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Jarvis/Synchronizing Cache for Caching Issues

symptom: Classic Application Insights ingestion pipeline issues — telemetry data lost, duplicated, or delayed after being accepted by the classic ingestion endpoint (client SDK confirmed data sent, ingestion accepted it, but data is missing/wrong in AI Kusto tables)
rootCause: Classic Application Insights ingestion pipeline (routing telemetry to AI Kusto clusters) was deprecated on February 29, 2024. Post-deprecation, these ingestion-pipeline issues are officially unsupported scenarios.
solution: Classify as unsupported scenario and encourage immediate migration to workspace-based Application Insights. Migration is seamless for components without Continuous Export (quick, no known issues). For Continuous Export users: assess whether consumers of exported data can handle schema changes (Continuous Export → Diagnostics Settings). Note: Microsoft auto-migration of Classic components to workspace-based begins May 2024. Migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/convert-classic-resource. Benefits include faster ingestion times.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Manage cases with Classic Application Insight Resources after Deprecation

symptom: After migrating Classic Application Insights to workspace-based, the 'Usage and estimated costs > Data Retention' blade shows warning: 'Since [date], this Applications Insights resource is sending its data to a Log Analytics workspace. This retention setting only applies to data collected before this date.'
rootCause: Post-migration, the retention setting displayed in Application Insights UI only governs pre-migration classic data. New data retention is governed by the associated Log Analytics workspace, which Application Insights cannot determine from its own blade.
solution: This is expected behavior, not a bug. Configure data retention on the associated Log Analytics workspace for post-migration data. The warning message and old retention UI will disappear automatically once all pre-migration classic data has been pruned per the configured retention period.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Manage cases with Classic Application Insight Resources after Deprecation

symptom: Application Insights Profiler emits '401 Unauthorized' exception in telemetry between StartProfiler triggered and StopProfiler triggered trace events; profiler fails to upload traces
rootCause: Application Insights component is associated with AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope), which blocks Profiler from uploading trace data to the default public endpoint. BYOS (Bring Your Own Storage) is required for Profiler to function when AMPLS is in use.
solution: Configure Bring Your Own Storage (BYOS) for Application Insights Profiler. BYOS routes profiler uploads through a customer-controlled storage account accessible within the private link scope. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/profiler/profiler-bring-your-own-storage. Verify with KQL in ASC Customer Query Data tab: union traces, customEvents, exceptions | where sdkVersion contains '_ap' or name in ('ServiceProfilerSample', 'ServiceProfilerIndex') | where timestamp > datetime(...) | project timestamp, itemType, sdkVersion, message, name, outerMessage, customDimensions | order by timestamp asc — look for 401 exceptions between Start/StopProfiler events.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Profiler & Snapshot Debugger/Determine if Profiler is being triggered and collecting data

symptom: 'Profile Traces' button in Azure Portal is disabled/grayed out even though Application Insights Profiler appears to be running and emitting telemetry (StartProfiler/StopProfiler events visible)
rootCause: Profiler captured a trace session and logged a request ID via ServiceProfilerSample/ServiceProfilerIndex customEvent, but the request corresponding to that ID was excluded by sampling and does not exist in the requests/AppRequests table. Portal enables the button only when a captured request ID exists in the requests table.
solution: Run KQL in ASC Customer Query Data tab to verify: let RQID=(){union traces, customEvents | where timestamp > ago(3d) | where sdkVersion startswith 'w_ap' or name in ('ServiceProfilerSample','ServiceProfilerIndex') | extend RequestId = tostring(customDimensions.RequestId) | where isempty(RequestId) != true | project customDimensions.RequestId}; requests | where timestamp > ago(3d) | where id in ((RQID)). If no rows: requests captured by profiler were sampled out. Options: reduce sampling rate, wait for profiler to capture a non-sampled request, or use Remote-Immediate manual trigger from portal during known traffic.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Profiler & Snapshot Debugger/Determine if Profiler is being triggered and collecting data

symptom: Application Insights Profiler telemetry shows trace message 'Upload trace was canceled as no activity detected from target iKey'; profiler sessions run but no trace is uploaded or available in portal
rootCause: Profiler session successfully started and created an ETL file, but no complete HTTP request/operation was captured during the profiling window (the app had no incoming traffic or requests completed outside the profiling window). ETL is discarded without upload.
solution: Ensure the application is receiving HTTP requests during the Profiler sampling window. Use the Remote-Immediate trigger (manual on-demand profiling) from the Azure Portal during a period of known active traffic. Review trigger settings (Memory, CPU, Default-Sampling thresholds) via: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/profiler/profiler-settings#trigger-settings. Confirm profiling session duration is sufficient for requests to complete.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Profiler & Snapshot Debugger/Determine if Profiler is being triggered and collecting data

symptom: Profiler traces not collected on Azure App Service; navigating to https://{sitename}.scm.azurewebsites.net/DiagnosticServices returns 'No route registered for /DiagnosticServices'
rootCause: Profiler webjob (ApplicationInsightsProfiler#) is not enabled or running on the App Service. The /DiagnosticServices route is only registered when at least one of Profiler or Snapshot Debugger is enabled.
solution: From App Service resource menu: select 'Application Insights' → choose the appropriate language tab → set 'Profiler' toggle to On → click Save. At least one of Profiler or Snapshot Debugger must be enabled for the /DiagnosticServices Kudu endpoint to respond. Once enabled, re-check: https://{sitename}.scm.azurewebsites.net/DiagnosticServices to confirm the route is now registered and status shows healthy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Profiler & Snapshot Debugger/Determine if Profiler is running

symptom: Application Insights Profiler shows unhealthy status on Kudu /DiagnosticServices page (red status icons), OR no StartProfiler/StopProfiler trace events appear in Application Insights when querying profiler telemetry for the past 30 days
rootCause: Profiler webjob has encountered errors and is in an unhealthy state, was disabled, or never properly started; the profiler process is not running at the base level
solution: 1) Check AppLens → Web Job detectors for profiler webjob status. 2) Run KQL in ASC Customer Query Data tab: union traces, customEvents | where timestamp > ago(30d) | where sdkVersion startswith 'w_ap' or name in ('ServiceProfilerSample','ServiceProfilerIndex') | extend trigger = tostring(customDimensions.Source) | project timestamp, message, name, trigger, customDimensions, operation_Name, cloud_RoleInstance, sdkVersion | order by timestamp asc. If no StartProfiler events: profiler is not running. Re-enable Profiler from Application Insights blade on App Service. If events exist but status is unhealthy: check exceptions between StartProfiler and StopProfiler events for specific error details.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Profiler & Snapshot Debugger/Determine if Profiler is running

symptom: ASC WebTest Results tab returns 'System.ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null' error when querying failed availability test execution details
rootCause: The 'Is this a failed result?' checkbox is not checked when querying a failed test result, causing the ASC diagnostics endpoint to receive a null value it cannot deserialize
solution: Check the 'Is this a failed result?' checkbox in the WebTest Results tab when looking up a failed availability test execution. Also ensure Test Execution Time (UTC) is copied correctly from the Executions tab, and Web Test Location matches the corresponding region.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Azure Support Center/Use webtest's Results tab

symptom: Application Insights shows React PageView telemetry with duration value of 0 (zero) — actual page load/transition time not captured
rootCause: By design, when enableAutoRouteTracking is enabled the default tracking logic sets React PageView duration to 0 because it does not measure route transition time
solution: Set 'enableAutoRouteTracking: false' in the Application Insights SDK config and implement custom trackPageView calls using React hooks (useEffect + useHistory or useLocation) to capture the actual page duration. Method 1: listen to history changes and call appInsights.trackPageView(). Method 2: use useLocation hook with useEffect to call trackPageView on each location change.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Customize telemetry/javascript

symptom: Spring Boot application using Application Insights Java Agent shows unexpected telemetry behavior, conflicts, or missing data
rootCause: Both JVM argument auto-instrumentation (javaagent) and programmatic manual instrumentation (enabling programmatically) are enabled simultaneously — this is an unsupported configuration and causes conflicts
solution: Use only ONE instrumentation approach for Spring Boot: either JVM argument auto-instrumentation OR programmatic manual instrumentation. Do not enable both at the same time. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/java-spring-boot
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Instrumentation/Manual instrumentation (OpenTelemetry)

symptom: Application Insights Java SDK (classic, 2.x) has unresolved bugs, missing telemetry, or no new feature support
rootCause: The Java SDK 2.x (classic API) is deprecated and no longer actively maintained — bug fixes and new features will not be delivered to this SDK
solution: Migrate from Java SDK 2.x to the Java Agent (OpenTelemetry-based). Migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/java-standalone-upgrade-from-2x
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Instrumentation/Manual instrumentation (classic API)

symptom: Application Insights Python OpenCensus SDK has unresolved bugs, missing telemetry, maintenance issues, or no new feature support
rootCause: Python OpenCensus SDK is deprecated and no longer actively maintained — bug fixes and new features will not be delivered to this SDK
solution: Migrate from Python OpenCensus SDK to Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Python Distro. Migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/opentelemetry-python-opencensus-migrate
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Instrumentation/Manual instrumentation (classic API)

symptom: Application Insights telemetry name resolution fails with NXDOMAIN errors even when Azure-provided DNS is configured on the VNET; DNS resolution to Application Insights ingestion endpoints (e.g., *.in.applicationinsights.azure.com) returns NXDOMAIN despite no explicit Private DNS Zone configuration intended
rootCause: A stale or unused Private DNS Zone linked to the VNET intercepts the *.privatelink.monitor.azure.com CNAME chain during DNS resolution. Because the zone has no A records configured, name resolution fails with NXDOMAIN. The CNAME chain resolves: {region}.in.applicationinsights.azure.com → *.in.ai.monitor.azure.com → *.in.ai.privatelink.monitor.azure.com — at this last hop the stale Private DNS Zone intercepts and returns NXDOMAIN instead of continuing to the public Traffic Manager endpoint
solution: Option 1: Remove the VNET link between the stale Private DNS Zone and the VNET (navigate to the DNS zone → Virtual network links → remove the link). This restores public DNS resolution. Option 2: If customers are not comfortable removing the link, enable 'fallback to internet' on the Private DNS Zone (navigate to DNS zone → Edit → enable 'Enable fallback to internet') — this allows resolution to fall back to public DNS if the zone cannot resolve the FQDN.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Validate Network Connectivity/Validate DNS connectivity in Application Insights

symptom: Application Insights telemetry is not being ingested; no data appears in the Ingestion tab in ASC (Azure Support Center); network capture shows TCP Reset from the Application Insights Ingestion Endpoint after the client sends a Client Hello using TLS 1.0 or TLS 1.1
rootCause: The application is attempting to connect to the Application Insights Ingestion Endpoint using an older TLS version (1.0 or 1.1), but the endpoint enforces TLS 1.2. The TLS handshake fails before any telemetry is transmitted, so the ingestion endpoint receives no data and nothing appears in the Ingestion tab. Note: this is not reflected in ASC Ingestion tab because the TLS mismatch occurs before data reaches Breeze (the ingestion endpoint).
solution: 1) For .NET 4.X apps: add the following line early in application startup (e.g., global.asax): System.Net.ServicePointManager.SecurityProtocol = SecurityProtocolType.Tls12; 2) Use Windows registry settings to enforce TLS 1.2 client-wide — see: https://docs.microsoft.com/mem/configmgr/core/plan-design/security/enable-tls-1-2-client 3) For .NET-specific registry settings: https://docs.microsoft.com/dotnet/framework/network-programming/tls — collect a network capture (NetMon/Wireshark/netsh) to confirm the TLS version being used by the app process before implementing the fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Validate Network Connectivity/Validate TLS connectivity in Application Insights

symptom: App Service Profiler WebJob log shows 'Reached maximum allowed output lines for this run, to see all of the job's logs you can enable website application diagnostics' — profiler log entries stop appearing in KUDU WebJobs dashboard
rootCause: The App Service WebJob output log has a maximum line limit; once reached, subsequent profiler log entries are not written to the KUDU output window
solution: Enable application diagnostics on the Web App via the portal, or restart the application to reset the WebJob output log counter
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Profiler & Snapshot Debugger/Find and analyze Profiler Logs

symptom: DiagnosticsPlugin log on Azure VM or Cloud Service shows 'Skipping Profiler bootstrap based on deployment configuration' — Application Insights Profiler not starting
rootCause: The Azure Diagnostics profiler sink is not configured in the deployment configuration, so the profiler bootstrap step is skipped
solution: Configure the profiler sink using Azure Resource Explorer: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/profiler-vm#set-profiler-sink-using-azure-resource-explorer; after configuring the sink, '[Profiler]' entries should appear in DiagnosticsPlugin log
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Profiler & Snapshot Debugger/Find and analyze Profiler Logs

symptom: No '[Profiler]' entries found in DiagnosticsPlugin log for Azure VM or Cloud Service — Application Insights Profiler never configured on the machine
rootCause: Profiler has not been installed or configured on the machine; this typically occurs on VM or on-premises machines, not App Service hosted apps
solution: Follow VM onboarding: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/profiler-vm or Cloud Service onboarding: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/profiler-cloudservice; if issue recurs after onboarding, investigate whether deployment processes are overwriting profiler configuration settings
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/How-To/Profiler & Snapshot Debugger/Find and analyze Profiler Logs

symptom: Clicking the 'collect snapshots' button in Application Insights Failures experience shows 'You don't have access' message even when the Snapshot Debugger RBAC role is assigned to an AAD group
rootCause: Bug: Application Insights Snapshot Debugger RBAC role does not work when assigned to AAD groups; only works when granted directly to individual users, contrary to documentation
solution: Explicitly grant the 'Application Insights Snapshot Debugger' RBAC role directly to individual users rather than groups. Product fix expected August 2025. ICM: 613574130
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/106335

symptom: Application Insights portal experiences stop working for AMPLS-associated resources: unable to query Logs, Failures/Performance blades show empty sections or 'No data available'. Issue is browser-specific (Chrome/Edge).
rootCause: Chrome/Edge 'Local Network Access Checks' feature (enabled by default) blocks private/local network requests in newer browser versions. This breaks AI portal experiences when using AMPLS private endpoints.
solution: Disable 'Local Network Access Checks' in browser flags: edge://flags/#local-network-access-check or chrome://flags/#local-network-access-check. Update SAP to Azure/Portal/Resource Blade Does Not Load. If workaround resolves, close with root cause 'Azure Portal/Framework/Core framework issue'. Otherwise redirect to Azure portal team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/156315

symptom: After migrating from classic Application Insights .NET SDK to Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Distro, custom tracestate header values are lost in distributed traces; downstream services expecting legacy correlation fields do not receive them
rootCause: Classic SDK uses TelemetryInitializers (coupled to TelemetryClient) for tracestate enrichment; OpenTelemetry SDK is vendor-neutral and requires SpanProcessors for custom enrichment. Hierarchical operation IDs and some legacy correlation features are not supported in OTel.
solution: Implement a custom LogRecord SpanProcessor to add activity.TraceStateString as a tag (data.Attributes). Alternatively use OpenTelemetry Propagators to customize context propagation. Enrich methods from instrumentation libraries are also supported. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/opentelemetry-add-modify
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/158085

symptom: Duplicate telemetry records appear in workspace-backed Application Insights (AI 2.1) tables; duplicate rows share identical values across all columns including timestamp, operation_Id, and id
rootCause: Diagnostic Settings configured to send Application Insights component logs to any Log Analytics workspace causes duplication. Draft API queries all linked LA workspaces, so telemetry sent via Diagnostic Settings to a LA workspace is returned twice (or more).
solution: Remove LA workspace from Diagnostic Settings (can still use Event Hub or Storage Account). Alternative: Use DCR Transformation Rules to rename tables in the second LA workspace. Or restrict user RBAC access to the target LA workspace (requires 'Require workspace permissions' access mode). Or use dedup/distinct in queries.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/22050

symptom: Application Insights Requests table client_Ip field shows the proxy/load balancer IP address instead of the actual end-user client IP address
rootCause: X-Forwarded-For header is present and Application Insights by design takes the rightmost (last) IP in the X-Forwarded-For list, which is the last proxy before the ingestion endpoint, not the original client IP
solution: Use TelemetryInitializer to intercept telemetry payload, parse the first (leftmost) IP from X-Forwarded-For header as the true client IP, perform custom geolocation lookup, and attach as a custom property. No built-in fix available; Microsoft monitoring demand for feature improvement.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/26201

symptom: After renaming or moving (between resource groups or subscriptions) a workspace-based Application Insights resource, only telemetry ingested after the ARM operation is visible; historical data appears missing in log-based experiences. MDM-based metrics in Overview blade are not affected.
rootCause: Workspace-based AI query experience uses the _ResourceId field to filter telemetry from the LA workspace. Rename/move operations change the resource ID, so all historical data with the old _ResourceId is no longer returned by AI query experience.
solution: No direct fix. Options: (1) Roll back the rename/move operation to restore old resource ID. (2) Query historical data from LA Logs blade using: AppRequests | where _ResourceId == '/old path' OR _ResourceId == '/new path'. (3) Create Dashboards/Workbooks querying LA workspace directly with both resource IDs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/26413

symptom: No telemetry data returned from workspace-based Application Insights (AI 2.1) in Azure Portal charts or Logs blade despite telemetry being actively sent. Draft API returns HTTP 200 with zero records.
rootCause: User has RBAC access to the Application Insights resource but lacks permissions on the underlying Log Analytics workspace. Draft API uses the user's AAD auth token to query LA and returns a silent HTTP 200 with no records when LA RBAC check fails.
solution: Assign the user 'Log Analytics Reader' RBAC role on the backend LA workspace. For strict access control, manage workspace RBAC and table-level permissions as needed. Reference: Manage access to log data and workspaces in Azure Monitor.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/26775

symptom: REST API calls to Application Insights Draft API (api.applicationinsights.io or api.applicationinsights.azure.com) using API Key return HTTP 200 with no telemetry records for a workspace-based AI component
rootCause: API Key (SAS-Key) requests to Draft API do not include an Azure AD auth token. If the backend Log Analytics workspace Access Control Mode is set to 'Require Workspace Permissions', all API Key requests silently return 200 with no data.
solution: Set the Log Analytics workspace Access Control Mode to 'Use resource or workspace permissions' on the LA Properties blade. This is required to allow API Key-based calls to Draft API. If AAD auth is feasible, use AAD tokens instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/26775

symptom: Availability Test starts failing consistently in one region with ~21000ms TCP-level connection timeout: 'DNS Status: Succeeded, Connection Status: Failed - A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond'. Issue intermittently self-resolves but recurs. Other regions pass and the website is confirmed up.
rootCause: Cause unclear; indications suggest an Azure-side issue with the availability test agent infrastructure in that specific region. Investigation was ongoing as of June 2023.
solution: No permanent resolution available. Mitigation: Remove the failing region from the test and add a different region. Caveat: the issue may eventually occur on the new region too. Recommend testing from multiple regions to reduce single-region failure impact.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27462

symptom: Application Insights Performance/Availability/Failures blades show inflated request counts compared to actual traffic. Discrepancy observed between initial chart load (accurate) and values after user interacts with chart or clicks refresh (inflated). Occurs when extreme sampling (<=10%) is active.
rootCause: Initial chart load uses pre-aggregated Application Insights Standard Metrics (accurate). After user interaction, blades switch to Logs-based queries using sum(itemCount). During extreme sampling, itemCount values on retained records are inflated to represent sampled-out records, making sum(itemCount) less accurate than pre-aggregated metrics.
solution: Advise customer to use 'Application Insights Standard Metrics' namespace on the Metrics blade for accurate counts. Collect browser HAR trace to confirm whether chart is using Logs query (api.applicationinsights.azure.com) or Metrics query (management.azure.com/.../metrics). Check sampling rate using Sampling identification guide.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27486

symptom: Application Insights shows request count as an exact multiple (2x, 3x, 4x, etc.) of expected count on Performance/Failures blades. Issue occurs in distributed architectures where a single user operation spans multiple services.
rootCause: Sampling is operation-based: all telemetry items sharing the same operationId are kept or dropped together. When a single user operation spans multiple services (root request → dependency → backend request), ALL resulting request records share the same operationId. Logs-based sum(itemCount) counts each request record's itemCount independently, causing the apparent request count to multiply by the number of services in the distributed operation.
solution: Check for distributed operations: query a single operationId for all telemetry records and confirm multiple Request telemetry records exist for one logical request. Use Application Insights Standard Metrics namespace on Metrics blade for accurate counts. Collect HAR trace to confirm logs-vs-metrics query type.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27486

symptom: Application Insights Availability Ping or Standard Tests return failures (e.g. HTTP 404) even though the website is up and accessible from browsers/Postman without custom headers.
rootCause: Some web servers (notably NGINX) reject requests whose User-Agent is 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MSIE 9.0; Windows NT 6.1; Trident/5.0; AppInsights)', treating it as an insecure/legacy browser (Internet Explorer 9). The User-Agent and HOST headers are hard-coded for Availability test clients and cannot be changed by customers.
solution: Customers must configure their web server to allow requests with the Availability test User-Agent value. To reproduce: send a Postman request including 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MSIE 9.0; Windows NT 6.1; Trident/5.0; AppInsights)' and verify the 404 response. PG is investigating support for customizable User-Agent but it is not yet available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/27863

symptom: Net Core Web App logs startup exceptions: 'System.InvalidOperationException: Startup assembly Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper failed to execute' or 'DiagnosticServices.HostingStartup failed to execute'. App function and AI data collection are not impacted despite the errors.
rootCause: Codeless auto-instrumentation attach is enabled for unsupported .NET Core scenarios: self-contained deployments, apps targeting full .NET Framework, or apps on unsupported framework versions (other than 3.1, 5.0, and 6.0+ for self-contained).
solution: 1. Remove or unset 'ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES' environment variable. 2. In App Services > Configuration, remove all Application Insights related app settings EXCEPT APPINSIGHTS_INSTRUMENTATIONKEY and APPINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING. Note: these exceptions only stop the startup error; the SDK will still not fully instrument unsupported scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/28165

symptom: Application Insights component (using Log Analytics / APM 2.1 workspace-based ingestion mode) shows missing or no telemetry data in tables. Other AI components sending to the same Log Analytics workspace show data normally.
rootCause: A pipeline bug causes data loss when the Application Insights resource ID string contains non-ASCII/Unicode characters. The resource ID (including resource group name, component name, etc.) is used in HTTP headers during the ingestion process, but HTTP headers only support ASCII characters.
solution: Mitigation: Create and use an Application Insights resource whose name, resource group, and all other components of the resource ID contain only ASCII characters. Use https://www.soscisurvey.de/tools/view-chars.php to inspect the resource ID for invisible Unicode characters. Permanent fix was under investigation at time of writing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/28300

symptom: Intermittent HTTP 4xx or 5xx errors returned when performing actions against Azure services via Azure portal, CLI, PowerShell, REST API, or SDK. Retrying the same action succeeds.
rootCause: Transient faults inherent to distributed client-server systems. Common causes: 409 Conflict (resource in non-modifiable state), 429 TooManyRequests (Azure limit reached), 500 InternalServerError (unknown backend error), 503 ServiceUnavailable (service temporarily unable to respond), 504 GatewayTimeout (ARM-proxied service did not respond within ~60s).
solution: Retry the operation. Implement transient fault handling in application code using Microsoft's retry guidance. Many Azure SDK implementations include built-in retry logic. If errors persist for extended durations (minutes) or recur frequently, raise a CRI to the relevant PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/28573

symptom: Activity Logs show entries initiated by 'Windows Azure Application Insights' or 'Azure Monitor System' (e.g. ListAccountSas operations on Storage Accounts), causing customer concern about unexpected access to their resources.
rootCause: By design. 'Windows Azure Application Insights' / 'Azure Monitor System' is a 1st-party AAD application used by Azure Monitor services (Metrics, Autoscale, Diagnostic Settings / Monitor Essentials family). Tenants created before March 2022 will continue to see the old 'Windows Azure Application Insights' name; tenants after March 2022 see 'Azure Monitor System'. Calls are triggered by Diagnostic Settings configured to write to Storage Accounts.
solution: Educate customer: calls are expected and required by Azure services. To identify the source resource: in ASC > Tenant Explorer search the caller ID as 'Serviceprincipal ObjectId'. Query Kusto: cluster('Azureinsights').database('Insights').RegistrationTelemetry | where customerStorageAccountId == '<id>' | summarize ... by name, resourceId, categories, customerStorageAccountId. To stop calls, disable Diagnostic Settings writing to the Storage Account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/29307

symptom: Azure App Service Web App reports incorrect cloud_RoleName in Application Insights telemetry after a deployment slot swap. The cloud_RoleName reflects the pre-swap slot identity rather than the post-swap one.
rootCause: Application Insights SDK versions prior to 2.14 use the 'WebSite_HostName' App Services environment variable to set cloud_RoleName. This variable is not updated correctly after a slot swap. SDK 2.14+ switched to reading the 'WAS-DEFAULT-HOSTNAME' HTTP header instead, which resolves the issue in most cases.
solution: 1. If using .NET/.NET Core SDK, upgrade to version >= 2.14. 2. If using codeless attach (uses older SDK): either switch to manual SDK >= 2.14, wait for App Services to fix WebSite_HostName behavior, or implement a custom Telemetry Initializer to control cloud_RoleName manually. In some edge cases an additional app restart after swap resolves the stale value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/29308

symptom: After the February 2021 App Service Web Apps Application Insights Extension update (v2.8.38), automatic instrumentation stops working and no telemetry is generated for apps targeting .NET Core 2.0, 2.1, 2.2, or 3.0.
rootCause: Extension v2.8.38 intentionally dropped support for .NET Core 2.0, 2.1, 2.2, and 3.0 as these versions are out of Microsoft support per the .NET Core support policy. By design.
solution: Migrate the application to a supported .NET Core version (3.1, 5.0, 6.0+). If migration is not immediately feasible, switch to manual SDK instrumentation (not recommended as a long-term solution). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/azure-web-apps#release-notes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/30206

symptom: A small random number of Application Insights telemetry records (exported via Diagnostic Settings to blob storage) appear in the wrong time-bucketed blob container. For example, records with TimeGenerated of 13:00 or 14:00 are written to the 12:00 blob container instead.
rootCause: Bug in the ingestion pipeline between Application Insights, Geneva, and Monitor Essentials (OBO). The original export logic uses each record's timestamp/TimeGenerated to select the target blob container, but processing delays cause records to be routed to the bucket corresponding to when ingestion started rather than when the record was actually generated.
solution: Workaround: When searching for specific telemetry records, scan all blob container files within a 48-hour window around the expected time. PG planned to fix this by switching blob routing to use 'Time Received' instead of 'TimeGenerated'; implementation was expected to take several months from November 2022.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/34075

symptom: Distributed tracing breaks when Azure EventGrid is in the middle of a call chain (e.g. APIM → EventGrid → Azure Functions, or Function App → EventGrid → App Services). Application Map and End-to-End Transaction Details show the chain split into two separate operations with different Operation IDs instead of one continuous trace.
rootCause: EventGrid uses the CloudEvents protocol instead of standard HTTP Correlation Protocol or W3C Trace Context headers. It does not propagate the 'Request-Id' or 'traceparent' headers required by Application Insights distributed tracing, causing a correlation break at the EventGrid boundary.
solution: Workaround: Insert an intermediate API between the calling service and EventGrid. The intermediate API must: (1) receive calls with App Insights SDK enabled, (2) extract the telemetry Operation ID from System.Diagnostics.Activity.Current.Context, (3) set it as 'traceparent' on the outgoing CloudEvent object via the EventGrid SDK, then call EventGrid. This propagates the correlation ID through EventGrid to downstream services. Note: workaround is untested by PG; set customer expectations accordingly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/37743

symptom: Customer receives an Availability Test alert notification but all monitored sites are up and responding normally. The alert notification looks identical to a genuine site-down alert.
rootCause: All Availability Standard Tests include a proactive SSL certificate lifetime check. When the certificate remaining lifetime crosses a configured threshold (default 7 days; options: 1, 30, 90, or 365 days), an alert notification is sent that is visually indistinguishable from a site-down alert.
solution: 1. Validate alert cause via webtest Results tab in the portal. 2. Query availabilityResults (or AppAvailabilityResults for workspace-based AI) for failed records and inspect the 'message' field for 'Certificate will expire in ### day(s)'. 3. If confirmed SSL expiration alert, renew the certificate. PG improvement tracking: ADO #14600490 - separate notification template for cert expiration alerts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/38005

symptom: Live Metrics 显示红色横幅 'Data is temporarily inaccessible'，Live Metrics 界面无数据
rootCause: 第三方浏览器扩展（如 UBlock Origin）拦截了对 *.monitor.azure.com 的请求，该域名专用于 Live Metrics 功能
solution: 逐一启用浏览器扩展定位罪魁祸首，然后禁用该扩展，或在其设置中放行 https://*.livediagnostics.monitor.azure.com/ 等 Live Metrics 所需 URL；可使用 HAR trace 捕获被阻止的请求
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/38124

symptom: ARM 模板或 Terraform 脚本尝试设置 Application Insights 的 ImmediatePurgeDataOn30Days 属性时，返回 400 Bad Request，错误消息 'ImmediatePurgeDataOn30Days cannot be set on current api-version'
rootCause: ImmediatePurgeDataOn30Days 设置仅适用于 Classic Application Insights 组件；Workspace-based 组件不支持修改此属性，属于 By Design
solution: Workspace-based Application Insights 组件无法调整此设置，这是预期行为。若必须使用此功能，需使用 Classic 类型的 Application Insights 资源
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/38329

symptom: Application Insights JavaScript SDK 记录的 PageView 遥测中，Windows 11 设备的操作系统显示为 Windows 10，影响 Edge、Firefox、Chrome 等所有基于 Chromium 的浏览器
rootCause: 浏览器 UserAgent 字符串冻结机制（Google 主导的行业决策）：浏览器故意将 UA 中的 OS 版本报告为 Windows 10，JS SDK 依赖 UA 字符串检测操作系统版本，因此无法获取真实 Windows 11 信息
solution: 目前无可用 Workaround。JS SDK 团队在研究使用 NavigatorUAData.getHighEntropyValues() API 的可行性，但该 API 存在异步延迟和用户同意弹窗问题，尚无修复 ETA
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/38332

symptom: .NET Framework 4.8.1 应用启用 Application Insights 后，看不到任何 SQL 依赖遥测（但 HTTP 依赖和请求遥测正常），无论是手动 SDK 还是自动插桩均受影响
rootCause: .NET 4.8.1 的 System.Data 命名空间存在回归 Bug：EventSource 配置失败，导致 SQL 事件从不触发；App Insights SDK 依赖 EventSource 监听 SQL 事件，因此在 .NET 4.8.1 上无法采集 SQL 依赖
solution: 当前唯一解决方案：降级到 .NET 4.x 旧版本（4.8.0 或更早），或将应用重写为 .NET Core/.NET 5+。.NET Framework 团队已有跟踪 Bug（DevDiv#1694192），但修复时间线不明确
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/39126

symptom: Palo Alto 防火墙虚拟设备配置了 Application Insights 遥测采集，出现：全部遥测丢失、部分字段缺失、特定时段遥测缺失，或客户询问如何修改遥测采集行为
rootCause: Palo Alto 使用已废弃的 Legacy Python SDK（zooba/AppInsights-Python）集成 Application Insights，且对 SDK 配置进行了抽象封装，CSS 能力有限。支持范围受限属于预期
solution: （1）检查 ASC Ingestion Tab 确认摄取端点是否丢弃数据；（2）通过 curl/nslookup 验证防火墙到 AI 端点的网络连通性；（3）若 CSS 排查指向 SDK 问题，告知客户联系 Palo Alto 支持继续调查；（4）明确设定支持边界预期
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/39351

symptom: 在 Application Insights 端到端视图（E2E View）中点击某个 operation 后，显示大量（有时近百个）不同的 operation ID，而非预期的单个 operation 链路
rootCause: By Design：_MS.links（Operation Links）属性用于在批处理场景（如 Service Bus、EventHub）中将多个独立 trace 关联起来——一个发送事件可被多个消费者读取，所有消费者的 operation ID 都被 stitched 到父 operation
solution: （1）禁用依赖跟踪（EnableDependencyTrackingTelemetryModule=false）——代价是失去所有出站调用遥测；（2）推荐：将 DependencyTrackingTelemetryModule.EnableAzureSdkTelemetryListener 设为 false，阻止 _MS.links 属性添加到依赖遥测中；（3）实现自定义 TelemetryInitializer 删除依赖遥测中的 _MS.links 属性
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/39404

symptom: Java 应用的 Application Insights 遥测完全停止流入，Java 自诊断日志中出现警告：'WARN c.m.applicationinsights.agent - System property -Djsse.enableSNIExtension=false is detected. If you have connection issues with Application Insights, please remove this.'
rootCause: JVM 参数 -Djsse.enableSNIExtension=false 禁用了 SSL 握手中的 Server Name Indication（SNI），导致与 Application Insights 端点的 SSL 连接失败
solution: 移除 -Djsse.enableSNIExtension=false 或将其改为 true（默认值）。若参数出现多次，确保没有重复出现设为 false 的情况（JVM 使用最后一个参数值），保持只有一个且值为 true
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/41351

symptom: 同时使用 Application Insights .NET SDK 和 OpenTelemetry Exporter 并行插桩（Side by Side）时，在负载情况下 Adaptive Sampling 生成的遥测记录数超出预期
rootCause: Application Insights .NET SDK 的 Adaptive Sampler 与 OpenTelemetry Exporter 并行使用时存在代码 Bug（GitHub Issue #2742），导致采样行为异常，摄取数据量超出采样配置上限
solution: 临时 Workaround：改用 Fixed Rate Sampling 替代 Adaptive Sampling，直到 OpenTelemetry GA 版本修复此问题
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/45601

symptom: 使用 workspace()/app() 跨资源查询时，若参数值使用资源名称（仅名称）或限定名称（subscriptionName/resourceGroup/componentName 格式），查询返回 '400 Bad Request: AmbiguousError' 或出现性能问题/超时
rootCause: 资源名称和限定名称两种格式在 cross-workspace 查询中已被废弃，存在歧义（用户有权访问多个同名资源时）和性能问题。公开文档已更新移除这两种推荐方式
solution: 在 workspace()/app() 函数参数中改用完整 Azure 资源 ID 或 workspace/app ID：推荐使用 workspace ID（不可变）；避免使用资源名或限定名
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/48639

symptom: （1）2023 年 3 月后出现数据丢失，而此前 Daily Cap 实际未被执行；（2）Daily Cap 配置后仍被超越；（3）Application Insights Daily Cap 未被触发但数据已丢失
rootCause: （1）2023 年 3 月修复前，Workspace-based AI 的 Daily Cap 实际未被正确执行，3 月后开始强制执行，之前超额摄取的情况不会再发生；（2）仅使用 Instrumentation Key（使用全球摄取端点）时 Daily Cap 不可靠，全球端点已废弃；（3）底层 Log Analytics Workspace 有独立的 Daily Cap，可能先于 AI 组件 Cap 被触发
solution: （1）使用带区域端点的 Connection String 替代纯 Instrumentation Key，确保 Daily Cap 可靠执行；（2）分别监控 AI 组件和底层 LA Workspace 的 Daily Cap；（3）使用 ASC Ingestion Tab 和 Kusto 查询（参见 How-to: Check if the daily cap was reached）判断是哪一层 Cap 被触发
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Known Issues/49764

symptom: AMPLS 私有链接配置后，Application Insights Ingestion Endpoint 和 Live Endpoint 的 DNS 解析未验证，无法确认流量是否走私有 IP
solution: SSH 进入 Web App/Function App 控制台，对 Application Insights Ingestion Endpoint 和 Live Endpoint 分别执行 nslookup；若两者均解析为 AMPLS->PE->Private DNS Zone 中配置的私有 IP（而非公网 IP），则 AMPLS 私有链接配置正确
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/AMPLs Setup Guide - Hub and Spoke Networks Private Link Design/Test the Azure Monitor Private Link Connection

symptom: Application Insights telemetry not being ingested through AMPLS; ingestion fails or errors when running queries when application is hosted on App Service with AMPLS configured
rootCause: App Service free tier does not support VNet integration. The application resolves ingestion endpoints to a public IP instead of the private AMPLS endpoint, causing ingestion failure when AMPLS is configured in private-only mode.
solution: Ensure the web app or function app hosting the application is on a paid App Service plan that supports VNet integration. The free App Service plan does NOT support VNet and cannot use AMPLS for private ingestion. Upgrade to at least a Basic or Standard plan to enable VNet integration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Support Topics/I receive no data or cannot run queries/Errors when running queries, or other issue accessing data

symptom: Resource resolves Azure Monitor endpoint FQDN to a public IP address instead of the expected private IP address within a private-link-enabled VNet
rootCause: The VNet is not using the Private DNS zones established by AMPLS and the Private Endpoint — either Private Endpoint DNS integration is disabled, or the client resource is not integrated into the VNet with AMPLS
solution: Ensure AMPLS is tied to a Private Endpoint on the same VNet as the client. During PE creation, confirm 'Integrate with private DNS zone' = Yes to auto-register private DNS zones. Run nslookup on the client to confirm the FQDN resolves to the private IP shown in the PE's DNS record sets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: Resource resolves Azure Monitor endpoint FQDN to a private IP but not the expected one (IP mismatch between nslookup result and AMPLS-defined IP)
rootCause: Another AMPLS resource was added to the same VNet or a peered VNet, creating duplicate/conflicting Private DNS zone records for Azure Monitor endpoints
solution: Follow guiding principle 'Avoid DNS overrides by using a single AMPLS'. In ASC, check App Insights component Properties > Private Links to identify all associated AMPLS resources. Remove the conflicting AMPLS so only one AMPLS covers each VNet. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/private-link-design#guiding-principle-avoid-dns-overrides-by-using-a-single-ampls
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: AMPLS Private Link 配置完成后，Web App / Function App 中对 Application Insights 端点（dc.services.visualstudio.com / rt.services.visualstudio.com）的 nslookup 仍解析到公网 IP，而非私有 IP
rootCause: 以下一个或多个配置缺失：(1) Private DNS Zone 中未为 Spoke VNET 添加 Virtual Network Link；(2) Spoke VNET 与 Hub VNET 之间的 VNET Peering 未处于 Connected 状态；(3) Web App / Function App 的 VNET Integration 未绑定正确的 Spoke VNET
solution: 1) 检查 AMPLs → PE → Private DNS Zone 中是否存在针对 Spoke VNET 的 Virtual Network Link，若无则添加；2) 验证 Spoke ↔ Hub VNET Peering 状态为 Connected；3) 确认 Web App 出站 VNET Integration 使用的是 Spoke VNET
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/AMPLs Setup Guide - Hub and Spoke Networks Private Link Design/Test the Azure Monitor Private Link Connection

symptom: Azure Monitor data ingestion fails from a resource (web app/function app) within a VNet configured with AMPLS; nslookup on the Azure Monitor ingestion endpoint returns a public IP address instead of a private IP
rootCause: The resource is not using the Private DNS zones established by AMPLS and the Private Endpoint within the VNet. The resource either lacks VNet integration or the Private DNS zones are not linked to the VNet, causing DNS resolution to fall back to public Azure DNS
solution: 1. Run nslookup from the resource to confirm it returns a public IP. 2. Verify the resource has VNet integration enabled pointing to the VNet containing the AMPLS-associated Private Endpoint. 3. Verify Private DNS zones created by AMPLS + Private Endpoint are linked to the resource's VNet. 4. In ASC, find the Azure Monitor resource > Properties > Private Links to confirm the AMPLS resource association. 5. On the AMPLS resource, confirm the Private Endpoint is on the same VNet as the resource. 6. For Windows App Services, use nameresolver.exe instead of nslookup to check DNS resolution from Kudu console.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: Azure Monitor data ingestion fails; nslookup on the Azure Monitor endpoint returns a private IP but it does not match the private IP shown in the AMPLS resource's Private Endpoint configuration
rootCause: A second AMPLS resource was added to the VNet or a peered VNet. AMPLS only allows one instance per VNet; adding a second AMPLS causes clients to resolve the Azure Monitor FQDN to the private IP defined by the wrong (conflicting) AMPLS resource
solution: 1. In ASC, find the Azure Monitor resource > Properties > Private Links to get the correct AMPLS and its Private Endpoint. 2. On the Private Endpoint properties, note the expected private IP for the Azure Monitor FQDN. 3. Compare the nslookup result with the expected IP. 4. Search all subscriptions in the network topology for other AMPLS resources that may be defining conflicting DNS records. 5. Consolidate to a single AMPLS per the guiding principle: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/private-link-design#guiding-principle-avoid-dns-overrides-by-using-a-single-ampls. 6. Remove or reconfigure the conflicting AMPLS resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: App/resource on VNet resolves AMPLS-monitored Azure Monitor endpoint FQDN (e.g. App Insights ingestion endpoint) to a public IP address instead of expected private IP
rootCause: Resource not using Private DNS zones within the VNet; AMPLS Private Endpoint DNS integration ('Integrate with private DNS zone') not properly configured, or Private Endpoint not on the same VNet as the client resource
solution: Verify AMPLS Private Endpoint is attached to the same VNet as the client resource; ensure 'Integrate with private DNS zone' is enabled during Private Endpoint creation; confirm nslookup from client returns a private IP matching the AMPLS PE config (not a public IP); use ASC to navigate AI/LA component > Properties > Private Links to identify the associated AMPLS and PE details
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: nslookup returns a private IP for an Azure Monitor endpoint but not the expected private IP defined in AMPLS configuration; telemetry or log data fails to reach the endpoint despite resolving to a private address
rootCause: Multiple AMPLS resources exist on the same VNet or peered VNets, causing DNS override conflicts; violates the 'single AMPLS per VNet' guiding principle — the incorrect AMPLS resource elsewhere in the network defines a different private IP for the same endpoint FQDN
solution: Identify the conflicting AMPLS resource: in ASC check all privateLinkScopes in the subscription and peered subscriptions; on each AMPLS check its associated Private Endpoint and 'Private DNS Zone Record Sets' to find which one defines the unexpected IP; remove the extra AMPLS resource or redesign network topology to use a single AMPLS per VNet per the 'Guiding principle: Avoid DNS overrides by using a single AMPLS' (https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/private-link-design#guiding-principle-avoid-dns-overrides-by-using-a-single-ampls)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope)/How-To/Validate Known Good AMPLS configuration

symptom: 配置 AMPLS 后，Application Insights 遥测端点（dc.services.visualstudio.com、live.applicationinsights.azure.com）DNS 解析仍返回公网 IP 而非私有 IP，遥测数据未经私有链路传输
rootCause: 以下一项或多项缺失/错误：(1) Spoke VNET 的 Virtual Network Link 未添加到 AMPLs→PE→Private DNS Zone；(2) Spoke VNET 与 Hub VNET 的 VNET Peering 状态非 Connected；(3) Web App/Function App 的 VNET Integration 未配置为路由全部出站流量到 Spoke VNET；(4) AMPLS Ingestion Access Mode 未设为 Private Only
