rootCause: Both autoinstrumentation (enabled via App Service UI) and manual SDK instrumentation (OpenCensus/OpenTelemetry) are active simultaneously in the Python web application, causing double collection of telemetry.
solution: Use only ONE instrumentation method: either autoinstrumentation (via App Service UI) OR manual SDK instrumentation, not both. If using manual SDK, disable autoinstrumentation in App Service > Monitoring > Application Insights.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Python/Requirements for autoinstrumention of Python web apps

symptom: Python autoinstrumentation does not work for container-based App Service deployment
rootCause: Python autoinstrumentation on App Services only supports Code publish type. Container publish type is not supported for Python autoinstrumentation.
solution: Use Code publish type for App Service deployment to leverage autoinstrumentation. For container deployments, implement manual SDK instrumentation using OpenTelemetry Python SDK.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Python/Requirements for autoinstrumention of Python web apps

symptom: AppLens App Insights Feature Status detector incorrectly shows Profiler and Snapshot Debugger as enabled for Python web app
rootCause: The App Insights Feature Status detector in AppLens does not correctly distinguish Python from .NET - it reports Profiler and Snapshot Debugger as enabled even though these features are NOT supported for Python applications.
solution: Ignore Profiler and Snapshot Debugger status in AppLens for Python apps. These features are only available for .NET. Use the Application Insights Java, Dot Net, Node, and Python Auto Instrumentation - Linux detector instead to validate actual autoinstrumentation status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Python/Validating successful autoinstrumentation with AppLens

symptom: Java App Service autoinstrumentation fails or conflicts when Startup Command contains -javaagent reference to old Application Insights 2.X SDK agent
rootCause: Startup Command in App Service General Settings contains reference to old 2.X Java agent (e.g. -javaagent:D:/home/site/wwwroot/applicationinsights-agent-2.6.4.jar) while App Service autoinstrumentation is enabled. The two approaches are mutually exclusive.
solution: In Azure Portal > App Service > Settings > Configuration > General Settings, clear the Startup Command field (remove any -javaagent:...applicationinsights-agent-2.x.x.jar reference). Autoinstrumentation manages agent injection automatically. If the classic 2.X SDK approach is needed, disable autoinstrumentation instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Java/Requirements and considerations for the autoinstrumentation of a Java web app

symptom: Java App Service autoinstrumentation agent not working correctly or crashing after adding custom configuration with preview properties
rootCause: Preview properties used in the Application Insights configuration (applicationinsights.json or the Configuration option field in Portal). The Java agent used with App Service autoinstrumentation is always a GA released version, so preview properties are not recognized and may cause agent malfunction.
solution: Remove all preview properties from the Application Insights configuration. Only use GA (generally available) configuration properties. If using APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONFIGURATION_FILE env var, check that custom config file also has no preview properties. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/java-standalone-config
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Java/Enabling autoinstrumentation for a Java web application

symptom: Python App Service autoinstrumentation not working or not collecting telemetry with Python version outside 3.8-3.11 range
rootCause: Python autoinstrumentation for App Service only supports Python versions 3.8 through 3.11 (as of Oct 2024). Earlier versions (3.7 and below) or newer versions (3.12+) are not supported.
solution: Verify App Service Python version in Portal > App Service > Settings > Configuration > General Settings > Stack settings. Ensure Python version is 3.8, 3.9, 3.10, or 3.11. If using an unsupported version, either change the runtime version or use manual instrumentation with OpenTelemetry SDK.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Python/Requirements for autoinstrumention of Python web apps

symptom: Need to identify the instrumentation method and language from Application Insights telemetry sdkVersion field
solution: Parse the sdkVersion prefix from requests or other telemetry tables. Examples: ali_py = a(autoinstrumentation) + li(linux) + py(python). KQL: requests | where timestamp >= ago(24h) | project sdkVersion, cloud_RoleName | take 10. The prefix encodes: instrumentation method (a=auto, m=manual), platform (li=linux, w=windows), and language (py=python, java, etc). See internal wiki: Break down SDKs used and their versions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Python/Generating telemetry and viewing it in ASC and Portal

symptom: Java App Service application or Application Insights autoinstrumentation not working properly due to Java version mismatch between App Service configuration and deployed application
rootCause: App Service web app is configured for one Java version (e.g. Java 8) but the application was built/compiled with a different Java version (e.g. Java 11). Code compiled in newer Java versions may not function on older runtimes due to new bytecode instructions or APIs.
solution: Verify Java version alignment: 1) Check App Service General Settings for configured Stack and Major version. 2) Check the application target Java version in build config (pom.xml, build.gradle). 3) Ensure App Service Java version >= application compiled Java version. Java 8+ is required for autoinstrumentation. Use jdeprscan (JDK 9+) to detect compatibility issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Java/Requirements and considerations for the autoinstrumentation of a Java web app

symptom: Application Insights shows as Enabled in App Service but autoinstrumentation actually failed - no telemetry data collected
rootCause: The Enabled status (in AppLens Feature Status detector or Portal) only means the autoinstrumentation toggle was set; it does NOT confirm that the Application Insights binaries were successfully injected into the application process. Injection can fail silently.
solution: Use AppLens 'Application Insights Auto Instrumentation' detector (not Feature Status) to verify actual injection status. For Java: check Kudu Advanced Tools > append /applicationinsights to URL to see injection status. Also verify via ASC Query Customer Data: union * | where timestamp >= ago(24h) | project timestamp, sdkVersion, itemType, cloud_RoleName
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Java/Validating successful autoinstrumentation with AppLens

symptom: App Service Python app autoinstrumentation status in Kudu (/applicationinsights) shows Auto-Instrumentation enabled successfully even when the Python app is not deployed or not running
rootCause: Unlike .NET apps, the Kudu /applicationinsights page for Python does not differentiate between a running and non-running app; it only reflects the instrumentation enablement state, not actual application status
solution: Do not rely solely on Kudu autoinstrumentation status for Python apps. Verify the application is actually deployed and running independently by checking App Service logs or accessing the application URL directly. For .NET apps the Kudu page shows a different status when the app is not running, but Python does not have this distinction.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Python/Validating successful autoinstrumentation with Kudu

symptom: Get-AzVMExtension returns ResourceNotFound error (HTTP 404) when checking Application Insights extension status on Azure VM
rootCause: The -Name parameter passed to Get-AzVMExtension does not match the exact name used when the extension was installed via Set-AzVMExtension. The name must be an exact match.
solution: Verify the exact extension name in the VM Extensions + applications blade in Azure Portal. Use that exact name (typically ApplicationMonitoringWindows) as the -Name parameter in Get-AzVMExtension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/Application Insights for Azure VMs and virtual machine scale sets/Post Install considerations

symptom: No telemetry data appears in Application Insights after successfully installing the ApplicationMonitoringWindows extension on Azure VM (ProvisioningState: Succeeded)
rootCause: Common causes: 1) Conflicting Application Insights SDK binaries already present; 2) Network connectivity issues to ingestion endpoint; 3) IIS Redfield module configured but w3wp processes not yet restarted
solution: 1) Check for conflicting SDK binaries in app bin folder; 2) Test connectivity to AI ingestion endpoint; 3) Use Process Explorer to verify AI binaries loaded in w3wp.exe; 4) Check extension logs at C:\Packages\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Diagnostics.ApplicationMonitoringWindows\<ver>; 5) Review Windows Application Event Log source Microsoft.ApplicationMonitoring.VmExtensionHandler; 6) Verify Live Metrics for real-time data flow
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/Application Insights for Azure VMs and virtual machine scale sets/Post Install considerations

symptom: Application Insights VM extension shows unhealthy/failed status but application monitoring works, or extension Succeeded but no applications instrumented
rootCause: Extension ProvisioningState only reflects whether IIS Redfield module was configured, not whether individual apps are instrumented. Extension is the delivery mechanism, not the agent itself.
solution: 1) Extension Succeeded = IIS Redfield configured, check VmExtensionHandler log for Successfully enabled Application Insights Status Monitor; 2) Verify instrumentation: use Process Explorer for AI binaries in w3wp.exe; 3) Check Live Metrics; 4) Extension unhealthy but app works = Redfield config persists from prior successful install; 5) Key log: No changes needed in applicationHost.config means already configured
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/Application Insights for Azure VMs and virtual machine scale sets/Post Install considerations

symptom: Function App with isolated worker (.NET out-of-process) shows different SDK versions for requests vs traces in Application Insights
rootCause: Out-of-process Functions use separate processes: Functions Host handles requests (host SDK version), isolated worker handles traces (azurefunctions-netiso SDK). Dual-SDK pattern is by design.
solution: Expected behavior for .NET isolated Functions. Verify both SDK patterns: 1) Requests table: Functions Host SDK; 2) Traces table: azurefunctions-netiso. Missing either indicates incomplete instrumentation. Use ASC Query Customer Data: requests | project timestamp, url, sdkVersion and traces | project timestamp, message, sdkVersion
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/Azure Function .Net Core Out of Process/Validate Telemetry in Application Insights

symptom: Python App Service autoinstrumentation status page in Kudu (/applicationinsights) shows 'Auto-Instrumentation enabled successfully' even when the Python application is not deployed or not running
rootCause: Unlike .NET apps, the Python autoinstrumentation Kudu status page does not differentiate between a running and non-running Python application. The status reflects IIS/extension readiness only, not the application process state.
solution: Do not rely on Kudu /applicationinsights status to determine if a Python app is actually running. Verify application deployment and runtime status separately via App Service logs, process explorer, or the App Service Overview blade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/App Service Web app Python/Validating successful autoinstrumentation with Kudu

symptom: Get-AzVMExtension returns 'ResourceNotFound' error when checking Application Insights VM extension status: 'The Resource Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}/extensions/{wrongName} was not found'
rootCause: The -Name parameter value passed to Get-AzVMExtension does not match the actual extension name that was used during Set-AzVMExtension installation. The name must exactly match what was registered.
solution: Check the actual extension name in the VM's 'Extensions + applications' blade in the Azure Portal. Use that exact name (typically 'ApplicationMonitoringWindows') in the -Name parameter of Get-AzVMExtension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/Application Insights for Azure VMs and virtual machine scale sets/Post Install considerations

symptom: Application Insights VM extension shows unhealthy or failed status in Azure Portal but the application is still sending telemetry to Application Insights successfully
rootCause: An extension is not an agent. The extension is the delivery/installation mechanism for the underlying Application Insights monitoring functionality. The extension status reflects the extension handler state, not the actual monitoring functionality. The underlying Redfield/StatusMonitor binaries injected into IIS can continue to function even if the extension itself reports unhealthy.
solution: Verify actual monitoring functionality independently: check Application Insights for incoming telemetry, review IIS applicationHost.config for Redfield module configuration, and check the ApplicationMonitoring.VmExtensionHandler log at C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Diagnostics.ApplicationMonitoringWindows\<ver>. Do not rely solely on extension status to determine if monitoring is working.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/Application Insights for Azure VMs and virtual machine scale sets/Post Install considerations

symptom: Out-of-process (.NET isolated) Azure Function App shows different SDK versions for different telemetry types in Application Insights: requests show Functions Host SDK version while traces show 'azurefunctions-netiso' SDK version
rootCause: In out-of-process Functions, the Functions Host process handles request telemetry (using the host SDK), while the isolated worker process handles other telemetry types like traces (using the netiso SDK). This dual-process architecture results in different sdkVersion values for different telemetry types.
solution: This is expected behavior for out-of-process Functions. When investigating SDK version discrepancies, check the sdkVersion field: 'azurefunctions:' prefix indicates host-generated telemetry, 'azurefunctions-netiso:' indicates isolated worker telemetry. Both are valid and expected in the same Application Insights resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Autoinstrumentation/Azure Function .Net Core Out of Process/Validate Telemetry in Application Insights

symptom: Application Insights availability test reports failure for a URL that appears to work in the browser
rootCause: The URL configured in the availability test is not accessible from the public internet. Availability tests run from dedicated Azure VMs that resolve DNS publicly and connect over the public internet on port 443. If the DNS is not publicly resolvable, or the endpoint is behind a firewall/VPN, the test will fail.
solution: 1) Ensure the URL's DNS is publicly resolvable (any machine on the public internet can resolve it). 2) Ensure the endpoint accepts inbound traffic over port 443 from the public internet. 3) The availability test follows up to 10 redirects. 4) For internal/private endpoints, use Custom Web Tests with trackAvailability() method instead of standard tests. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/availability-azure-functions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Availability/Availability Tests/Availability Test Architecture

symptom: Application Insights availability test consistently or sporadically fails from one or two specific regions while other test locations succeed
rootCause: Three common causes: (1) The Azure VMs running availability tests in that region have connectivity issues (e.g., DNS resolution failure, network problems). (2) Firewalls, CDNs, load balancers, or security features block inbound traffic from specific regions. (3) Failover configurations cause transient failures when traffic from specific regions is rerouted during failover hiccups.
solution: 1) Verify the site is accessible from the affected regions (test from outside the internal network). 2) Check if firewall/CDN rules block availability test IPs from specific regions. 3) For transient failures, check failover configurations. 4) Use ASC 'Application Insights Auto Instrumentation' detector and availability test troubleshooting steps. 5) If site returns an unexpected response code, the issue is with the site, not the availability test.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Availability/Availability Tests/Availability Test Location Recommendation

symptom: Application Insights availability test alerts firing frequently with false positives — site is available to users but alerts keep triggering
rootCause: Too few test locations configured (less than the recommended minimum of 5). With fewer locations, the alert threshold is too sensitive and transient internet connectivity issues from a single location can trigger alerts. Default alert threshold requires failures from (locations - 2) regions.
solution: 1) Configure at least 5 test locations (minimum recommendation). 2) Set alert location threshold to (number of test locations - 2). 3) Enable retries — approximately 80% of transient failures disappear on retry. 4) Verify the alert is not a true positive by checking if actual users report the same issue. 5) If fewer than 5 locations needed, adjust the alert sensitivity accordingly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Availability/Availability Tests/Availability Test Location Recommendation

symptom: Application Insights Java SDK starts successfully but no telemetry data is collected. Self-diagnostics logs show connectivity failure to the ingestion endpoint with cipher suite errors
rootCause: The JVM does not have any of the TLS cipher suites supported by the Application Insights ingestion endpoint. The SDK initializes but cannot establish a secure connection to transmit telemetry.
solution: 1) Enable Java SDK self-diagnostics to capture detailed logs. 2) Check logs for cipher suite related errors. 3) Update the JVM to a version that includes supported TLS cipher suites, or configure JVM security settings (java.security) to enable compatible ciphers. 4) Ensure TLS 1.2+ is supported by the JVM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/Understanding Self-Diagnostics in Application Insights/What is Logged?

symptom: Application Insights SDK fails to send telemetry. Self-diagnostics logs show Azure Identity token acquisition failure during SDK initialization
rootCause: Local authentication (Local Auth) is disabled on the Application Insights resource. The SDK requires AAD/Entra ID authentication but is not configured with proper Azure Identity credentials (Managed Identity, Service Principal, etc.).
solution: 1) Check self-diagnostics logs for token acquisition errors. 2) Configure the SDK with Azure Identity authentication — use Managed Identity for Azure-hosted apps, or Service Principal/Client Certificate for other environments. 3) Alternatively, if appropriate, re-enable Local Auth on the AI resource. 4) Verify the identity has the 'Monitoring Metrics Publisher' role on the AI resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/Understanding Self-Diagnostics in Application Insights/What is Logged?

symptom: No telemetry data appearing in Application Insights. Self-diagnostics logs show the ingestion endpoint cannot be resolved (DNS resolution failure)
rootCause: DNS resolution failure for the Application Insights ingestion endpoint (e.g., *.in.applicationinsights.azure.com). Common causes: network restrictions, Private Link/AMPLS misconfiguration, custom DNS that doesn't resolve Azure endpoints, or proxy/firewall blocking DNS.
solution: 1) Verify DNS resolution from the application host: nslookup <region>.in.applicationinsights.azure.com. 2) Check if Private Link/AMPLS is configured and verify private DNS zones are set up correctly. 3) Check proxy settings and firewall rules. 4) For AMPLS environments, ensure the ingestion endpoint resolves to a private IP. 5) Test connectivity: curl -v https://<region>.in.applicationinsights.azure.com/v2.1/track
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/Understanding Self-Diagnostics in Application Insights/What is Logged?

symptom: No telemetry data in Application Insights. Self-diagnostics logs indicate the instrumentation key or connection string is invalid
rootCause: The instrumentation key or connection string configured in the SDK is invalid, malformed, or belongs to a deleted or different Application Insights resource.
solution: 1) Check self-diagnostics logs for invalid ikey/connection string errors. 2) Copy the correct connection string from Azure Portal > Application Insights resource > Overview > Connection String. 3) Verify the connection string format: InstrumentationKey=<guid>;IngestionEndpoint=https://<region>.in.applicationinsights.azure.com/. 4) Ensure environment variables or config files are not overriding the connection string with stale values.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/Understanding Self-Diagnostics in Application Insights/What is Logged?

symptom: Telemetry data stops appearing in Application Insights partway through the day. Self-diagnostics logs show daily cap was reached and data is being dropped
rootCause: The Application Insights daily data cap (daily quota) has been reached. The ingestion service drops new telemetry items once the cap is hit. The SDK itself continues to operate normally but the backend rejects the data.
solution: 1) Check self-diagnostics logs for daily cap reached messages. 2) Review daily cap setting: Azure Portal > Application Insights > Configure > Usage and estimated costs. 3) Increase the daily cap if the current volume is expected. 4) If unexpected volume, investigate which telemetry types are contributing most and consider sampling or filtering. 5) Note: with regional endpoints (connection strings), overage is limited to max(DailyCap*1.1, DailyCap+1GB).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/Understanding Self-Diagnostics in Application Insights/What is Logged?

symptom: Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Java agent does not work with Quarkus native applications — no telemetry collected
rootCause: The Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Java agent is not compatible with Quarkus native apps due to native compilation (GraalVM) limitations. The Java agent relies on bytecode instrumentation which is not available in native images.
solution: Use the community Quarkus OpenTelemetry Exporter for Azure instead of the standard Java agent: quarkus-opentelemetry-exporter-azure (https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-opentelemetry-exporter/dev/quarkus-opentelemetry-exporter-azure.html). Note: this is NOT a Microsoft-supported library. For non-native Quarkus apps (JVM mode), the standard Java agent may work.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/What is an Application Framework?

symptom: Application Insights daily cap is significantly exceeded — data ingestion is 5-10x the configured daily cap limit
rootCause: Customer is using global ingestion endpoint (ikey-only configuration via dc.services.visualstudio.com) instead of regional endpoint with connection string. Global endpoint distributes traffic across multiple datacenters, each with separate Redis cache instances. Daily cap enforcement requires coordinated tracking which only works reliably with regional endpoints (single datacenter, unified Redis cache).
solution: 1) Migrate from instrumentation key (ikey-only) to connection string with regional endpoint. With connection strings, overage is bounded: max(DailyCap*1.1, DailyCap+1GB). 2) Check if SDK/agent is configured with just InstrumentationKey= (global) vs full ConnectionString= (regional). 3) For billing disputes due to daily cap overage on global endpoint, reference the fast-capping SLA (rolled out March 2023). 4) Public docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/sdk-connection-string
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Ingestion/Daily Cap Explained

symptom: Customer reports missing telemetry data in Application Insights; not seeing all expected request/dependency/exception data
rootCause: Sampling is enabled - either client-side (adaptive or fixed-rate in SDK config) or server-side (ingestion sampling in portal). When active, itemCount field shows values > 1 indicating telemetry reduction.
solution: Check itemCount field in KQL query results (>1 = sampling active). Review SDK config for adaptive/fixed-rate settings (EnableAdaptiveSampling, UseAdaptiveSampling, UseSampling). Check portal: Usage and Estimated Costs > Data Sampling. Note: if client-side adaptive/fixed-rate is enabled, ingestion sampling is automatically disabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Ingestion/Sampling

symptom: Security scan flags Application Insights JavaScript SDK cookies (ai_user, ai_session) as missing HttpOnly attribute; customer concerned about cookie security vulnerabilities
rootCause: By design - the JS SDK must read and write ai_user/ai_session cookies client-side for user/session tracking. Setting HttpOnly would prevent the SDK from accessing cookie values, breaking telemetry correlation.
solution: Explain: (1) cookies contain no secrets - values only populate user_Id/session_Id fields; (2) SameSite=None and Secure attributes added by default in latest SDK; (3) Setting HttpOnly server-side causes SDK to generate random values, breaking correlation. Advise updating to latest JS SDK.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Manual instrumentation/Classic SDK/JavaScript/JavaScript SDK and cookies usage

symptom: CORS preflight (OPTIONS) request fails when Application Insights JavaScript SDK with distributed tracing makes cross-domain dependency calls; distributed correlation broken between frontend and backend
rootCause: JS SDK adds traceparent/Request-Id HTTP header to outbound cross-domain requests for distributed correlation, triggering CORS preflight that destination server may not be configured to accept.
solution: If customer controls destination server: enable CORS and configure Access-Control-Allow-Headers to accept traceparent/Request-Id. If customer does NOT control destination: use correlationHeaderExcludedDomains config option to exclude those domains from correlation header injection.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Manual instrumentation/Classic SDK/JavaScript/Understanding CORS

symptom: Inbound Request telemetry stops appearing in Application Insights after enabling Entra ID authentication and disabling local auth on Azure Function (isolated worker)
rootCause: Azure Function auto-instrumentation for isolated worker does not natively support Entra ID auth for initial inbound Request telemetry when local auth is disabled on Application Insights component.
solution: Use StartOperation() from TelemetryClient to manually create inbound Request telemetry. Ensure: (1) Function App has system-assigned Managed Identity; (2) Identity has Monitoring Metrics Publisher role; (3) Azure.Identity + WorkerService NuGet packages; (4) DefaultAzureCredential via SetAzureTokenCredential().
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Manual instrumentation/Classic SDK/Azure Function .Net Core Out of Process/Entra ID authentication for Application Insights using isolated worker function

symptom: Noisy synthetic request telemetry from Availability Tests inflating ingestion costs and cluttering Application Insights data
rootCause: No telemetry processor configured to filter out synthetic source requests generated by Availability Tests or health probes.
solution: Create ITelemetryProcessor (SyntheticRequestFilter) checking RequestTelemetry.Context.Operation.SyntheticSource - if non-empty, return without forwarding. Register via AddApplicationInsightsTelemetryProcessor<T>() in Program.cs. Validate: requests | summarize max(timestamp) by operation_SyntheticSource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Manual instrumentation/Classic SDK/ASPNet Core/Telemetry Processors and Initializers Exploration

symptom: Application Insights availability tests failing from 1-2 specific regions sporadically or consistently while other regions show success
rootCause: Backend VMs running web tests in specific regions may have connectivity issues; application network configuration (firewalls, load balancers, CDN) may block traffic from specific regions; failover configuration hiccups can cause brief regional failures
solution: Configure at least 5 test locations with alert threshold = locations - 2; verify endpoint accessibility from failing regions; check for region-specific firewall rules or network restrictions blocking availability test IP ranges; review if failures correlate with transient network issues vs persistent blocking
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Availability/Availability Tests/Availability Test Location Recommendation

symptom: Java Application Insights SDK starts successfully but no telemetry data received; self-diagnostics log shows connectivity failure to ingestion endpoint with cipher suite error
rootCause: JVM does not have any of the TLS cipher suites supported by the Application Insights ingestion endpoint
solution: Update JVM to a version that supports required TLS cipher suites, or manually configure supported cipher suites in JVM security settings; check self-diagnostics logs for specific cipher suite errors
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/Understanding Self-Diagnostics in Application Insights/What is Logged?

symptom: No telemetry data in Application Insights; self-diagnostics log shows Azure Identity token acquisition failure during SDK initialization
rootCause: Local Auth is disabled on the Application Insights resource but the SDK is not configured with Entra ID (AAD) authentication
solution: Configure SDK with proper Entra ID authentication using managed identity or service principal; alternatively re-enable Local Auth if Entra ID auth is not required
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/Understanding Self-Diagnostics in Application Insights/What is Logged?

symptom: No telemetry data in Application Insights; self-diagnostics log shows ingestion endpoint DNS resolution failure
rootCause: Application Insights ingestion endpoint cannot be resolved from the application network environment due to DNS misconfiguration or network restrictions
solution: Verify DNS resolution for ingestion endpoints (*.in.applicationinsights.azure.com); if using AMPLS/Private Link check private DNS zone configuration and VNet link setup; verify outbound network connectivity
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/Understanding Self-Diagnostics in Application Insights/What is Logged?

symptom: No telemetry data in Application Insights; self-diagnostics log indicates invalid instrumentation key or connection string
rootCause: Instrumentation key or connection string configured in the SDK does not match any valid Application Insights resource
solution: Verify and correct the instrumentation key or connection string in SDK configuration; copy the correct value from Azure Portal Application Insights resource Overview page
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/Understanding Self-Diagnostics in Application Insights/What is Logged?

symptom: Telemetry data stops appearing in Application Insights at a certain point during the day; self-diagnostics log indicates daily cap was hit
rootCause: Application Insights daily cap (quota) was reached causing ingestion service to drop subsequent telemetry for the remainder of the day
solution: Increase daily cap limit in Application Insights settings; investigate root cause of high ingestion volume; consider enabling sampling to reduce data volume
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/Understanding Self-Diagnostics in Application Insights/What is Logged?

symptom: Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Java agent fails to instrument Quarkus native applications; no telemetry data collected from Quarkus native app
rootCause: Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Java agent is not compatible with Quarkus native apps due to native compilation limitations
solution: Use community package quarkus-opentelemetry-exporter-azure instead of standard Azure Monitor Java agent; note this library is not officially supported by Microsoft
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Conceptual/What is an Application Framework?

symptom: Application Insights daily cap exceeded significantly despite being configured; unexpected high billing with iKey-only configuration using global ingestion endpoint
rootCause: Application uses iKey-only config with global ingestion endpoint (dc.services.visualstudio.com); telemetry resolves to multiple datacenter regions each with separate Redis cache counters for daily cap tracking causing cap not enforced accurately
solution: Migrate to connection strings with regional endpoint addresses for accurate daily cap enforcement; regional endpoints use shared Redis cache within datacenter ensuring proper cap coordination across ingestion stamps
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Ingestion/Daily Cap Explained

symptom: Application Insights daily cap shows slight overage with ingestion slightly above configured limit (up to 10% or 1GB extra)
rootCause: By design since March 2023 fast-capping feature; overage limit is max(DailyCap*1.1, DailyCap+1GB) due to distributed ingestion architecture with local in-memory cache and regional Redis cache sync delay
solution: Expected behavior since March 2023; overage bounded by max(DailyCap*1.1, DailyCap+1GB); ensure connection strings with regional endpoints for optimal behavior; not part of official SLA but can be referenced in billing refund discussions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Ingestion/Daily Cap Explained

symptom: Application Insights ingestion endpoint rejects telemetry items with timestamp older than 48 hours; curl POST returns error about time field being too old
rootCause: The timestamp in the telemetry payload exceeds the 48-hour ingestion window. This can happen when applications temporarily lose network connectivity and retry sending buffered telemetry hours later.
solution: Ensure the time field in the telemetry payload is within the last 48 hours (ISO 8601 format). For applications with intermittent connectivity, implement retry logic that refreshes timestamps or handles 48h expiry gracefully.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Networking/Network Connectivity/Validate TCP connectivity against Application Insights endpoints

symptom: Application Insights ingestion endpoint rejects telemetry payload sent via curl or SDK; itemsAccepted=0
rootCause: The instrumentation key (iKey) in the telemetry payload is invalid, placeholder, or does not match any existing Application Insights resource.
solution: Replace the iKey with the correct instrumentation key from the Application Insights resource. Preferred: use connection string (which includes regional ingestion endpoint) instead of standalone iKey.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Networking/Network Connectivity/Validate TCP connectivity against Application Insights endpoints

symptom: Application Insights telemetry not arriving in portal from web app (on-premises, Azure VM, or App Services) - no data visible despite SDK being configured
rootCause: Network connectivity failure at one of three stages: DNS resolution failure (cannot resolve ingestion endpoint FQDN), TCP connectivity failure (cannot reach endpoint IP on port 443 - blocked by NSG/firewall/proxy), or TLS handshake failure (TLS version or cipher suite mismatch between client and server)
solution: Diagnose in order: 1) DNS: run nslookup/nameresolver against regional endpoint (e.g., eastus-8.in.applicationinsights.azure.com); 2) TCP: use 'curl -v --tlsv1.2 --tls-max 1.2 https://<endpoint>:443' to verify 3-way handshake; 3) TLS: check Client Hello TLS version and cipher suites. Use connection strings (regional endpoints) instead of instrumentation keys (global endpoint).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Networking/Network Connectivity/Understanding DNS, TCP, and TLS connectivity in Application Insights

symptom: Application Insights endpoint resolves to unexpected IP address - nslookup shows private IP instead of public IP (or vice versa) for ingestion endpoint
rootCause: AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope) modifies the DNS CNAME chain: *.privatelink.monitor.azure.com private DNS zone intercepts resolution and returns private IP instead of public *.cloudapp.azure.com IP. Without AMPLS, resolution follows full CNAME chain to public IP.
solution: Examine nslookup CNAME chain aliases: without AMPLS, resolution goes through *.cloudapp.azure.com → public IP; with AMPLS, *.privatelink.monitor.azure.com → private IP. Verify: 1) AMPLS configuration and linked scopes; 2) Private DNS zone records; 3) Use mxtoolbox.com/SuperTool for external DNS chain validation to compare public vs private resolution paths.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Networking/Network Connectivity/Understanding the nslookup command

symptom: App Service web app fails to resolve Application Insights ingestion endpoint - nameresolver times out or returns no response in App Service Console
rootCause: Custom DNS server configured via WEBSITE_DNS_SERVER environment variable in App Service points to unreachable or non-existent DNS server IP. DNS queries are sent to the custom server instead of Azure DNS (168.63.129.16) and time out without response.
solution: Check App Service Environment Variables for WEBSITE_DNS_SERVER setting. If set to unreachable IP (e.g., 10.0.0.1): correct to actual DNS server IP in VNET, or remove the setting to revert to default Azure DNS (168.63.129.16). Validate with 'nameresolver eastus-8.in.applicationinsights.azure.com' in App Service Console blade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Networking/Network Connectivity/Validate DNS connectivity against Application Insights endpoints

symptom: Application Insights ingestion endpoint rejects buffered telemetry items after network outage recovery - error message indicates timestamp is too old
rootCause: Application Insights ingestion endpoint rejects telemetry items with timestamps older than 48 hours. Telemetry buffered during extended network outage accumulates stale timestamps beyond the acceptance window.
solution: Inform customer that ingestion endpoint has a 48-hour timestamp acceptance window. For connectivity validation: use curl POST with sample AvailabilityData payload to /v2/track endpoint with valid iKey and recent timestamp. Replace dc.applicationinsights.azure.com with regional endpoint from connection string. Successful response shows 'itemsReceived' and 'itemsAccepted'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Networking/Network Connectivity/Validate TCP connectivity against Application Insights endpoints

symptom: GET request to Application Insights ingestion endpoint (e.g. eastus-3.in.applicationinsights.azure.com:443) returns HTTP 404 Not Found; engineer or customer believes endpoint is down
rootCause: Application Insights ingestion endpoints are designed to accept only HTTP POST requests with telemetry payloads. GET requests are not supported and return 404 by design.
solution: A 404 response to a GET request against an App Insights ingestion endpoint is expected behavior, not an error. To validate connectivity, use curl with POST and a sample telemetry payload: curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" -X POST -d '{sample_payload}' https://<endpoint>/v2/track. A successful response with itemsAccepted > 0 confirms end-to-end connectivity (DNS + TCP + TLS + ingestion).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Networking/Network Connectivity/Validate TCP connectivity against Application Insights endpoints

symptom: App Service web app stops sending telemetry to Application Insights after configuring custom DNS via WEBSITE_DNS_SERVER environment variable; nameresolver shows failed DNS resolution
rootCause: The custom DNS server specified in the WEBSITE_DNS_SERVER App Service environment variable cannot resolve Application Insights ingestion endpoints (e.g. *.in.applicationinsights.azure.com). The DNS server may be unreachable, misconfigured, or lacks forwarders for public name resolution.
solution: 1. Open App Service Console blade → run 'nameresolver <regional-endpoint>.in.applicationinsights.azure.com' to verify DNS resolution. 2. If resolution fails, check the IP in WEBSITE_DNS_SERVER: ensure the DNS server is reachable and can resolve public FQDNs. 3. If using private DNS in a VNET, configure a DNS forwarder to Azure DNS (168.63.129.16) for public name resolution. 4. Default behavior (no WEBSITE_DNS_SERVER) uses Azure DNS which resolves AI endpoints correctly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Networking/Network Connectivity/Validate DNS connectivity against Application Insights endpoints

symptom: Application fails to send telemetry to Application Insights ingestion endpoint; TLS handshake failure / connection refused when using TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, or SSL3
rootCause: Application Insights ingestion endpoints no longer support TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, or SSL3. Only TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 are accepted. The client process negotiates TLS version based on OS and app-level settings, and if it selects a deprecated version the endpoint closes the connection.
solution: Ensure the application uses TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3. In PowerShell: [System.Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [System.Net.SecurityProtocolType]::TLS12. For .NET apps, set targetFramework to 4.7+ or explicitly configure SecurityProtocol. Validate with Qualys SSL Labs tool against the ingestion endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Networking/Network Connectivity/Validate TLS connectivity against Application Insights endpoints

symptom: End-to-End transaction view shows total operation duration significantly longer than the sum of visible dependency durations; durations do not add up
rootCause: The gap between total operation duration and tracked dependency durations is caused by in-process code execution (CPU-bound methods, Thread.Sleep, untracked internal calls) that Application Insights cannot automatically instrument.
solution: Check application code for synchronous/blocking calls between dependency operations. Look for untracked dependencies or CPU-bound processing within the method. Use Profiler to identify where time is spent in application code.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Portal Experiences/Understand End-to-End transaction experience

symptom: Application Insights telemetry ingestion fails with connection refused/closed error when application sends telemetry to ingestion endpoint
rootCause: Application or server using deprecated TLS version (TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, or SSL3) to connect to Application Insights ingestion endpoint which only supports TLS 1.2+
solution: Set SecurityProtocol to TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3. Verify with Qualys SSL Labs tool against ingestion endpoint URL. Note: each process (w3wp.exe, dotnet.exe, java.exe, node.exe) determines TLS version independently based on OS and app-level settings - validate with network trace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Networking/Network Connectivity/Validate TLS connectivity against Application Insights endpoints

symptom: Application Map blade in Application Insights shows no nodes or connections - map appears empty
rootCause: No application activity (requests/dependencies) occurred during the selected lookback time range. Application Map dynamically queries requests and dependencies tables for the selected period.
solution: Extend the lookback time range on Application Map (e.g., from 1h to 24h). Verify application has active traffic by running: requests | where timestamp > ago(1h) in Logs blade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Portal Experiences/How the Application Map works

symptom: Application Map shows unexpected or incorrect application node label (e.g., showing generic appName instead of expected cloud_RoleName)
rootCause: cloud_RoleName is not set in telemetry. Application Map preferentially uses cloud_RoleName for node labels and falls back to appName when cloud_RoleName is empty.
solution: Configure cloud_RoleName via SDK TelemetryInitializer or WEBSITE_CLOUD_ROLENAME env var for App Service. Verify with: requests | project cloud_RoleName, appName | take 10
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Portal Experiences/How the Application Map works

symptom: Performance blade Operations tab shows different request counts or durations compared to manual KQL queries in Logs blade
rootCause: Performance blade uses TOP 10 operations by itemCount so individual sums do not total Overall count. It uses weighted average sum(itemCount*duration)/sum(itemCount) which is affected by sampling.
solution: Use exact Performance blade queries: requests | summarize sum(itemCount), avg_duration=sum(itemCount*duration)/sum(itemCount) by operation_Name | top 10 by sum_itemCount desc. For Overall query separately without grouping. Sampling impacts itemCount calculations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Portal Experiences/How the Performance blade works

symptom: End-to-End transaction view shows total request duration significantly longer than sum of visible dependency call durations
rootCause: Application code executing in-process between tracked dependency calls (synchronous methods, Thread.Sleep, CPU computation) is not tracked as separate dependency operations by auto-instrumentation.
solution: Review application code for time-consuming in-process operations between dependency calls. Look for untracked custom dependencies or synchronous code. FAQ: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/transaction-diagnostics#faq
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Portal Experiences/Understand End-to-End transaction experience

symptom: Metric Explorer values for Application Insights metrics (e.g., Server requests) differ from KQL queries in Logs blade
rootCause: Metric Explorer defaults to standard metrics namespace (pre-aggregated MDM with fewer dimensions). Logs uses log-based metrics with full telemetry. Aggregation types differ: standard uses Count, log-based uses Sum of itemCount.
solution: In Metric Explorer select Log-based metrics namespace + Sum aggregation to match Logs. Use Drill into Logs feature (log-based namespace only). For standard metrics use Count aggregation. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/metrics-overview
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Course Materials/Portal Experiences/Understand Metrics and Application Insights

symptom: Visual Studio publish to Azure App Service fails with 'User unauthorized' error after basic authentication is disabled on internal applications
rootCause: Internal Azure App Service applications have basic authentication disabled for security. Visual Studio versions older than 17.5 do not support Azure AD authentication for Web Deploy, causing deployment to fail with 401/unauthorized.
solution: 1) Update Visual Studio to version 17.5 or newer. 2) If still failing on latest VS: sign out → install Feature Flags extension (PaulHarrington.FeatureFlagsPreview) → enable Publish.EnableAzureADAuth in Tools > Options → re-sign in and retry publish.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Pre-reqs/Deploying with Visual Studio to Azure App Services

symptom: Python App Service Web Apps are not visible in Visual Studio IDE Publish wizard; cannot deploy Python web app via VS Publish UI
rootCause: Visual Studio IDE limitation — as of Oct 2024 the VS Publish experience does not display Python App Service Web Apps in its resource picker, even though the web app exists in Azure.
solution: Use Azure CLI from VS Developer PowerShell instead: az webapp up --name <appName> --resource-group <rg> --runtime PYTHON:<version> --sku B1 --logs. This deploys the Python app directly via CLI zip deployment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Pre-reqs/Deploying with Visual Studio to Azure App Services

symptom: Visual Studio publish to Azure App Service fails with 'User unauthorized' error when basic authentication is disabled on internal applications
rootCause: Internal Azure App Service applications have basic authentication disabled for security. Visual Studio versions older than 17.5 do not support Azure AD authentication for Web Deploy, causing 401/unauthorized failures during publish.
solution: 1) Update Visual Studio to version 17.5 or newer. 2) If still failing on latest VS: sign out → install Feature Flags extension (PaulHarrington.FeatureFlagsPreview) → enable Publish.EnableAzureADAuth in Tools > Options → re-sign in and retry publish.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Learning Resources/Training/Pre-reqs/Deploying with Visual Studio to Azure App Services

symptom: Application Insights Availability test starts failing for a URL endpoint after TLS 1.0/1.1 retirement rollout reaches the test region (May-June 2025 schedule). Test results show SSL/TLS handshake errors.
rootCause: Azure is retiring TLS 1.0, 1.1, and legacy TLS 1.2 cipher suites/elliptical curves. Availability test clients in public cloud regions are updated region-by-region to no longer fall back to deprecated TLS versions. The customer's tested endpoint does not support TLS 1.2 with approved cipher suites.
solution: 1) Verify endpoint TLS support using ssllabs.com/ssltest/. 2) Ensure endpoint supports TLS 1.2 with cipher suites listed at https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/availability?tabs=standard#supported-tls-configurations. 3) The hosting provider (App Services, IIS, VMSS, etc.) must update the TLS configuration — App Insights team cannot fix this. 4) Use ASC webtests Properties tab to locate tested URLs. 5) Check region retirement schedule: brs 5/13, eus 5/19, frs 5/27, ncus 5/29, jpe 6/2, eas 6/3, cus 6/4, aue 6/9, sea 6/10, scus 6/11, wuk 6/16, frc 6/17, suk 6/18, wus2 6/23, neu 6/24, weu 6/25.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/Deprecation of TLS 1.0, 1.1, and legacy 1.2 Cypher suites and Ellipticals curves

symptom: Application Insights telemetry stops flowing after TLS retirement. ASC Ingestion tab shows data routing through global ingestion endpoint (dc.services.visualstudio.com). Application uses instrumentation key only, not connection string.
rootCause: The global ingestion endpoint (dc.services.visualstudio.com) is removing TLS 1.0/1.1 support on a rolling schedule (North Europe → West US2 → West Europe → East US → Australia East). Applications using only instrumentation key route through this deprecated global endpoint which now requires TLS 1.2+.
solution: 1) Migrate from instrumentation key to connection string per https://app.azure.com/h/CL61-B_8/9ced88 — this routes to regional ingestion endpoint (already TLS 1.2 only). 2) Verify migration via ASC Ingestion by Endpoint — should show only regional endpoint. 3) For many subscriptions, open CRI to App Insights ingestion team for bulk analysis. 4) Use ASC Geneva Action 'CheckGlobalIngestion' extension to list all components still using global endpoint in a subscription.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/Deprecation of TLS 1.0, 1.1, and legacy 1.2 Cypher suites and Ellipticals curves

symptom: Application Insights telemetry still not flowing after migrating from instrumentation key to connection string. ASC Ingestion by Endpoint and Drop By show no data at all (empty). TLS negotiation fails before any connection is established.
rootCause: The application runtime or OS does not support TLS 1.2. App Insights classic SDKs and OTEL solutions do NOT dictate or set TLS settings — the connection relies entirely on what the application or OS negotiate. If the runtime (.NET, Java, Node.js) or OS defaults to TLS 1.0/1.1, the handshake fails silently.
solution: 1) Check runtime TLS defaults: .NET depends on OS + .NET runtime version + any explicit TLS version in code; Java 3.X requires Java 8+ (TLS 1.2 default); JavaScript CDN already requires TLS 1.2. 2) The App Insights team is NOT the correct team to fix this — escalate to the application developer, OS admin, or the Azure service team owning the integration (e.g., API Management team). 3) Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/application-insights-faq#determine-if-tls-retirement-affects-you
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/Deprecation of TLS 1.0, 1.1, and legacy 1.2 Cypher suites and Ellipticals curves

symptom: Application Insights Availability tests start failing after TLS 1.0/1.1 retirement is rolled out in the test's region (May-June 2025 schedule). Tests return TLS handshake failures or connection errors.
rootCause: The target URL endpoint configured in the Availability test only supports deprecated TLS versions (1.0/1.1) or uses unsupported TLS 1.2 cipher suites and elliptical curves that are being removed.
solution: 1) Verify endpoint TLS support using ssllabs.com/ssltest. 2) Check supported cipher suites at https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/availability?tabs=standard#supported-tls-configurations. 3) Update the endpoint server to support TLS 1.2+ with supported cipher suites. Note: TLS version is controlled by the hosting server, not Application Insights — refer customer to App Services/VMSS/IIS teams or hosting provider.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/Deprecation of TLS 1.0, 1.1, and legacy 1.2 Cypher suites and Ellipticals curves

symptom: Application Insights telemetry ingestion fails or data stops appearing after TLS retirement date. Customer uses instrumentation key (not connection string) pointing to global ingestion endpoint dc.services.visualstudio.com.
rootCause: Application is configured with instrumentation key only, sending telemetry to the deprecated global ingestion endpoint which is removing TLS 1.0/1.1 support on a per-region schedule (North Europe, West US2, West Europe, East US, Australia East). Regional endpoints already require TLS 1.2+.
solution: 1) Migrate from instrumentation key to connection string (uses regional endpoint, already supports TLS 1.2+). See https://app.azure.com/h/CL61-B_8/9ced88. 2) Verify via ASC Ingestion tab → Aggregate by Ingestion Endpoint to confirm switch to regional endpoint. 3) For bulk subscription checks, use ASC Geneva Action 'CheckGlobalIngestion' or open CRI for App Insights ingestion team for large-scale analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/Deprecation of TLS 1.0, 1.1, and legacy 1.2 Cypher suites and Ellipticals curves

symptom: After migrating from instrumentation key to connection string, Application Insights telemetry still does not flow. No data appears in the portal and ASC Ingestion shows nothing (TLS negotiation fails before connection).
rootCause: The application or OS is forcing TLS 1.0/1.1 for outbound connections. Application Insights classic SDKs and OTEL solutions do NOT control TLS settings — the connection relies entirely on what the application code or OS dictates (.NET runtime version, OS TLS settings, or application code forcing a specific TLS version).
solution: 1) For .NET: check OS, .NET runtime version, and whether app code forces specific TLS version. 2) For Java 3.x: requires Java 8.x+ which defaults to TLS 1.2. 3) Refer to application developer or OS server administrator. 4) If the resource is an Azure service (e.g. API Management), route to that service's support team. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/application-insights-faq#determine-if-tls-retirement-affects-you
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/Deprecation of TLS 1.0, 1.1, and legacy 1.2 Cypher suites and Ellipticals curves

symptom: Application Insights endpoint returns HTTP 307 or 308 redirect; telemetry may be dropped at the original endpoint
rootCause: Connection string used differs from the one defined on the instrumentation key resource; SDK is pointed to a different stamp endpoint
solution: Update the connection string to match the iKey resource actual endpoint. If the SDK follows redirects, telemetry can still be ingested. NodeJS SDK receives 307; others receive 308.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/HTTP Response Code Quick Reference Guide

symptom: Application Insights endpoint returns HTTP 402 or 439 indicating monthly quota exceeded; telemetry stops being ingested
rootCause: The number of telemetry items has exceeded the configured monthly quota limit for the Application Insights resource
solution: Wait for the monthly quota to reset, or increase the monthly quota limit in the Application Insights resource settings. HTTP 439 is deprecated in new SDKs in favor of 402.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/HTTP Response Code Quick Reference Guide

symptom: Application Insights endpoint returns HTTP 404; telemetry is silently dropped and never ingested
rootCause: Connection string specifies a different region than the instrumentation key resource actual region
solution: Update the connection string to use the correct region matching the Application Insights resource location.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/HTTP Response Code Quick Reference Guide

symptom: Application Insights endpoint returns HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests); telemetry is being throttled and not ingested
rootCause: Client is sending too many telemetry items within a short time interval, exceeding the endpoint rate limit
solution: Reduce telemetry volume using adaptive sampling, fixed-rate sampling, or telemetry filtering. Throttling is temporary and auto-recovers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/HTTP Response Code Quick Reference Guide

symptom: Application Insights telemetry shows result code 0, Canceled, or Faulted instead of normal HTTP status codes
rootCause: Result code 0 = undefined/no response/premature termination. Canceled = request not completed (client cancellation or timeout). Faulted = no valid status code, infrastructure issue, or client-side exception.
solution: These are application-level dependency call results, not Application Insights issues. Investigate the application outbound calls for timeouts, cancellations, DNS failures, or connection issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/HTTP Response Code Quick Reference Guide

symptom: Customer reports unexpected exceptions appearing in Application Insights and asks why their application is throwing them
rootCause: Application Insights logs application exceptions by design. Exceptions in AI telemetry are thrown by the application or its dependencies, not introduced by AI.
solution: If ask is about AI not logging exceptions -> AI team handles. If ask is about WHY an exception occurs -> route to the relevant API/service team (e.g., SQL team for SQLExceptions).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Application's exceptions

symptom: Customer reports missing data in Azure API Management API Analytics dashboard and believes it is an Application Insights issue
rootCause: APIM API Analytics uses Diagnostic Settings to send data to a Log Analytics workspace, NOT Application Insights. The UI queries the workspace directly.
solution: Clarify API Analytics is powered by Diagnostic Settings -> Log Analytics, not AI. Route to APIM + Monitoring Essentials CSS. For APIM-to-AI integration, APIM CSS should check their wiki first. SAP: Azure/API Management Service/Monitoring and Diagnostics/Application Insights Integration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure API Management

symptom: Customer requests support for Azure AD B2C VS Code extension used to view Application Insights logs for B2C custom policies
rootCause: The Azure AD B2C VS Code extension (AzureADB2CTools.aadb2c) is NOT developed by Microsoft. It is a community/third-party tool explicitly unsupported by CSS.
solution: Not supported by CSS. Direct customer to file GitHub issue at https://github.com/azure-ad-b2c/vscode-extension/issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Active Directory B2C logs for custom policies/Azure Active Directory B2C VS Code extension

symptom: Customer reports issues with Azure Container Apps managed OpenTelemetry agent sending telemetry to Application Insights
rootCause: ACA managed OpenTelemetry agent (Preview) uses unsupported community version of OTel. Application Insights team does NOT support this integration.
solution: Route to App Services OSS team (SAP: Azure/Container Apps/Monitoring resources). For supported AI integration, use manual instrumentation with supported AI SDKs per https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/container-apps/observability.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Container Apps

symptom: Application Insights SDK/ingestion endpoint returns HTTP 404 - telemetry is dropped and not ingested
rootCause: Connection string specifies a region that does not match the iKey resource actual region. The ingestion endpoint only accepts telemetry from the stamp-specific endpoint matching the resource region.
solution: Update the Application Insights connection string to match the resource actual region. Verify the IngestionEndpoint in the connection string corresponds to the correct regional endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/HTTP Response Code Quick Reference Guide

symptom: Application Insights SDK receives HTTP 402 or 439 - telemetry ingestion stops with monthly quota exceeded error
rootCause: The Application Insights resource has exceeded its monthly data cap (daily/monthly quota limit). HTTP 402 is used by new SDKs, HTTP 439 by older SDKs (deprecated).
solution: Review and increase the daily cap in Application Insights resource settings (Settings > Usage and estimated costs > Daily Cap). Consider moving to a commitment tier or Log Analytics workspace-based pricing for higher volume.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/HTTP Response Code Quick Reference Guide

symptom: Application Insights SDK receives HTTP 307/308 redirect - telemetry may be dropped if SDK does not follow redirects
rootCause: Connection string does not match the iKey resource stamp endpoint. The ingestion endpoint returns a redirect to the correct stamp. HTTP 307 is returned to NodeJS SDK specifically; HTTP 308 to other SDKs.
solution: Update the connection string to use the correct IngestionEndpoint matching the resource stamp. If using NodeJS SDK, ensure it follows 307 redirects. Always use the full connection string from the Azure portal rather than just the instrumentation key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/HTTP Response Code Quick Reference Guide

symptom: Application Insights SDK receives HTTP 429 - telemetry is being throttled and some data may be lost
rootCause: Client is sending too many telemetry items to the endpoint within a short time interval, triggering rate limiting. This is separate from monthly quota (402/439).
solution: Implement sampling (adaptive or fixed-rate) to reduce telemetry volume. Use TelemetryProcessors/Filters to exclude low-value telemetry. Check for telemetry loops or excessive custom events.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/HTTP Response Code Quick Reference Guide

symptom: Application Insights shows Result Code 0, Canceled, or Faulted for dependency calls - customer believes App Insights is introducing errors
rootCause: Result Code 0 = undefined/no response (request never completed). Canceled = request initiated but not finalized (client cancellation or timeout). Faulted = no valid status code received. These are actual application-level outcomes, NOT introduced by App Insights.
solution: Investigate the application outbound dependency calls. Code 0 typically means timeout or DNS failure. Canceled means client-side cancellation. Faulted means exception during the call. Use App Insights End-to-End Transaction to correlate with exceptions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/References/HTTP Response Code Quick Reference Guide

symptom: Customer asks why Application Insights shows specific exceptions (e.g. SQLException) or how to prevent them - believes App Insights is causing the errors
rootCause: Application Insights logs exceptions thrown by the monitored application as expected behavior. The exceptions originate from the application code or APIs the application calls, not from App Insights itself.
solution: Clarify that App Insights is working correctly by surfacing application exceptions. If the question is why the exception is thrown or how to prevent it, route to the owning product team (e.g. SQL exceptions -> SQL team). App Insights team only handles scenarios where App Insights is not logging exceptions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Application's exceptions

symptom: Customer reports missing data in APIM API Analytics dashboard - confuses API Analytics with Application Insights telemetry
rootCause: API Analytics in Azure API Management uses Diagnostics Settings (sent to Log Analytics workspace), NOT Application Insights. The API Analytics UI queries are powered by workspace data, not App Insights data.
solution: Clarify: APIM API Analytics = Diagnostics Settings -> Log Analytics workspace (owned by Monitoring Essentials + APIM teams). APIM App Insights integration = separate feature for request tracing. Route API Analytics issues to Monitoring Essentials CSS. SAP: Azure/API Management Service/Monitoring and Diagnostics/Application Insights Integration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure API Management

symptom: Customer reports issues with Azure AD B2C VS Code extension for viewing Application Insights logs
rootCause: The Azure AD B2C VS Code extension (AzureADB2CTools.aadb2c) is NOT developed by Microsoft. It is a community/third-party tool. CSS does not support this extension.
solution: Direct customer to file a GitHub issue at https://github.com/azure-ad-b2c/vscode-extension/issues. Recommend using the Azure portal Application Insights blade or Azure Monitor Logs directly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Active Directory B2C logs for custom policies/Azure Active Directory B2C VS Code extension

symptom: Customer reports App Insights issues with Azure Container Apps managed OpenTelemetry agent - missing telemetry or agent not working
rootCause: ACA managed OpenTelemetry agent (Preview) was developed by the ACA team using an unsupported community version of OpenTelemetry. The Application Insights team does NOT support this integration.
solution: Route to ACA team (App Services OSS): SAP Azure/Container Apps/Monitoring resources. For supported App Insights integration, recommend manual SDK instrumentation per https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/container-apps/observability.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Container Apps

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Cosmos DB - dependency tracking questions or issues with Cosmos DB calls not appearing in Application Insights dependency telemetry
rootCause: Prior to June 2023, Cosmos DB was only trackable as HTTP dependency. After June 2023 update, native Cosmos DB protocol is automatically hooked and tracked by Application Insights (similar to SQL/TDS protocol).
solution: For Cosmos DB dependency tracking in App Insights: post-June 2023, native protocol is auto-tracked. For Cosmos DB-side issues, route to queue: MSaaS Big Data - Database, SAP: Azure Cosmos DB, Area Path: Azure/Cosmos DB. PG email: azurecosmossdk@microsoft.com, IcM: Azure Cosmos DB/SQL API SDKs and Spark Connector. Collect: precise ask, Cosmos DB resource URI, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure CosmosDB

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Dashboards - dashboard rendering issues or incorrect data display involving Application Insights metrics in Azure portal dashboards
solution: Distinguish between: (1) Functionality/rendering issues with dashboard itself -> Azure Portal Team, SAP: Azure/Portal/Issues with Dashboard Loading or Refreshing. (2) Data correctness issues where App Insights data is incorrect -> Azure Monitor owns it. Collect: precise ask, resource URIs, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Dashboards

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Databricks - customer references stale MSDN article about monitoring Databricks jobs with Application Insights using unsupported community Python SDK (applicationinsights 0.11.5 on PyPI)
rootCause: The public article (MSDN Magazine June 2018) is stale and instructs users to use an unsupported community Application Insights Python SDK (pypi.org/project/applicationinsights/0.11.5). This SDK is not maintained or supported by Microsoft.
solution: If customer is configuring the community Python SDK referenced in the stale article, engage Azure Databricks support team to work directly with Databricks PG. Do NOT troubleshoot the community SDK. SAP: Azure/Databricks/Monitoring and diagnostics/Diagnostics logs. Collect: precise ask, Databricks resource URI, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Databricks

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Logic Apps Standard - Application Insights configuration or data collection issues in Logic Apps Standard workflows, need to find host.json for telemetry config
rootCause: Logic Apps Standard runs on Azure Functions backend, so App Insights integration follows Azure Functions SDK implementation. This is NOT an integration scenario owned by Azure Monitor. Configuration is managed through host.json like standard Azure Functions.
solution: Logic Apps Standard uses Function App backend -> App Insights config via host.json file. To access: Logic Apps resource -> Advanced Tools blade -> Go -> navigate to C:\home\site\wwwroot\host.json. For integration issues, route to Azure Functions team. SAP: Azure/Logic App-Logic App (Standard)/Tools/Application Insights. Collect: Function App resource URI, workflow names, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Logic Apps Standard

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Machine Learning - monitoring integration questions or issues with ML services Application Insights enablement
solution: Machine Learning team provides prescribed means of enabling App Insights within ML services (see docs.microsoft.com/azure/machine-learning/how-to-enable-app-insights). For ML-specific monitoring issues, route to: SAP: Azure/Machine Learning/Monitoring/Other issue with model monitoring. Collect: precise ask, ML resource URIs, App Insights resource URI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Machine learning

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Monitor Networks Insights - case misfiled under Application Insights for Network Insights or Load Balancer Insights which are NOT Application Insights features
rootCause: The Insights keyword causes confusion - Network Insights (including Load Balancer Insights) is a custom solution created by Azure Networking team. It is NOT related to Application Insights despite sharing the Insights naming.
solution: Network Insights / Load Balancer Insights -> owned by Azure Networking team, not Application Insights. If error is specifically about saving/editing the workbook displayed by Network Insights -> Azure Monitor workbook team can assist. SAP: Azure/Network Watcher/Network Insights/Topology view.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Application Insights/Support Boundaries/Azure Monitor Networks Insights

symptom: App Insights support boundary for Azure Service Fabric microservice level - ServiceFabric telemetry initializer NuGet package (SDK prefix serviceremoting) is deprecated and no longer actively maintained by PG
rootCause: The NuGet package Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.ServiceFabric is from an old collaboration between .NET and Service Fabric Product teams and is no longer active/maintained. No further updates or fixes will be made. OpenTelemetry distro replacement is under early discussion.
solution: CSS offers best-effort support only on this SDK. Telemetry from this package shows SDK prefix serviceremoting. The standard App Insights SDK can be added to microservices on Service Fabric containers same as standard web apps. For customer feedback/feature requests, direct to Azure Monitor feedback channel (feedback.azure.com). No specific SAP routing listed - handle as standard App Insights instrumentation.
