solution: Query Log Analytics to verify CPU/memory usage against default limits using Perf table (memoryRssBytes, cpuUsageNanoCores). If exceeding limits, submit ICM to Container Insights PG requesting limit increase with cluster resource ID and suggested values. Confirm with customer before increasing as it takes more node resources. For high log scale, consider enabling High Scale Logs Collection preview.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Agent Management TSGs/TSG High CPU or Memory in ama-logs Pods

symptom: Container Insights agent cannot change Log Analytics Workspace because the previously connected workspace has been deleted; unable to remove or reconfigure Container Insights monitoring
rootCause: When the Log Analytics Workspace is deleted, the Container Insights agent still references it via the DCR resource ID, preventing workspace change through normal UI flow
solution: Delete the DCR associated with the cluster (named MSCI-{region}-{clusterName}) from Data Collection Rules blade: 1) Remove resource associations from Resources tab, 2) Delete the DCR itself, 3) Navigate to AKS cluster > Insights > Monitoring Settings > Advanced settings to select a new/existing workspace, 4) Click Configure to re-onboard
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Agent Management TSGs/TSG Unable to Change Log Analytics Workspace for Container Insights Agent After Deletion

symptom: Fluent-bit built-in multiline Java parser does not support custom exceptions in Container Insights multiline logging feature; custom Java exception stack traces not stitched into single log entry
rootCause: Known limitation - Fluent-bit built-in multiline Java parser only supports standard Java exceptions, not custom exception types
solution: This is a known limitation (by design). Custom Java exceptions are not supported by the Fluent-bit multiline parser. File ICM to Container Insights PG if customer has strong business need. Ref ICM 560446623.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Agent Management TSGs/TSG Multiline Logging Issues

symptom: Container Insights multiline logging does not stitch arbitrary log lines together; only exception call stack traces for supported languages (.NET, Go, Python, Java) are supported
rootCause: By design - multiline logging feature only stitches exception call stack traces for supported languages, not arbitrary multi-line log entries
solution: This is by design. Multiline logging only supports stitching exception call stacks for .NET, Go, Python, and Java. Arbitrary log lines are not stitched. Max supported log message with this feature is up to 64KB (default runtime truncation is 16KB). Ref ICM 565341097.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Agent Management TSGs/TSG Multiline Logging Issues

symptom: Associating more than 30 ContainerLogV2 DCRs to an AKS cluster in Container Insights multi-tenancy logging causes data loss
rootCause: Known limitation in Container Insights multi-tenancy logging feature - exceeding 30 ContainerLogV2 DCR associations on a single cluster leads to data loss
solution: Keep the number of ContainerLogV2 DCR associations per AKS cluster at or below 30. If more DCRs are needed, consider consolidating namespace filtering rules into fewer DCRs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Container Insights Configuration TSGs/TSG Container Insights Multi-Tenancy Logging Issues

symptom: Container Insights identified as root cause for high data ingestion into Log Analytics Workspace; customer wants to reduce the amount of data collected by Container Insights agent
rootCause: By default Container Insights collects all logs (stdout, stderr, environment variables) from all namespaces, which can result in very high data volume especially in clusters with verbose logging workloads
solution: Multiple mitigation options: 1) Use cost optimization feature (requires Managed Identity); 2) Disable stdout/stderr collection via ConfigMap cluster-wide or per namespace; 3) Disable environment variable collection via ConfigMap; 4) Auto cleanup completed jobs with TTL in job definition; 5) Move to Azure Monitor managed service for Prometheus and/or basic logs tier for ContainerLogV2. Identify top data sources using LA queries on ContainerLogV2 by namespace and LogSource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/High Data Ingestion or Cost TSGs/TSG Container Insights Sending Too Much Data to Workspace

symptom: Container Insights Insights page in Azure Portal shows no data but Log Analytics workspace has the data when queried directly
rootCause: Network isolation settings on the Log Analytics Workspace have public access for querying set to disabled, preventing the Azure Portal Insights UI from retrieving data
solution: Check the network isolation settings on the Log Analytics Workspace and enable public access for querying. The Insights portal UI requires public query access to display data.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Container Insights Configuration TSGs/TSG Container Insights

symptom: Container Insights logs appear intermittently or stop being collected; some time periods have data and others do not in Log Analytics workspace
rootCause: Log Analytics Workspace has a daily cap (daily ingestion limit) configured; once the cap is reached, all subsequent data is dropped until the next day
solution: Run query: _LogOperation | where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d) | search 'OverQuota' to confirm daily cap is being hit. If results returned, customer needs to either remove the daily cap or raise it to accommodate Container Insights data volume. Also check Operation table for full visibility.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Container Insights Configuration TSGs/TSG Container Insights

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana SMTP email notification setup fails in Microsoft internal (1P) tenants because SFI restrictions prevent creating client secrets on App Registrations, which are required for Azure Communication Services SMTP authentication
rootCause: SFI (Secure Future Initiative) restrictions block secret creation on Entra ID App Registrations in MS tenants. The SMTP setup with Azure Communication Services requires an App Registration client secret for authentication.
solution: This is a known limitation for MS internal tenants. SMTP email notifications via Azure Communication Services cannot be configured in MS tenants due to SFI restrictions. Consider alternative notification methods (webhook, Teams) or use a non-MS tenant for testing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/How-To/Setting up and testing SMTP with Azure Managed Grafana

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU resource shows Evicted status and the Grafana instance is inaccessible
rootCause: Essential SKU Azure Managed Grafana instances are hosted on SPOT VMs with no SLA. When Azure reclaims the SPOT VM capacity, the instance gets evicted.
solution: Wait for the instance to come back (can take 30 minutes up to 24 hours based on excess VM capacity), create a new Azure Managed Grafana resource in a different region, or upgrade to Standard SKU for SLA-backed availability.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Support Boundaries

symptom: User with proper permissions logs into Azure Managed Grafana endpoint but only sees a Welcome to Grafana Enterprise splash screen instead of the normal Grafana UI
rootCause: Instance requires patching by the Azure Managed Grafana PG team. This is not resolvable by CSS.
solution: Submit an ICM immediately to Azure Managed Grafana PG with all relevant info. The instance needs to be patched to restore normal operation. CSS cannot resolve this issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Support Boundaries

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana with private access enabled cannot connect to services (e.g. Prometheus, MySQL) running on Azure VMs in the same VNET. Data source connection test fails even though VMs are accessible from other VMs in the VNET.
rootCause: AMG private access only establishes a private endpoint for browser-to-Grafana connectivity. It does not enable Grafana-to-VM connectivity directly. VMs are not listed as supported resources for Managed Private Endpoint.
solution: Create an Internal Load Balancer in the VM VNET with Inbound NAT rules pointing to each VM service port. Create a Private Link Service on top of the LB. Then create a Managed Private Endpoint in AMG targeting the Private Link Service. Approve the connection manually. Use the Private Link Service private IP as the data source host in Grafana.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Learning Resources/Lab | Connecting services running on Azure VMs as a Grafana data source

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana cannot connect to Azure Database for MySQL/PostgreSQL Flexible Server with public network access disabled. Managed Private Endpoint does not list MySQL/PostgreSQL as supported resources.
rootCause: MySQL and PostgreSQL Flexible Servers are not supported as direct Managed Private Endpoint targets in Azure Managed Grafana. Deterministic outbound IPs method also fails when public access is disabled on the database.
solution: Use Azure Firewall DNAT approach: Create an Azure Firewall in the database VNET with AzureFirewallSubnet. Create a Route Table associated with the database subnet routing to the firewall. Create a NAT rule in the firewall translating the firewall public IP to the database private IP on the service port (3306 for MySQL, 5432 for PostgreSQL). Use the firewall public IP as the Grafana data source host.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Learning Resources/Lab | Connecting Private Databases (MySQL Flexible Server and PostgreSQL Flexible Server) to Azure Managed Grafana

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana cannot connect to MySQL or PostgreSQL databases running inside an AKS cluster as data sources
rootCause: Services running inside AKS pods are not directly accessible from Azure Managed Grafana. A Private Link Service with an internal load balancer is needed to expose the database ports.
solution: Deploy a Kubernetes Service with type LoadBalancer and azure-pls annotations (azure-load-balancer-internal=true, azure-pls-create=true) targeting the database pod (port 3306 for MySQL, 5432 for PostgreSQL). This creates an Internal LB and a Private Link Service automatically. Create a Managed Private Endpoint in AMG targeting the Private Link Service. Approve the connection and use the Private Link Service private IP as the Grafana data source host.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Learning Resources/Lab | Connecting Databases (MySQL and PostgreSQL) running on an AKS cluster to Azure Managed Grafana

symptom: Container Insights data missing from ContainerLog/ContainerLogV2 or KubeMonAgentEvents tables collected by Fluent-Bit. Fluent-bit log at /var/opt/microsoft/docker-cimprov/log/fluent-bit.log shows mem buf overlimit messages.
rootCause: Fluent-Bit memory buffer limit is too low for the volume of logs being generated on the node, causing data to be dropped.
solution: Increase Fluent-Bit memory buffer limit via ConfigMap (agent_settings.fbit_config section): set tail_mem_buf_limit_megabytes, tail_buf_chunksize_megabytes, tail_buf_maxsize_megabytes. Or migrate to High Scale logs collection (Preview). If pods OOMKilled, also increase memory limits per TSG High CPU or Memory in ama-logs Pods.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Collection Issues With Data From fluent-bit

symptom: ama-logs pods in CrashLoopBackOff with no ContainerLogV2 logs ingested. Fluent-bit logs show Too many open files errors. File descriptor exhaustion prevents fluent-bit from tailing container log files.
rootCause: Node file descriptor limit (fs.file-max or inotify) is too low for the number of container log files being tailed by fluent-bit, causing process crash.
solution: For AKS clusters: create new node pool with custom kernel parameters to increase fs.file-max. For Arc-enabled K8s: directly increase limits via sysctl (fs.inotify.max_user_instances=8192, fs.inotify.max_user_watches=524288) and persist in /etc/sysctl.conf. Temporary workaround: reduce log files to tail or use tail_ignore_older in ConfigMap.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Collection Issues With Data From fluent-bit

symptom: Annotation fluentbit.io/exclude true is present on pods but Container Insights data is still being collected and appearing in Log Analytics Workspace. No ConfigMap setting for filter_using_annotations is active.
rootCause: Container Insights agent does not follow Fluent Bit annotations by default. Annotation-based filtering must be explicitly enabled via ConfigMap setting.
solution: Enable annotation filtering in ConfigMap: set [log_collection_settings.filter_using_annotations] enabled = true. Apply ConfigMap via kubectl. After applying, ama-logs pods restart automatically. Verify excluded pods no longer send data. Manual restart: kubectl delete pod -n kube-system -l rsName=ama-logs-rs and kubectl delete pod -n kube-system -l component=ama-logs-agent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Collection Issues With Data From fluent-bit

symptom: Missing data or gaps in KubePodInventory, KubeNodeInventory, KubeEvents, ContainerNodeInventory, KubePVInventory, or KubeServices tables on large AKS clusters with high pod/node count.
rootCause: Known issue in the ruby plugin (Fluentd in ama-logs-rs) parsing large JSON payloads. Default PODS_CHUNK_SIZE is 1000, which is too large for clusters with many pods/nodes, causing parsing failures and data gaps.
solution: Configure smaller PODS_CHUNK_SIZE in container-azm-ms-agentconfig ConfigMap: download template from GitHub, uncomment agent_settings.chunk_config and PODS_CHUNK_SIZE lines, apply via kubectl. If still seeing gaps after reducing chunk size, enable the large cluster toggle. Monitor for remaining data gaps after applying.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Missing Data in Kube Tables on Large Clusters

symptom: Missing logs, InsightsMetrics, and Prometheus metrics in Container Insights for AKS cluster behind a firewall. Error messages in ama-logs-daemonset-mdsd (appinsights_err.log, mdsd.err, mdsd.info) and ama-metrics prometheus-collector show connection refused or transport dial errors to Azure Monitor endpoints.
rootCause: Firewall rules blocking outbound connections from AKS cluster to required Azure Monitor endpoints. Container Insights agent requires outbound access to specific endpoints for data ingestion.
solution: Add firewall rules (Azure Firewall Application Rule Collection or on-premise) to allow connections to required Azure Monitor endpoints per Network firewall requirements docs. Collect agent logs using troubleshooting script to identify specific blocked endpoints. Check describe_ama-logs-xxxx.txt for container status errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Missing Logs and Metrics in Container Insights and Prometheus - Firewall rules

symptom: Container Insights data missing on AKS cluster configured with HTTP Proxy. ama-logs mdsd.err shows proxy connection failures to Azure Monitor endpoints.
rootCause: HTTP Proxy configuration on AKS cluster interfering with Container Insights agent (MDSD) communication to Azure Monitor endpoints. Proxy settings are applied to agent traffic, blocking or corrupting connections.
solution: Apply ConfigMap for CI Agent to ignore proxy configuration: download container-azm-ms-agentconfig.yaml from GitHub, uncomment the proxy ignore settings section, apply via kubectl. Verify proxy config from ASC: AKS cluster > HTTP Proxy Config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Missing Logs and Metrics in Container Insights and Prometheus - Firewall rules

symptom: Missing logs and metrics in Container Insights and Prometheus on AKS cluster using Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS). MDSD error: Data collection endpoint must be used to access configuration over private link (ErrorCode:-2146172665). OTLP exporter shows connection refused to 127.0.0.1:55680.
rootCause: AMPLS misconfiguration for Container Insights and Managed Prometheus - missing or incorrect Data Collection Endpoint (DCE) configuration, Private Link not properly set up for both Container Insights ingestion and Prometheus metrics collection.
solution: Reconfigure AMPLS: create private link connection through Azure portal, ensure DCE is properly configured for private link. Set up Private Link for data ingestion for Managed Prometheus and Azure Monitor workspace. Check ama-logs container logs (kubectl logs) and ama-metrics prometheus-collector container (mdsd.err at /opt/microsoft/linuxmonagent/mdsd.err) for specific error messages.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Missing Logs and Metrics in Container Insights and Prometheus using AMPLS

symptom: Intermittent or missing Container Insights data in Log Analytics workspace. Customer reports data appearing and disappearing. _LogOperation table shows OverQuota entries indicating daily cap reached.
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace daily cap is being reached, causing all subsequent data ingestion to be dropped until the cap resets. This affects all data sources including Container Insights.
solution: Query: _LogOperation | where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d) | search OverQuota to confirm. Also check Operation table for full visibility. Resolution: remove or raise the daily cap on the Log Analytics workspace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG No Data in Log Analytics and Missing Logs in Container Insights

symptom: IIS logs not appearing in ContainerLogV2 in Log Analytics for IIS deployed in AKS cluster. All other container logs are collected normally. IIS writes W3C access logs to files inside container, not to STDOUT.
rootCause: IIS writes W3C access logs to files inside the container (e.g. C:\\inetpub\\logs\\LogFiles), not to STDOUT/STDERR. Container Insights only collects STDOUT and STDERR streams stored in ContainerLogV2. This is by-design Kubernetes behavior.
solution: Use Microsoft-supported LogMonitor (Windows Container Tools) to forward IIS W3C logs, EventLog, and ETW to STDOUT. Test-only workaround: add PowerShell to container spec to tail IIS log files to STDOUT. No AKS/ConfigMap/DCR setting can make Container Insights read IIS log files from inside a container.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Missing IIS Container Logs in Log Analytics

symptom: Syslog data not appearing in Log Analytics after enabling syslog collection in Container Insights for AKS cluster using Cilium enterprise networking. HostPort on port 28330 not bound.
rootCause: HostPort functionality is not enabled by default when using Cilium enterprise for AKS networking. Syslog collection requires HostPort to be enabled on port 28330 for syslog events to be received by the agent.
solution: Verify HostPort status: exec into ama-logs pod, run netstat -a | grep 28330 and cat /var/log/syslog | grep 28330. If netstat returns empty, HostPort is disabled. Involve AKS team or customer networking team to enable HostPort.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Syslog Collection Issues

symptom: Syslog data not appearing in Log Analytics after enabling syslog collection in Container Insights for AKS. DCR missing syslog data source, or rsyslog config file /etc/rsyslog.d/70-rsyslog-forward-mdsd-ci.conf missing on AKS node.
rootCause: Syslog onboarding did not complete successfully, or DCR was modified after onboarding removing syslog as a data source. Missing rsyslog forwarding configuration prevents syslog events from reaching the Container Insights agent.
solution: 1) Verify DCR has syslog data source and is associated with cluster. 2) Check parsed DCR configs under /etc/mdsd.d/config-cache/configchunks in ama-logs DaemonSet pods. 3) Verify rsyslog config at /etc/rsyslog.d/70-rsyslog-forward-mdsd-ci.conf on AKS nodes (debug via kubectl debug node). 4) Check ci-syslog-watcher.path is active. 5) Ensure AKS Linux node image meets minimum requirements and CI Agent >= ciprod12032022-c9f3dc30. Re-onboard if missing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Container Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/Missing Data TSGs/TSG Syslog Collection Issues

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana dashboards or alerts return HTTP 529 errors from Azure Monitor Metrics API. In Grafana Alerting, appears as DatasourceNoData alert with 529 error payload.
rootCause: HTTP 529 is a backend throttling error indicating the Metrics API server is busy (different from 429 per-customer throttling). The REST API query during alert evaluation or dashboard refresh fails to retrieve data.
solution: For alerting: modify alert configuration to show state as OK when no data is returned. Recommended: create equivalent alert in Azure Monitor instead of Grafana alerting. For dashboards: wait and retry refresh.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Managed Grafana Alerts and Dashboards Returning 529 Errors

symptom: Grafana dashboard panels querying Log Analytics produce HTTP 429 throttling errors. Panels fail to load data due to too many concurrent queries from the Managed Identity or App Registration.
rootCause: All Grafana dashboard queries run under a single identity (Managed Identity or App Registration), which can exceed Log Analytics concurrent query limits (max 5 concurrent queries per user). Long-running or complex queries and large time ranges exacerbate the issue.
solution: 1) Enable query caching in data source settings (set Min Interval). 2) Reduce dashboard time range. 3) Simplify queries or reduce panel count per dashboard. 4) Verify via Kusto query against armprodgbl cluster for 429 status codes on MICROSOFT.DASHBOARD targetResourceProvider. 5) PG is working on long-term mitigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Managed Grafana Dashboard Errors with 429 Throttling Error

symptom: When creating a new Azure Managed Grafana private endpoint, the SSO private DNS zone record (sso.weu) gets overwritten with the new instance IP address, causing concern about SSO access for existing Grafana instances.
rootCause: The SSO endpoint (e.g. sso.weu.privatelink.grafana.azure.com) is a global/regional login endpoint shared by all Managed Grafana instances in the same region. It does not distinguish between instances. The overwrite is expected behavior and does not break SSO for existing instances.
solution: No action needed for the overwrite itself - SSO traffic works regardless of which PE IP the SSO record points to. Caveat: if the PE whose IP is in the SSO record is deleted, all SSO records are removed. Manually re-add the sso record pointing to another PE IP. For isolated VNETs, disable Private DNS integration and create a CNAME: sso.<region> -> <region>.rp-prod.grafana.azure.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Multiple Managed Grafana instances sharing the same SSO private DNS zone record

symptom: After deleting a Managed Grafana private endpoint, other Grafana instances sharing the same private DNS zone lose SSO login capability. The sso.<region> DNS record disappears.
rootCause: When a private endpoint with DNS integration is deleted, Azure automatically removes all associated DNS records including the shared SSO record. Other Grafana instances depending on this SSO record lose their login endpoint.
solution: Manually re-add the sso.<region> A record in the private DNS zone pointing to another existing PE private IP. Alternatively, use CNAME record: sso.<region> -> <region>.rp-prod.grafana.azure.com to route SSO through the public endpoint (works even with public access disabled via AzureManagedGrafana service tag).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Multiple Managed Grafana instances sharing the same SSO private DNS zone record

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana plugin management feature installs an older version of a plugin than expected, or customer requests a new plugin that is not available in the plugin management catalog.
rootCause: By design, the Managed Grafana PG vets each plugin through a testing process before allowing it. Plugin versions available through plugin management may lag behind the latest upstream release.
solution: Submit an ICM to the Grafana PG with: plugin name, currently installed version, desired version (or new plugin details for new requests). The PG testing/approval process takes approximately 3-4 weeks. Set customer expectations accordingly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Plugin Management Feature Installing Old Version of Plugins or Requests for new Plugins

symptom: Customer accidentally deleted their Azure Managed Grafana resource and wants to restore it. The Grafana instance and all its dashboards/data are gone.
rootCause: Grafana resource was deleted (accidentally or intentionally). ARM does not support direct restore of Grafana resources.
solution: 1) Verify deletion within last 30 days via Kusto query on armprodgbl (filter httpMethod DELETE on resource URI). 2) Have customer create NEW Grafana instance in SAME REGION. 3) Submit ICM to Grafana PG requesting restore of deleted instance contents into new instance. Prevention: use az grafana backup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Restore a Grafana Resource or Dashboard

symptom: Customer accidentally deleted a Grafana dashboard within their Azure Managed Grafana instance and wants to restore it.
rootCause: Grafana does not natively support restoring deleted dashboards (per GitHub issue #9235). Dashboard deletion is permanent.
solution: If deleted within last 30 days: submit ICM to Grafana PG requesting the dashboard JSON schema. Customer can import the JSON to recreate the dashboard. Note: if full Grafana resource is restored, only data is restored not dashboards - but PG may provide JSON schema. Prevention: use az grafana backup regularly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Restore a Grafana Resource or Dashboard

symptom: Microsoft Entra Team Sync settings operation for Azure Managed Grafana fails. Error appears when attempting to sync Grafana teams with Microsoft Entra groups via Portal.
rootCause: Public access is disabled on the Grafana instance and the user is accessing the Azure Portal from a machine outside the VNET connected to the Private Endpoint. The Portal API call to /api/teams cannot reach the Grafana instance.
solution: 1) If PE exists and public access is disabled: access Portal from a machine within the PE VNET. 2) If no PE exists: create a PE (Grafana > Networking > Private Access) then access from within that VNET. 3) Enable public access (last resort). 4) Verify user has Grafana Admin role. Diagnostic: check ASC Workspace Metadata PublicNetworkAccess (0=Enabled, 1=Disabled). Analyze HAR file for /api/teams call result.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Sync Grafana teams With Microsoft Entra Groups Failure

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana SMTP email notification setup fails in Microsoft internal (1P) tenants because SFI restrictions prevent creating client secrets on App Registrations, which are required for Azure Communication Services SMTP authentication
rootCause: SFI (Secure Future Initiative) restrictions block secret creation on Entra ID App Registrations in MS tenants. The SMTP setup with Azure Communication Services requires an App Registration client secret for authentication.
solution: This is a known limitation for MS internal tenants. SMTP email notifications via Azure Communication Services cannot be configured in MS tenants due to SFI restrictions. Consider alternative notification methods (webhook, Teams) or use a non-MS tenant for testing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/How-To/Setting up and testing SMTP with Azure Managed Grafana

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU resource shows Evicted status and the Grafana instance is inaccessible
rootCause: Essential SKU Azure Managed Grafana instances are hosted on SPOT VMs with no SLA. When Azure reclaims the SPOT VM capacity, the instance gets evicted.
solution: Wait for the instance to come back (can take 30 minutes up to 24 hours based on excess VM capacity), create a new Azure Managed Grafana resource in a different region, or upgrade to Standard SKU for SLA-backed availability.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Support Boundaries

symptom: User with proper permissions logs into Azure Managed Grafana endpoint but only sees a Welcome to Grafana Enterprise splash screen instead of the normal Grafana UI
rootCause: Instance requires patching by the Azure Managed Grafana PG team. This is not resolvable by CSS.
solution: Submit an ICM immediately to Azure Managed Grafana PG with all relevant info. The instance needs to be patched to restore normal operation. CSS cannot resolve this issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Support Boundaries

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana with private access enabled cannot connect to services (e.g. Prometheus, MySQL) running on Azure VMs in the same VNET. Data source connection test fails even though VMs are accessible from other VMs in the VNET.
rootCause: AMG private access only establishes a private endpoint for browser-to-Grafana connectivity. It does not enable Grafana-to-VM connectivity directly. VMs are not listed as supported resources for Managed Private Endpoint.
solution: Create an Internal Load Balancer in the VM VNET with Inbound NAT rules pointing to each VM service port. Create a Private Link Service on top of the LB. Then create a Managed Private Endpoint in AMG targeting the Private Link Service. Approve the connection manually. Use the Private Link Service private IP as the data source host in Grafana.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Learning Resources/Lab | Connecting services running on Azure VMs as a Grafana data source

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana cannot connect to Azure Database for MySQL/PostgreSQL Flexible Server with public network access disabled. Managed Private Endpoint does not list MySQL/PostgreSQL as supported resources.
rootCause: MySQL and PostgreSQL Flexible Servers are not supported as direct Managed Private Endpoint targets in Azure Managed Grafana. Deterministic outbound IPs method also fails when public access is disabled on the database.
solution: Use Azure Firewall DNAT approach: Create an Azure Firewall in the database VNET with AzureFirewallSubnet. Create a Route Table associated with the database subnet routing to the firewall. Create a NAT rule in the firewall translating the firewall public IP to the database private IP on the service port (3306 for MySQL, 5432 for PostgreSQL). Use the firewall public IP as the Grafana data source host.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Learning Resources/Lab | Connecting Private Databases (MySQL Flexible Server and PostgreSQL Flexible Server) to Azure Managed Grafana

symptom: Azure Managed Grafana cannot connect to MySQL or PostgreSQL databases running inside an AKS cluster as data sources
rootCause: Services running inside AKS pods are not directly accessible from Azure Managed Grafana. A Private Link Service with an internal load balancer is needed to expose the database ports.
solution: Deploy a Kubernetes Service with type LoadBalancer and azure-pls annotations (azure-load-balancer-internal=true, azure-pls-create=true) targeting the database pod (port 3306 for MySQL, 5432 for PostgreSQL). This creates an Internal LB and a Private Link Service automatically. Create a Managed Private Endpoint in AMG targeting the Private Link Service. Approve the connection and use the Private Link Service private IP as the Grafana data source host.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Learning Resources/Lab | Connecting Databases (MySQL and PostgreSQL) running on an AKS cluster to Azure Managed Grafana

symptom: AKS Windows node metrics not collected in Managed Prometheus after enabling monitoring on the cluster
rootCause: windowsexporter and windowskubeproxy values in ama-metrics-settings-configmap are set to false by default; Windows scrape targets are configured but disabled out of the box
solution: Deploy or update ama-metrics-settings-configmap in kube-system namespace with windowsexporter=true and windowskubeproxy=true, then deploy the windows-exporter-config ConfigMap in the monitoring namespace to install the Windows exporter
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Learning Resources/Lab | Collecting AKS Windows metrics (default and custom) to Managed Prometheus

symptom: ama-metrics-win-node pods not created on AKS cluster with Windows node pools after enabling Managed Prometheus
rootCause: Managed Prometheus metrics addon was not enabled with the Windows recording rules parameter
solution: Disable monitoring with az aks update --disable-azure-monitor-metrics -n <cluster> -g <rg>, then re-enable with the --enable-windows-recording-rules parameter to properly deploy Windows metric collection pods
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Learning Resources/Lab | Collecting AKS Windows metrics (default and custom) to Managed Prometheus

symptom: Customer sees 'You might not have enough access' banner when attempting to create an Azure Managed Grafana resource in Azure Portal
rootCause: Customer is not an admin or owner of the subscription, so upon resource creation it will not auto-assign the user as Grafana Admin or assign the system managed identity the Monitoring Reader role
solution: 1) Customer can proceed with resource creation but needs someone with proper permissions to add Grafana Admin/Editor/Viewer role 2) System assigned managed identity needs Monitoring Reader role over subscription 3) Alternatively create resource on another subscription where customer has proper permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Grafana/Troubleshooting Guides/Customer Sees 'You might not have enough access' Banner When Creating Resource

symptom: After deleting Azure Monitor Workspace (AMW), it still shows under Insights > Monitor settings next to Managed Prometheus in Azure Portal, clicking it returns 404 error
rootCause: The Data Collection Rule (DCR) associated with the AKS cluster for Prometheus still references the deleted AMW Resource ID, which is why the portal still displays it
solution: 1) Navigate to Data Collection Rules 2) Find DCR named MSProm-(region)-(cluster name) 3) Delete associated resources and the DCR itself 4) Go back to AKS Cluster > Capabilities > Managed Prometheus 5) Click Reconfigure to associate with new or existing AMW. Alternative: Disable Prometheus then re-enable with new AMW
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Azure Monitor Workspace Still Shows Connected to AKS Cluster After Deletion

symptom: Customer attempts to delete the managed Resource Group (MA_AZUREMONITORWORKSPACENAME_REGION_managed) created by Azure Monitor Workspace and receives error DenyAssignmentAuthorizationFailed
rootCause: There is a built-in deny assignment on the managed Resource Group that prevents its deletion; this deny assignment cannot be removed
solution: Locate the associated Azure Monitor Workspace (identifiable by the managed RG name) and delete the AMW resource itself, which will trigger automatic deletion of the managed Resource Group
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Cannot Delete Azure Monitor Workspace Managed Resource Group

symptom: Azure Monitor Workspace deployment fails with error 'The resource provision operation did not complete within the allowed timeout period'
rootCause: Something blocks the deployment of related resources (managed RG, DCR, DCE) or deployment fails after the 2-hour time limit, commonly caused by insufficient permissions (AuthorizationFailed) for creating DCE/DCR/RG in the managed resource group
solution: 1) Run Kusto query on ARM Prod cluster to identify the blocking issue from message field 2) Error typically shows AuthorizationFailed for specific resource actions 3) Customer needs to obtain proper permissions to deploy the resource shown in the error message
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Creating Azure Monitor Workspace Result in Timeout Error

symptom: Azure Monitor Workspace deployment fails with error 'Failed to provision Managed resources for Monitoring Account'
rootCause: Azure Tagging Policy or other Azure Policy blocking the deployment of managed resources (managed RG, DCR, DCE), resulting in RequestDisallowedByPolicy error
solution: 1) Run Kusto query on ARM Prod cluster (ARMProd database) to identify blocking policy from message field 2) Check Activity Logs/Portal banner for error details 3) Advise customer to exclude AMW from the blocking policy or create AMW satisfying policy requirements 4) Engage Azure Policy team if further assistance needed
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Creating Azure Monitor Workspace Results in Error 'Failed to Provision Managed Resources for Monitoring Account'

symptom: User cannot see any data on the Azure Monitor Workspace Metrics Usage Insights tab because no diagnostic setting is configured for the AMW
rootCause: User has not created a Diagnostic Setting for their Azure Monitor Workspace to route Insights data to a Log Analytics Workspace
solution: 1) Create a Diagnostic Setting for the AMW 2) Select Metrics Usage Details category 3) Select corresponding Log Analytics Workspace as destination 4) Wait ~24 hours for data to flow into LAW
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Metrics Usage Insights

symptom: User cannot see data on Azure Monitor Workspace Metrics Usage Insights tab even after configuring diagnostic settings and waiting 24 hours
rootCause: No data is being ingested to the Azure Monitor Workspace itself (active time series max is zero), so there are no insights to display
solution: 1) Verify diagnostic settings are created and 24 hours have elapsed 2) Check AMW metrics - if Active Time Series (max) is zero, no data is being ingested 3) Troubleshoot the upstream data ingestion pipeline (Managed Prometheus setup, DCR, pod status)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Metrics Usage Insights

symptom: Partial or malformed records appear in Syslog/CommonSecurityLog table in Log Analytics when using Azure Monitor Pipeline for Syslog/CEF ingestion
rootCause: CEF prep processor fails to parse incoming data because the Syslog receiver output does not contain expected message attributes. Error: 'message attribute is not found or not a string'. The input is not in valid CEF format or the Syslog receiver is not correctly preceding the CEF processor in the pipeline.
solution: 1) Check stateful set pod logs: kubectl get pods -A, then kubectl logs -n <namespace> <stateful-set> 2) Look for 'cefprepprocessor' warnings about 'message attribute is not found' 3) Ensure the Syslog receiver is correctly configured to precede the CEF processor and that input data conforms to CEF format 4) Verify processor chain order in pipeline config: Syslog receiver -> MicrosoftSyslog/MicrosoftCommonSecurityLog processor -> optional transform -> exporter
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Monitor Pipeline/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG: Syslog and CEF ingestion

symptom: Syslog receiver fails to process entries with error 'expecting a priority value within angle brackets' - Syslog data not ingested to Log Analytics
rootCause: Incoming Syslog messages do not include a PRI (priority) header in angle brackets as required by RFC 5424/3164, and the Syslog receiver is not configured to handle messages without PRI headers.
solution: 1) Check pod logs for syslog_parser errors with 'expecting a priority value within angle brackets' 2) Either configure the source device to send properly formatted Syslog messages with PRI header (e.g., '<134>') 3) Or update the Syslog receiver configuration to handle messages without PRI headers
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Monitor Pipeline/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG: Syslog and CEF ingestion

symptom: VM Insights Map page does not display expected data or VMBoundPort/VMComputer/VMConnection/VMProcess tables are missing data - Dependency Agent running in degraded mode
rootCause: Customer's OS or Linux kernel version is not supported by Dependency Agent. With DA version 9.10.15 and above, installation is not blocked for unsupported kernel versions, but the agent runs in degraded mode where connection and port data (VMConnection, VMBoundPort) is not collected.
solution: 1) Check supported OS and kernel versions at https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/vm/vminsights-dependency-agent-maintenance 2) On Linux, check kernel version with: uname -srm / hostnamectl / cat /proc/version 3) If kernel is unsupported, customer needs to upgrade kernel to a supported version or accept degraded mode (VMProcess table will have minimal data, VMConnection/VMBoundPort will not be collected)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/How-To/Check if Dependency Agent is Supported on Customer's Machine

symptom: Dependency Agent data collection issues after customer switched from Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) back to Log Analytics Agent (OMSAgentForLinux) - DA not sending data correctly
rootCause: MicrosoftDependencyAgent.properties file still contains 'amaio.subsystem.enabled=true' from previous AMA configuration, causing DA to try to route data through AMA subsystem instead of OMSAgent.
solution: 1) Check /etc/opt/Microsoft/dependency-agent/config/MicrosoftDependencyAgent.properties on Linux 2) If it contains 'amaio.subsystem.enabled=true' and customer is using OMSAgentForLinux, remove that line 3) Restart Dependency Agent: sudo /etc/init.d/microsoft-dependency-agent restart
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/How-To/Collect logs for Dependency agent

symptom: VM Insights Map feature not working after migrating from Log Analytics Agent (MMA) to Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - no data in Map tab despite DA extension installed and running
rootCause: The {"enableAMA":"true"} property is not set in Dependency Agent configuration settings. After migrating from MMA to AMA, the Dependency Agent needs this flag to route data through AMA instead of MMA.
solution: 1) Check DA settings via ARG query to list VMs with DA installed and inspect DAsettings property 2) Or check via Azure Portal: VM > Overview > JSON View > look for DependencyAgent extension settings 3) Or via Azure CLI: az vm extension show --resource-group RG --vm-name VM --name DependencyAgentWindows/Linux 4) If enableAMA is not set to true, redeploy the DA extension with the correct setting via Portal or CLI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/How-To/How to check {"enableAMA":"true"} property is set in Dependency Agent

symptom: VM Guest Health Extension (GuestHealthLinuxAgent/GuestHealthWindowsAgent) still installed on VM after retirement on Nov 30 2022 - uninstall from portal may time out with an error
rootCause: VM Guest Health extension was retired on November 30th 2022 but was not automatically removed from existing VMs; portal-based uninstall can time out
solution: Use PowerShell to force remove: Remove-AzResource -ResourceId "/subscriptions/{subId}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}/extensions/{extensionName}" -Force. Extension name is GuestHealthLinuxAgent or GuestHealthWindowsAgent depending on OS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/How-To/Remove VM Insights Guest Health Extension

symptom: VM Insights Map feature (Processes and dependencies) not available after enabling VM Insights using AMA/DCR - Map view is blank because DCR was created without Dependency Agent data source
rootCause: Customer enabled VM Insights without selecting Processes and dependencies (Map) option during initial DCR setup; VM Insights auto-creates DCR and Microsoft does not recommend modifying it manually
solution: Recommended: Delete the existing VM Insights DCR and re-onboard VM Insights with the Processes and dependencies (Map) option enabled. Workaround: Edit DCR JSON to add DependencyAgent extension data source (Microsoft-ServiceMap stream) and corresponding dataFlow using the Edit DCR PowerShell script via Azure Cloud Shell. Test in sandbox first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/How-To/VM Insights: How to edit a Data Collection Rule to include Dependency Agent after its initial deployment

symptom: Dependency Agent on Linux shows error BSERR_BLUECHANNEL_LOAD_FAIL - Dependency Agent kernel modules fail to load
rootCause: Dependency Agent kernel modules are not cryptographically signed and the Linux machine is running with kernel signature checking enabled (Secure Boot or module signing enforcement)
solution: Known limitation - Dependency Agent will not work when this error is present. Customer must either disable kernel module signature checking or accept that Dependency Agent (and VM Insights Map) cannot function on this machine.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/Known Issues and Errors

symptom: Dependency Agent on Linux shows error BSERR_DRIVER_LOAD_FAIL - kernel module cannot allocate enough memory
rootCause: The Dependency Agent kernel module cannot allocate sufficient memory on the Linux machine to load properly
solution: Machine needs more memory to support the Dependency Agent. Customer should increase VM memory allocation or resize to a larger VM SKU to reduce memory pressure.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/Known Issues and Errors

symptom: Dependency Agent on Linux shows error BSERR_KERNEL_VERSION_UNSUPPORTED - kernel version is not supported by Dependency Agent
rootCause: The Linux kernel version is not in the list of supported kernel versions for the Dependency Agent
solution: Dependency Agent will run in degraded mode on supported OS but unsupported kernel. Customer should upgrade to a supported kernel version if possible. Check supported versions in Azure Monitor documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/Known Issues and Errors

symptom: Auto Extension Upgrade for Dependency Agent fails with VMAgentStatusCommunicationError - VM agent cannot download newest extension version
rootCause: VM Agent cannot communicate to external servers outside the VM to download the newest extension version due to network connectivity issues
solution: 1) Verify VM has outbound internet connectivity to Azure extension download endpoints 2) Check NSG/firewall rules blocking outbound traffic 3) Run Kusto query on azcrp.kusto.windows.net/crp_allprod: VMAutoExtensionUpgradeEvent (single VM) or VmssAutomaticExtensionUpgradeEvent (VMSS) filtered by subscriptionId to check detailed error
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Auto Extension Upgrade - Dependency Agent

symptom: Auto Extension Upgrade for Dependency Agent fails with VMExtensionProvisioningError - extension could not be provisioned after installing
rootCause: Extension provisioning failed during the auto-upgrade process. The specific failing extension details are in the error message. May be caused by VM agent issues or extension handler problems.
solution: 1) Check error message for which extension is causing the issue 2) Investigate VM agent logs on the guest OS for detailed provisioning errors 3) Query VMAutoExtensionUpgradeEvent or VmssAutomaticExtensionUpgradeEvent in azcrp.kusto.windows.net/crp_allprod to get activityId 4) Use ContextActivity table with that activityId for CRP errors
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Auto Extension Upgrade - Dependency Agent

symptom: VM Insights Dependency Agent not sending data via AMA after migration from Log Analytics Agent - no data in VMComputer/VMProcess/VMConnection tables, Map view shows no systems
rootCause: After migrating from Log Analytics Agent to AMA, the Dependency Agent config was not updated to use AMA for sending data. The amaio.subsystem.enabled property is not set to true.
solution: 1) Log into affected system as Administrator 2) Edit C:\Program Files\Microsoft Dependency Agent\config\MicrosoftDependencyAgent.properties 3) Add line: amaio.subsystem.enabled=true 4) Save changes 5) Wait 5 minutes. If issue persists, remove and reinstall Dependency Agent via Azure CLI with AMA flag.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG VM Insights using AMA

symptom: Azure Monitor Pipeline extension heartbeat fails with 403 error: Error response code when sending heartbeat to send to GigLA with responseCode 403 in collector pod logs
rootCause: The Azure Monitor Pipeline Arc extension does not have the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role assigned on the Data Collection Rule (DCR), resulting in permission denied when publishing heartbeat data
solution: 1) Run az k8s-extension show to get extension principal ID 2) Assign Monitoring Metrics Publisher role to the extension on the DCR via az role assignment create 3) Wait up to 6 hours for the role assignment to take effect 4) Re-check heartbeats in LA workspace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Monitor Pipeline/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Azure Monitor Pipeline Scenarios

symptom: Azure Monitor Pipeline StatefulSet keeps replacing/recreating pods before stabilizing, pipeline group appears unstable during initial deployment
rootCause: Known product issue (Bug 27445907): StatefulSet is recreated before ultimately starting up, causing transient pod churn for up to 3 minutes after deployment
solution: Wait approximately 3 minutes after deployment for the StatefulSet to stabilize. If pods continue restarting beyond 3 minutes, check operator controller-manager logs for errors: kubectl logs -n <ns> deployment/<ext-name>-pipeline-operator-controller-manager. Reference: Bug 27445907
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Monitor Pipeline/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Azure Monitor Pipeline Scenarios

symptom: Observability data sent to Azure Monitor Pipeline endpoint but no data arrives in Log Analytics workspace custom tables (e.g. DefaultAEPOTelLogs_CL, DefaultAEPSyslogLogs_CL)
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: 1) Looking at wrong LA workspace - workspace in DCR destinations key may differ from expected 2) Table not yet created in workspace 3) Stream name in DCR dataFlows key points to wrong table 4) New onboarding latency up to 6 hours
solution: 1) Verify correct LA workspace from DCR JSON view > destinations key (match immutable ID with pipeline config) 2) Check DCR dataFlows key for stream-to-table mapping 3) Verify target table exists in LA workspace Tables section; create manually if missing 4) For new onboarding allow 10-15 min for new logs, up to 6 hours for first-time setup 5) If still missing collect collector logs: kubectl logs -n <ns> statefulset/<name>-statefulset -c collector 6) If network errors check firewall settings
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Monitor Pipeline/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Azure Monitor Pipeline Scenarios

symptom: Azure Monitor Pipeline Group resource shows Failed in Azure portal, or remains stuck in Accepted status for more than 30 minutes without transitioning to Succeeded
rootCause: Pipeline Group creation failure typically caused by: 1) Arc extension not installed correctly 2) Operator controller-manager errors preventing reconciliation 3) Extension lacks required permissions (403) 4) Custom location misconfiguration
solution: 1) Verify extension: kubectl get azuremonitorpipelines -n <ns> <name> 2) Check operator logs: kubectl logs -n <ext-ns> deployment/<ext-name>-pipeline-operator-controller-manager 3) Check statefulset: kubectl describe -n <ns> statefulset/<name>-statefulset - should show 1 Running 4) Check collector: kubectl logs -n <ns> statefulset/<name>-statefulset -c collector - should show Everything is ready 5) If 403 error add Monitoring Metrics Publisher role 6) If extension not found validate via helm history
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Monitor Pipeline/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Azure Monitor Pipeline Scenarios

symptom: Syslog data not collected by Azure Monitor Pipeline - customer reports syslog events not appearing in Log Analytics custom table after sending to pipeline endpoint
rootCause: Syslog collection issues caused by incorrect syslog format (RFC3164 vs RFC5424), wrong target IP/port, or pipeline not configured for syslog dataflow
solution: 1) Manually test syslog via netcat: RFC3164: echo '<34>Jan 12 06:30:00 1.2.3.4 apache_server: test' | nc <external-IP> 514; RFC5424: echo '<34>1 2003-10-11T22:14:15.003Z mymachine su - ID47 - test' | nc <external-IP> 514 2) Get external IP from kubectl get services -n azure-monitor-ns 3) If test succeeds but data missing check DCR dataFlows for syslog stream mapping 4) If connection fails check network/firewall
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Monitor Pipeline/Support Topics/Issues related to data flow/Issue collecting Syslog data

symptom: Syslog receiver fails to process entries with error expecting a priority value within angle brackets - Syslog data not ingested to Log Analytics
rootCause: Incoming Syslog messages do not include a PRI (priority) header in angle brackets as required by RFC 5424/3164, and the Syslog receiver is not configured to handle messages without PRI headers.
solution: 1) Check pod logs for syslog_parser errors with expecting a priority value within angle brackets 2) Either configure the source device to send properly formatted Syslog messages with PRI header 3) Or update the Syslog receiver configuration to handle messages without PRI headers
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Monitor Pipeline/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG: Syslog and CEF ingestion

symptom: Managed Prometheus no longer reports metric labels from kube-state-metrics job (ama-metrics-ksm pod). Labels such as kube_resource_labels and kube_resource_annotations are missing from Grafana dashboards.
rootCause: The metricLabelsAllowlist in the AKS cluster azureMonitorProfile is empty or missing specific label entries, so the metrics addon does not collect those labels.
solution: Run az aks show to check azureMonitorProfile.metricLabelsAllowlist. If empty, update addon: az aks update --enable-azure-monitor-metrics --ksm-metric-labels-allow-list namespaces=[*] (for labels) or --ksm-metric-annotations-allow-list namespaces=[*] (for annotations). If not all metrics collected, apply ama-metrics-settings-configmap with minimalingestionprofile=false.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Missing Prometheus Metric Labels From Kube-State-Metrics Job

symptom: All Managed Prometheus scrape jobs succeed except the node job which shows failure. Node-based metrics (CPU, memory, disk from node-exporter) are missing from Grafana dashboards and PromQL queries return no data for node metrics.
rootCause: The node-exporter service is not running on AKS cluster nodes. Typically caused by AKSLinuxExtension not installed on VMSS or node-exporter service stopped/crashed.
solution: 1) Check VMSS Extensions tab for AKSLinuxExtension - if missing engage AKS team. 2) Create debug pod: kubectl debug node/NODENAME -it --image=mcr.microsoft.com/cbl-mariner/busybox:2.0, chroot /host, bash. 3) systemctl status node-exporter. 4) If stopped: systemctl restart node-exporter.service. 5) Verify: curl NODE_IP:19100/metrics.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Missing Prometheus Metrics for Only Node Scrape Job

symptom: View AMW metrics in editor entry point is not visible or disabled on the resource Monitoring > Metrics blade in Azure Portal. Customer cannot explore AMW (Azure Monitor Workspace) metrics for a specific resource from its context.
rootCause: AMW Access Control Mode is set to workspace only instead of workspace and resource context access, or resource-context query feature is disabled on the AMW (common for brownfield/existing AMWs created before the feature defaulted on).
solution: 1) In AMW Query Options, set Access Control Mode to Workspace and resource context access. 2) Enable Resource context query (brownfield AMWs may require enablement). 3) Ensure user has Monitoring Reader role on target resource. 4) If AMW is workspace-only, user also needs Reader/Monitoring Reader on the AMW. 5) Verify metrics ingested via AMA with Microsoft.resourceid label stamping.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Resource-centric query and metrics

symptom: Resource-scoped PromQL query returns empty results or 403 error when querying AMW metrics via programmatic API (curl with x-ms-azure-scoping header) or Grafana resource-scoped data source, even though metrics exist in the AMW.
rootCause: Querying identity (user, managed identity, or AAD app) lacks Monitoring Reader role on the target resource. For workspace-only AMWs, identity also needs Reader/Monitoring Reader on AMW. Alternatively, ingestion path uses older agent without label stamping (Microsoft.resourceid not populated).
solution: 1) Grant Monitoring Reader to querying identity on scoped resource. 2) If AMW is workspace-only, also grant Reader/Monitoring Reader on AMW. 3) Verify ingestion uses AMA with resourceId label stamping. 4) For agentless Prometheus Remote Write, resource-scope is not currently supported. 5) Test with curl using Authorization header and x-ms-azure-scoping header to regional query endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Resource-centric query and metrics

symptom: Self-managed Prometheus running on on-premises server or Azure VM sends metrics via remote_write to Azure Monitor Workspace but metrics do not appear in AMW or Managed Grafana. Jobs not listed when querying sum by(job, cluster) (up).
rootCause: The identity (managed identity or Entra ID app registration) used for remote_write authentication does not have Monitoring Metrics Publisher role assigned on the Data Collection Rule (DCR) associated with the AMW.
solution: 1) Verify prometheus.yml remote_write section has correct AMW metrics ingestion endpoint URL. 2) Check authentication config (managed_identity client_id or oauth client_id/secret/tenant_id). 3) Assign Monitoring Metrics Publisher role to the identity on the DCR used by AMW. 4) Verify connectivity: curl -v <AMW_ingestion_endpoint>. 5) If firewall blocks, allow access to the AMW ingest endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Sending self-managed metrics from Prometheus running on an on-premises servers and on-premises Kubernetes to Azure Monitor Workspace

symptom: Self-managed Prometheus on-premises server cannot establish connection to Azure Monitor Workspace ingestion endpoint. curl to AMW metrics ingestion endpoint fails or times out.
rootCause: Network connectivity issue: firewall, proxy, or NSG rules block outbound HTTPS access from the on-premises server to the AMW metrics ingestion endpoint (*.ingest.monitor.azure.com).
solution: 1) Test connectivity: curl -v https://<amw_metrics_ingestion_endpoint>. 2) If blocked, configure firewall/proxy rules to allow outbound HTTPS to *.ingest.monitor.azure.com. 3) Verify DNS resolution of the endpoint. 4) If using proxy, configure proxy settings in prometheus.yml remote_write section.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Managed Prometheus/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG Sending self-managed metrics from Prometheus running on an on-premises servers and on-premises Kubernetes to Azure Monitor Workspace

symptom: Dependency Agent data collection issues after switching from AMA back to OMSAgentForLinux - DA not sending data correctly
rootCause: MicrosoftDependencyAgent.properties still has amaio.subsystem.enabled=true from previous AMA config.
solution: 1) Check /etc/opt/Microsoft/dependency-agent/config/MicrosoftDependencyAgent.properties 2) Remove amaio.subsystem.enabled=true 3) Restart DA: sudo /etc/init.d/microsoft-dependency-agent restart
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/How-To/Collect logs for Dependency agent

symptom: VM Insights Map not working after MMA to AMA migration - no data in Map tab despite DA extension installed
rootCause: enableAMA:true property not set in Dependency Agent config. DA needs this flag to route data through AMA.
solution: 1) Check DA settings via ARG query or Portal JSON View or Azure CLI 2) If enableAMA not set, redeploy DA extension with correct setting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/VM Insights/How-To/How to check {"enableAMA":"true"} property is set in Dependency Agent

symptom: Azure Workbook only shows a limited number of resources (e.g., 200) as a result of a query, customer expects to see more resources
rootCause: Azure Workbooks has visualization result limits per visualization type. Each visualization component has a maximum number of results it can display.
solution: Check the Workbook Results Limits documentation (https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/visualize/workbooks-limits) to understand per-visualization limits. Adjust query to filter or aggregate data within the limits, or split across multiple visualizations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Workbooks/Support Topics/Issue with using workbooks/My workbook only shows 200 resources

symptom: Updating Azure Workbook tags results in error: 'A Workbook with the same name already exists within this subscription' (Code: Conflict)
rootCause: DisplayName is inconsistent with hidden-title in the Workbook resource properties, causing a duplication check exception when adding tags
solution: In the Azure Portal, navigate to the Workbook overview page and verify that the display name matches the hidden-title. If they do not match, rename the Workbook using the rename option so both names are consistent, then retry updating tags. PG work item: https://msazure.visualstudio.com/One/_workitems/edit/16868239
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Workbooks/Troubleshooting Guides/Updating Tags in Workbooks Resulting in Conflict Error

symptom: Azure Workbook recently broke or changed unexpectedly, customer wants to verify what was modified or revert to a previous working version
rootCause: Unintended or unnoticed edits to workbook configuration or content by the customer or co-owners
solution: Navigate to the Workbook > click Edit > click gear icon > Versions tab. View version history showing timestamps, authors, and how long ago each change was made. Click a version to Compare (shows JSON diff vs current version), View the older version, or Restore it. Analyze the JSON diff before restoring to ensure you are not reverting to a very old version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Workbooks/How-To/Check Version History of a Workbook

symptom: Customer cannot find their Azure Workbook in the Azure Portal
rootCause: Subscription scope filter may not include the target subscription, workbook may be located in a Sentinel workspace, or workbook may have been recently deleted
solution: 1) Check Monitor > Workbooks > Gallery, click Browse across galleries and use filters. Workbook colors: green=empty, blue=custom, purple=built-in. 2) Check All resources with correct subscription filter scoped to the workbook subscription. 3) For Sentinel workbooks, check the Sentinel workspace Workbooks section. 4) If recently deleted within 30 days, file an ICM to recover. 5) For BYOS (Bring Your Own Storage) workbooks stored in customer Storage Account, engage Storage team to recover the Storage Account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Insights, Workbooks and Managed Products/Workbooks/How-To/Find an Azure Workbook in the Portal

symptom: Log Analytics workspace Resource Health shows Degraded for ingestion latency signal. Customer reports high ingestion latency alerts.
rootCause: One or more monitored P0Type tables have estimated ingestion latency (max P90) exceeding 1 hour for more than 15 consecutive minutes. Note: App* prefixed tables (Application Insights) are excluded from Resource Health latency monitoring since mid-July 2023. Custom logs (all types) are also not monitored by this signal. AzureActivity table is in process of being excluded.
solution: 1) Check Service Health for corresponding outage affecting the workspace region. 2) Access Resource Health from Monitor > Service Health > Resource Health filtered for Log Analytics, or from workspace > Resource health, or workspace > Insights > Health tab. 3) Use Geneva dashboard (LA-SLIM) to identify which specific P0Type tables have elevated latency. 4) Unknown state means no data ingested for 3+ days or health cannot be determined - not necessarily a latency issue. 5) App* tables and custom logs are excluded from monitoring - latency on these tables will NOT trigger Resource Health signals.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Resource health - "Ingestion latency" signal

symptom: Log Analytics workspace Resource Health shows Degraded for query success rate. Customer sees message: The query success rate for this workspace in the last 45 minutes was too low.
rootCause: Query success rate for user queries (excludes S2S/internal queries like Sentinel, scheduled alert rules, internal metadata calls) dropped below thresholds. Internal thresholds (DO NOT SHARE): <99% with >500 requests, <90% with 300-500 requests, or <80% with 100-300 requests. Thresholds subject to change.
solution: 1) Check Service Health for corresponding outage. 2) Use AvailabilityRate_Query SLI metric from workspace Metrics Explorer to check impact (docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-health#view-log-analytics-workspace-health-metrics). 3) Check query draft telemetry from ASC (Log Analytics workspace > Query Draft Telemetry tab) - use KQL: requests table filtered by workspaceId + resultCode != 200, project timestamp, tokenizedPath, clientApp, query, resultCode, error message. 4) Check dependencies table for partial errors on 2xx queries. 5) Correlate with Service Health issues reported by SRE team for root cause.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Common Concepts/Resource health - "Query success" signal
Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Azure Diagnostics/How-to: Check if AzureDiagnostics table schema is locked
symptom: New fields added to AzureDiagnostics table by resource providers are collapsed into AdditionalFields column instead of appearing as top-level columns
rootCause: AzureDiagnostics table schema is locked as part of migration to dedicated tables. New schemas or field changes are not allowed for RPs: MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT, MICROSOFT.NETWORK, MICROSOFT.AUTOMATION, MICROSOFT.SERVICEBUS, MICROSOFT.SQL, MICROSOFT.LOGIC. When enforcement is enabled, new fields are moved to AdditionalFields dynamic column.
solution: 1. Run KQL query against ActivityCompletedEvent in omsgenevainmemprod.eastus cluster (OperationInsights_InMem_PROD database) filtering on NewCommonCustomFieldLogicTriggered to verify schema lock status. 2. Check enableEnforcement flag - if True, capturedFieldNames shows which fields were moved to AdditionalFields. 3. Guide customer to migrate from AzureDiagnostics to dedicated/resource-specific tables.

symptom: Customer needs more than 500 custom log tables in a Log Analytics workspace, hitting the default limit of 500 custom tables per workspace.
rootCause: Azure Log Analytics enforces a default limit of 500 custom log tables per workspace. The hard maximum is 1000 tables.
solution: Raise IcM under Log Analytics Core PG template with feature area 'Custom table count increase request'. Include workspace Resource ID, Workspace ID, Region, and current table count. Contact authorized EEE/STA/SME to perform the increase via Geneva Actions (AMBackend > CMS > Invoke CMS Api Command) using Workspace_UpdateWorkspaceInfo API with maxTables set to new value (max 1000).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/HT: Increase custom table count beyond 500 per workspace

symptom: Customer accidentally changed Log Analytics table plan and cannot wait for the 7-day self-service limitation to expire before reverting the change.
rootCause: Log Analytics table plan changes are limited to once every 7 days via self-service. Accidental changes require an engineering exception process to reset early.
solution: Raise IcM using Log Analytics Core template with feature area 'Reset Azure Log Analytics Table Plan'. Include table name, Workspace ID, and Workspace resource ID. Notify EEE team for acknowledgment. If EEE unavailable, reach out to STA to direct the IcM to control plane engineering team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/HT: Reset log analytics table plan within 7 days

symptom: Customer reports incomplete or truncated data in one or more columns of their Log Analytics data.
rootCause: Data is being trimmed because the InMem column size limit is 32KB. Fields exceeding this limit are truncated during ingestion.
solution: Query ActivityCompletedEvent in omsgenevainmemprod.eastus Kusto cluster (OperationInsights_InMem_PROD) filtering for activityName 'IMTrM.MessageProcessorCompleteMessage' with 'TrimmedFields' in properties and customer workspaceID. Parse DataTypeId, TrimmedFields, and ResourceId to identify affected columns. For governance clouds (Azure Gov/Mooncake), use Jarvis queries. Follow TSG 'Investigate truncated or trimmed data in Log Analytics' for next steps. Ref: Azure Monitor service limits documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Ingestion/HT: Check for trimmed data in InMem

symptom: AzureDiagnostics data is dropped with 'InvalidDataFormat' error in the Operation table or Log Analytics Workspace Insights workbook, e.g., 'Data of type AzureDiagnostics is being dropped due to incorrect format'.
rootCause: The Resource Provider is sending malformed JSON payload to Log Analytics via diagnostic settings. The data format does not meet the expected schema, causing ingestion failure in the InMem legacy pipeline.
solution: Query ActivityCompletedEvent in omsgenevainmemprod.eastus (OperationInsights_InMem_PROD) for MessageStatus=InvalidDataFormat. Use context and Environment values to correlate with ODS Jarvis telemetry to get OBO CorrelationId. Walk backwards from InMem to ODS to OBO to identify the MDS table/event and the originating Resource Provider. Engage RP team with evidence. Resolution is on RP side - set SAP and root cause on RP product, not Log Analytics. Note: 99% of data now flows through Northstar, not legacy InMem pipeline.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Ingestion/HT: Check for Invalid Data Format in AzureDiagnostics data ingestion process

symptom: AzureDiagnostics data from Microsoft Storage resource provider is dropped with InvalidJsonFormat error, specifically for XSTORE_STORAGEWRITE data types with invalid JSON in objectKey field.
rootCause: Known issue with Microsoft Storage resource provider sending logs with invalid JSON format to Log Analytics workspaces. The payload contains malformed JSON in the objectKey field.
solution: This is a known Storage RP issue. Create a collaboration task to Storage CSS team for follow-up and state updates. Set SAP and root cause on the Storage RP product, not on Log Analytics. Reference CRI: 261506155.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Ingestion/HT: Check for Invalid Data Format in AzureDiagnostics data ingestion process
Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/General/How-To: Synchronize ARM subscription resources
symptom: Customer has orphaned objects in Log Analytics workspace (e.g., solutions, saved searches) that cannot be deleted through Azure portal, PowerShell, or other methods
rootCause: ARM resource cache is out of sync with the actual state of resources, causing orphaned entries that block normal deletion operations
solution: Request an ARM cache refresh (also known as ARM sync / ARM Rehydration / Resource Rehydration). As of July 2025, the previous Jarvis-based process no longer works due to security permission changes. The current approach is to create a collaboration with the ARM team to perform the ARM cache refresh. The resource provider for Log Analytics is Microsoft.OperationalInsights.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/General/How-to: Check the API message or properties
symptom: Customer gets unclear API error message when performing portal actions (e.g., deleting diagnostic settings). Portal shows a generic failure without specific error details.
rootCause: ScopeLocked error - a resource lock exists on the target resource or its parent scope, preventing the operation (e.g., delete) from completing. The portal UI may not surface the specific lock error, showing only a generic HTTP 409 Conflict.
solution: 1) Check ArmProd Kusto - query HttpIncomingRequests table filtered by subscriptionId, targetUri, and httpStatusCode > 399 to find the failing request. 2) Query EventServiceEntries table for more detailed error messages (e.g., search for 'scopelocked'). 3) Alternatively, ask customer for a network trace (HAR/Fiddler) where the full error JSON will be visible. 4) Once ScopeLocked is confirmed, advise customer to remove the resource lock before retrying the operation.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Azure Monitor/Collaboration Guides/Support for  “Azure Enhanced Monitoring Extension for SAP” or “ Azure Extension for SAP”
