symptom: Cases misrouted to Azure Monitor queue for 'Azure Enhanced Monitoring Extension for SAP' or 'Azure Extension for SAP'. Customer opened support case expecting Azure Monitor team to handle it.
rootCause: The 'Azure Enhanced Monitoring Extension for SAP' / 'Azure Extension for SAP' is NOT owned or released by the Azure Monitor Product team. It is a SAP-owned product. This is different from 'Azure Monitor for SAP Solutions' which IS supported by Microsoft.
solution: 1. Inform the customer that support for Azure Extension for SAP is provided through SAP support channels. Direct them to open a case with SAP Support. 2. Microsoft personnel (Oliver Doll, Max Reusch) sit inside SAP offices and can help via the SAP support system. 3. If customer insists on Microsoft support, reach out to Oliver Doll / Max Reusch. 4. Do NOT transfer cases to Monitor queue. 5. Note: 'Azure Monitor for SAP Solutions' is a DIFFERENT product supported by Microsoft (see collaboration guide).

symptom: Customer hits the 500 custom log field limit in a Log Analytics workspace table and cannot add more custom fields
rootCause: Default custom log field limit per table per workspace is 500. This limit can be increased up to 1000 but requires backend intervention via Geneva Actions (CMS API).
solution: 1) Validate current field count and get workspace ID, region, table name. 2) Raise IcM under Log Analytics Core PG template with resource ID, workspace ID, region. 3) EEE/STA/SME with SAW access runs Geneva Actions > AmBackend/CMS > Invoke CMS Api Command: Endpoint=CMS, CMS Stamp={region}, Api Operation=Customizations_UpdateCustomFieldLimit, Parameters={tenantId, customLogName, customFieldsLimitIncrement}. 4) Max 1000; if field missing defaults to 500; negative increment reduces (not below zero).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Jarvis/HT: Using Jarvis to increase the custom field limit in a table

symptom: Customer wants table-level RBAC so specific users can only view dashboard tiles of a particular table in Log Analytics without access to other tables
rootCause: Default Log Analytics Reader grants access to all tables. Table-level RBAC requires a custom role scoped to specific table read actions.
solution: 1) Create custom role via PowerShell: Get-AzRoleDefinition 'Log Analytics Reader', modify Actions to Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/read, /query/read, /query/{TableName}/read, set AssignableScopes to workspace. 2) Assign custom role via IAM. 3) Create LA query for the table, pin to dashboard. 4) Share dashboard via RBAC with Reader role.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/RBAC permissions/How-to: Allow users to view dashboard tile of a specific table only in Log Analytics

symptom: Customer wants to grant a user read access to logs of a single VM or resource only, without access to other workspace logs
rootCause: Previously required custom role or Log Analytics Reader plus resource-level Reader. Behavior changed: resource-level Reader role alone is now sufficient for resource-scoped log access.
solution: Assign Reader role at VM/resource level via IAM. User can: 1) Resource > Logs > query scoped to that resource, 2) Monitor > Logs > scope to resources with Reader permission. No custom role with Microsoft.Insights/logs/*/read needed anymore. User cannot save queries or perform workspace-level actions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/RBAC permissions/How-to: Grant read access on single VM logs only (applies to other single resource logs)

symptom: User cannot view VM Update Management blade or onboard VMs to Update Management due to insufficient permissions
rootCause: Opening Update Management solution blades requires both Log Analytics query permissions (workspaces/analytics/query/action and search/action) and VM-level permissions, even when VM is already onboarded.
solution: Grant two roles: 1) Log Analytics Reader at subscription level (provides OperationalInsights workspaces/analytics/query/action and search/action), 2) Virtual Machine Operator custom role at resource group level of the VM. Both required for viewing Update Management data through VM blade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/RBAC permissions/How-to: RBAC permissions to view VM blade and Update Management

symptom: Log Analytics workspace solution cannot be removed via Azure Portal, CLI (az monitor log-analytics solution delete), or PowerShell (Remove-AzMonitorLogAnalyticsSolution). Solution still appears listed but is inaccessible.
rootCause: The solution underlying Intelligence Pack may still be enabled in the workspace even though the solution resource was deleted, or deletion did not fully propagate.
solution: 1) Check if Intelligence Pack is disabled: use Workspace Dashboard Tool or Get-AzOperationalInsightsIntelligencePack cmdlet. 2) If still enabled, disable via: a) REST API Intelligence Packs Disable, b) PowerShell Set-AzOperationalInsightsIntelligencePack -Enabled $false, c) CLI az monitor log-analytics workspace pack disable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Solutions/How to remove solution completely
Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Solutions/How-to: Remove the scope configuration
symptom: Machines not appearing in Update Management dashboard, or solution targeting status shows as not Succeeded after scope configuration was applied
rootCause: Solution scope configuration is misconfigured or incorrectly applied, preventing machines from being targeted by the monitoring solution
solution: Navigate to Azure Portal > Log Analytics workspace > Solutions > select the solution (e.g., Updates) > Solution Targeting (Preview). Click the ellipsis and select Remove scope configuration. Wait at least 2 hours for machines to reappear in the dashboard. Can re-add scope configuration after 30 minutes if needed.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Solutions/Network Performance Monitor (NPM)/How-to: Change the port used by the Linux NPM agent
symptom: Linux NPM agent default port 8084 conflicts with another application running on the machine
rootCause: Default NPM agent port (8084) is already in use by another process on the Linux machine, causing a port conflict
solution: Edit the NPM configuration file at /var/opt/microsoft/omsagent/npm_state/npmdregistry, change the PortNumber value from 8084 to the desired port number, save the file, then restart the OMS agent service so it spawns a new npmd_agent process with the new port number.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Solutions/Service Map/How-to: Check Service Map unlicensed machines
symptom: Machines not appearing in Service Map or intermittently showing up and then disappearing from the Service Map view
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace is on Free tier which only allows 5 machines connected to Service Map; additional machines are treated as unlicensed and excluded
solution: Use Jarvis query (https://jarvis-west.dc.ad.msft.net/9D7F41DB) with the workspace ID and workspace region to identify which machines are licensed vs unlicensed. Results show agentIDs mappable to server names via Heartbeat query. Upgrade workspace pricing tier if more than 5 machines are needed.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Tables fields increase limitations
symptom: Data ingestion fails for a custom log table in Log Analytics workspace; new columns/fields are not being created
rootCause: Custom table has reached the 500 column limit per Log Analytics workspace table
solution: For V1 custom tables: raise an IcM to PG via ASC to request column increase (up to maximum 1000 columns). V2 or migrated tables are NOT eligible for field limit increases. First validate using How-to: Validate if custom fields limit is being reached procedure. Get SME/TA approval before submitting the CRI. When using HTTP Data Collector API, customers should control custom field creation to avoid hitting the limit.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Tables fields increase limitations
symptom: New columns in AzureDiagnostics table are not being created as expected; data appears in unexpected AdditionalFields dynamic column instead of dedicated columns
rootCause: AzureDiagnostics table has reached or exceeded the 500 column limit. Since January 2021, excess data beyond 500 columns is automatically stored in a dynamic property bag column called AdditionalFields.
solution: This is by-design behavior since January 2021. Access overflow data by querying the AdditionalFields dynamic column using parse_json() or bag_unpack(). Column increase requests for AzureDiagnostics are NOT supported. For exceptions, refer to the How-to: Escalate AzureDiagnostic table exception internal procedure.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Workspace Management/How-to: Check if a workspace was permanently deleted
symptom: Customer cannot recover a recently deleted Log Analytics workspace even within the 14-day soft-delete recovery period
rootCause: Workspace was deleted using the force parameter which permanently deletes the workspace and releases its name immediately, bypassing the 14-day soft-delete recovery period. Force-deleted workspaces are not recoverable.
solution: Verify using Kusto query on armprodgbl.eastus cluster (ARMProd database): query Unionizer(Requests, EventServiceEntries) filtering by subscriptionId and operationName =~ Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/delete. Check the wasPermanentlyDeleted field (extracted from force parameter in httpRequest). If wasPermanentlyDeleted=true, workspace cannot be recovered. IMPORTANT: Only share deletion user identity with the subscription owner.

symptom: HttpError_NameResolutionFailure errors in OBO pipeline (ODSPostTelemetry table) when sending Azure resource logs via diagnostic settings to a Log Analytics workspace. The workspace was previously working but data stopped flowing.
rootCause: The target Log Analytics workspace has been deleted, so the workspace DNS name (workspaceId.ods.opinsights.azure.com) no longer resolves. Can verify by querying WorkspaceSnapshot table: if SnapshotTimestamp > 48h old, workspace is deleted.
solution: 1) Check if workspace exists in ASC or query: cluster('oibeftprdflwr.kusto.windows.net').database('AMSTelemetry').WorkspaceSnapshot | where WorkspaceId contains '<id>' | summarize arg_max(SnapshotTimestamp, *) | extend isDeleted = iff(SnapshotTimestamp < ago(48h), True, False). 2) If deleted, customer needs to create a new workspace and reconfigure all diagnostic settings to point to it.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Ingestion/HT: Investigate HttpError_NameResolutionFailure errors generated in OBO when sending data to a workspace

symptom: HttpError_NameResolutionFailure errors in OBO pipeline after customer deleted and recreated a Log Analytics workspace with the same name under the same resource group. Diagnostic settings show data not flowing to the new workspace.
rootCause: When a workspace is deleted and recreated with the same name, a NEW workspace ID is assigned. The diagnostic settings pipeline still references the OLD workspace ID internally, causing DNS resolution failure since the old workspace endpoint no longer exists. This is the MOST COMMON cause of NameResolutionFailure in OBO.
solution: Customer must recreate or edit the existing diagnostic settings to point to the newly created Log Analytics workspace. Simply having the same workspace name is insufficient - the diagnostic settings must be updated to reference the new workspace ID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Ingestion/HT: Investigate HttpError_NameResolutionFailure errors generated in OBO when sending data to a workspace

symptom: HttpError_NameResolutionFailure errors in OBO pipeline. Workspace exists and was not recreated, but nslookup of {workspaceId}.ods.opinsights.azure.com fails to resolve.
rootCause: Workspace DNS records ({workspaceId}.ods.opinsights.azure.com) are missing or not properly configured in the DNS infrastructure, preventing OBO pipeline from resolving the workspace endpoint.
solution: 1) Validate DNS: run PowerShell 'nslookup {workspaceId}.ods.opinsights.azure.com'. 2) If DNS fails to resolve but workspace exists, create an IcM to 'Azure Log Analytics\Control Plane' team to fix the missing/broken DNS records for the workspace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Ingestion/HT: Investigate HttpError_NameResolutionFailure errors generated in OBO when sending data to a workspace

symptom: Data not being ingested in NorthStar pipeline for a specific data type. Target Log Analytics table shows no new data despite data flowing through the pipeline. LogTrace table shows 'GenevaTransformConfig_TransformNotFound' messages.
rootCause: The correct transform version for the data type is not deployed in the NorthStar pipeline. Without the proper transform configuration, NorthStar cannot process and ingest the data type.
solution: 1) Query LogTrace in omsgenevatlm/GenevaNSProd: let datatype_id = '<DATA_TYPE_ID>'; LogTrace | where PreciseTimeStamp between (ago(1d) .. now()) | where Message startswith 'Message complete' and Message contains 'Result=GenevaTransformConfig_TransformNotFound' and Message contains datatype_id. 2) If results returned, transform is missing. 3) The PG owning the data type must deploy the transform using 1DS deployment instructions: https://1dsdocs.azurewebsites.net/articles/loganalytics-onboarding-guide/deployment.html
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Ingestion/HT: Validate that the correct transform version got deployed

symptom: Customer unable to perform actions on Log Analytics workspace (e.g., remove Performance Counter, save changes) without meaningful error messages.
rootCause: A scope lock (ReadOnly or CanNotDelete) is applied on the workspace or a parent scope (resource group / subscription), blocking modifications.
solution: Check ARM Prod Kusto (armprodgbl.eastus.kusto.windows.net/Armprodgbl) for ScopeLocked errors using Unionizer query filtered by subscription and httpStatusCode=Failed with properties containing ScopeLocked. Then navigate to the workspace > Locks (under Settings) in Azure portal to remove the blocking lock.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Workspace Management/How-to: Check if lock is avoiding any modification

symptom: Logs stopped flowing to Log Analytics workspace. Customer has Free tier or a custom daily cap configured.
rootCause: The workspace daily cap was reached, causing ingestion to be suspended (DataIngestionStatus = OverQuota). Once the daily cap resets (at the configured reset time), ingestion resumes automatically.
solution: Validate daily cap status via: 1) ASC insights (shows current OverQuota status) 2) Query Operation table on ASC: Operation | where OperationCategory == 'Data Collection Status' and OperationStatus == 'Warning' 3) Query WorkspaceSnapshot on oibeftprdflwr/AMSTelemetry for DataIngestionStatus field 4) Query ActivityCompletedEvent on omsgenevainmemprod for IngestionStatus == OverQuota. Customer can increase/remove daily cap in Azure portal under workspace Usage and estimated costs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Workspace Management/How-to: Check if the daily cap was reached

symptom: Daily cap limit is not honored: ingestion continues well beyond configured daily cap, impacting customer billing. GetWorkspaceIngestionStatusUpdates function shows updateStatus != completed.
rootCause: Control Plane status flag fails to propagate to ingestion pipeline in time, so data continues to be accepted past the cap. Previously common in high-ingestion-rate scenarios in regions like EastUS and WestEurope.
solution: As of Jan 2026, PG has mitigated with a redesigned flow. Do NOT raise ICMs for this. Discuss with STA Sahil for feasibility of refund and customer-ready message. Verify using GetWorkspaceIngestionStatusUpdates function on lacontrolplane.eastus.kusto.windows.net/LogsLACP. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/daily-cap#how-the-daily-cap-works
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Workspace Management/How-to: Check if the daily cap was reached

symptom: Need to determine when a Log Analytics workspace was created.
rootCause: Workspace creation date is not directly visible in the Azure portal.
solution: Query GetLastWorkspaceSnapshot function on oibeftprdflwr.kusto.windows.net/AMSTelemetry: GetLastWorkspaceSnapshot('WorkspaceNameOrId') | project-reorder CreatedDate. The CreatedDate field reflects when the workspace was created.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Workspace Management/How-to: Check the creation or deletion date of a workspace

symptom: Need to determine when a Log Analytics workspace was deleted.
rootCause: Workspace deletion date is not directly visible after deletion.
solution: Query ARMProd Kusto (armprodgbl.eastus.kusto.windows.net/ARMProd): Unionizer('Requests', 'EventServiceEntries') | where TIMESTAMP > ago(35d) | where subscriptionId == cx_subscriptionId | where resourceUri contains 'workspace_name' | where operationName contains 'microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces/delete'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Workspace Management/How-to: Check the creation or deletion date of a workspace

symptom: Need to determine when each Log Analytics table last received data to identify tables that stopped ingesting.
rootCause: No built-in portal view showing last record timestamp per table.
solution: Run KQL on workspace or ASC Query Customer Data: search * | summarize max(TimeGenerated) by $table | sort by max_TimeGenerated desc. Returns most recent record timestamp for every table.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Workspace Management/How-to: Check when the last record was sent to a Table

symptom: Customer cannot remove a custom tile from Workspace Summary via Azure portal (Edit > Delete does not work).
rootCause: The tile/view may have a locked scope preventing deletion through the portal UI, or the portal delete button does not function for certain legacy tiles.
solution: 1) Install ARMClient (chocolatey.org/packages/ARMClient) 2) armclient login 3) List views: armclient get /subscriptions/{subId}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/{ws}/views?api-version=2015-11-01-preview 4) Delete: armclient delete .../views/ViewName?api-version=2015-11-01-preview 5) If scope locked, remove lock from workspace > Locks first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Workspace Management/How-to: Delete a tile from Workspace summary

symptom: Data fails to ingest into AzureDiagnostics table due to hitting the 500 column limit. New columns from resource logs cannot be added.
rootCause: AzureDiagnostics table has a hard limit of 500 columns per workspace. PG has a workflow to add an Additionalfield column when this limit is reached, but sometimes this workflow fails to trigger.
solution: 1) Confirm 500-column limit reached (wiki: Validate if custom fields limit is being reached) 2) Raise IcM via ASC template 'Azure Log Analytics | Core PG', Feature Area: 'AzureDiagnostic Increase', target: Application Insights/Kusto-LogAnalytics Backend 3) If no response in 2 business days, email: tzachie, josecons, Ron.Frenkel, Einat.Urbach @microsoft.com. USNAT not onboarded, Fairfax is.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Workspace Management/How-to: Escalate AzureDiagnostic table exception

symptom: Customer needs to collect a browser network trace (HAR file) to troubleshoot Azure portal issues with Log Analytics or Azure Monitor.
rootCause: Portal issues require client-side network trace to identify failing API calls, CORS errors, or timeout issues.
solution: 1) Open browser in private mode 2) Navigate to the error page 3) F12 > Network tab 4) Reproduce error 5) Stop recording 6) Right-click > Save as HAR with content. Works for Edge/Chrome. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/devtools-guide/network
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Workspace Management/How-to: Get a browser network trace
Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/How-To Guides/Workspace Management/How-to: Validate if custom fields limit is being reached
symptom: AzureDiagnostics or custom log table stops ingesting data or is missing from the Log Analytics workspace. Data ingestion silently fails without obvious errors.
rootCause: The workspace has reached the 500 custom field limit (default) for the table. When this limit is hit, new fields cannot be created and data with new schema columns is dropped. The ProcessedChunk table shows 'CustomFieldsLimitReached' error code.
solution: Option 1: Delete unnecessary custom fields via undocumented API - GET customFields list then DELETE specific fields (destructive, irreversible). Option 2: For AzureDiagnostics, switch resources to Resource-Specific diagnostic settings mode. Option 3: Request custom field limit increase via Jarvis action (custom log tables only, not AzureDiagnostics). Validate using Kusto query on omsgenevatlm cluster ProcessedChunk table or ASC Custom Log Summary tab.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/AMPLS/Azure Monitoring Private Link Scope (AMPLS) ARM Call fails
symptom: ARM API calls to Log Analytics workspace fail with 'InsufficientAccessError' and inner error 'PrivateLinkValidationFailedError' stating access is only permitted through a Private Link. Table API calls and workspace summary loading fail.
rootCause: By design - when AMPLS is configured with Private Only access mode, all ARM calls that don't traverse the private link will be blocked. This is expected behavior for the private link security model.
solution: This is by design. Inform customer that with AMPLS set to Private Only, ARM-based queries must go through the private endpoint. If customer needs public ARM access, they need to change the AMPLS access mode to allow public access for queries.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/AMPLS/Azure monitor private link scope: Azure Monitor Resources shows 'Failed' provisioning state
symptom: Under Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS), connected resources in Azure Monitor Resources show 'Failed' provisioning state. Re-adding the resources does not help. Activity logs show provisioning failure errors.
rootCause: Known bug in private endpoint connection handling that causes the provisioning state to get stuck in 'Failed'.
solution: Workaround: 1) Navigate to the Private Endpoint resource associated with the AMPLS (via Private Endpoint connections blade). 2) On the Private Endpoint Overview blade, follow the yellow warning banner instruction to click Refresh. 3) Navigate back to AMPLS resource and confirm all scoped resources show Succeeded state (click Refresh if needed).

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Azure Monitor Logs Custom Fields Retirement
symptom: Customer receives notification about Azure Monitor Logs Custom Fields feature retirement on 31 March 2026. Custom fields used for extracting data from logs to custom columns will stop working after the retirement date, and corresponding log columns will remain empty.
rootCause: Microsoft is retiring the Custom Fields feature in Azure Monitor Logs. Custom fields not transitioned to DCR-based KQL transformations will stop working after March 31, 2026.
solution: Transition to DCR-based KQL transformations before March 31, 2026. Migration guide: https://aka.ms/customfieldmigrate. DCR-based KQL transformations offer enhanced capabilities: multiple columns from single input, additional string operators, data type conversion, regex support, scalable deployment. For troubleshooting table transformations and IcM escalation, refer to the Issues related to table transformation wiki.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Billing/By design: Cannot create new workspaces or move existing ones to the 'Free' tier since July 1st 2022
symptom: Customer cannot create new Log Analytics workspaces in the Free Trial pricing tier, or cannot move existing workspaces to the Free tier. Error occurs when attempting to select Free tier during workspace creation or pricing tier change.
rootCause: By design - starting July 1, 2022, the legacy Free Trial pricing tier has been deprecated/disabled. New workspaces cannot be created in this tier and existing workspaces cannot be moved to it.
solution: Inform customer this is by design. Existing workspaces already in Free Trial tier can remain there. For new workspaces, customer must select a different pricing tier (Pay-as-you-go or Commitment Tiers). Refer to public documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/cost-logs#free-trial-pricing-tier

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Billing/By design: Usage table latency
symptom: Data in the Usage table in Log Analytics has significant latency, up to 3 hours. Customer reports Usage table data is delayed or not appearing in real-time.
rootCause: By design. The Usage table records are an hourly aggregation and not populated real-time like most other tables.
solution: Explain to the customer that Usage table latency up to 3 hours is by design (hourly aggregation). Internal monitors: Sev3 IcM if average latency >= 3h, Sev2 if >= 6h across a region.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Billing/Know Issue: Unable to modify Capacity Reservation Value via PS
symptom: Customer tries to change Log Analytics cluster capacity reservation to 500 GB via PowerShell/CLI and receives error: CapacityReservationLevel must be in the min of 1000.
rootCause: Known bug in AMS/Control plane pipeline that prevents lowering capacity reservation below 1000 GB via CLI/PowerShell.
solution: Workaround: Use REST API with api-version=2021-06-01 to set capacity reservation to 500 GB. A fix PR has been submitted to allow lowering capacity reservation below 1000 GB.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Billing/Known Issue: LA Data collection is stopped after subscription is suspended and then reactivated
symptom: After a subscription is suspended and reactivated, Log Analytics workspace stops collecting logs. No new data flows to the workspace even though the subscription is active/renewed.
rootCause: Control Plane receives SubscriptionSuspended flag from ARM + Billing and stops Log Analytics data collection. The flag is not properly cleared after subscription reactivation.
solution: Running a resource sync will NOT help. This requires a manual fix by PG. Use the Workspace Dashboard to check data ingestion status. In ASC, verify the subscription is in Active state. All regions are affected. PG work item: 24622575.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Billing/Known issue: Unable to modify the retention of the AzureDiagnostics table
symptom: Customer cannot change the retention period for the AzureDiagnostics table. Error: NotFound - The specified table AzureDiagnostics does not exist.
rootCause: AzureDiagnostics is a custom log table without the mandatory _CL suffix, so the system detects it as a Microsoft table. Since there is no schema for it at global scope, it is treated as a non-existing table, forbidding explicit operations.
solution: RESOLVED (fix deployed 3/1/2022). Customers should now be able to update the retention for AzureDiagnostics table. If still failing, verify the fix is applied in the customer region.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Data sources/Azure Active Directory Signin logs missing or empty fields
symptom: Missing or empty fields in AAD Sign-in log tables: AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs, AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs, AADManagedIdentitySignInLogs, AADProvisioningLogs. Note: SigninLogs table is unaffected.
rootCause: Product bug introduced when AAD RP PG team moved their workflow from legacy transform to NorthStar transform (since approximately November 2020).
solution: No customer workaround. PG fix rolling out to all regions. Track PG work item 9018479. Note: only new AAD sign-in tables are affected, not legacy SigninLogs.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Data sources/Azure Active Directory Signin logs not being received in Log Analytics
symptom: AAD Sign-in logs do not reach Log Analytics workspace despite being properly configured via Diagnostic Settings. Audit logs DO arrive, but Sign-in logs do not.
rootCause: Latency in internal data stream that reflects the tenant premium license information. Sign-in logs only flow for premium tenants; the system treats the tenant as a free tenant due to this delay.
solution: Workaround: Wait a few days for the internal data stream to sync. If urgent, raise an IcM to the AAD team. AAD team is working on a fix (work item 972730). Verify customer has AAD Premium license.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Data sources/MicrosoftInsightsAzureActivityLog table deprecation
symptom: Customer reports the MicrosoftInsightsAzureActivityLog table is missing from their Log Analytics workspace (especially in westus2 region).
rootCause: The MicrosoftInsightsAzureActivityLog table was deprecated on 01/02/2022. It was a testing table that should never have had customer content; it was only available in westus2 region.
solution: Advise customer to use the AzureActivity table instead. The data is already being ingested to AzureActivity. The MicrosoftInsightsAzureActivityLog table was deprecated and removed.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Data sources/Unable to create an operation insights data export resource allTables=true
symptom: When creating a Log Analytics data export rule with allTables=true, customer receives error: InvalidOperationArgument - You must provide a table or tables array TableNames.
rootCause: The --export-all-tables option in CLI and REST was disabled by PG because allTables=true is not supported in data export. The option will be removed.
solution: Customer must explicitly list the tables to export in the TableNames property (e.g., Heartbeat, SecurityEvent, Perf). Data for unsupported tables will not be exported; once more tables are supported, data will auto-export.

symptom: Under Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS), connected resources show 'Failed' provisioning state. Re-adding does not help. Activity logs show private endpoint connection error.
rootCause: Known bug in private endpoint connection causing provisioning state to get stuck in Failed for AMPLS-scoped resources.
solution: Navigate to the Private Endpoint resource associated with the AMPLS (via 'Private Endpoint connections' blade). On the Private Endpoint Overview blade, click Refresh as instructed in the yellow warning banner. Return to AMPLS and confirm resources show Succeeded state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/AMPLS/Azure monitor private link scope: Azure Monitor Resources shows 'Failed' provisioning state

symptom: Cannot create new Log Analytics workspaces in the 'Free' (Free Trial) pricing tier, or move existing workspaces to the Free tier since July 1st 2022.
rootCause: By design: the Free Trial pricing tier has been deprecated and disabled for new workspaces. Existing workspaces already in Free Trial can remain.
solution: Inform the customer that the Free tier is deprecated. Point to the public documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/cost-logs#free-trial-pricing-tier
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Billing/By design: Cannot create new workspaces or move existing ones to the 'Free' tier since July 1st 2022

symptom: When using Python azure-identity package with VisualStudioCodeCredential to create a Log Analytics query client, get error: 'VisualStudioCodeCredential.get_token failed: AADSTS50173 The provided grant has expired due to it being revoked'.
rootCause: Known issue: VisualStudioCodeCredential does not work with Azure Account VS Code extension versions newer than 0.9.11.
solution: Option 1: Downgrade Azure Account extension to version < 0.9.11. Option 2: Use DefaultAzureCredential or ManagedIdentityCredential instead of VisualStudioCodeCredential. See Python azure-identity troubleshooting guide.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Query experience/Query client for Python: VisualStudioCodeCredential failed with get_token error

symptom: Running a KQL query with a comment containing an existing Log Analytics function name causes a syntax error 'Query could not be parsed'. The error line number may not match visible lines. Nested function calls can also produce unexpected syntax errors.
rootCause: Log Analytics service (Draft) expands all function names found in the query text, including those in comments. If any referenced function has a syntax issue, the entire query fails with a misleading error location.
solution: Review all functions called in the query for syntax errors. For function names appearing in comments, add a digit/char prefix or suffix to prevent matching (e.g., rename 'myFunc' to 'myFunc1' in the comment). Underscore is allowed; avoid special characters like +,-.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Query experience/Query could not be parsed (Syntax issue)

symptom: Log Analytics queries show 1-2 minute latency between data changes/ingestion and query results reflecting those changes. Log Search Alerts may produce unexpected results when ingestion_time() is close to query time.
rootCause: By design: Kusto clusters backing Log Analytics use a weak consistency model. Changes are not immediately visible in query results.
solution: This is expected behavior. Refer customer to: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-explorer/kusto/concepts/queryconsistency. For Log Search Alert issues, see ADO work item 19139.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Query experience/Weak Consistency

symptom: Using parse_json(), extend, or project operators to access a property with a reserved keyword name (e.g., 'title') returns error 'Query could not be parsed at \'.\''.
rootCause: KQL reserved keywords (like 'title') cannot be used directly as property accessors after the dot operator. This applies in both Log Analytics and ADX.
solution: Use identifier quoting: parse_json(json).['title'] or parse_json(json).["title"]. Alternatively, rename the property to a non-reserved keyword (e.g., 'Title' with uppercase). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-explorer/kusto/query/schema-entities/entity-names#identifier-quoting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Query experience/parse_json() may return error "Query could not be parsed at '.'" once you parse special items

symptom: Deploying 'Azure Application Gateway analytics' solution fails with error: 'Enabling solution of type AzureAppGatewayAnalytics is not allowed'.
rootCause: The Azure Application Gateway analytics solution has been deprecated (retired Feb 29, 2024) in favor of Azure Monitor Network Insights workbook.
solution: Migrate to Azure Monitor Network Insights workbook: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/insights/azure-networking-analytics#new-capabilities-with-azure-monitor-network-insights-workbook. For additional help, customer should create a support request under Application Gateway service.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Solutions/Azure Application Gateway analytics solution deprecation

symptom: Azure Data Factory Analytics summary tile is stuck in 'Performing Assessment' even though ADF data exists in the workspace.
rootCause: The ADF Analytics summary box has been deprecated by the ADF product team.
solution: Use the 'AzureDataFactoryAnalytics' workbook instead of the deprecated summary tile. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-factory/monitor-using-azure-monitor#monitor-data-factory-metrics. Close the case under ADF root cause tree as the issue is in Azure Data Factory.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Solutions/Azure Data Factory Analytics summary tile stuck in "Performing Assessment"

symptom: When navigating to Service Map or Azure Migrate and selecting a single machine, the view shows all machines instead of the selected single machine view.
rootCause: Service Map and Azure Migrate use version 1 of the Map API. VM Insights uses version 2. New regions support v2 API and redirect Service Map to VM Insights Map view.
solution: Use the Map view from VM Insights instead of Service Map. Azure Migrate plans to support v2 Map API in the future. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/insights/vminsights-enable-overview#log-analytics
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Solutions/Known Issue: Service Map Solution shows all machine views instead of single machine view from Service Map or Azure Migrate

symptom: Surface Hub devices fail to communicate with Log Analytics. Operations Manager logs show error 21400: 'Windows cannot verify the digital signature for this file' when executing MonitorKnowledgeDiscovery.vbs.
rootCause: Unknown root cause. The digital signature verification failure prevents the Microsoft Monitoring Agent health service workflow from running on Surface Hub.
solution: Collect Surface Hub logs (Settings > Update and Security > Recovery > Collect logs, attach USB). Check Operations Manager logs for error 21400. If confirmed related, transfer the case to Surface Hub team. If no Azure-side ingestion issues found and logs cannot be collected, also transfer to Surface Hub team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Solutions/Known Issue: Surface Hub Log Analytics Integration (Windows cannot verify the digital signature for this file)

symptom: Customers ingesting high volume of custom logs via Log Analytics Agent experience duplicate records. Multiple rows appear with different TimeGenerated values but identical payloads (RawData). Duplicates appear in clusters up to 20,000 rows. Affects IIS and custom logs on both Linux and Windows. May also see HTTP 400 invalid blocklist errors.
rootCause: Design limitation of the InMem component (InMemoryTransferManagerRole) responsible for queueing custom log data from Log Analytics Agents. Under high load, processing large blobs takes longer than anticipated, causing the blob to be re-queued for processing a second time (race condition with WatermarkUpdatedByOtherInstance).
solution: Issue was resolved with Stage 1 (Feb 2021) and Stage 2 (Sept 2021) fixes deployed globally. For any new duplicate issues, follow the general duplicates TSG. Workaround details in linked SharePoint docs. Refund process available for affected customers by calculating duplicate data volume and extrapolating.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Ingestion/Duplication when ingestion is high

symptom: When typing KQL queries in the Logs portal, IntelliSense shows diagnostic log table names that do not actually exist in the workspace. Running queries against these tables returns 0 results, misleading customers to think data is missing.
rootCause: By design - there is no signal to determine if a customer has enabled Diagnostics Logs for a workspace, so IntelliSense always suggests diagnostic log related table names.
solution: By design behavior. Advise customers to check the Tables tab in the Logs blade to verify which tables actually exist in their workspace. This has always been the behavior.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Portal Issues/By Design: Intellisense shows table names that do not exist in the workspace

symptom: KQL query fails with error: Maximum response size of 100000000 bytes exceeded. Actual response Size is N bytes. Returns HTTP 200 with partial query failure (not an HTTP error code).
rootCause: By design - PG enforces 100MB response size limit in portal for memory and performance reasons. Limit was increased from 70MB to 100MB. The enforcement is relatively recent but the limit always existed.
solution: Optimize queries to reduce response size: add filters, use summarize, limit columns, or use truncationmaxsize request option. For large data exports, use Azure Storage export or other bulk methods. Verify issue using dependencies table with Kusto.partialQueryFailures custom dimension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Portal Issues/By Design: Query Size Exceeded, Response Size too Large

symptom: Log Analytics Logs blade displays up to 100 unique values in charts, but when pinned to Azure Dashboard, the chart only shows 25 unique values with the rest aggregated to OTHERS.
rootCause: By design - the dashboard part has a 25 unique values limit compared to 100 in the Logs blade. This is due to the legacy dashboard part not yet being transitioned to the new OMS part.
solution: Workaround: refine queries and create multiple views in the dashboard. The 25-value limit was planned to be removed after OMS part transition (ETA mid Feb 2021). Suggest customers submit feedback via User Voice.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Portal Issues/By Design: Query and Dashboard Views Limitations

symptom: Pin to Dashboard option is greyed out in Logs blade, showing tooltip: Pin to Dashboard is not supported in this environment.
rootCause: By design - occurs when customer has linked a storage account to Saved queries under Log Analytics Workspace > Linked storage account. This is part of CMK (Customer Managed Key) configuration for saved queries.
solution: Customer must decide on the tradeoff: unlink the storage account for Saved queries to enable Pin to Dashboard functionality, or keep it linked and use alternatives such as Azure Workbooks or Grafana for dashboarding.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Portal Issues/By design: 'Pin to' dashboard is greyed out

symptom: Error 'No tabular expression statement found' when running KQL queries in Log Analytics.
rootCause: The query does not query any table - it only defines variables or expressions without calling a tabular data source. A tabular expression requires actually querying a table.
solution: Ensure the query includes a tabular expression by querying a table. If using let statements to define variables, make sure to call/reference the variable at the end of the query so the table is actually queried.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Portal Issues/Error - No tabular expression statement found

symptom: Log Analytics Workspace page never completes loading - blank screen with no animated ellipses or error indicators when attempting to access workspace assets.
rootCause: Browser is blocking all third-party cookies. Several cookies served by Log Analytics are designated as third-party cookies by browsers.
solution: Add browser exceptions to allow third-party cookies for: Commercial - *.mms.microsoft.com, *.microsoft.com; Azure Government (Fairfax/MAG) - *.omsworkspace.hosting.azureportal.usgovcloudapi.net, *.portal.azure.us, *.oms.microsoft.us.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Portal Issues/Known Issue: Workspace Inaccessible if Blocking Third Party Cookies

symptom: Logs blade tables list not populated with error: Register resource provider Microsoft.Insights for this subscription to enable this query. Occurs when opening Log Analytics workspace on Azure Lighthouse managed subscription.
rootCause: Microsoft.Insights resource provider is not registered on a subscription in the managing tenant. Lighthouse requires RP registration in both customer and managing tenants.
solution: Register Microsoft.Insights RP on managing tenant using PowerShell: Connect-AzAccount -Tenant $ManagingTenantId, then run New-AzADServicePrincipal for ApplicationIds: 1215fb39-1d15-4c05-b2e3-d519ac3feab4, 6da94f3c-0d67-4092-a408-bb5d1cb08d2d, ca7f3f0b-7d91-482c-8e09-c5d840d0eac5 with Contributor role.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Portal Issues/Unable to use the Logs blade when the workspace is on Azure Lighthouse managed subscription

symptom: Query execution fails on a table where schema was recently altered (usually via DCR transformation). Error references scalar expression with custom fields that were added or removed. Error reproducible for up to 1 hour after schema change.
rootCause: By design - internal caching mechanisms cause query failures for up to 1 hour after table schema changes. PG has committed to improving this behavior.
solution: No immediate resolution - customer must wait up to 1 hour for the internal cache to refresh after schema changes. Ref: IcM 345290648.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Query experience/By Design: Query execution failures after the table schema was altered

symptom: KQL query exported to PowerBI via PowerBI (New Dataset) does not contain full results. Exported record count is less than expected total records.
rootCause: Export operation is restricted by query API limits: 500,000 maximum records returned in a single query, or 64MB maximum data size returned.
solution: Enable workspace diagnostic setting with Audit category. Check LAQueryLogs table for the export CorrelationID to verify actual records exported. Investigate response size via dependencies table. Consider splitting queries into smaller time ranges or using alternative export methods for large datasets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Query experience/PowerBI Dataset export does not contain full query results - query API Limit

symptom: Unable to link Azure US Government Log Analytics workspace to Services Hub. Customer attempts to link workspace but is unsuccessful.
rootCause: Services Hub does not support linking to Azure Log Analytics workspaces in the Azure US Government Cloud.
solution: Open an ICM for the Advanced Services Delivery (ASD) team per Collaboration Guide: Services Hub Health Assessments. Include: Tenant ID, Subscription ID, Resource Group, Region, Workspace ID/Name, Schedule ID, Assessment Name. Customer must view results directly in Azure Portal via their LA workspace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Solutions/Known Issue: Unable to link Azure US Government Log Analytics workspace to Services Hub

symptom: Customer using deprecated Log Analytics batch query API (v1/$batch) or beta API (beta/) needs to migrate before retirement deadlines.
rootCause: Beta API (api.loganalytics.azure.com/beta/) retires March 31, 2026. Batch API (api.loganalytics.azure.com/v1/$batch) retires March 31, 2028. Log Analytics via ARM and Application Insights beta API also part of March 31, 2026 retirement.
solution: Migrate to standard Log Analytics query API v1. See migration doc: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/api/migrate-batch-and-beta. LogsBatchQuery SDK class (Python/JS/.NET/Java/Go) does NOT use the deprecated batch API endpoint - no customer action needed for SDK users. Direct API calls need path change from /beta/ to /v1/.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Retirements/Batch query and beta query APIs retirement

symptom: Customer asks how to check if they are using deprecated beta or batch Log Analytics query APIs.
rootCause: Customer may be calling deprecated APIs directly (via SDKs, API clients) or indirectly (via Sentinel, Portal). Direct calls require customer action; indirect calls (OMS extension, IbizaExtension) are internal S2S and handled by Microsoft.
solution: Use ASC "Query draft telemetry" tab on workspace to run KQL: requests | where timestamp > ago(7d) | extend Request_apiVersion_ = tostring(customDimensions.["Request.apiVersion"]) | where Request_apiVersion_ == "beta" | where customDimensions has_any ("<sub_id>"). Check Request_clientApp: csharpsdk/powershell = customer direct call; IbizaExtension/OMS = internal. Do not overshare internal telemetry with customers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Retirements/Batch query and beta query APIs retirement

symptom: Log ingestion or query failures after TLS 1.0/1.1 retirement enforcement for Azure Monitor Logs. Customer resources using legacy TLS versions cannot send logs or query data.
rootCause: Azure Monitor Logs enforces TLS 1.2+ for queries starting July 1, 2025 and for log ingestion starting March 1, 2026. Agents, APIs, and connectors using TLS 1.0/1.1 will be rejected. Phased rollout: West Central US (March wk1) -> Japan East (wk2) -> all regions (wk3).
solution: Transition to TLS 1.2+: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/fundamentals/best-practices-security#secure-logs-data-in-transit. Use internal ADX dashboard (https://dataexplorer.azure.com/dashboards/72b75146-7f83-4819-b2ff-4fde8303cd79) to identify impacted workspaces. Use Kusto query on omsgenevaodsprod cluster IISLog table filtering crypt_protocol 40 (TLS 1.0) or 100 (TLS 1.1). Note: VM behind firewall may show gateway IP, share c_ip with customer. Close case under TLS retirement RCA tree.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Retirements/Older TLS Versions 1.0,1.1 Retirement - Log-Analytics - Azure-Monitor-logs-guidance
Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/AMPLS/Azure monitor private link scope: Azure Monitor Resources shows Failed provisioning state
symptom: Under Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS), connected resources in Azure Monitor Resources show provisioning state stuck in "Failed". Re-adding the resources does not help. Activity logs show private endpoint connection errors.
rootCause: Known bug on private endpoint connection causing provisioning state to not update correctly after connecting resources to AMPLS.
solution: Navigate to the Private Endpoint resource associated with the AMPLS (via "Private Endpoint connections" blade). On the Private Endpoint Overview blade, follow the yellow warning banner instruction to click Refresh. Navigate back to the AMPLS resource and confirm all scoped resources are now in Succeeded state (click Refresh if needed).

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Billing/By design: Cannot create new workspaces or move existing ones to the Free tier since July 1st 2022
symptom: Starting July 1, 2022, customers cannot create new Log Analytics workspaces in the Free Trial tier or move existing workspaces to the Free tier.
rootCause: By design - the Free Trial pricing tier has been deprecated and disabled. Workspaces already in the Free Trial tier can remain.
solution: Inform customer this is by-design. Point to public documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/cost-logs#free-trial-pricing-tier. Existing workspaces on Free Trial can remain on that tier.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Query experience/parse_json() may return error Query could not be parsed at . once you parse special items
symptom: When using parse_json(), extend, or project operators in KQL to parse dynamic/JSON fields, the query returns error "Query could not be parsed at '.'" if the field name is a reserved keyword (e.g., "title"). Affects both Log Analytics workspace and ADX queries.
rootCause: KQL/ADX does not allow directly accessing dynamic fields whose names are reserved keywords (e.g., title, type, source). The parser interprets the keyword in its reserved meaning rather than as a property name.
solution: Use identifier quoting with bracket notation: parse_json(json).["title"] or parse_json(json).["title"]. Alternatively, rename the property to a non-reserved keyword (e.g., "Title" with capital T). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-explorer/kusto/query/schema-entities/entity-names#identifier-quoting

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Solutions/Azure Data Factory Analytics summary tile stuck in Performing Assessment
symptom: Azure Data Factory Analytics summary tile in Log Analytics workspace is stuck in "Performing Assessment" status, even when ADF data exists in the workspace.
rootCause: The ADF Product Group confirmed the summary box (tile) is deprecated and no longer functional.
solution: Use the AzureDataFactoryAnalytics workbook instead of the deprecated summary tile. Close the support case under the ADF root cause tree (not Log Analytics). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-factory/monitor-using-azure-monitor#monitor-data-factory-metrics

symptom: Log Analytics daily cap is exceeded well beyond the configured amount, especially in high ingestion rate scenarios and regions like EastUS and WestEurope
rootCause: The daily cap mechanism had a design limitation where it could not stop data collection precisely at the configured cap level under high ingestion rates. Data collected above the cap is still billed.
solution: Issue mitigated with redesigned flow from PG side (Jan 2026 update). Daily cap still will not stop at exact configured value but extreme overshoot is now controlled. Do not raise ICMs with PG for this. Discuss with STA Sahil and check for feasibility of refund and customer-ready message. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/daily-cap#how-the-daily-cap-works
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Billing, unexpected volume, data cap and retention/Control the data volume to your workspace and ingestion daily cap issues

symptom: Customer expects using the Log Analytics purge API to reduce retention billing costs, but billing is not reduced after purge
rootCause: The Log Analytics purge API does not affect retention billing. It is intended for very limited compliance/privacy use cases only (e.g., GDPR data deletion), not for cost optimization.
solution: Explain to customer that purge API is not a billing optimization tool. For retention cost management, configure data retention at workspace or table level properly. Refer to TSG: Troubleshoot Log Analytics billing. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/azure-monitor/logs/cost-logs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Billing, unexpected volume, data cap and retention/Configure data retention

symptom: Customer raises billing case about Defender for Cloud or Sentinel costs visible in Log Analytics workspace billed costs view
rootCause: Defender for Cloud and Sentinel billing are managed by the Security POD, not by Azure Monitor/Log Analytics team. These costs appear in the workspace view but are billed and supported separately.
solution: Transfer the case or create a collaboration task with the Security POD for Defender for Cloud and/or Sentinel billed costs issues. Do not attempt to troubleshoot Security product billing within Monitor scope.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Billing, unexpected volume, data cap and retention/Understand my workspace's billed costs

symptom: Azure Activity logs are not flowing into Log Analytics workspace. Customer is using legacy Activity Logs collection method.
rootCause: The legacy Activity Logs collection experience has been deprecated. Data may stop flowing or become unreliable when using the legacy connector.
solution: Migrate customer from legacy Activity Logs collection mode to diagnostic settings mode. Guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/activity-log?tabs=powershell#send-to-log-analytics-workspace. If customer is already using diagnostic settings but data is not flowing, refer to TSG: Troubleshooting Resource Log data not being received by target destinations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Collecting Azure Activity logs, Diagnostics logs/Collect Azure Activity logs

symptom: Under Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS), connected Azure Monitor Resources show 'Failed' provisioning state. Re-adding does not help. Error visible in activity logs.
rootCause: Known bug on private endpoint connection causing provisioning state to get stuck in Failed.
solution: Navigate to the Private Endpoint resource associated with the AMPLS (via 'Private Endpoint connections' blade). On the Private Endpoint's Overview blade, follow the yellow warning banner instruction to click 'Refresh'. Then navigate back to AMPLS and confirm all scoped resources show 'Succeeded' state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/AMPLS/Azure monitor private link scope: Azure Monitor Resources shows 'Failed' provisioning state

symptom: Cannot create new Log Analytics workspaces in the 'Free' tier or move existing workspaces to the 'Free' tier since July 1st 2022. Existing workspaces already in Free Trial tier are unaffected.
rootCause: By design: the legacy Free Trial pricing tier has been deprecated and disabled since July 1, 2022.
solution: Inform customer that the Free tier is deprecated. Workspaces already on Free tier can remain. For new workspaces, use Pay-As-You-Go or other available tiers. Refer to: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/cost-logs#free-trial-pricing-tier
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Billing/By design: Cannot create new workspaces or move existing ones to the 'Free' tier since July 1st 2022

symptom: parse_json() or extend/project operators return error 'Query could not be parsed at .' when parsing fields with reserved keyword names such as 'title'. Affects both Log Analytics and ADX queries.
rootCause: KQL reserved keywords (e.g., 'title') cannot be used directly as dynamic property accessors. ADX PG is working on error message improvement.
solution: Use identifier quoting with bracket notation: parse_json(json).['title'] or parse_json(json).["title"]. Alternatively, rename the field to a non-reserved keyword (e.g., 'Title' with uppercase). Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-explorer/kusto/query/schema-entities/entity-names#identifier-quoting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Query experience/parse_json() may return error "Query could not be parsed at '.'" once you parse special items

symptom: Azure Data Factory Analytics summary tile stuck in 'Performing Assessment' even though ADF data exists in the workspace.
rootCause: The ADF Summary box tile is deprecated by the ADF Product Group.
solution: Use the 'AzureDataFactoryAnalytics' workbook instead of the deprecated summary tile. Close the support case under the ADF root cause tree since the issue is in Azure Data Factory. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-factory/monitor-using-azure-monitor#monitor-data-factory-metrics
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Solutions/Azure Data Factory Analytics summary tile stuck in "Performing Assessment"

symptom: When using custom tables with ingestion-time transformation, records arrive with only TimeGenerated populated while other fields (e.g. SyslogMessage) are silently dropped and RawData is empty.
rootCause: The destination custom table schema does not contain columns matching the fields in the incoming records. Fields not present in the table schema are silently dropped during ingestion-time transformation.
solution: Ensure the destination custom table schema contains custom columns matching all fields in the records being sent. Add missing columns via Azure portal (add or delete custom columns). Verify the transformation KQL output schema matches the table schema before applying.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Configure and Manage Log Analytics tables/Issues related to table transformation

symptom: Cannot switch Log Analytics table plan back from Basic to Analytics immediately after setting it to Basic. Customer may have set table plan to Basic by mistake.
rootCause: Azure Monitor enforces a one-week cooldown period between table plan switches. This is by design and cannot be overridden by the Product Group on the backend.
solution: Wait one week before attempting to switch the table plan again. Inform the customer that PG cannot change this configuration on the backend. Do not raise IcMs requesting a table plan switch.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Configure and Manage Log Analytics tables/Issues related with Basic Logs
Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Create and manage Log Analytics workspaces/Move workspace to a different subscription or resource group
symptom: Customer wants to move Log Analytics workspace between different Azure AD tenants
rootCause: Cross-tenant workspace move is not supported by design. Workspaces can only be moved between subscriptions within the same AAD tenant.
solution: Inform customer that workspace move between AAD tenants is not possible. Workspaces can only be moved between subscriptions within the same AAD tenant.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Create and manage Log Analytics workspaces/Move workspace to a different subscription or resource group
symptom: Customer wants to move Log Analytics workspace to a different Azure region
rootCause: Cross-region workspace move is not supported. Azure does not allow moving a workspace to a different region directly.
solution: Workspace cannot be moved to a different region. Customer can clone workspace in a different region using guidance at https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/move-workspace-region. Data will not be moved.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Create and manage Log Analytics workspaces/Move workspace to a different subscription or resource group
symptom: Log Analytics workspace move operation fails with validation errors
rootCause: Before the move, Azure performs validations to confirm the operation can happen. Failures typically due to unmet requirements in workspace move considerations.
solution: Run ARM Kusto query against ARMProd to check validation errors (filter by subscription + workspace name + moveResources/action). Review error details via correlationId. Check workspace move considerations: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/move-workspace#workspace-move-considerations

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Create and manage Log Analytics workspaces/Restore deleted workspace
symptom: Customer wants to recover a permanently deleted (force-deleted) Log Analytics workspace
rootCause: Only soft-deleted workspaces can be recovered. Permanently deleted workspaces cannot be restored.
solution: Inform customer permanently deleted workspaces cannot be recovered. Use How-to guide to check if workspace was permanently deleted. Only soft-deleted workspaces within 14-day retention can be restored.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Create and manage Log Analytics workspaces/Restore deleted workspace
symptom: Customer wants to recover Log Analytics workspace after the 14-day soft-delete period has expired
rootCause: The soft-delete retention period is 14 days. After this period the workspace is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.
solution: Recovery after 14-day soft-delete period is not possible. Use How-to guide to determine when workspace was deleted. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/delete-workspace

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Create and manage Log Analytics workspaces/Restore deleted workspace
symptom: Solutions and data are missing after restoring a deleted Log Analytics workspace
rootCause: When workspace is restored, previously installed solutions are not automatically re-added. Data visibility depends on corresponding solution being installed.
solution: Customer needs to re-add the solutions that were present before workspace deletion. Until solutions are re-added, some data types will not be visible. Restore operation is actually a create workspace operation behind the scenes.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Create and manage Log Analytics workspaces/Setting permissions on a workspace or a table
symptom: Customer cannot access Log Analytics workspace despite having the required RBAC role assigned
rootCause: RBAC role assignment may not be effective due to scope mismatch, inheritance issues, or condition-based access policies.
solution: 1. Check RBAC details from ASC to verify effective permissions. 2. Review role assignments at correct scope. 3. Advise per https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/manage-access?tabs=portal. 4. Check for deny assignments or conditional access policies.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Solutions and Insights/A solution I use is being retired
symptom: Customer receives error 'Enabling solution of type AzureAppGatewayAnalytics is not allowed' when trying to enable Azure Application Gateway analytics solution
rootCause: Azure Application Gateway analytics solution has been retired. New deployments are blocked.
solution: Migrate to Azure Monitor Workbooks for Application Gateway analytics: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/insights/azure-networking-analytics#migrating-from-azure-gateway-analytics-solution-to-azure-monitor-workbooks

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Solutions and Insights/A solution I use is being retired
symptom: Network Performance Monitor (NPM) solution is being retired; customer cannot add new tests after July 1, 2021
rootCause: NPM solution deprecated. No new tests can be added since July 1, 2021.
solution: Migrate tests from NPM to Connection Monitor in Azure Network Watcher: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/network-watcher/migrate-to-connection-monitor-from-network-performance-monitor. NPM functionality supported by Azure Network team.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Solutions and Insights/A solution I use is being retired
symptom: Wire Data 2.0 solution is no longer working or collecting data (support ended March 31, 2022)
rootCause: Wire Data 2.0 solution support ended March 31, 2022. No longer maintained.
solution: Migrate to VM Insights: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/insights/wire-data#migrate-to-azure-monitor-vm-insights-or-service-map. Wire Data issues will not be fixed.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Solutions and Insights/A solution I use is being retired
symptom: Office 365 management solution removed after October 31, 2022; customer needs alternative for O365 log monitoring
rootCause: Azure Monitor Office 365 solution onboarding scripts removed. Solution removed October 31, 2022.
solution: Migrate to Office 365 connector in Microsoft Sentinel: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/sentinel/data-connectors-reference#microsoft-office-365. O365 connector fully supported by Sentinel team.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Support Topics/Solutions and Insights/A solution I use is being retired
symptom: Alert Management solution not working as expected or returns incorrect data in Alert table
rootCause: Alert Management solution no longer in active development. Known issues will not be fixed.
solution: Stop using Alert Management solution. Use Azure Resource Graph to query alerts: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/resource-graph-samples. Alternative: Azure Monitor Workbook with Resource Graph data source.

symptom: Under Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS), after connecting target components in Azure Monitor Resources, the provisioning state of certain scoped resources is stuck in Failed. Re-adding does not help. Activity logs show private endpoint connection error.
rootCause: Known bug in private endpoint connection for AMPLS. The private endpoint connection state becomes stale and does not automatically refresh after initial setup.
solution: Navigate to the Private Endpoint resource associated with the AMPLS (via Private Endpoint connections blade). On the Private Endpoint Overview blade, follow the yellow warning banner instruction to click Refresh. Then navigate back to AMPLS and confirm all scoped resources show Succeeded state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/AMPLS/Azure monitor private link scope: Azure Monitor Resources shows 'Failed' provisioning state

symptom: Starting July 1 2022, customers cannot create new Log Analytics workspaces in the legacy Free Trial pricing tier, nor move existing workspaces into this tier.
rootCause: By design: the Free pricing tier has been deprecated and disabled by Microsoft. Existing workspaces already on Free Trial tier can remain.
solution: Inform the customer this is by design. Point them to the public documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/cost-logs#free-trial-pricing-tier. Existing Free Trial workspaces are unaffected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Billing/By design: Cannot create new workspaces or move existing ones to the 'Free' tier since July 1st 2022

symptom: When using parse_json(), extend, or project operators in Log Analytics or ADX queries to parse fields with reserved keyword names (e.g. title), the query fails with error: Query could not be parsed at '.'.
rootCause: KQL reserved keywords (like title) cannot be used directly as property accessors after parse_json() or dynamic column navigation. The query parser interprets them as keywords instead of property names.
solution: Use identifier quoting to access reserved keyword properties: parse_json(json).['title'] or parse_json(json).["title"]. Alternatively, rename the property to a non-reserved name (e.g. Title with capital T). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-explorer/kusto/query/schema-entities/entity-names#identifier-quoting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Query experience/parse_json() may return error "Query could not be parsed at '.'" once you parse special items

symptom: Azure Data Factory Analytics summary tile in Log Analytics workspace is stuck in Performing Assessment even though ADF data exists in the workspace.
rootCause: The ADF Product Group confirmed the Summary box (assessment tile) is deprecated and no longer updated.
solution: Advise customers to use the AzureDataFactoryAnalytics workbook instead of the deprecated summary tile. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-factory/monitor-using-azure-monitor#monitor-data-factory-metrics. Close the support case under the ADF root cause tree since the issue originates in Azure Data Factory.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Known Issues/Solutions/Azure Data Factory Analytics summary tile stuck in "Performing Assessment"

symptom: ARM template deployment for Log Analytics workspace data sources (performance counters, syslog, etc.) fails with Error 500 / Bad Request / ResourceDeploymentFailure with terminal provisioning state Failed.
rootCause: Data source name conflicts: (1) Performance counters already set up via portal have randomly generated names that conflict with ARM template names; (2) Modifying counter names in ARM template after initial deployment causes conflicts; (3) Each data source item must have a unique name. Resource locks on resource group or resource level can also cause deployment failures.
solution: 1. Check and remove any Resource Locks on the resource group or resource level. 2. Identify the conflicting data source name from the error message, HTTP request, or ARM template. 3. Delete the conflicting data source via REST API (https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/loganalytics/datasources/delete) - this is safe and will not wipe data from tables. 4. Redeploy the ARM template or re-enable the data source.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Troubleshooting Guides/ARM Template/ARM Template Data Sources Error 500 Bad Request

symptom: ARM template deployment for Log Analytics workspace fails with BadRequest error: RoleAssignmentUpdateNotPermitted - Tenant ID, application ID, principal ID, and scope are not allowed to be updated. Template succeeds on brand new subscription but fails on redeployment.
rootCause: The subscription used for redeployment is in a disabled state, causing role assignment operations to fail. Alternatively, role assignment conflicts exist from previous deployments where the same role assignment GUID is reused with different principal/scope.
solution: 1. Check HttpIncomingRequests in ARM Kusto for the correlation ID to identify the failing operation. 2. Check EventServiceEntries to confirm the specific role assignment failure. 3. Verify the subscription status - if disabled, enable it or resolve the subscription state. 4. If subscription is enabled, refer to: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/templates/common-deployment-errors
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Troubleshooting Guides/ARM Template/Bad Request error for LA workspace deployment

symptom: Cannot save query or function using 'Save as legacy query' in Log Analytics workspace. Error: 'Query Update/Create is restricted to user assigned storage, please link user assigned storage with affiliation to Query data source type'
rootCause: Workspace property ForceCmkForQuery is set to true, requiring a linked storage account for query encryption, but no storage account has been linked to the workspace
solution: Either link a storage account for query storage (see private-storage doc) OR set ForceCmkForQuery to false via Set-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace -ForceCmkForQuery $false. Can also use REST API: Workspaces - Create Or Update
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Troubleshooting Guides/Query execution/TSG: ForceCmkForQuery workspace property behaviors: can not save function or query and policy non-compliant

symptom: Azure Policy 'Saved-queries in Azure Monitor should be saved in customer storage account for logs encryption' shows Log Analytics workspace as non-compliant
rootCause: Workspace property ForceCmkForQuery is not set to true (default is None/Blank). The policy requires this property to be true to enforce encryption of saved queries via CMK
solution: Set ForceCmkForQuery to true: Set-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace -ForceCmkForQuery $true. Then link a storage account. If workspace is on commitment tier, use REST API instead of PowerShell cmdlet (known bug with cmdlet on capacity reservation)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Troubleshooting Guides/Query execution/TSG: ForceCmkForQuery workspace property behaviors: can not save function or query and policy non-compliant

symptom: Set-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace PowerShell cmdlet fails with error when workspace is on commitment tier (capacity reservation pricing)
rootCause: Known bug in the PowerShell cmdlet that does not handle commitment tier / capacity reservation workspaces correctly
solution: Use REST API (Workspaces - Create Or Update) to change workspace properties instead of PowerShell cmdlet when workspace is on commitment tier
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Troubleshooting Guides/Query execution/TSG: ForceCmkForQuery workspace property behaviors: can not save function or query and policy non-compliant

symptom: Summary Rule create/update returns HTTP 404 with error 'Requested workspace does not exist'
rootCause: Customer specifies a non-existing workspace ID in the resource URI when creating or updating a summary rule
solution: Verify the workspace resource ID in the API request URI is correct and points to an existing Log Analytics workspace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Troubleshooting Guides/Summary Rules

symptom: Summary Rule create/update returns HTTP 404 with error 'Workspace not active, WorkspaceId={0}'
rootCause: Target workspace is in soft-deleted or purged state and not currently active
solution: If workspace is soft-deleted, restore it. If workspace is purged, switch to another active workspace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Troubleshooting Guides/Summary Rules

symptom: Summary Rule create/update returns HTTP 400 'The operation is not supported while the summary log rule is in [state] state. Please wait for Succeeded state'
rootCause: Summary rule is locked during an in-progress update or deletion. Another control action was committed and has not yet completed
solution: Wait for the in-progress operation to complete. Monitor via: 1) Poll Azure-Async header URL for status 2) Poll the resource until provisioningState returns Succeeded. For deletion, 404 is returned on success
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Log Analytics/Troubleshooting Guides/Summary Rules

symptom: Summary Rule create/update returns HTTP 400 'The operation is not supported while the summary log rule is on Failed state. Please delete the resource'
rootCause: Summary rule creation previously failed, resulting in terminal provisioningState of Failed. Resource cannot be updated once in Failed state
solution: Delete the failed summary rule resource, then recreate it with the correct configuration
