rootCause: The APM (Application Performance Monitoring) feature in SCOM 2016 Agent causes IIS app pool crash under .NET Framework 2.0 runtime. MMA from Azure portal does not include the old SCOM fix for this known issue.
solution: Option 1: Install the SCOM agent instead of the Azure portal MMA. Option 2: Remove the APM feature (disable APM discoveries, then run Remove-SCOMDisabledClassInstance). See docs.microsoft.com/troubleshoot/system-center/scom/remove-corrupted-apm-components.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Known Issues/Upgrade is not supported for currently Installed Version
symptom: MMA installation fails with MSI error Event ID 1013: 'Upgrade is not supported for the currently installed version.' Installer log shows BLOCK_UPGRADE_BETWEEN_MMA_DECEMBER_2018_TO_MMA_MARCH_2019 property set.
rootCause: The currently installed MMA agent version (e.g., SCOM 1801 RTM 8.0.13053.0) is not in the supported upgrade path list. Only specific version ranges are eligible for in-place upgrade.
solution: Remove the existing MMA agent and perform a clean install of the Azure Log Analytics agent. Take a system backup before removal. By design.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Known Issues/Windows Event not uploaded to workspace
symptom: Windows Security Event IDs (e.g., Event 4647 user-initiated logoff) generated right before shutdown are not uploaded to Log Analytics workspace after VM restarts the next day.
rootCause: MMA agent only looks back 1 hour when restarted or when connectivity is restored. Data older than 1 hour in local cache is not transmitted for ingestion, even if still available locally.
solution: Upgrade to the latest MMA version (post July 2022 release) which extends the lookback window from 1 hour to 24 hours.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Known Issues/Windows MMA agent only collects SecurityEventIDs with EventSourceName Microsoft-Windows-AppLocker
symptom: After MMA agent restart, only SecurityEventIDs with EventSourceName Microsoft-Windows-AppLocker are collected. Other security event IDs are not uploaded to the workspace.
rootCause: The MaxExpressionDepth registry value (used for regex matching of security event rules) is too low. Default value of 2000 can cause early failure in regex evaluation, resulting in only AppLocker events matching.
solution: Apply KB3004791: increase MaxExpressionDepth registry value from default 2000 to 10000 on the affected machine. Ensure latest MMA version and correct security event collection tier (Common vs All).

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Known Issues/Windows could not start the Microsoft Monitoring Agent
symptom: MMA service fails to start with Event ID 7024 (Service Control Manager): 'The Microsoft Monitoring Agent service terminated with service-specific error Access is denied.'
rootCause: Default permissions on registry key HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\services\HealthService\Parameters\SecureStorageManager changed from Full Control (System + Administrators) to more restrictive, likely due to server hardening.
solution: Restore default permissions on HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\services\HealthService\Parameters\SecureStorageManager: Full Control for System and Administrators. MMA service will start successfully after permission restoration.

symptom: MMA (Windows) agent stops reporting data/heartbeat to Log Analytics after June 2024 Root CA migration - agent uses outdated Baltimore CyberTrust Root CA certificate pinning
rootCause: Log Analytics backend switched from Baltimore CyberTrust CA Root to DigiCert Global G2 CA Root. Older MMA versions (Log Analytics branch < 10.20.18053 / Extension < 1.0.18053.0, SCOM branch < 2019 UR3) have hardcoded certificate pinning to the old Baltimore Root CA and cannot establish TLS connection after the migration
solution: 1) Upgrade Log Analytics branch MMA to version 10.20.18053+ (Bundle) or 1.0.18053.0+ (Extension) 2) For SCOM branch: upgrade to SCOM 2019 UR3 or later 3) Verify certificate chain using Kusto query: Heartbeat | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) | summarize by Computer, Version | where Version < '10.20.18053' 4) Consider migrating to Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) as MMA is deprecated
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Breaking Change - Log Analytics Root CA Migration

symptom: MMA agent installation/uninstallation fails with Error 0x80070643 Fatal error during installation, Status 1603, or Error 1714 The older version of Microsoft Monitoring Agent cannot be removed
rootCause: Windows Update store/cache corruption, pending reboots, or pending Windows updates interfere with MMA MSI-based install/uninstall operations on the OS
solution: 1) Ensure no pending Windows updates remain 2) Reboot the machine properly 3) Follow https://support.microsoft.com/help/10164/fix-windows-update-errors to fix WU errors 4) If still failing, create a collab task with UEX team (SAP: Windows Servers > Application Technologies and Compatibility > MSI and MSIX)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/MMA Setup Failed. Error: 0x80070643 Fatal error during installation | Status 1603 | Portal Error Error 1714

symptom: Agents behind Log Analytics Gateway unable to connect to workspace - no heartbeats from newly configured agents. Event ID 4008: certificate authority Baltimore CyberTrust Root not in Third-Party Root Certification Authorities store. OMS Gateway Event ID 106: Server certificate chain does not include a trusted root certificate - Root cert: CN=DigiCert Global Root G2
rootCause: Older OMS Gateway versions (< 1.0.448.0) have pinned Baltimore CyberTrust Root CA. After Microsoft updated Azure Monitor endpoints to use DigiCert-issued certificates, the Gateway certificate validation fails because the new certificate chain has DigiCert Global Root G2 instead of Baltimore CyberTrust Root
solution: 1) Update OMS Gateway to version 1.0.448.0 or later 2) Before upgrading, backup allowed hosts: Get-OMSGatewayAllowedHost 3) After upgrade, restore: Add-OMSGatewayAllowedHost 4) Restart Gateway service. Download: https://learn.microsoft.com/services-hub/health/log-analytics-gateway
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Network Agents having Baltimore CyberTrust Root issue when using LA Gateway

symptom: Log Analytics performance counters are logged in a non-English locale language instead of English. Counter names appear in local language format, breaking monitoring alerts. Affects both MMA and AMA agents on Windows Server/Windows 10/11
rootCause: Administrator changed the login/new user account regional settings and applied them to system accounts via Copy settings to welcome screen and system accounts. Since MMA/AMA agents run as system accounts, the locale change propagates to performance counter reporting
solution: 1) Open Windows Settings > Region > Language > Administrative language settings 2) Set English as preferred language 3) Copy settings to Welcome screen and system accounts 4) Reboot the VM 5) Verify logs flow in English after reboot. Ref: Copy-UserInternationalSettingsToSystem PowerShell cmdlet
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshoot in Workspace Performance counters are logged in a different or locale language (non-English)

symptom: MMA/SCOM agent installation fails with Error 25211 Failed to install performance counters. Error Code: -2147024809 (The parameter is incorrect.) - LoadPerfCounterTextStrings() returns 0x80070057
rootCause: Windows Performance Counter registry corruption prevents the MMA installer from registering HealthService performance counters via lodctr.exe/LoadPerfCounterTextStrings API
solution: 1) Run lodctr.exe /R from %windir%\system32 and %windir%\sysWOW64 to rebuild perf counter registry 2) Retry MMA installation 3) If still failing, manually register counters: create HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HealthService\Performance key, then run lodctr.exe HealthServiceCounters.ini 4) If manual registration also fails, escalate to Windows team (Performance Counters) with TTD trace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting  Mon-agent fails to install with error: Failed to install performance counters (The parameter is incorrect)

symptom: MMA agent on Virtual Desktop (RDS/AVD) cannot connect to Log Analytics workspace after successful installation - no heartbeat. TestCloudConnection.exe reports: The workspace key could not be validated due to the local system time not matching the time in Azure
rootCause: Group Policy Time zone redirection is disabled (Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Remote Desktop Services > Remote Desktop Session Host > Device and Resource Redirection), causing the RDS session host system clock to drift and workspace key validation to fail
solution: 1) Run gpresult /h c:\reports.html to identify Time zone redirection GPO 2) Enable the GPO or manually sync time 3) Registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services fEnableTimeZoneRedirection=1 4) Run gpupdate /force or reboot 5) Clear MMA cache and restart Health Service
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Agents on Virtual Desktops do not connect to log analytics workspace

symptom: MMA (Windows) agent stops uploading data to Log Analytics after April 14 2023 - older agent versions using SSL V1 certificates are rejected by Log Analytics backend
rootCause: Log Analytics backend deprecated SSL V1 certificate support as of April 14 2023. MMA versions older than Bundle 10.20.18053 / Extension 1.0.18053.0 (prior to Winter 2020 release) use SSL V1 certs and can no longer establish connections
solution: 1) Upgrade MMA to latest version (Bundle >= 10.20.18053 / Extension >= 1.0.18053.0) following https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/agents/agent-manage 2) Consider migrating to Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) since MMA is on deprecation path
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Breaking Change SSL V1 Agent

symptom: MMA agent MSI installation fails with Error 1402 Could not open key: UNKNOWN\Components\...  System error 5 - occurs on machines where default administrator permissions have been modified
rootCause: Registry permissions on HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Installer\UserData\S-1-5-18\Components have been modified, removing System and Local Administrator access required by the MSI installer to register components
solution: 1) Open Registry Editor 2) Navigate to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Installer\UserData\S-1-5-18\Components 3) Right-click > Permissions > ensure System and Local Administrator have Full Control on key and all subkeys 4) Use procmon to capture exact registry key details if needed 5) Retry MMA installation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting MMA Agent install Error 1402.Could not open key

symptom: After migrating from MMA/OMS to AMA, VMInsights or File Integrity Monitor (FIM) queries return incomplete or duplicate results. Dashboards and alerts that filter on the Computer value show mismatched data between old MMA-collected records and new AMA-collected records.
rootCause: MMA/OMS agent reports the Computer value as hostname+domainName (e.g. server1.contoso.com), while AMA reports only the hostname (e.g. server1). Queries, dashboards, and alert rules that filter on Computer may not match between the two naming formats.
solution: 1) Identify all queries, dashboards, alerts, and workbooks that filter on the Computer field 2) Update filter logic to handle both formats (e.g. use startswith or extract hostname) 3) For FIM Heartbeat+ConfigurationChange join queries, account for the Computer value difference 4) After full migration, standardize on the AMA hostname-only format
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Migration from LA Agents to AMA/Troubleshooting Guides/AMA: Migration Scoping

symptom: Syslog messages collected by OMS Agent for Linux appear truncated at 2048 bytes. Large syslog messages are cut off and incomplete data appears in Log Analytics workspace.
rootCause: The fluentd syslog input plugin used by OMS Agent has a default message_length_limit of 2048 bytes. Any syslog messages larger than this limit are silently truncated.
solution: 1) Edit /etc/opt/microsoft/omsagent/<WORKSPACE_ID>/conf/omsagent.d/syslog.conf 2) Add "message_length_limit 8192" (or larger value) to the <source> element 3) Restart the agent: sudo /opt/microsoft/omsagent/bin/service_control restart. Note: future agent updates may override this customization and require re-applying the change.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/How-To/How to increase the size of syslog messages

symptom: OMS Agent for Linux loses data or cannot sustain high event ingestion rates (above ~5000 EPS). Data gaps appear in Log Analytics workspace under heavy syslog or custom log workloads.
rootCause: By default the agent or rsyslog may use UDP protocol for localhost forwarding, which causes data loss at high throughput. The agent local cache only retains data for 9 minutes (30 minutes in v1.13+) before dropping oldest events. Without proper TCP configuration and VM sizing, the agent cannot handle high EPS workloads.
solution: 1) Switch to TCP protocol for rsyslog-to-agent forwarding (even on localhost) to prevent data loss 2) Use a powerful VM (F4s_v2 or F8s_v2 with ~2.7GHz CPU) for high EPS workloads 3) Configure rsyslog with local persistency (queue.type=fixedArray, queue.spoolDirectory, queue.maxdiskspace=5g) 4) Use numbered rsyslog config prefix (e.g. 30-) to load before 50-default.conf to prevent duplicate writes to /var/log/syslog 5) Official support is 5K EPS; ~8500 EPS is experimental and requires dedicated CPU core + ~1GB memory
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/How-To/How to: Change Rate Limits and Caching - Linux Agent

symptom: MMA (Log Analytics or SCOM agent) installation fails with error 'ConvertStringSecurityDescriptorToSecurityDescriptor failed : 87' and 'ModifyEventLogAccessForNetworkService(): Could not grant read access to SecurityLog: 0x00000057' in the MSI installation log, with Action ended InstallFinalize returning value 3.
rootCause: The Security descriptor for the Windows Security Event Log has a custom/corrupted Security Descriptor (CustomSD registry value) that cannot be decoded by ConvertStringSecurityDescriptorToSecurityDescriptor, causing the installer GrantAuditLogAccess custom action to fail with ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER (87).
solution: From an elevated PowerShell, export and reset the CustomSD registry value under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Eventlog\Security, then re-register scecli.dll: 1) reg export the key for backup, 2) Remove-ItemProperty -Path the backup path -Name CustomSD -Force, 3) regsvr32 scecli.dll. Retry the MMA installation. If the same error persists, the Security Descriptor may need further manual repair.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting MMA fails to install with error ConvertStringSecurityDescriptorToSecurityDescriptor failed : 87

symptom: MMA (Log Analytics or SCOM agent) installation fails with 'Error 25211. Failed to install performance counters. Error Code: -2147024809 (The parameter is incorrect.)'. Installation log shows LoadPerfCounterTextStrings() failed with 0x80070057 for HealthService counters.
rootCause: Windows Performance Counter registry corruption prevents the MMA installer from registering HealthService performance counters via LoadPerfCounterTextStrings/lodctr.
solution: Run '%windir%\system32\lodctr.exe /R' and '%windir%\sysWOW64\lodctr.exe /R' from an elevated command prompt to rebuild the performance counter registry, then retry the MMA installation. If this fails, manually register HealthService counters: 1) Download HealthServiceCounters.zip, 2) Ensure registry key HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HealthService\Performance exists, 3) Run 'lodctr.exe HealthServiceCounters.ini'. If manual registration also fails, engage the Windows Performance Counters team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting MMA fails to install with error: Failed to install performance counters (The parameter is incorrect)

symptom: OMS Gateway Log shows Event ID 106, Error level: 'ERROR TcpConnection - Invalid client certificate: CN=Hybrid Runbook Worker, E=azureautomation@microsoft.com, OU=Azure Automation, O=Microsoft Corporation' from a connected agent IP address.
rootCause: The OMS Gateway server does not have the Hybrid Runbook Worker client certificate in its allowed certificate list, causing it to reject connections from agents that present this certificate.
solution: On the OMS Gateway server, run from elevated PowerShell: 'Add-OMSGatewayAllowedClientCertificate -Subject "Hybrid Runbook Worker"' then 'Restart-Service -Name "OMS Gateway"'. If other endpoints also need to be allowed, add each with 'Add-OMSGatewayAllowedHost "<Target_host>"' (wildcards not supported). Verify with 'Get-WinEvent -LogName "OMS Gateway Log" -FilterXPath "*[System[(EventID=105 or EventID=106)]]" | Select Message'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting OMS Gateway Log: ERROR TcpConnection - Invalid client cert

symptom: IIS application pool crashes after deploying MMA downloaded from Azure portal in a SCOM 1801 environment (no SCOM integration configured, direct MMA only).
rootCause: The Application Performance Monitoring (APM) feature in SCOM 2016 Operations Manager Agent causes crashes for IIS Application Pools running under .NET Framework 2.0 runtime. The MMA downloaded from Azure portal does not contain the SCOM agent fixes for this known issue (fixed in SCOM 2016 UR3+), so even the latest MMA version exhibits the bug.
solution: Workaround Option 1: Install the SCOM agent from the SCOM package instead of the MMA from Azure portal. Workaround Option 2: Remove the APM feature by disabling APM discoveries and running Remove-SCOMDisabledClassInstance in the Operations Manager shell (see docs.microsoft.com/troubleshoot/system-center/scom/remove-corrupted-apm-components). Permanent fix: PG plans to merge SCOM agent code into MMA in future versions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Updated IIS application pool crashed in SCOM 1801 with latest MMA version

symptom: MMA installation fails with Error 25211: Failed to install performance counters. Error Code: -2147024809 (The parameter is incorrect) or Error Code: -2147024894 (The system cannot find the file specified)
rootCause: Default permissions on the Security Event Log registry key (HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Eventlog\Security) have been modified, specifically the CustomSD key is interfering with performance counter installation
solution: 1) Backup registry key HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Eventlog\Security 2) Delete the CustomSD key under that path 3) Run regsvr32 scecli.dll from elevated prompt 4) Retry MMA agent installation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting MMA Deployment Error 25211 Failed to install performance counters.. Error Code: -2147024809 (The parameter is incorrect.)

symptom: MMA extension uninstallation fails - extension status shows provisioning failed after uninstall attempt, with leftover artifacts (agent folder under C:\Program Files\Microsoft Monitoring Agent, extension folder under C:\Packages\Plugins, or MMA certificates)
rootCause: Either the Guest Agent did not attempt the uninstall, or the uninstall process failed. If MMA was also managed by SCOM/OMS outside the extension, the extension intentionally skips uninstallation of the agent
solution: 1) Check C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\Plugins\...\MMAExtensionUninstall[N].log 2) If file missing: Guest Agent did not attempt uninstall, escalate to AzureRT/GuestAgent 3) If file exists with managed by customer with SCOM/OMS connection: expected behavior 4) For genuine failures: remove extension from portal, restart WindowsAzureGuestAgent, delete extension folder, run Update-AzVM 5) If still failing, use Remove-AzVMExtension
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting MMA Extension Installation and Uninstallation Issues

symptom: Windows MMA Agent stops uploading heartbeats after 1-4 days. Restarting Microsoft Monitoring Agent service temporarily mitigates the issue but it reoccurs after the same period
rootCause: PowerShell transcription is enabled to C:\Windows\System32\LogFiles\PowerShell\ but CREATOR OWNER is denied permission to the folder. SYSTEM account (used by MMA) fails to create date-based subfolders, causing repeated UnauthorizedAccessException errors that exhaust the Worker Thread Pool
solution: 1) Check PowerShell transcription setting via registry HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\Transcription\EnableTranscripting 2) Check GPO via gpresult /h 3) Verify CREATOR OWNER permissions on C:\WINDOWS\SYSTEM32\LOGFILES\POWERSHELL 4) Fix: Allow CREATOR OWNER and SYSTEM Full Control on that folder via GPO, or disable the Deny policy 5) Restart Microsoft Monitoring Agent service 6) Verify heartbeats resume
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting MMA Missing Heartbeats caused by SYSTEM permission issue to PowerShell Transcription folder

symptom: MMA agent logs error Keyset does not exist (0x80090016) in event viewer or agent logs, or DecryptSecureData Unable to acquire certificate private key context: -2146893802 (NTE_BAD_KEYSET)
rootCause: Stale or corrupted MMA certificates stored in the Local Machine certificate store under Microsoft Monitoring Agent have lost their private key associations
solution: 1) Stop Microsoft Monitoring Agent service 2) Open mmc.exe, add Certificates snap-in for Local Machine 3) Navigate to Certificates > Microsoft Monitoring Agent > Certificates 4) Delete all certificates in the middle pane 5) Restart Microsoft Monitoring Agent service without closing the cert window 6) Wait 1 minute and press F5 to verify new certs are regenerated 7) Monitor agent logs and LA workspace for resolution
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting MMA Old Cert Cleanup Keyset does not exist(0x80090016)

symptom: MMA agent proxy settings are erased/cleared once the agent is connected to a SCOM Management Group - proxy configuration disappears from MMA properties
rootCause: SCOM Management Group is configured to distribute or override proxy settings to MMA agents. When MMA is multi-homed (SCOM + Log Analytics), SCOM can push blank or different proxy settings that overwrite the manually configured proxy
solution: 1) Confirm MMA is integrated with SCOM 2) Check if SCOM is integrated with a Log Analytics workspace 3) Verify which MMA agents SCOM allows to connect 4) Check if SCOM is distributing proxy settings to MMA via workflow rules 5) Remove the SCOM proxy distribution configuration to allow MMA agents to retain their manually defined proxy settings 6) If removing SCOM integration resolves it but customer needs both, escalate to SCOM support per support boundaries
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting MMA Proxy Settings Erase Issue

symptom: MMA on legacy OS (Windows 7 SP1, Server 2008 R2 SP1, Server 2008 SP2) stops reporting heartbeats and data. Operations Manager event log shows Event ID 6000: Verifying authenticode signature for downloaded management pack failed with 0x800B0109 (certificate chain not trusted)
rootCause: MMA Management Packs changed from dual-signed (SHA-1 + SHA-2) to single-signed SHA-2 cab files. Legacy OS versions lack SHA-2 code signing support, causing signature verification failure for all downloaded management packs
solution: 1) Install latest MMA build (10.20.18029.0 or later) 2) Install Windows patches for SHA-2 signing support on the legacy OS per KB4472027 3) Verify SHA-2 support by checking Digital Signatures tab of MP files - should show sha256 as Digest algorithm instead of Unknown
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Microsoft Monitoring Agent (MMA) for Windows/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting MMA SHA-2 signing enforcement on legacy OS

symptom: OMSAgentForLinux extension removed via Portal/CLI keeps reappearing automatically on the VM because Security Center monitoring is enabled for the subscription
rootCause: Security Center auto provisioning is enabled for the subscription, which automatically reinstalls the OMS agent extension on VMs after manual removal
solution: Disable Auto Provisioning in Microsoft Defender for Cloud: 1) Navigate to Defender for Cloud > Management > Environment Settings 2) Select the subscription 3) Select Auto provisioning 4) Set Auto Provisioning to Off for Log Analytics agent / Azure Monitor agent
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/How-To/How-to: Disable OMS agent in Security Center Auto Provisioning

symptom: Cloned Linux VMs with OMS Agent all have the same SourceComputerId (Agent ID), causing issues in Azure Automation Update Management and Azure Security Center
rootCause: Cloning a VM with OMS Agent for Linux installed and configured copies the SourceComputerId from the original image, resulting in duplicate agent IDs across all cloned machines
solution: 1) Offboard agent: sudo /opt/microsoft/omsagent/bin/omsadmin.sh -X 2) If 'No such file or directory' error, manually create /var/opt/microsoft/omsagent and /etc/opt/microsoft/omsagent folders 3) Re-onboard with workspace credentials: sudo /opt/microsoft/omsagent/bin/omsadmin.sh -w <WS_ID> -s <WS_KEY> 4) If Azure Automation Hybrid Worker, re-register the worker in User worker groups
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/How-To/How-to: Regenerate the SourceComputerId (Agent ID) on the Linux Agent

symptom: Defunct (zombie) omsagent processes appear on Linux VM, consuming CPU. Output of 'ps aux' shows omsagent processes in Z (zombie) state.
rootCause: Bug in fluentd in_exec plugin in OMS Agent version 1.12 that fails to properly reap child processes after execution.
solution: 1) Download fixed in_exec.rb from GitHub gist (abenbachir/8b9bb6039836b6dd0097684cf1c05199) 2) Copy to /opt/microsoft/omsagent/ruby/lib/ruby/gems/2.6.0/gems/fluentd-0.12.40/lib/fluent/plugin/ 3) Restart agent: sudo /opt/microsoft/omsagent/bin/service_control restart. Permanent fix in agent version 1.13+.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Defunt (zombie) "omsagent" processes

symptom: OMS Agent for Linux enable/install fails with exit code 52 'Couldn't create Marker File'. Error appears in extension.log.
rootCause: Broken symlinks in /etc/opt/microsoft/omsagent (certs and conf directories) pointing to non-existent workspace directory, left over from previous installation.
solution: 1) Check symlinks: ls -la /etc/opt/microsoft/omsagent 2) Remove broken symlinks: rm /etc/opt/microsoft/omsagent/certs && rm /etc/opt/microsoft/omsagent/conf 3) Reinstall the OMS extension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Enable fails with exit code 52 "Couldn't create Marker File"

symptom: OMS Agent for Linux enable/install fails with exit code 52 'Couldn't create Marker File' on hardened Linux machine.
rootCause: SELinux hardening (enforcing mode) on the Linux machine prevents the OMS agent from creating required marker files during installation.
solution: 1) Check SELinux status: sestatus 2) If SELinux is enforcing, temporarily set to permissive or configure appropriate SELinux policies for OMS agent directories. 3) Retry agent installation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Enable fails with exit code 52 "Couldn't create Marker File"

symptom: OMS Agent for Linux fails to connect to OMS/ODS endpoints. omsagent.log shows 'SSL_connect returned=1 errno=0 state=error: certificate verify failed (unable to get local issuer certificate)'.
rootCause: Azure TLS certificate changes - the Baltimore CyberTrust Root certificate is missing or not trusted in the local certificate store on the Linux machine.
solution: 1) Download Baltimore cert: wget https://cacerts.digicert.com/BaltimoreCyberTrustRoot.crt 2) sudo cp BaltimoreCyberTrustRoot.crt /etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted/openssl/ 3) chmod 444 the cert 4) Create symlink in /etc/pki/tls/certs/ pointing to the cert 5) Restart OMS agent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting OMS Baltimore Certificate Trust Issue

symptom: omiagent process crashes with segfault in libnss_dns. Kernel log (dmesg) shows 'omiagent: segfault at ... in libnss_dns-2.17.so'. High memory consumption by OMI/SCX subcomponents.
rootCause: Bug in DSC (Desired State Configuration) extension for Linux causing omiagent memory corruption and segfault.
solution: 1) Verify segfault in dmesg matches libnss_dns pattern 2) Download latest DSC release: wget https://github.com/microsoft/PowerShell-DSC-for-Linux/releases/download/v1.2.1-0/dsc-1.2.1-0.ssl_100.x64.rpm 3) Upgrade: yum upgrade dsc-1.2.1-0.ssl_100.x64.rpm
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Issues related to high CPU or Memory consumption by OMS Agent or its subcomponents

symptom: OMS Agent installation/upgrade/removal fails with error: %pre(omi-1.6.3-0.x86_64) scriptlet failed, exit status 127 in extension.log
rootCause: SELinux is enabled and set to Enforcing mode, which blocks the OMI package scriptlet execution during OMS agent install/upgrade/removal
solution: 1) Set SELinux to Permissive mode: sudo setenforce 0  2) Retry the OMS agent installation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting OMS Scriptlet Failed Error 127

symptom: Removal of OMSAgentForLinux extension fails with error: /opt/microsoft/omsagent/bin/service_control does not exist. Disable cannot be called before install
rootCause: Certain versions of OMSAgentForLinux extension are partially removed during upgrade. Dependency requirements of LinuxDiagnostic and DSCForLinux extensions prevent complete removal, blocking new version installation
solution: 1) Download latest OMS shell bundle from https://github.com/microsoft/OMS-Agent-for-Linux/releases  2) Run with --install --force to override package dependencies: chmod 500 omsagent-*.sh && ./omsagent-*.sh --install --force  3) Once installed, remove extension via portal/CLI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting OMS Service Control Script Does Not Exist

symptom: OMS Agent not collecting logs for enabled data sources (Perf, Syslogs) on Linux distros with Python3 as default. dsc_host segfault errors in /var/log/messages. Affected OS: RHEL 8, CentOS 8, Oracle 8, Ubuntu 20.04, SLES 15 SP1+
rootCause: OMS agent plugins (nxOMSWLIResource, nxAutomationWorker) are not upgraded for Python 3. DSC engine crashes when applying solutions because it cannot locate correct binaries for Python3
solution: 1) Install Python2: sudo dnf install python2 -y  2) Alias Python to Python2: sudo alternatives --set python /usr/bin/python2  3) Reinstall omsagent - OMS and DSC will use Python2 by default even if Python3 is present
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting OmsAgent plugins issue for distros running Python3

symptom: OMS Agent installation fails with exit code 52 (missing dependencies) when VM has both rpm and dpkg package managers installed. Shell bundle exits with code 19. Logs show it tries to unpack .deb packages on CentOS/RPM-based systems
rootCause: Bug in OMS agent installer: when both rpm and dpkg package managers exist on the VM, the installer incorrectly tries to use dpkg to unpack .deb packages on RPM-based distros, causing dependency resolution failure. Fixed in OMS 1.13.27+
solution: Upgrade to OMS Agent version 1.13.27 or later which contains the fix for this bug (PG work item: https://msazure.visualstudio.com/One/_workitems/edit/7248723)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Omsagent installation fails if we have both the package managers i.e. rpm & dpkg on the VM

symptom: Syslogs are dropped by ingestion pipeline after being sent by OMS agent. Roughly 10% of syslogs missing. No errors/warnings on the agent. Missing syslogs exist in /var/log/messages but not in Log Analytics workspace
rootCause: Bug in the Log Analytics ingestion pipeline causing a percentage of syslogs to be discarded during ingestion (server-side issue, not agent-side)
solution: To confirm drops: 1) Download omsagent-compute-metrics-from-log.sh and omsagent-loadtest.py from GitHub  2) Set omsagent.conf log level to debug, restart OMS agent  3) Stop syslog daemon, create clean baseline  4) Start syslog + load generator: python omsagent-loadtest.py --run-time 360 --eps 5000 --no-profiling --plugins syslog --syslog-protocol udp  5) Periodically run log detection script and compare agent vs workspace counts  6) If counts diverge, confirmed pipeline drop - escalate to PG
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Syslogs are dropped during ingestion

symptom: OMS agent process is running but no data is being collected and no additional logs are output to omsagent.log. Agent appears hung/unresponsive
rootCause: OMI agent process is hung (root cause unknown). OMS agent queries OMI for performance counter data; if perf counter collection is enabled, OMI hang causes OMS agent to block waiting for query results. Single point of failure in the OMS-OMI interface code
solution: Restart OMI agent: sudo /opt/omi/bin/service_control restart. Note: this is a known architectural flaw with no permanent fix - customer should migrate to Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) for long-term resolution
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting omsagent Hang Issue

symptom: Defunct (zombie) omsagent processes appear on Linux VMs, potentially impacting CPU. Caused by bug in fluentd in_exec plugin in OMS Agent version 1.12.
rootCause: Bug in the fluentd in_exec plugin of OMS Agent version 1.12 that fails to properly reap child processes, creating zombie entries in the process table.
solution: Upgrade to OMS Agent v1.13+. Workaround: download patched in_exec.rb from GitHub (PR #1133) and copy to /opt/microsoft/omsagent/ruby/lib/ruby/gems/2.6.0/gems/fluentd-0.12.40/lib/fluent/plugin/, then restart agent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Known Issues/Defunt (zombie) "omsagent" processes

symptom: Syslog messages using RFC5424 format are dropped by OMS Linux agent. Errors in omsagent.log: 'invalid time format' and 'pattern not match' for RFC5424 formatted syslog entries.
rootCause: The fluentd v0.12.40 bundled with OMS agent uses an outdated regex for RFC5424 parsing (REGEXP_RFC5424_WITH_PRI) that cannot parse the RFC5424 timestamp format correctly.
solution: Option 1 (recommended): Migrate to AMA which does not use fluentd/regex. Option 2 (workaround): Modify syslog.conf to use message_format=auto AND replace REGEXP_RFC5424_WITH_PRI regex in parser.rb with updated pattern from fluentd docs. Workaround is customer-maintained.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Known Issues/KI: syslog with RFC5424 is dropped

symptom: OMI vulnerability CVE-2021-38647 allows local privilege escalation on Linux VMs with OMS agent. Remote execution NOT possible with OMS-bundled OMI (no network ports exposed).
rootCause: OMI component bundled with OMS agent contains CVE-2021-38647. OMS-bundled OMI only listens on local domain sockets, so only local privilege escalation is exploitable (requires machine access).
solution: Upgrade to OMS Agent v1.13.40+. Verify: 'sudo netstat -tulpn | grep omi' should return nothing. Check version: 'sudo /opt/omi/bin/omiserver --version'. Query workspace: Heartbeat | where Version < '1.13.40'. AKS not affected unless using legacy container monitoring.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Known Issues/OMSLinux hotfix for OMI vulnerability

symptom: No logs collected (Perf, Syslogs) on Linux distros with Python3 default (RHEL 8, CentOS 8, Oracle 8, Ubuntu 20.04, SLES 15 SP1+). dsc_host segfault errors in /var/log/messages. DSC errors: 'Native provider failed to run SET operation'.
rootCause: OMS agent plugins (nxOMSWLIResource, nxAutomationWorker) not upgraded for Python 3. DSC engine cannot locate correct binaries on Python3-default distros, causing dsc_host crash.
solution: Install Python2 and set default: 'sudo dnf install python2 -y && sudo alternatives --set python /usr/bin/python2'. Then purge and reinstall omsagent. Agent/DSC will use Python2 even if Python3 is present.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Known Issues/OmsAgent plugins issue for distros running Python3

symptom: OMS agent process running but no data collected and no logs output to omsagent.log. Agent appears hung/unresponsive.
rootCause: OMI agent process hangs (root cause unknown). OMS agent queries OMI for perf counters; if perf collection enabled, OMI hang causes OMS to hang. Known flaw 4+ years, no fix planned as AMA replaces OMS.
solution: Restart OMI: 'sudo /opt/omi/bin/service_control restart'. No permanent fix planned. Migrate to AMA (Azure Monitor Agent).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Known Issues/Omsagent hang issue

symptom: OMS agent installation fails (exit code 52) on Linux VMs with both rpm and dpkg package managers. Extension.log shows 'Shell bundle exiting with code 19' and attempts to unpack .deb on RPM-based distros.
rootCause: Bug in OMS agent installer: when both rpm and dpkg present, installer incorrectly selects dpkg and tries to install .deb packages on RPM-based systems, causing dependency failures.
solution: Upgrade to OMS Agent v1.13.27+ which contains the fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Known Issues/Omsagent installation fails if we have both the package managers i.e. rpm & dpkg on the VM

symptom: Perf entries for deleted/non-existent Logical Disks on Linux with Docker. Excessive Perf log volume. SCX logs: 'statvfs() failed for /var/lib/docker/devicemapper/mnt/...; errno = 2'.
rootCause: Unsupported Docker storage driver (devicemapper) used. Only overlay2 is supported for SCX disk metrics.
solution: Switch Docker storage driver to overlay2, then restart SCX: 'scxadmin -restart'. Both steps required - switching driver alone does not clear stale disk entries.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Known Issues/Perf log entries for non-existent Logical Disks (Docker)

symptom: Large amount of syslogs missing from workspace. High Recv-Q on port 25224. Errors: 'Unable to resolve the IP' and 'Failed to get the IP' in omsagent.log.
rootCause: Known omsagent limitation: DNS resolution failures cause syslog messages to be dropped. The syslog filter attempts IP resolution and drops messages on DNS lookup failure.
solution: Migrate to AMA which has planned DNS failure handling. Workaround (non-production only): modify filter_syslog.rb and filter_syslog_security.rb to bypass DNS resolution.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Known Issues/Syslogs are dropping due to DNS Issues

symptom: OMS agent Ruby crashes with segfault when Cylance anti-virus installed. GDB: 'SIGSEGV in stacklookup_check() from cymemdeflinux_x64.so'.
rootCause: Cylance and Jemalloc (OMS agent memory allocator since v1.11) incompatible. Cylance memory defense library loaded first interferes with Jemalloc init.
solution: Upgrade to OMS Agent v1.12+ which disables Jemalloc at install time, resolving the conflict.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Required permissions for OS Hardening Customizations - Linux Agent

symptom: OMS Linux agent not working after OS hardening changed directory permissions on /var, /var/log, /etc, or /tmp.
rootCause: Hardening scripts changed required directory permissions. OMS agent requires: 755 /var, 775 /var/log, 775 /etc, 775 /tmp.
solution: Restore permissions: chmod 755 /var; chmod 775 /var/log; chmod 775 /etc; chmod 775 /tmp.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Required permissions for OS Hardening Customizations - Linux Agent

symptom: OMS agent error: 'ThreadError: can't create Thread: Resource temporarily unavailable' in omsagent.log.
rootCause: Security limits.conf sets omsagent hard nproc too low (e.g., 75). OMS agent needs ~50 threads alone, plus DSC/OMI/NPM.
solution: Increase nproc in /etc/security/limits.conf: 'omsagent hard nproc 200'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Required permissions for OS Hardening Customizations - Linux Agent

symptom: Only Heartbeat collected, Perf/Syslog missing. /var/log/cron: 'crond: (omsagent) FAILED to authorize user with PAM (Permission denied)'.
rootCause: access.conf denies all access ('- : ALL : ALL') except root/admin, blocking crond from running OMSConsistencyInvoker as omsagent. DSC cannot apply workspace config.
solution: Add rule in /etc/security/access.conf before deny-all: '+ : omsagent : ALL'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Required permissions for OS Hardening Customizations - Linux Agent

symptom: OMS agent Ruby crashes or fails to load gems when RVM (Ruby Version Manager) installed. OMS Ruby uses RVM GEM paths instead of bundled gems.
rootCause: RVM sets GEM_HOME/GEM_PATH env vars pointing to /usr/local/rvm/gems/ which override OMS bundled Ruby gem paths.
solution: Remove RVM from PATH: 'mv /etc/profile.d/rvm.sh /etc/profile.d/rvm.sh.bak' and re-login. For permanent fix, remove RVM entirely.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Required permissions for OS Hardening Customizations - Linux Agent

symptom: OMS agent error: 'libssl.so.1.1: cannot open shared object file - openssl.so (LoadError)'.
rootCause: Custom OpenSSL installed in non-standard path set in system PATH. OMS Ruby looks for libssl.so.1.1 in custom path where it is inaccessible.
solution: Ensure system OpenSSL libraries are at standard paths or fix LD_LIBRARY_PATH. Remove custom OpenSSL from PATH for omsagent context.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/OMS Agent for Linux (omsagent)/Troubleshooting Guides/Required permissions for OS Hardening Customizations - Linux Agent

symptom: On-Demand Assessment not submitting data due to connectivity issues with OMS
rootCause: Computer unable to establish connection to OMS workspace; Event Viewer shows connection/upload errors under Operations Manager
solution: 1) Check if computer ever submitted data by querying workspace for assessment data source 2) Open Event Viewer and check for errors related to connection or data upload to OMS within Operations Manager logs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/On-Demand Assessments/Connectivity Issues

symptom: On-Demand Assessment data not getting uploaded to OMS portal on Free tier workspace
rootCause: Free OMS workspace has a daily limit of 500 MB which has been reached, blocking further data ingestion
solution: Run query 'Operation | where OperationCategory == "Data Collection Status"' on customer workspace to validate if the 500 MB daily limit has been reached; if no output, limit not yet reached — investigate other causes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/On-Demand Assessments/Free tier limit reached

symptom: On-Demand Assessment stuck in 'Performing assessment' status or customer does not want to wait 7 days for next scheduled run after failure
rootCause: Assessment solution is scheduled to run every 7 days; the LastExecuted registry value prevents immediate re-run
solution: 1) Navigate to HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HealthService\Parameters\Management Groups\AOI-[WorkspaceId]\Solutions\{Assessment} 2) Update the LastExecuted string key to 7+ days back (e.g. 2017-02-10T01:13:54.2760580Z) 3) Restart HealthService via net stop/start healthservice. Not recommended on Production servers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/On-Demand Assessments/How to: Force on OnDemand Assessment

symptom: On-Demand Assessment solutions (SQL/AD/AD Replication/SCOM) not uploading data to OMS workspace
rootCause: Prerequisites not met: SQL Assessment requires agent on SQL Server + .NET 4; AD Assessment requires agent on domain controllers + .NET 4.5.2+; AD Replication requires agent on DCs or member servers + .NET 4.5.2+
solution: Verify prerequisites for the specific solution type: 1) SQL Assessment — agent installed on SQL Server machine + .NET Framework 4 2) AD Assessment — agent on domain controllers + .NET 4.5.2+ 3) AD Replication — agent on DCs or member servers + .NET 4.5.2+. Query customer workspace for data source (e.g. ADAssessment) to validate upload.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/On-Demand Assessments/Pre-requisite Failure

symptom: SCOM console throws 'System.ServiceModel.CommunicationException: An error occurred while making the HTTP request to https://service.systemcenteradvisor.com' when connecting to Log Analytics Connections Tab. May also occur when onboarding a non-SCOM-integration-supported workspace.
rootCause: TLS/SSL configuration issue - the .NET Framework is not configured to use strong cryptography (TLS 1.2), causing HTTPS connection failures to the System Center Advisor service endpoint.
solution: 1) Open Registry Editor 2) Navigate to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework4.0.30319 and create DWORD 'SchUseStrongCrypto' = 1 3) Navigate to HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\.NETFramework4.0.30319 and create same DWORD 'SchUseStrongCrypto' = 1 4) Restart the system. If this is the entire issue, consider transferring to SCOM Support team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/System Center Operations Manager/Error "An error occurred while making the HTTP request" in SCOM console

symptom: MMA agent proxy settings configured manually on the agent are not working as expected - agent still uses different proxy settings when SCOM Management Group is integrated.
rootCause: When MMA agent is integrated with SCOM Management Group, the proxy settings configured in the SCOM console override any manual proxy configuration set directly on the MMA agent.
solution: 1) Check SCOM console for proxy configuration (Administration > Settings > System Center Operations Manager > Proxy Settings) 2) If SCOM manages proxy, update proxy settings in SCOM console instead of directly on MMA agent 3) The SCOM-managed proxy setting will always take precedence over manual MMA agent proxy configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/System Center Operations Manager/How to find out SCOM distributing Proxy settings to MMA

symptom: Customer has Free Health Check Assessment solutions (Active Directory Health Check, AD Replication, SQL & SCOM Health Check) enabled in Azure Log Analytics workspace - solutions no longer functioning or supported
rootCause: Free Health Check Assessment solutions via MMA (Microsoft Monitoring Agent) are deprecated and no longer supported. Must be replaced with On-Demand Assessment solutions using AMA (Azure Monitor Agent)
solution: 1) Inform customer that Free Health Check solutions are deprecated and must be migrated to On-Demand Assessment (ODA) solutions using AMA 2) For migration assistance and ODA setup, direct customer to Services Hub Support (email-based support only) 3) For case transfer: set Product Family=Cloud and Online Services, Product=Services Hub 4) Note: Azure Monitor team handles MMA agent connectivity and data upload; Services Hub team handles assessment prerequisites, execution issues, and results troubleshooting
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Agents/Support Boundaries/Support Boundaries: Free Health Check (Active Directory Health Check, AD Replication, SQL & SCOM Health Check

symptom: Fluent-bit on AMA Linux reports Too many open files error in debug log (errno=24). Error only appears when debug logging is enabled.
rootCause: Root user open file descriptor soft limit (ulimit -Sn) is too low for the number of files fluent-bit needs to monitor. Affects fluent-bit versions 1.3.11 and 1.9.6 shipped with AMA.
solution: 1) Check current open files count: lsof -u root | wc -l. 2) Check current limit: ulimit -Sn. 3) Permanent fix: add root soft nofile 10000 to /etc/security/limits.conf and restart system. 4) Temporary fix: prlimit -p $(pidof fluent-bit) --nofile=10000:262144. 5) Verify with: lsof -p $(pidof fluent-bit) | wc -l.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Linux/How-To/AMA Linux: HT: Fluentbit - Set log level (debug, error, warn, info, off)
Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Linux/How-To/AMA Linux: HT: Purge AMA
symptom: AMA Linux extension stuck in transitioning state and cannot be reinstalled through conventional means; or cloned VM with residual agent metadata causing conflicts with deployment or monitoring services
rootCause: Residual or corrupted AMA agent components, cached configurations, or metadata from a previous installation or VM cloning persist on the system, preventing clean reinstallation of the AMA extension
solution: Perform a complete AMA purge: (1) Stop all azuremonitoragent services (azuremonitoragent, azuremonitoragentmgr, azuremonitor-coreagent, azuremonitor-kqlextension, azuremonitor-agentlauncher, metrics-extension), (2) Remove azuremonitoragent package via apt/yum, (3) Delete cached folders (/var/lib/dpkg/info/azuremonitoragent*, /etc/default/azuremonitoragent, /etc/opt/microsoft/azuremonitoragent, /var/opt/microsoft/azuremonitoragent, /opt/microsoft/azuremonitoragent, /var/lib/waagent/*AzureMonitorLinuxAgent*), (4) Restart guest agent (walinuxagent/waagent), (5) Reinstall via DCR

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Linux/How-To/AMA Linux: HT: Review mdsd.warn
symptom: AMA Linux mdsd.warn shows 'Throttling ingestion for <facility>.<severity>' and 'Input data burst ingestion rate exceeded recommended limits of 20000 events per second' warnings; data ingestion may be temporarily paused
rootCause: Incoming syslog event rate exceeds the mdsd LocalSink burst limit of 20000 EPS (recommended sustained rate is 10000 EPS). The LocalSink pauses ingestion for the remainder of the current second to protect the agent from resource exhaustion
solution: Throttling alone does not imply data loss due to upstream buffering (TCP/UDP Recv-Q, Rsyslog action/main queues). To mitigate: (1) Distribute ingestion across multiple syslog forwarders using a load balancer, (2) Reduce log verbosity at the source by filtering facility/severity/message volume. Monitor for sustained throttling which may indicate buffering capacity is insufficient

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Linux/How-To/AMA Linux: HT: Review mdsd.warn
symptom: AMA Linux mdsd.warn shows '[GC] Disk usage exceeds disk quota' and '[GC] Dropped N events from sink' warnings; oldest unsent events are being deleted causing confirmed data loss
rootCause: Agent's local cache files exceed the MaxDiskQuotaInMB limit (default 10 GB). Mdsd triggers garbage collection deleting oldest unsent events until disk usage falls below 90% of quota. Common upstream causes: required endpoints blocked by firewall/proxy, slow/degraded network connectivity, Azure ingestion service degradation, or sustained high ingestion rates
solution: Increase MaxDiskQuotaInMB via agent configuration parameter (see 'Supported Parameters' in Azure Monitor Agent docs). Resolve downstream connectivity issues: verify firewall/proxy rules allow required endpoints, check network performance. Unlike throttling, GC always implies data loss - treat repeated GC events as high-severity. Reduce ingestion volume if persistent

symptom: AMA Linux fails to upload data to Azure Monitor endpoints using default cpprestsdk HTTP client - data transmission errors or timeouts
rootCause: The default cpprestsdk HTTP client used by AMA Linux has known issues causing upload failures (IcM 608384367); libcurl is the planned future default
solution: Switch AMA HTTP client to libcurl: add 'export ENABLE_CURL_UPLOAD="true"' to /etc/default/azuremonitoragent, set CURL_CA_BUNDLE to match SSL_CERT_FILE value, then restart AMA (systemctl restart azuremonitoragent). Note: change reverts after reboot unless you also create a systemd override at /etc/systemd/system/azuremonitoragent.service.d/override.conf. Change also reverts after agent upgrade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Linux/How-To/AMA Linux: HT: Switch http client from cpprestsdk to libcurl

symptom: AMA Linux cannot connect to IMDS (169.254.169.254) - curl returns connection refused when run as syslog user but works as root
rootCause: Firewall iptables rule filters outbound traffic to IMDS by user ID (e.g., only allowing uid 0/root), blocking the syslog user that AMA runs as
solution: Check iptables OUTPUT rules: 'iptables -L OUTPUT -v -n --line-numbers'. Remove the restrictive rule: 'sudo iptables -D OUTPUT -d 169.254.169.254/32 -m owner ! --uid-owner 0 -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable'. Verify AMA can reach IMDS as syslog user: 'sudo -u syslog curl -v http://169.254.169.254/metadata/instance?api-version=2019-11-01'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Linux/How-To/AMA Linux: HT: Test connectivity to IMDS

symptom: AMA Linux TLS handshake fails when connecting to Azure Monitor endpoints (global.handler.control.monitor.azure.com, ODS, DCE) - connection reset by server during SSL handshake
rootCause: Client system is using TLS 1.0 to establish the TLS session, which is deprecated and not enabled on Azure Monitor endpoints; the endpoint correctly resets the connection to prevent an insecure session
solution: 1) Verify TLS handshake: 'curl -v -s -S -k https://global.handler.control.monitor.azure.com/ping' - check which TLS version is negotiated. 2) Update system TLS configuration to enforce TLS 1.2 or higher. 3) Verify DNS resolution first with nslookup for all required endpoints. 4) Check mdsd.err for errors like 'Could not obtain configuration from https://global.handler.control.monitor.azure.com'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Linux/How-To/AMA Linux: HT: Test connectivity to endpoints

symptom: AMA Linux heartbeat missing from Log Analytics Workspace - extension installed, processes running (agentlauncher, amacoreagent, mdsd), configchunks present, but no Heartbeat events in LAW
rootCause: mdsd process is running but not actively logging heartbeats (zombie process state) - process appears running but is not functional; may also be caused by agent config not matching expected DCR destination or errors in mdsd.err
solution: Follow 5-step TSG: 1) Verify heartbeat is actually missing in LAW. 2) Check agent instruction set matches expected heartbeat destination (review associated DCRs). 3) Check mdsd.err for known issues. 4) Check mdsd.info for local heartbeat logging - if mdsd running but not logging heartbeats (zombie), upgrade to latest AMA version. 5) Check Azure ingestion pipeline for HEALTH_STATUS_BLOB in ProcessedChunks - if present but no heartbeat in LAW, escalate to PG. If not present, capture network trace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/AMA Linux: TSG:  Collection - Heartbeat

symptom: Windows performance counters not reporting data; typeperf returns 'Error: No valid counters' for specific counter providers (e.g., LogicalDisk, PerfDisk)
rootCause: 'Disable Performance Counters' DWORD registry key set to 1 under HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\<serviceName>\Performance, preventing the OS from generating performance counter data for that service
solution: Set the 'Disable Performance Counters' registry value to 0 under the affected service's Performance registry path (e.g., HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\PerfDisk\Performance). No reboot required. Verify with 'typeperf -q | find "<CounterName>"' and 'typeperf -sc 5 "\LogicalDisk(*)\% Free Space"' to confirm counters are generating data. Use PowerShell script to scan all services: Get-ChildItem -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services' -Recurse | Get-ItemProperty -Name 'Disable Performance Counters' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Windows/How-To/AMA Windows: HT: Manage "Disable Performance Counters"

symptom: TLS/SSL handshake failure when Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) or other Windows services attempt to connect to Azure endpoints; connection rejected due to cipher suite mismatch
rootCause: Required TLS cipher suites not present in the client's offered cipher suite list, due to restrictive GPO policy (HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Cryptography\Configuration\SSL�10002\Functions) or manual registry configuration (HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Cryptography\Configuration\Local\SSL�10002\Functions). GPO-based policy takes precedence over manual registry settings when both are defined.
solution: 1) Check configured cipher suites: Get-TlsCipherSuite or inspect registry keys under both SYSTEM and SOFTWARE\Policies paths. 2) Identify which method is active (GPO vs manual) - if SOFTWARE\Policies path exists, it takes precedence. 3) Ensure the server-required cipher suites are included in the configuration. 4) After changes, run 'gpupdate /force' from elevated command prompt. 5) Use PowerShell to verify: '(Get-TlsCipherSuite).Name' to list active suites. Reference OS-specific supported ciphers at learn.microsoft.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Windows/How-To/AMA Windows: HT: Manage SSL Cipher Suites

symptom: Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) Windows extension stuck in transitioning state and cannot be reinstalled; or cloned VM has residual AMA metadata causing deployment conflicts preventing AMA installation
rootCause: Residual AMA components (running processes, plugin folders, registry keys) from a previous failed installation or VM cloning interfere with clean reinstallation of the agent
solution: Perform full AMA purge (last resort): 1) Remove AMA extension from VM via Azure portal. 2) Stop all AMA processes: MonAgentLauncher, MonAgentHost, MonAgentManager, MonAgentCore, AMAExtHealthMonitor, fluent-bit, MetricsExtension.Native, KqlExtension. 3) Delete folders: C:\Packages\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Monitor.AzureMonitorWindowsAgent, C:\Resources\Directory\AMADataStore.<SERVERNAME>, C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Monitor.AzureMonitorWindowsAgent, C:\WindowsAzure\Resources\AMADataStore.<SERVERNAME> (Arc servers). 4) Delete registry keys: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\AzureMonitorAgent, HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\HandlerState\Microsoft.Azure.Monitor.AzureMonitorWindowsAgent_<version>. 5) Restart-Service RdAgent and WindowsAzureGuestAgent. 6) Reinstall AMA by re-adding VM to DCR. PowerShell script available for steps 2-5 via VM Run Command.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Windows/How-To/AMA Windows: HT: Purge AMA

symptom: Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) fails normal operations on Azure VM; AMA incorrectly identifies VM as Arc-connected machine instead of native Azure VM; AMA uses wrong IMDS endpoint
rootCause: Azure Arc Connected Machine Agent (azcmagent) installed on an Azure VM creates conflicting environment variables (IDENTITY_ENDPOINT, IMDS_ENDPOINT, MSFT_ARC_TEST, AzureMonAgentClientLocation) and HIMDS service (Azure Hybrid Instance Metadata Service) that cause AMA to use Arc identity path instead of native Azure IMDS
solution: 1) Remove all Arc extensions first. 2) Uninstall Arc agent per docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-arc/servers/manage-agent. 3) Remove residual folders: C:\Resources\Directory\AMADatastore.*, C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Monitor.AzureMonitorWindowsAgent, C:\WindowsAzure\Resources\AMADataStore.*, C:\Packages\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Monitor.AzureMonitorWindowsAgent. 4) Remove Arc registry env vars from HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Environment: AzureMonAgentClientLocation, MSFT_ARC_TEST, IDENTITY_ENDPOINT, IMDS_ENDPOINT. 5) Ensure HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\VMType = 'IAAS' (REG_SZ). 6) Remove HIMDS service: 'sc delete himds'. 7) Reboot server. 8) Verify Windows Azure Guest Agent is running. 9) Reinstall AMA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Windows/How-To/AMA Windows: HT: Remove ARC from Azure Virtual Machine
Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/AMA Windows/How-To/AMA Windows: HT: Test connectivity to endpoints
symptom: AMA Windows data upload fails. MAQosEvent.csv shows Success=FALSE. MAEventTable.csv shows 'WinHttpSendRequest: 12029: A connection with the server could not be established' and 'ODS chunk transmit failed to workspace ID'.
rootCause: Network connectivity issue preventing AMA from reaching required endpoints. Common causes: DNS resolution failure for AMA endpoints (global.handler.control.monitor.azure.com, *.ods.opinsights.azure.com), firewall/NSG blocking outbound HTTPS (443), TLS handshake failure, or misconfigured proxy server.
solution: 1) Verify DNS resolution: nslookup global.handler.control.monitor.azure.com, nslookup {workspaceId}.ods.opinsights.azure.com. 2) Test TCP+TLS connectivity: curl -v -k https://global.handler.control.monitor.azure.com/ping (expect 200). For ODS endpoints, expect 404 but confirms connectivity. 3) If AMPLS associated: verify private DNS resolves to private IPs. 4) If proxy configured: curl -v https://endpoint/ping -x {proxyIP}:{port}. 5) If DCE associated: also test DCE handler and ingest endpoints. 6) Allow required endpoints per https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/agents/azure-monitor-agent-network-configuration.

symptom: Computer and FilePath columns are missing from Custom Log table (e.g. MSRepro_CL) when using AMA agent to collect custom logs, preventing identification of which machine and file path produced each log entry
rootCause: The Custom Log table schema does not include Computer and FilePath columns, and the DCR streamDeclarations section does not declare these fields. Requires AMA Windows >= 1.29 or AMA Linux >= 1.32.6
solution: 1) Go to LAW > Tables > find Custom Log table > Edit Schema > Add Column "Computer" (String) and "FilePath" (String) > Save. 2) Go to Data Collection Rules > Export Template > Deploy > Edit Template > add Computer and FilePath to streamDeclarations columns array > Create. For new deployments, include these columns from the start in both table schema and DCR template
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/How-To/AMA: HT: Add Computer and FilePath fields to AMA Agent Custom Log table

symptom: ASC Test Connectivity diagnostic shows "Traffic ALLOWED" for Azure VM outbound connection, but actual traffic is blocked; network trace shows TCP handshake failure to the destination endpoint
rootCause: VM NIC is part of an internal load balancer and has a user-defined route (UDR) with destination InternetGateway. Per Azure Load Balancer outbound rules Scenario 5, this combination prevents outbound connectivity
solution: Review Azure Load Balancer outbound rules documentation (Scenario 5). Options: 1) Add an outbound rule to the load balancer, 2) Use a NAT Gateway, 3) Modify the UDR to avoid routing through the ILB, 4) Use a public load balancer instead. Check Process Tuples download from Test Connectivity results for detailed routing information
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/How-To/AMA: HT: Azure VM Diagnostics - Test Connectivity & Process Tuples

symptom: Custom columns in Log Analytics custom table are not being populated with data from Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) data collection rule
rootCause: Data type mismatch between KQL transform output and custom table column definition (e.g., transform outputs integer but column defined as string). For text log tables, required columns TimeGenerated (datetime) and RawData (string) may be missing or have wrong types.
solution: 1) Check table schema via ASC Kusto query: <TableName> | getschema 2) Or via Azure Portal: Workspace > Settings > Tables > Edit Schema 3) For text logs verify required columns: TimeGenerated (datetime), RawData (string), optional: FilePath (string), Computer (string) 4) Ensure all custom column data types exactly match KQL transform output types
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/How-To/AMA: HT: Check if Table schema is correctly defined

symptom: AMA extension appears to be missing from Azure VM or Arc Machine when verifying by extension name in Portal or ASC
rootCause: Extension name is customizable by the user during installation, making it an unreliable field for verifying if a specific extension is present in the VM/Arc Machine configuration
solution: Verify AMA extension presence using publisher and type instead of name: Windows (Publisher=Microsoft.Azure.Monitor, Type=AzureMonitorWindowsAgent), Linux (Publisher=Microsoft.Azure.Monitor, Type=AzureMonitorLinuxAgent). Check via: 1) Portal: Extensions blade > Type column 2) ASC: Resource Explorer > extensions > Extension Properties 3) Resource JSON: Overview > JSON View 4) ARG query: resources | where id startswith "<vm-resource-id>" | where type contains "extension"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/How-To/AMA: HT: Check if an Azure Arc Machine has an extension in its config

symptom: CT&I V2 Linux: Inventory and Change Tracking blades appear empty/blank in Azure Portal for VM or Arc machines with CTv2 extension installed
rootCause: CTv2 extension is installed on the machine but no Data Collection Rule (DCR) is associated with it. Without DCR association, the blades cannot display data.
solution: Manually associate the machine to a CT V2 DCR. DCR creation is part of the CT V2 onboarding experience in the portal. PG plans to display an error message in the blade when DCR is not associated (no ETA yet, ref ICM 465276234).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Linux/Known Issues/KI: Blank blade when DCR is not associated with machine

symptom: CT&I V2 Linux: Change Tracking service generates qformat syslog entries in /var/log/messages every 5 minutes on rpm-based OS (e.g. RedHat, CentOS). Logs contain 'qformat Name@@%{NAME}$--@--$...' pattern. Not observed on dpkg-based OS (Ubuntu).
rootCause: Change Tracking service uses a different logging mechanism on rpm-series OS vs dpkg-series OS, causing rpm query format strings to be written to syslog.
solution: Create rsyslog filter to suppress the qformat logs: 1) Create /etc/rsyslog.d/09-changetracking.conf with content: if $programname == 'changetracking' and $msg contains 'qformat' then { stop } 2) Restart rsyslog: systemctl restart rsyslog. PG plans to fix this behavior (ref ICM 513799817, ADO work item 28470071).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Linux/Known Issues/KI: Change Tracking service is generating syslog in RedHat

symptom: CT&I V2 Linux: Customer application conflicts with Change Tracking extension default port 18080, causing port binding failure or application interference
rootCause: Change Tracking extension uses port 18080 by default (configured in application.yml), which may conflict with customer applications using the same port.
solution: Modify the CT extension port: 1) cd /var/lib/waagent/Microsoft.Azure.ChangeTrackingAndInventory.ChangeTracking-Linux-VERSION/ 2) Edit application.yml and change the port parameter 3) Restart: systemctl restart cxp.service. Note: port change must be reapplied after extension upgrade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Linux/How-To/How To: Modify Change Tracking Port

symptom: Legacy monitoring agent (MMA/OMS) automatically reinstalls and resumes data ingestion after manual removal during AMA migration
rootCause: Azure Policy or Defender for Cloud auto-provisioning is enabled for the subscription, enforcing Log Analytics agent deployment. The policy reinstalls the legacy agent after each manual removal.
solution: 1) Check Azure Policy: go to Azure Policy > Assignments > search for Log Analytics agent or monitoring policies and disable/remove them 2) Check Defender for Cloud: open Defender for Cloud > Environment settings > select subscription > Auto provisioning > disable legacy agent auto-provisioning 3) Check Azure Update Manager for policies deploying legacy agent 4) After disabling all auto-deployment policies, re-run the legacy agent removal tool
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/How-To/AMA: HT: Stop automated deployment of legacy agents

symptom: AMA Linux custom text log collection misses log entries or re-ingests duplicate data after log file is truncated or rotated with copytruncate
rootCause: Fluent Bit (used by AMA for text log collection on Linux) tracks file position by byte offset stored in its database. When a file is truncated (via copytruncate, sed -i, or echo > overwrite), the offset becomes invalid causing Fluent Bit to re-read from offset 0 (duplicates) while content between the truncation point and original EOF is lost.
solution: 1) Configure logrotate to use rename rotation instead of copytruncate 2) Use append (>>) not overwrite (>) for writing to monitored log files 3) Avoid sed -i in-place edits on monitored files 4) Supported operations: create empty file, copy/move, append, rename/compress/delete rotation 5) Unsupported: truncate, copytruncate, echo > overwrite, sed -i replacement
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/Learning Resources/Concepts/AMA Linux: Concept: Fluent Bit

symptom: AMA Linux ignores Arc proxy bypass list - AMA traffic still routes through Arc proxy even when AMA is added to the proxy bypass list (AMA versions 1.34-1.35)
rootCause: Bug in AMA versions 1.34-1.35: the proxy bypass list pattern matching logic never finds a match due to a code defect (GitHub PR azure-linux-extensions#2038), so AMA always inherits Arc proxy settings regardless of bypass list entries.
solution: 1) Upgrade AMA to version 1.36 or later which contains the fix 2) Workaround: roll back to AMA version 1.33 which does not have the bypass list feature but avoids the bug 3) Alternative: configure proxy directly in AMA extension settings (overrides Arc proxy without needing bypass list)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/Learning Resources/Concepts/AMA Linux: Concept: Proxy Settings Precedence

symptom: Vulnerability scanner (MDE/Tenable) reports CVE-2025-12969, CVE-2025-12970, CVE-2025-12972, CVE-2025-12977, CVE-2025-12978 on AMA Linux agent files (versions <1.39.0)
rootCause: AMA Linux versions prior to 1.39.0 contain vulnerable dependencies affected by these five CVEs
solution: Upgrade AMA Linux extension to version 1.40 or later which contains fixes for all five CVEs (CVE-2025-12969/12970/12972/12977/12978)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/Learning Resources/Concepts/AMA: Critical Vulnerability (CVE)

symptom: Vulnerability scanner reports CVE-2025-12969, CVE-2025-12970, CVE-2025-12972, CVE-2025-12977, CVE-2025-12978 on AMA Windows agent files (versions <1.39.0)
rootCause: AMA Windows versions prior to 1.39.0 contain vulnerable dependencies affected by these five CVEs
solution: Upgrade AMA Windows extension to version 1.41 or later which contains fixes for all five CVEs (CVE-2025-12969/12970/12972/12977/12978)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/Learning Resources/Concepts/AMA: Critical Vulnerability (CVE)

symptom: After migrating from legacy MMA to AMA, the Event table UserName field shows SID format (S-1-5-21-...) instead of domain\user format that MMA returned
rootCause: Schema mismatch between MMA and AMA: AMA returns the Event table UserName property in SID format while legacy MMA returned it in DOMAIN\username format. Known bug tracked in IcM 521492838 with pending fix (ADO Bug 28861140).
solution: 1) Known issue with pending bug fix (ADO Bug 28861140) - monitor AMA release notes for deployment 2) Workaround: use KQL transformation in DCR to resolve SID to username or adjust downstream queries/workbooks to handle SID format 3) Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/agents/azure-monitor-agent-data-field-differences
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) - NEW STRUCTURE/Learning Resources/Concepts/Schema & Functionality Mismatch (Legacy Agents to AMA)

symptom: CT&I v2 Linux extension uninstall fails with error 'Driver’s ServiceManager could not remove Service' in CommandExecution.log
rootCause: The cxp.service removal is blocked by the OS, possibly due to service dependency or lock preventing systemd from removing the unit
solution: 1) Check detailed error message after the ServiceManager error for clues 2) Follow How To: Manually Purge CT&I Linux Agent Method 2 to manually remove the service 3) If still failing, engage Linux Escalation team to help remove the service
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshoot Offboard Issues

symptom: CT&I v2 Linux extension uninstall fails with error 'Driver’s PacakageManager could not remove Package' in CommandExecution.log
rootCause: The change-tracking-retail package removal is blocked by the OS package manager (dpkg/rpm), possibly due to dependency conflicts or package lock
solution: 1) Check detailed error message after the PacakageManager error for clues 2) Follow How To: Manually Purge CT&I Linux Agent Method 2 to manually remove the package 3) If still failing, engage Linux Escalation team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshoot Offboard Issues

symptom: CT&I v2 Linux extension install fails with 'Package to be installed not found pattern' or 'More/Less than one Package to be installed' in CommandExecution.log
