rootCause: Corrupted extension package - no agent package found under /var/lib/waagent/Microsoft.Azure.ChangeTrackingAndInventory.ChangeTracking-Linux-*/
solution: Uninstall and reinstall the CT&I extension from Azure Portal. VM Agent will re-download the correct extension package. Verify package installation with: dpkg-query -f '${Version} ${Status}' -W 'change-tracking-retail' (Debian/Ubuntu) or rpm -qi 'change_tracking_retail' (RHEL/CentOS/SUSE)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshoot Onboard Issues

symptom: CT&I v2 Linux extension install fails with 'Driver’s PackageManager could not install Package' in CommandExecution.log
rootCause: Linux PackageManager (rpm/dpkg) failed to install the change-tracking-retail package, possibly due to dependency issues, disk space, or OS compatibility
solution: 1) Try manually installing the package per How To: Manually Onboard CT&I Linux Agent Method 2 to reproduce and diagnose 2) If issue persists, collaborate with Linux Escalation team as this is a Linux Package Manager issue
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshoot Onboard Issues

symptom: CT&I v2 Linux extension install fails with 'Driver’s ServiceManager could not install Service' in CommandExecution.log
rootCause: Linux systemd failed to install cxp.service, possibly due to existing corrupted service unit, permission issues, or systemd configuration problems
solution: 1) Try manually installing the service per How To: Manually Onboard CT&I Linux Agent Method 2 2) If issue persists, collaborate with Linux Escalation team as this is a Linux service installation issue
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshoot Onboard Issues

symptom: CT&I v2 Linux cxp.service shows failed status after successful extension installation (systemctl status cxp.service shows failed)
rootCause: cxp.service crashed or failed to start after installation, possibly due to corrupted binary, missing dependencies, or configuration issues
solution: 1) Try manually restarting per How To: Manually Restart CT&I Linux Agent 2) If restart does not help, check error logs at /var/log/azure/Microsoft.Azure.ChangeTrackingAndInventory.ChangeTracking-Linux/cta_linux_*.log 3) Enable debug logging for deeper analysis
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Linux/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshoot Onboard Issues

symptom: Application on server uses port 18080 which conflicts with Change Tracking v2 extension default port, causing port binding conflict
rootCause: Change Tracking v2 Windows extension defaults to port 18080 which may conflict with other applications running on the same server
solution: 1) Navigate to C:\Packages\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.ChangeTrackingAndInventory.ChangeTracking-Windows\VERSION_NUMBER 2) Open application.yml file 3) Modify the port parameter to a non-conflicting port 4) Save the file 5) Restart the ChangeTracking-Windows service from Windows Services Manager
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Windows/How To/How To: Modify Change Tracking Port

symptom: For VM and Arc machines, Inventory and Change Tracking blades appear empty/blank in Azure portal after CT&I v2 extension is installed
rootCause: CT&I v2 extension has been installed on the machine but no Data Collection Rule (DCR) is associated with it
solution: Manually associate the machine to a CT&I v2 DCR. DCR creation is part of the CT v2 onboarding experience. PG has a plan to display an error message in the blade when DCR is not associated (tracked via ICM 465276234)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Windows/Known Issue/KI: Blank blade when DCR is not associated with machine

symptom: Windows registry values defined in DCR are not collected by Change Tracking v2. Log shows: Error getting registry for hive name HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE, error The system cannot find the file specified
rootCause: DCR registry configuration has the full path including valueName in the keyName field, while valueName field is left empty. The path ending with a registry valueName was incorrectly defined as a keyName
solution: Fix the DCR registry configuration: split the path so keyName contains only the registry key path (e.g., HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Vendor\Product) and valueName contains the specific value name (e.g., DataCenterPublisherName). Example: keyName='HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Serengeti\Tracker 11.0', valueName='DataCenterPublisherName'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitor Agents/Change Tracking(v2) and Inventory/Change Tracking and Inventory (CT&I) V2 - Windows/Known Issue/KI: Error getting registry for hive name "The system cannot find the file specified"

symptom: Customer confused about which Activity Log events appear in diagnostic settings exports at management group level vs subscription level - missing expected events in export destinations
rootCause: Activity logs are exported differently based on diagnostic settings scope: subscription-level events with hierarchy info go to both sub and MG destinations; without hierarchy info only to subscription; management group events only to MG destinations; tenant events only to tenant destinations
solution: Refer to the Activity Log export behavior matrix: 1) Subscription-level events WITH hierarchy info -> exported to both subscription-level and management group diagnostic settings destinations 2) Subscription-level WITHOUT hierarchy -> only subscription-level destinations 3) Management group events -> only management group destinations 4) Tenant events -> only tenant-level destinations. Set diagnostic settings at the appropriate scope to capture desired events.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Activity Logs/How-To/How Management level and Subscription level activity logs are exported

symptom: Activity Log "Event Initiated by" column is empty or not populated for activity log events in Azure portal
rootCause: The EventServiceEntries claims for the operation do not contain any of the required identity claims (emailaddress, UPN, SPN, or objectidentifier) that populate the Caller column in EventData table
solution: 1) Query EventData table in AzureInsights Kusto cluster (oibebt) using correlation ID to confirm Caller is empty 2) Check EventServiceEntries table in ARMPROD cluster to verify if claims contain email/UPN/SPN/OID 3) If claims are missing all four identity claim types, escalate to ARM team via aka.ms/armhelp 4) If Caller is populated in EventServiceEntries but not in Activity Log, work with Log Analytics CSS team (ICM queue: Azure Log Analytics/Draft (API)-OMS)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Activity Logs/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Activity logs : Event Initiated by column is empty

symptom: Azure Activity Logs not appearing in Log Analytics workspace query results despite export being configured
rootCause: Customer is using the deprecated Log Analytics Activity Log Data Connector (legacy collection method) instead of Diagnostic Settings. Legacy connector uses different field names (Category/OperationName) vs Diagnostic Settings (CategoryValue/OperationNameValue)
solution: 1) Identify the data source: check if AzureActivity data has 'Category'/'OperationName' fields (legacy) or 'CategoryValue'/'OperationNameValue' (Diagnostic Settings) 2) If using legacy Data Connector, recommend disabling it and creating a Diagnostic Setting instead 3) Remove any log profiles to prevent duplicate data export 4) Wait for new Diagnostic Setting to take effect and retest queries
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Activity Logs/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Activity Logs not viewable in Log Analytics

symptom: Azure Activity Logs missing from Log Analytics workspace - all data or specific categories not appearing despite Diagnostic Settings configured
rootCause: Diagnostic Setting may not exist for the subscription, may not include the relevant Activity Log categories, may target a different workspace, or the OBO (OnBehalfOf) pipeline may be failing to process and deliver the data to the destination workspace
solution: 1) Verify Diagnostic Setting exists for subscription and targets correct workspace with relevant categories 2) Check OBO pipeline using Kusto query on oibebt cluster: InputBlobFirstTagMetadata to confirm Activity Log data is being received 3) Query ODSPostTelemetry to check for export failures (filter by subscriptionId + serviceIdentity=AzureResourceManager + odsDataTypeId=MICROSOFTINSIGHTS_AZUREACTIVITYLOG) 4) If data delivered successfully to workspace, investigate downstream LA issues (retention/quotas/ingestion)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Activity Logs/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Activity Logs not viewable in Log Analytics

symptom: Customer accidentally deleted an Autoscale Setting and wants to recover the previous configuration.
rootCause: Deleted Autoscale Settings cannot be recovered through Azure portal. The configuration is permanently removed once deleted.
solution: Query Kusto TelemetryV2 table in Azureinsights cluster (Insights database) to retrieve the raw JSON of the deleted Autoscale Setting. Use either the Autoscale Setting Resource ID or the Target Resource ID: TelemetryV2 | where resourceIdentifier =~ autoscaleSettingResourceId | summarize max(PreciseTimeStamp) by resourceIdentifier, targetResourceIdentifier, resourcePayload. The resourcePayload contains the full configuration JSON. Note: Kusto data is only updated approximately twice per day.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Autoscale/How-To/How to get Autoscale Settings configurations from Kusto (Recover Deleted Autoscale Settings)

symptom: Autoscale is not taking any scale actions (scale out or scale in) when expected.
rootCause: The Autoscale Setting is disabled (Enabled = false). No evaluations or scale actions will occur while the setting is disabled.
solution: Check the Autoscale Setting configuration in ASC under Properties tab. Verify the Enabled property is True. If False, advise customer to enable it via Azure portal or ARM API. Note: the setting may have been recently disabled but active during the reported timeframe, so continue investigating trace logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Autoscale/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Autoscale not taking scale actions when expected

symptom: Autoscale is not taking scale actions during a specific timeframe, even though scale rules are configured.
rootCause: The active Autoscale Profile recurrence schedule does not cover the expected timeframe. Profiles with recurrence settings only evaluate during their configured windows. Auto created scale condition profiles fill gaps using Default Profile configuration.
solution: Review Autoscale Profile(s) in ASC Profiles tab. Verify: (1) profile recurrence covers expected time, (2) profile has rules for expected action, (3) min/max instance counts allow scaling. Example: profile Mon-Fri 06:00-18:00 UTC will not scale on Sunday. Identical min and max counts prevent all scaling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Autoscale/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Autoscale not taking scale actions when expected

symptom: Autoscale evaluation shows MetricFailures or unable to retrieve metrics, and no scale actions are taken.
rootCause: Autoscale was unable to retrieve the target metrics during evaluation. The default behavior when metrics are unavailable is to take no scale action.
solution: Use Metrics How-To Guide in ASC to check if the metric has data for the same time period. If no data, treat as a metrics availability issue. If data exists, the issue was likely metrics not available at evaluation time (NRT delay). Advise customer to use time periods of at least 5-10 minutes in scale rules to account for near-real-time delays.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Autoscale/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Autoscale not taking scale actions when expected

symptom: All Scale In (decrease) conditions are triggered but Autoscale does not scale in.
rootCause: Anti-flapping logic is engaged. Autoscale runs capacity projections for each Scale Out condition using potential new instance count. If any Scale Out capacity projection evaluates as Triggered, Autoscale prevents Scale In to avoid oscillation.
solution: Analyze Autoscale trace logs (JobTraces by ActivityId). After Scale In EstimateScaleRule events, look for capacity projection EstimateScaleRule events. If any capacity projection is Triggered, Scale In was blocked by anti-flapping (by design). Customer may need to adjust Scale Out thresholds or increase gap between Scale In and Scale Out thresholds.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Autoscale/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Autoscale not taking scale actions when expected

symptom: Autoscale logged that it took a scale action but the actual instance count did not change.
rootCause: The Autoscale service submitted the scale request to ARM, but the Resource Provider of the target resource failed to execute it. Possible causes: RP not receiving ARM request, provisioning failure, or quota limits.
solution: Verify instance count unchanged by checking trace logs from next ActivityId after cooldown period. If confirmed unchanged, engage the Resource Provider team of the target resource to investigate why scaling request was not fulfilled (ARM request not received, provisioning failure, quota block).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Autoscale/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Autoscale not taking scale actions when expected

symptom: Diagnostic setting appears orphaned - it exists but the associated Azure resource no longer exists in the subscription
rootCause: The Azure resource was deleted or moved to another subscription/resource group without first deleting the associated diagnostic settings
solution: 1) Query RegistrationTelemetry in Kusto (AzureInsights cluster) with the resource ID to confirm the diagnostic setting exists. 2) In ASC Resource Explorer, navigate to the resource group and verify the resource is no longer present. 3) Run ARG query (resources | where id contains resourceId) to confirm resource deletion. 4) If confirmed orphaned, advise customer to delete the orphaned diagnostic setting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/How-To/How to confirm that a diagnostic setting is orphaned

symptom: Unnamed failures (EventHubError_60000) when exporting diagnostic data to Event Hub endpoint via diagnostic settings; results in data loss
rootCause: Event Hub Basic SKU has a 256KB publication size limit (vs Standard/Premium 1MB). The diagnostic settings export pipeline is configured for the 1MB limit and does not adjust for Basic SKU, causing all export attempts >256KB to fail and retry until exhausted.
solution: 1) Check Event Hub SKU in ASC Overview page. 2) Query EventHubSendBatchTelemetry in Kusto with isFailed=True and check publicationSizeInKB > 256. 3) For regular Diagnostic Settings: recommend upgrading Event Hub to Standard SKU (1MB limit). 4) For Log Analytics Data Export: consider using KQL transformations to reduce exported columns below 256KB. 5) Note: failures will show repeated retries (4 total tries) for same blobpath before giving up.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/How-To/How to identify Event Hub failures related to publication size

symptom: Diagnostic Settings retention settings for Azure Storage are deprecated but data is still being deleted or retained based on old retention configuration
rootCause: The DS retention feature is deprecated. Retention records are written at data export time with a delayed delete job. Once written, extending retention does not affect existing records (old data still deleted at original retention), but shortening retention retroactively deletes existing data sooner.
solution: 1) Do not use DS retention settings - disable if currently enabled 2) Use Azure Storage Lifecycle Management (LCM) instead for data retention control 3) Inform customer that extending retention won't recover data already scheduled for deletion at shorter intervals
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Concepts/How Diagnostic Settings retention settings for Azure Storage works

symptom: After increasing Diagnostic Settings retention period (e.g., 10 days to 30 days), existing data in Storage Account is still deleted at the original shorter retention period
rootCause: Retention records are written at data export time and define deletion schedule. Extending retention only applies to newly exported data; existing retention records maintain their original delete schedule and cannot be retroactively extended.
solution: 1) Advise customer this is by-design behavior 2) Recommend migrating to Azure Storage Lifecycle Management (LCM) for more flexible retention control 3) If data preservation is critical, back up existing data before changing retention settings
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Concepts/How Diagnostic Settings retention settings for Azure Storage works

symptom: Customer cannot create or manage Management Group Diagnostic Settings from Azure Portal, PowerShell, or Azure CLI
rootCause: Management Group Diagnostic Settings currently only support REST API interaction. No Azure Portal UI, PowerShell cmdlets, or Azure CLI commands exist for this feature.
solution: Use REST API to create/update/delete Management Group Diagnostic Settings: https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/monitor/management-group-diagnostic-settings/create-or-update
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Concepts/Management Group Diagnostic Settings

symptom: Duplicate Activity Log records appearing in Log Analytics workspace when using Management Group Diagnostic Settings alongside Subscription-level Diagnostic Settings
rootCause: When Diagnostic Settings exist on multiple nested Management Groups or on both MG and Subscription levels, each DS exports the same Activity Log events independently, causing duplication. Events with hierarchy matching multiple DS scopes are exported once per matching DS.
solution: 1) Create DS only on the highest-level Management Group (typically Root MG) 2) To get all Activity Log events (some are MG-only, some are subscription-only), export MG and Subscription logs to different destinations 3) Use hash-based deduplication in KQL queries (summarize on unique row hashes) 4) Use Activity Log Alerts instead of Log Search Alerts to avoid duplicate alert triggers
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Concepts/Management Group Diagnostic Settings

symptom: Management Group Diagnostic Settings not working or cannot be created in Azure China (Mooncake)
rootCause: Management Group Diagnostic Settings are not supported in the Mooncake (Azure China) cloud environment.
solution: Inform customer this is a platform limitation. Management Group Diagnostic Settings are only available in Azure Public cloud. For Mooncake, use subscription-level Diagnostic Settings as an alternative.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Concepts/Management Group Diagnostic Settings

symptom: Diagnostic setting deletion via portal or API appears successful but the setting reappears after approximately 20 minutes
rootCause: The diagnostic setting is orphaned - the associated Azure resource was deleted or moved without first removing the diagnostic setting. The DS registration still exists in the backend and gets re-created automatically.
solution: 1) Get the resource ID of the deleted/moved resource from customer 2) Query RegistrationTelemetry Kusto table to identify all orphaned DS for that resource 3) Validate with customer which resources no longer exist 4) Raise IcM using the orphaned diagnostic setting ICM template for PG to delete from backend 5) Educate customer to always delete diagnostic settings before moving or deleting resources
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/How-To/Handling orphaned diagnostic settings cases

symptom: Metrics or logs continue to flow to Storage Account/Event Hub/Log Analytics destinations via diagnostic settings for a resource that has been deleted
rootCause: Orphaned diagnostic setting registration persists in the backend after the source resource was deleted or moved, causing the monitoring pipeline to continue exporting data.
solution: 1) Query RegistrationTelemetry in Kusto: RegistrationTelemetry | where PreciseTimeStamp > ago(1d) | where subscriptionId =~ '<sub-id>' | distinct resourceId, diagnosticsettingsname = name 2) Validate each resource exists via API/PowerShell (404 = orphaned) 3) Raise IcM for PG to clean up orphaned registrations 4) Advise customer to delete DS before deleting resources in future
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/How-To/Handling orphaned diagnostic settings cases

symptom: Customer sees JSON parsing error in Log Analytics workspace: Data of type AzureDiagnostics is being dropped due to incorrect format. Error mentions invalid property identifier character at a specific lineOffset/position.
rootCause: Resource Provider (e.g. SQL) emits audit log records where the Statement field exceeds 4000 character limit, causing the query to overflow the statement field and produce malformed JSON. The diagnostic settings pipeline exports these records as-is.
solution: This is an RP-side issue. Investigate using Kusto (azureinsights + omsgenevaodsprod clusters) to identify the specific records/blob paths causing the error. Collaborate with the Resource Provider team to fix their JSON output. Customer can mitigate by reducing query sizes below the character limit.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/Investigate the invalid json records exported via diagnostic setting

symptom: Customer receives notification about diagnostic settings retention policy deprecation. The retention policy configured in diagnostic settings will stop working after September 2025. Only affects export to Storage Account destination.
rootCause: Microsoft deprecated the retention feature built into diagnostic settings. The retention configuration (e.g. AllMetrics=90 days) set during diagnostic settings creation will no longer be honored.
solution: Customer must migrate to Azure Storage lifecycle management (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/lifecycle-management-policy-configure) to manage retention on the storage account. Use RegistrationTelemetry Kusto query to identify which resources/diagnostic settings have retention enabled (retention != 0).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG : Diagnostic settings retention feature deprecation

symptom: Customer received retention deprecation notification but does not know which resources or diagnostic settings are affected. They need to identify all diagnostic settings with retention configured across their subscription.
rootCause: Retention was configured on diagnostic settings that export to Storage Account. Customer needs to audit their subscription to find all affected settings before the deprecation takes effect.
solution: Query RegistrationTelemetry in azureinsights Kusto cluster: filter by subscriptionId, where usingStorage==1, summarize by resourceId, retention, name. If retention=0, not affected. Values like AllMetrics=90 or AutoscaleEvaluations=7 mean retention is enabled. Also check for orphaned diagnostic settings on deleted resources via ASC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/TSG : Diagnostic settings retention feature deprecation

symptom: Customer cannot configure diagnostic settings to export resource logs to partner solutions (Datadog, Dynatrace, Elastic, Logz.io, New Relic) in sovereign cloud environments (MoonCake or Fairfax).
rootCause: Liftr/Partner Solutions feature for diagnostic settings is only supported in Public Cloud. MoonCake (China) and Fairfax (US Gov) are not supported environments.
solution: Inform customer that partner solution export via diagnostic settings is not available in sovereign clouds. They need to use alternative log export methods (Storage Account, Event Hub, or Log Analytics workspace) and then forward data to partner solutions separately.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Export to Liftr destination via diagnostic settings

symptom: Customer expects to export metrics to partner solutions (Datadog/Dynatrace/Elastic/Logz.io/New Relic) via diagnostic settings but metrics are not appearing at the partner destination.
rootCause: Only Logs are supported for export to partner solutions (Liftr). Metrics export is NOT supported for partner solution destinations.
solution: Inform customer that diagnostic settings only supports exporting Logs (not Metrics) to partner solutions. For metrics, customer should export to Log Analytics workspace or Storage Account and use alternative integration methods with the partner solution.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Export to Liftr destination via diagnostic settings

symptom: Customer is creating or updating a diagnostic setting in the Azure portal but no Log Analytics workspaces appear in the dropdown for selection as a destination.
rootCause: The Log Analytics endpoint returns empty results when the portal queries for available workspaces. This is a Log Analytics Control Plane issue, not a diagnostic settings issue.
solution: Capture a HAR file while opening the diagnostic settings blade. Look for the LA workspace list API request. If response contains empty results (value:[]), escalate to Log Analytics Control Plane CSS team. If response contains non-empty results but workspaces still do not display, escalate to Azure Monitor CSS team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Log analytics workspaces not displayed when configuring a diagnostic setting

symptom: Customer reports resource log data not being received in their Log Analytics workspace destination. Diagnostic settings are configured correctly and OBO pipeline shows successful export, but no data appears in the workspace.
rootCause: The Log Analytics workspace has reached its daily cap (over quota). When the daily cap is reached, no further logs will be ingested into the workspace until the cap resets.
solution: Check if the workspace daily cap was reached using ASC. If over quota, advise customer to increase daily cap or wait for reset. Also verify using ODS InMem check to confirm data was received but rejected due to quota. For non-quota issues, follow the full DS TSG to trace the pipeline from RP blob emission through OBO to ODS ingestion.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting Resource Log data not being received by target destinations

symptom: Customer reports ADF/Synapse long-running job alerts firing frequently. Alert query checks for Run IDs with InProgress/Queued status that do not receive Succeeded event within expected time. Customer believes diagnostic logs are missing.
rootCause: The diagnostic pipeline has a normal loss rate (~0.01%). For high-volume ADF/Synapse workloads, the alert catches both genuinely long-running jobs AND missing Succeeded events due to pipeline loss. This creates a high false positive rate proportional to total log volume.
solution: Compare the total record volume with the number of missing records to determine the loss ratio. If less than 0.01% missing, this is within normal pipeline loss rate and the alert needs redesigning to account for transient pipeline loss. If greater than 1% missing, investigate the diagnostic settings pipeline for actual issues using the standard DS TSG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/[TSG] ADF and Synapse Diagnostic Settings

symptom: Service or Resource Health events not appearing in Activity Logs at all, despite the health event having occurred
rootCause: Health event was not emitted by the service team due to monitoring agent issue, or the event was not forwarded by Geneva Health to OBO pipeline
solution: 1) Verify event existence via Azure Support Center Activity Log view 2) Check service health DGREP (https://portal.microsoftgeneva.com/s/DD3701EE) or resource health DGREP (https://portal.microsoftgeneva.com/s/58A214C2) with correlation ID and timestamp 3) If no DGREP results for service health, engage Azure CXP Engineering Platform/ACM via ICM 4) If DGREP shows event but not in Activity Log, check OBO InputBlobFirstTagMetadata and HoboPostTelemetry Kusto queries to trace the pipeline
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Service Health/Investigating Service Or Resource Health Issues with Activity logs

symptom: Service or Resource Health events appear in Activity Logs with significant delay (latency >15 minutes)
rootCause: Known upstream issue where the time gap between event creation (Time column in DGREP) and event publication (Precise Timestamp) is abnormally large; or OBO/Diagnostic settings pipeline introduces additional latency when >4K unique first tags exist in a blob path
solution: 1) Normal latency up to ~15 mins is expected 2) Check DGREP Time vs Precise Timestamp columns to determine if delay is upstream 3) If upstream delay, escalate via ICM to Azure Service Health team (ref ICM 491802323 / ADO 13057965) 4) If minimal upstream delay, check OBO pipeline latency via InputBlobFirstTagMetadata and HoboPostTelemetry Kusto queries 5) If OBO latency >15 min, escalate to Azure Monitor Essentials/Activity Logs team via ICM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Service Health/Investigating Service Or Resource Health Issues with Activity logs

symptom: Service or Resource Health event was exported to Activity Logs pipeline but customer still cannot see it (HoboPostTelemetry shows isFailed=True)
rootCause: Activity Log ingestion pipeline failure - the event was received by OBO and forwarded but failed during the post/ingestion step to Activity Logs
solution: 1) Run HoboPostTelemetry Kusto query with firstPartyBlobPath and customerIdentities (subscription ID) 2) Check isFailed column - if True, there is an ingestion failure 3) Escalate to Activity Logs PG via ICM providing the blob path from DGREP/InputBlobFirstTagMetadata 4) If isFailed=False but still not visible, engage Log Analytics team to investigate event ingestion
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Service Health/Investigating Service Or Resource Health Issues with Activity logs

symptom: Customer reports discrepancy between Azure Resource Graph query results and the count shown in Service Health Planned Maintenance portal UI
rootCause: The Service Health Planned Maintenance UI applies additional filters not visible in the underlying ARG query: 1) Azure Portal advanced subscription filters 2) Filters out events where impactMitigationTime < current time 3) Rolls up records by unique tracking IDs reducing the count
solution: Use adjusted ARG query matching portal behavior: servicehealthresources | where type =~ Microsoft.ResourceHealth/events | extend eventType = properties.EventType, impactMitigationTime = todatetime(tolong(properties.ImpactMitigationTime)) | where eventType == PlannedMaintenance and impactMitigationTime > now() | distinct trackingId. This filters by active mitigation time and deduplicates by trackingId
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Service Health/Service Health Snippets (WIP)

symptom: Customer cannot find Impacted Resources information for Health Advisory events in Service Health
rootCause: By design - Impacted Resources are not supported for Health Advisory events. Only Service Issues, Planned Maintenance, and Security Incidents support impacted resources. Most RPs have not yet onboarded to sending impacted resources via BRAIN service
solution: Inform customer that Impacted Resources are only available for Service Issues, Planned Maintenance, and Security Incidents (not Health Advisories). A subscription can be listed as notified without impacted resource data. To validate impacted resources for eligible event types, use ARG query: servicehealthresources | where type == microsoft.resourcehealth/events/impactedresources joined with event data
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Service Health/Service Health Snippets (WIP)

symptom: Customer wants to request PIR (Post Incident Review) or perform Health History operations programmatically via API
rootCause: By design - there are no public-facing APIs to perform Health History tasks such as requesting PIR
solution: Inform customer that PIR requests and Health History operations can only be performed through the Azure Portal UI (Health History section in Service Health blade). There is no REST API, CLI, or SDK support for these operations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Service Health/Service Health Snippets (WIP)

symptom: Customer unable to ingest custom metrics into Azure Monitor via REST API
rootCause: Custom metric ingestion is handled by Geneva Monitoring/Ingestion Gateway, not by Azure Metrics team. Issue may be in API call format, authentication, or ingestion pipeline.
solution: 1) Review Azure custom metrics REST API documentation (https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/metrics-store-custom-rest-api) 2) Collect HAR file of the ingestion request from customer 3) Raise ICM to Geneva Monitoring/Ingestion Gateway Support - Tier 2. Do NOT engage Azure Metrics team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Metrics/Troubleshooting Guides/Issues Ingesting Custom metrics

symptom: In Azure Portal metrics chart, supported metrics names are not loading for a resource, showing No Results Found when attempting to generate a chart
rootCause: Customer lacks Monitoring Reader RBAC permission on the resource, causing Metrics Definition API to return HTTP 200 with empty response body
solution: 1) Collect HAR file from customer while reproducing 2) Try reproducing on own subscription to isolate infrastructure issue 3) Check Monitoring Reader RBAC permissions (https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/metrics-troubleshoot) 4) Inspect HAR file for Metrics Definition API response: status 200 with empty response = permission issue; status 400/500 = code issue, escalate to Azure Metrics team with correlation ID + HAR file
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Metrics/Troubleshooting Guides/Metrics Name are not shown in the portal

symptom: Customer reports metrics not being exported as configured through DCR (Data Collection Rules) for certain metric names
rootCause: Metrics with hourly granularity (time grain PT1H) are not supported for export via DCR Metrics Export v2 feature (Public Preview limitation). Only supported resource types can use this feature.
solution: 1) Verify resource type is in supported list (VM, VMSS, Redis Cache, AKS, Key Vault, Storage accounts, SQL, IoT Hub, Media Services, Backup Vaults, LA workspaces) 2) Confirm DCR has KIND=PLATFORMTELEMETRY property via ASC 3) Check if metric uses PT1H time grain - hourly metrics are NOT supported for export 4) Use ASC Metrics Export v2 feature at subscription level to validate ingestion pipeline and check export failures 5) If non-hourly metric still not ingested, raise ICM to Azure Monitor Essentials / Sev 3 and Sev 4 Autoscale queue with ASC screenshots
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Metrics/Troubleshooting Guides/Metrics export through data collection rules/TSG : Metrics Export v2 via Azure Support Center

symptom: Metrics chart in Azure Portal shows An error occurred during data retrieval with empty chart; ARM requests returning HTTP 400 errors for metrics definitions API calls
rootCause: Resource provider registration for the subscription is in an inconsistent state, causing metrics definitions API to fail
solution: 1) Ask customer to re-register the resource provider specific to their resource type (e.g., Microsoft.Web for Microsoft.Web/ServerFarms) 2) Ask customer to re-register Microsoft.Insights resource provider 3) Wait 24 hours for registration to propagate 4) Verify fix via Kusto query on ARMProd: Unionizer filtering by subscriptionId and operationName contains Metrics to check httpStatusCode changes 5) If still failing after 24h, post in AzMon POD Swarming Metrics Teams channel
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Metrics/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting : No Metrics names are shown on the portal

symptom: No metrics returned and no metric namespaces available for Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces resource
rootCause: Network Interface Card (NIC) resource is not associated with a virtual machine; NIC metrics require a VM association to generate metric data
solution: Inform customer that they need to associate a virtual machine to the NIC resource before metrics become available. Until a VM is associated, no metrics or namespaces will be returned.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Metrics/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting : No Metrics names are shown on the portal

symptom: Metrics definitions API calls returning HTTP 400 consistently for resources in a specific Azure region while other regions work normally
rootCause: Shoebox manifest is not deployed to the region where the resource exists, causing metric definitions to fail in that region
solution: 1) Validate via Kusto query on ARMProd filtering operationName for METRICDEFINITIONS and checking httpStatusCode by region (RoleLocation) 2) Confirm via Azure Support Center that the specific region consistently returns 400 3) Work with the Resource Provider (RP) team to deploy shoebox manifest to the affected region. Do NOT raise ICM to Azure Metrics team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Metrics/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting : No Metrics names are shown on the portal

symptom: Customer receives HTTP 429 (throttled) responses when querying Azure Monitor Metrics API through ARM
rootCause: API call rate exceeds ARM token bucket throttling limit: burst limit of 250 calls/second with refill rate of ~25 tokens/second, scoped per unique combination of operation-type (GET/PUT/DELETE), region, service-principal, and subscription
solution: 1) Query ARMProd Kusto for 429s: filter by subscriptionId, targetResourceType=METRICS, httpStatusCode=429 2) Check Traces table using correlationId to determine if legacy or token bucket throttling (different error messages per ARM docs) 3) If token bucket: query +/-1 min around 429 timestamp to get per-second call rate by principalOid + RoleLocation + httpMethod 4) Advise customer to distribute API calls evenly over time, max 250/sec burst, sustained ~25/sec. Note: does NOT apply to Metrics Batch (Data Plane) API calls.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Metrics/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting ARM Throttling (429) responses, Metrics API

symptom: Customer reports unexpectedly high billing charges for Azure Monitor Metrics API calls on their invoice
rootCause: Third-party monitoring tools (e.g., Datadog) making large volumes of Metrics API calls using customer service principal/app registration, contributing to billing without being distinguishable in the invoice meter
solution: 1) Check Metrics REST API (ARM) calls: query ARMProd Kusto filtering operationName contains microsoft.insights/metrics and summarize count() by userAgent per hour to identify which applications make the most calls 2) Check Metrics Data Plane API (MetricsBatch/getBatch): use Geneva dashboard (https://portal.microsoftgeneva.com/dashboard/share/2B76CD2F) with customer subscription ID to see userAgent breakdown 3) If customer believes usage is incorrect after identifying all sources of API calls, escalate to Azure Monitor Metrics PG for billing investigation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Metrics/Troubleshooting Guides/Troubleshooting billing issues with Azure Monitor Metrics

symptom: Diagnostic setting creation fails or setting silently disappears because the resource ID or request body contains non-ASCII characters
rootCause: Non-ASCII characters in the resource ID or request body are not handled correctly by the Diagnostic Settings API, causing the setting to fail creation or disappear after creation
solution: 1) Check resource ID and request body for non-ASCII characters using ARMProd Kusto query 2) Remove/replace non-ASCII characters from the resource path 3) Recreate the diagnostic setting. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings#setting-disappears-due-to-non-ascii-characters-in-resourceid
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/[TSG] Diagnostic Setting Creation failures

symptom: Diagnostic setting creation fails with Conflict error: The limit of 5 diagnostic settings was reached
rootCause: Azure enforces a maximum of 5 diagnostic settings per resource; attempting to create a 6th returns a Conflict (409) error
solution: 1) Check existing diagnostic settings on the resource 2) Delete unused diagnostic settings to free up slots 3) Consolidate settings if possible. Refer to KI article: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureMonitor/_workitems/edit/52766
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/[TSG] Diagnostic Setting Creation failures

symptom: Diagnostic setting creation fails with unauthorized_client error: AADSTS7000112 Application Azure Monitor System is disabled
rootCause: The Azure Monitor System first-party application (AppId: 11c174dc-1945-4a9a-a36b-c79a0f246b9b) has been disabled at the customer tenant level, preventing diagnostic settings API from authenticating to downstream services
solution: 1) Verify via SvcErrors Kusto query using ActivityId to find the AADSTS7000112 error 2) Advise customer to re-enable the Azure Monitor System application (11c174dc-1945-4a9a-a36b-c79a0f246b9b) in their Entra ID tenant 3) Retry diagnostic setting creation after re-enabling
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/[TSG] Diagnostic Setting Creation failures

symptom: Azure Policy marks resources as non-compliant for diagnostic settings despite having DS configured, because not all log categories are enabled
rootCause: Built-in Azure Policy uses a broad existenceCondition that checks ALL log categories to be enabled (logs.enabled equals true). Resources with intentionally disabled categories (e.g., Storage Account hidden Capacity metrics, Azure Firewall mutually exclusive categories, SQL hidden audit categories) are falsely flagged as non-compliant
solution: 1) Review which categories are actually enabled vs required 2) Either enable all categories, or modify the policy to use a more granular existenceCondition that checks only specific required categories. See KI: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureMonitor/_workitems/edit/63573
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/[TSG] Diagnostic Settings marked non-compliant by Azure Policy

symptom: Azure Policy marks App Service Environment (ASE) as non-compliant for diagnostic settings, or DS not visible in Azure Portal, due to case sensitivity in resource ID
rootCause: App Service Environments use case-sensitive resource identifiers internally. If the casing in the GET call (from Policy or Portal) differs from the casing used in the original PUT call that created the DS, the DS will not be found
solution: 1) Verify casing mismatch between the original PUT and the Policy/Portal GET calls 2) Delete and recreate the DS using casing that matches what the Policy expects 3) In some cases, a CRI may be needed to manually fix the internal identifier casing. See KI: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureMonitor/_workitems/edit/14014
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/[TSG] Diagnostic Settings marked non-compliant by Azure Policy

symptom: Azure Policy marks VM as non-compliant for diagnostic settings when customer has configured Diagnostic Settings via the VM blade (WAD/LAD extension), not actual Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings
rootCause: The VM blade in Azure Portal labels the Diagnostic Extension (WAD/LAD) configuration as Diagnostic Settings, but these are VM extensions (/extensions resource), not Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings. Azure Policy only audits for the latter resource type
solution: 1) Clarify to customer the difference between VM Diagnostic Extension (WAD/LAD) and Diagnostic Settings (Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings) 2) If actual platform diagnostic logs are needed, create a proper Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings on the VM resource. See KI: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureMonitor/_workitems/edit/18198
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Diagnostic Settings/Troubleshooting Guides/[TSG] Diagnostic Settings marked non-compliant by Azure Policy

symptom: Customer reports platform metrics data appears delayed, shows zero values, or lower-than-expected counts for the most recent 1-3 minutes in Metrics Explorer
rootCause: Platform metrics ingestion pipeline performs multi-stage aggregation: 1 minute at Metrics Extension, then 5s at front end, 15s at aggregator, and up to 30 minutes settling at metric store. Data for recent minutes may not have fully arrived or been aggregated yet, leading to incomplete or zero values
solution: 1) Explain to customer this is expected near-real-time behavior with approximately 3 minute stabilization period 2) Data for the most recent 1-3 minutes should be considered tentative 3) Wait 3-5 minutes and re-check as the values will stabilize 4) Note: different resource providers may have different stabilization times (e.g., Web/serverfarms CPU Percentage takes longer than VMSS)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Metrics/Concepts/Metrics Flow and Latency Explanation

symptom: Customer unable to see metrics data in Azure Metrics Explorer but no errors shown, API returns HTTP 200 with empty data
rootCause: The Azure Monitor Metrics API is a LIST call. ARM applies RBAC filtering on the return data rather than rejecting with 403 upfront. If the user lacks sufficient permissions (e.g., missing Monitoring Reader role), the resource is silently filtered out from the response, resulting in empty data with 200 status
solution: 1) Obtain correlation ID from HAR trace of Metrics Explorer 2) Query ARMProd Unionizer with correlation ID to verify the request 3) Query ARMProd Traces for message like Received 1 resources Returning 0 resources after filtering to confirm RBAC filtering 4) Advise customer to assign Monitoring Reader or Monitoring Contributor role to the user on the target resource
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureMonitor/AzureMonitor.wiki:/Monitoring Essentials/Metrics/How-To/How to identify RBAC filtering (permissions issues) with Metrics

symptom: Activity logs not appearing in Microsoft Sentinel after configuring diagnostic settings
rootCause: Diagnostic settings misconfigured — wrong Log Analytics workspace selected or settings not saved
solution: Navigate to Azure Monitor > Diagnostic Settings, verify the correct Log Analytics workspace is selected under Destination details, save settings, then validate with query: AzureActivity | where SubscriptionId contains '<SubId>'. Also validate Sentinel connector settings.

symptom: PowerShell/Azure CLI commands fail when configuring activity log export behind a proxy
rootCause: Missing proxy certificates — proxy intercepts HTTPS traffic but the required CA certificates are not in the Trusted Root store
solution: 1) Verify proxy settings in Network & Internet; 2) Import proxy certificates into Trusted Root Certification Authorities via certmgr.msc; 3) Test with Get-AzActivityLog -MaxRecord 5; 4) Run 'az upgrade' and 'az configure' to ensure CLI is current.

symptom: Permission errors when exporting audit logs to another tenant via Azure Lighthouse
rootCause: User lacks required permissions on target workspace or has incorrect cross-tenant role assignment
solution: 1) Check user role assignments in IAM — assign Log Analytics Contributor or Reader on target workspace; 2) If guest user, reset invitation status; 3) Verify ARM template RoleDefinitionId includes correct permissions; 4) Test configuration and monitor activity logs for errors.

symptom: Activity log CSV download fails with 'file not found' or 'CSV file not prepared' error
rootCause: Excessive log volume requested — the system cannot process a large number of logs in a single download request
solution: 1) Reduce the time range to decrease log volume; 2) Break download into smaller batches; 3) Clear browser cache; 4) Verify the storage account is reachable and correctly configured.

symptom: Authorization Failed error when configuring Activity Log diagnostic settings export to Log Analytics
rootCause: Custom role missing Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings/write permission, or scope set to resource group instead of subscription level
solution: 1) Duplicate Log Analytics Contributor role; 2) Ensure Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings/write permission is included; 3) Set scope to subscription level; 4) If access recently granted, refresh credentials.

symptom: Activity log export to Event Hub in another subscription fails due to permission issues
rootCause: Event Hub shared access policy missing Manage/Send/Listen permissions, or Azure system-assigned managed identity lacks Azure Event Hubs Data Sender role
solution: 1) Check Event Hub namespace shared access policy — enable Manage, Send, Listen; 2) Verify Azure Event Hubs Data Sender role on managed identity via 'az role assignment list --scope <EventHubNamespaceResourceID>'; 3) Check for Event Hub throttling in metrics blade.

symptom: No telemetry data in workspace-based Application Insights after enabling AKS autoinstrumentation
rootCause: Deployment not instrumented - missing monitor.azure.com/instrumentation annotation on deployment or replica set, or network connectivity issues blocking ingestion endpoint from pod
solution: 1) Verify monitor.azure.com/instrumentation annotation on deployment and replica set; 2) If missing, ensure Instrumentation CR is in correct namespace with correct connection string and platform, then restart deployment; 3) Check /var/log/applicationinsights for network errors; 4) kubectl exec into pod and test connectivity to endpoint.

symptom: OpenTelemetry .NET Azure Monitor Exporter: operation name missing from dependency telemetry, device model missing from request/dependency telemetry
rootCause: Known limitations in Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporters for .NET
solution: Known issue, no fix available. Missing operation name causes failures and affects Performance tab. Missing device model affects device cohort analysis.

symptom: Application Insights Java agent JAR file corrupted (about half expected size) when downloaded from browser
rootCause: Browser download mechanism corrupts the JAR file during download
solution: Download using curl or wget instead of browser: curl --location --output applicationinsights-agent-X.Y.Z.jar <github-release-url>

symptom: Warning during Spring Boot native image startup: The OpenTelemetry version is not compatible with spring-cloud-azure-starter-monitor dependency
rootCause: Incompatible OpenTelemetry version with spring-cloud-azure-starter-monitor dependency
solution: Import the OpenTelemetry Bills of Materials (BOM) following the OpenTelemetry Spring Boot starter docs. Enable self-diagnostics via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_SELF_DIAGNOSTICS_LEVEL=DEBUG.

symptom: OpenTelemetry Node.js Azure Monitor Exporter: operation name missing, device model missing, database server name missing from dependency name
rootCause: Known limitations in Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporters for Node.js
solution: Known issues. Database server name omission causes incorrect aggregation of tables with same name on different servers. Enable diagnostic logging via diag.setLogger for troubleshooting.

symptom: Duplicate trace logs in Application Insights when using Azure Functions with OpenTelemetry Python SDK
rootCause: Both native Azure Functions logging and azure-monitor-opentelemetry logging enabled simultaneously
solution: Set OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER=None to disable distribution logging. For Always On: set to Off, or shutdown providers between configure_azure_monitor calls.

symptom: Missing Requests table data in Application Insights when using FastAPI or Flask with Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Distro Python SDK
rootCause: Importing fastapi.FastAPI or flask.Flask before calling configure_azure_monitor() prevents proper HTTP framework instrumentation
solution: Import module as whole (import fastapi), call configure_azure_monitor() before accessing FastAPI class. Correct order: 1) import configure_azure_monitor; 2) import fastapi; 3) configure_azure_monitor(); 4) app = fastapi.FastAPI()

symptom: No telemetry after enabling autoinstrumentation on App Service because Application Insights SDK is already installed in the .NET application
rootCause: Autoinstrumentation detects existing SDK assemblies (Microsoft.ApplicationInsights, System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource, Microsoft.AspNet.TelemetryCorrelation) and backs off
solution: Remove the Application Insights SDK NuGet packages from the application. Check AppAlreadyInstrumented status at https://{sitename}.scm.azurewebsites.net/ApplicationInsights. Clean bin and wwwroot directories of leftover DLLs.

symptom: ASP.NET Core autoinstrumentation fails silently when application configures ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES explicitly
rootCause: Application explicit hosting startup assembly configuration overrides App Service environment settings, excluding Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper
solution: Remove custom hosting startup configuration or explicitly include Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper in the configured hosting startup assemblies list

symptom: Java Azure Functions slow startup time after enabling Application Insights autoinstrumentation
rootCause: Legacy extension settings (XDT_MicrosoftApplicationInsights_Java=1, ApplicationInsightsAgent_EXTENSION_VERSION=~2/~3) from before Feb 2023 cause slow initialization
solution: Remove legacy settings (XDT_MicrosoftApplicationInsights_Java, ApplicationInsightsAgent_EXTENSION_VERSION). Add APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_ENABLE_AGENT=true to enable the latest version.

symptom: Duplicate logs in Application Insights when using Java Azure Functions with log4j or logback console logging
rootCause: Distributed tracing for Java Functions captures console output, creating duplicates when log4j/logback also writes to console
solution: For log4j: add ThresholdFilter with level=ALL onMatch=DENY to Console appender. For logback: add ThresholdFilter with level=OFF to Console appender encoder.

symptom: 500 URL rewrite error on App Service when APPINSIGHTS_JAVASCRIPT_ENABLED=true and content encoding (gzip) is active
rootCause: Outbound rewrite rules for JavaScript injection cannot be applied when HTTP response content is encoded with gzip
solution: Remove APPINSIGHTS_JAVASCRIPT_ENABLED app setting. Use manual JavaScript SDK references in webpages for client-side monitoring instead.

symptom: System.IO.FileNotFoundException for System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll after App Insights extension upgrade to 2.8.44
rootCause: Extension 2.8.44 upgrades SDK to 2.20.0 which has indirect reference to System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll via DiagnosticSource; binding redirect points to missing assembly
solution: Remove binding redirect for System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll from web.config, or update to oldVersion=0.0.0.0-4.0.4.1 newVersion=4.0.4.1. Temporary workaround: set ApplicationInsightsAgent_EXTENSION_VERSION=2.8.37.

symptom: Duplicate telemetry and unexpected cost increase in Python App Service with Application Insights
rootCause: Both App Service autoinstrumentation and manual OpenTelemetry instrumentation (Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Distro) enabled simultaneously
solution: Use only one instrumentation method. If using autoinstrumentation, remove manual OpenTelemetry from code. If using manual instrumentation, disable autoinstrumentation.

symptom: High CPU, memory leaks, thread leaks, or TCP port exhaustion in .NET application after enabling Application Insights SDK
rootCause: Application code leaks TelemetryConfiguration objects - each leaked instance creates 2 additional threads. Common causes: creating TelemetryConfiguration per request, or calling services.BuildServiceProvider() in ConfigureServices
solution: For .NET Framework: use TelemetryConfiguration.Active singleton. For .NET Core: use TelemetryConfiguration.CreateDefault() and never call services.BuildServiceProvider() in ConfigureServices.

symptom: FileNotFoundException: Could not load file or assembly Microsoft.AspNet.TelemetryCorrelation after upgrading Application Insights SDK from pre-2.4
rootCause: Missing HTTP modules and telemetry module configuration in web.config and ApplicationInsights.config after SDK upgrade
solution: Add TelemetryCorrelationHttpModule before ApplicationInsightsHttpModule in web.config modules. Add AspNetDiagnosticTelemetryModule in ApplicationInsights.config TelemetryModules.

symptom: JVM fails to start with Error opening zip file or JAR manifest missing when using Application Insights Java agent
rootCause: Agent JAR file not found at specified path or corrupted during file transfer
solution: Verify -javaagent JVM argument points to valid JAR path. Re-download agent JAR using curl/wget instead of browser to avoid corruption.

symptom: Tomcat application takes several minutes to start after enabling Application Insights Java agent
rootCause: Tomcat JAR scanning mechanism scans Application Insights JAR files during startup unnecessarily
solution: Exclude Application Insights JAR files from Tomcat list of scanned files in catalina.properties or context.xml.

symptom: UnknownHostException after upgrading Application Insights Java agent to version later than 3.2.0
rootCause: Java agent versions >3.2.0 use new ingestion endpoint v2.1/track which redirects to nearest regional endpoint; network may not resolve new endpoint
solution: Upgrade network configuration to resolve the new ingestion endpoint shown in the exception. The endpoint automatically redirects to nearest ingestion endpoint for the AI resource.

symptom: Application Insights JavaScript SDK LOAD Failure exception: Failed to load Application Insights SDK script
rootCause: SDK script cannot be downloaded from CDN or fails to initialize. Common causes: intermittent network failure, CDN outage, corporate firewall blocking, invalid SDK configuration
solution: 1) Check network tab in browser DevTools for download status; 2) Verify CDN endpoint js.monitor.azure.com is not blocked; 3) For corporate networks whitelist CDN or use npm packages; 4) Enable loggingLevelConsole:2 for diagnostics; 5) Test minimal config with only instrumentationKey.

symptom: App Insights JS SDK reports 0 duration for page views in SPA
rootCause: By design - SPA route changes do not trigger traditional page load events
solution: Use trackPageView() with manual duration, startTrackPageView()/stopTrackPageView(), or overridePageViewDuration config

symptom: Failed to get Request-Context correlation header error in App Insights JS SDK
rootCause: Third-party server missing correlation headers in Access-Control-Allow-Headers
solution: Add domain to correlationHeaderExcludedDomains or update server CORS headers to include Request-Id, Request-Context, traceparent

symptom: Duplicate telemetry from App Insights JS SDK with connection strings
rootCause: SDK recursive correlation causes duplicate auto-tracked requests to ingestion endpoint
solution: Set excludeRequestFromAutoTrackingPatterns config with endpoint URL

symptom: Third-party server rejects App Insights distributed tracing headers breaking correlation
rootCause: Server Access-Control-Allow-Headers missing Request-Id, traceparent, Request-Context
solution: Add Request-Id, traceparent, Request-Context to server Access-Control-Allow-Headers

symptom: Node.js App Insights SDK not sending telemetry silently
rootCause: SDK misconfiguration or connectivity issues; self-diagnostics not enabled to surface errors
solution: Enable setInternalLogging(true,true); set APPLICATION_INSIGHTS_ENABLE_DEBUG_LOGS env var; check applicationinsights.log in temp dir

symptom: Application Map incomplete - too many nodes exceeding 10K limit
rootCause: Too many unique cloud role names or dependency types/targets exceeding Application Map rendering limits
solution: Set cloud role name properly; use logical dependency targets/types not unique IDs; reduce cardinality

symptom: Application Map Intelligent View does not load or very slow
rootCause: Time frame set to more than 6 days, or Update map components selected, or multiple AI resources enabled
solution: Set time frame to 6 days or less; do not select Update map components; use single AI resource

symptom: Live Metrics pane shows no data or fails to connect
rootCause: Firewall blocking live metrics endpoints (different from standard telemetry) or TLS version older than 1.2
solution: Open live metrics endpoints in firewall; enable TLS 1.2; check ad-blockers not blocking *.livediagnostics.monitor.azure.com

symptom: Missing QuickPulseTelemetryModule causes Live Metrics failure in .NET
rootCause: ApplicationInsights.config missing QuickPulseTelemetryModule and/or QuickPulseTelemetryProcessor entries
solution: Add QuickPulseTelemetryModule to TelemetryModules and QuickPulseTelemetryProcessor to TelemetryProcessors; install PerfCounterCollector NuGet; restart app

symptom: Data is temporarily inaccessible message in Live Metrics pane
rootCause: Azure service outage or ad-blockers/firewall blocking live metrics endpoints
solution: Check Azure status at aka.ms/aistatus; add ad-blocker exclusion for *.livediagnostics.monitor.azure.com

symptom: Low monitored server count in Live Metrics vs actual allocated instances
rootCause: Web servers unload idle applications; Live Metrics only counts servers currently running the app process
solution: Expected behavior. Count increases when traffic resumes and servers reload the application.

symptom: Create work item option missing in App Insights transaction details
rootCause: Work item integration not enabled or resource not linked to Azure DevOps or GitHub
solution: Configure integration in Application Insights > Work Items settings

symptom: Work item creation fails with permission errors in App Insights
rootCause: Missing Contribute (DevOps) or Write (GitHub) permissions, or PAT missing Work Items scope
solution: Assign Contribute for DevOps, Write for GitHub; add Work Items read/write scope to PAT

symptom: Contextual data missing from work items created via App Insights integration
rootCause: KQL query in workbook template returns wrong schema for work item bindings
solution: Run query in Logs to verify output fields; update query/bindings to match expected schema

symptom: Availability test fails with DNS resolution error: The remote name could not be resolved
rootCause: Misconfigured DNS records or temporary DNS server failures
solution: Verify DNS records for the target URL; check for transient DNS outages; ensure the endpoint is publicly resolvable

symptom: Availability test fails at connection establishment: connected party did not properly respond after a period of time
rootCause: Server firewall blocking test agents; server not responding to HTTP requests
solution: Add the Availability service tag to firewall/NSG rules to allow test agent IPs

symptom: Availability test fails at TLS transport: client and server cannot communicate because they do not possess a common algorithm
rootCause: Endpoint uses unsupported TLS version (only TLS 1.2 and 1.3 supported); SSL not supported; some regions have TLS 1.3 restrictions
solution: Ensure endpoint supports TLS 1.2 or 1.3; check TLS 1.3 regional restrictions if issue is region-specific

symptom: Availability test content validation fails: required text did not appear in the response
rootCause: Content match is case-sensitive and exact; fails if response body exceeds 1MB (connection dropped); page content changed
solution: Use exact case-sensitive match string; keep response under 1MB; update match string if page content changed; only English characters supported

symptom: Application Insights availability test intermittently fails with protocol violation error
rootCause: Malformed HTTP response headers not using CRLF line endings; caused by load balancers or CDNs violating HTTP spec
solution: Inspect response headers for CRLF violations; fix server/load balancer/CDN header formatting

symptom: Cannot run Application Insights availability tests on internal server behind firewall
rootCause: Firewall blocks incoming requests from availability test agent IP addresses
solution: Option 1: Whitelist web test agent IPs in firewall. Option 2: Use TrackAvailability API running behind firewall with outgoing access to AI ingestion endpoint

symptom: Application Insights daily cap setting does not limit actual ingested data volume as expected
rootCause: Since March 2023, Application Insights and Log Analytics have separate daily cap implementations; using ikey (global endpoint) makes AI daily cap ineffective across regions
solution: Set daily cap independently for both Application Insights and Log Analytics; use connection strings (regional endpoint) instead of ikey

symptom: Log Analytics workspace receives less telemetry than daily cap allows; daily cap never reached
rootCause: A Data Collection Rule (DCR) filters/reduces telemetry volume before it reaches the workspace
solution: Review and modify or delete the DCR to allow full telemetry volume to reach the Log Analytics workspace

symptom: CPU Total and Committed Memory metrics missing in Application Insights Live Metrics, Performance, and Metrics blades
rootCause: Application pool identity lacks permissions to read computer-level performance counters on Windows
solution: Add the app pool identity to both Performance Log Users and Performance Monitor Users local security groups on the host

symptom: Error retrieving data or Missing localization resource errors on Application Insights portal pages
rootCause: Browser extension interference, network failure, or backend API failure preventing required API calls
solution: Open InPrivate/Incognito window; disable browser extensions; test on other browsers/machines; check DNS/network; capture browser trace if persists

symptom: Application Insights shows only 1 user/session count instead of actual many users/sessions
rootCause: Anonymous user ID and session ID telemetry properties not populated with unique values; JavaScript SDK not added
solution: Add Application Insights JavaScript SDK to all monitored pages; or create telemetry initializer to populate anonymous user ID and session ID

symptom: Application Insights Agent (Status Monitor v2) monitoring fails silently; no telemetry collected
rootCause: Conflicting DLLs in app bin folder (Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.dll, System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource.dll) cause Redfield binaries to be skipped
solution: Remove conflicting DLLs from app bin folder; use PerfView/Handle.exe/ListDLLs.exe to verify correct DLLs loaded

symptom: Az.ApplicationMonitor module not working with PowerShell 6 or 7
rootCause: Module only supports PowerShell 5.1; incompatible with PowerShell 6/7
solution: Use PowerShell 5.1 side-by-side with newer PowerShell versions for Application Insights Agent operations

symptom: Application Insights Agent HTTP module not injected when IIS uses shared configuration across web server cluster
rootCause: Enable command installs to local GAC but shared ApplicationHost.config does not reference the module
solution: Run Enable command on each web server; manually add ManagedHttpModuleHelper module entry to shared ApplicationHost.config modules section

symptom: Availability test fails with too many redirects: 'This webpage has too many redirects. This loop will be terminated here since this request exceeded the limit for auto redirects'
rootCause: The target URL performs more than 10 redirects. Application Insights availability tests enforce a limit of 10 redirects per test.
solution: Reduce the redirect chain to 10 or fewer. Use the direct final URL in the availability test configuration instead of the initial redirecting URL.

symptom: Availability test results missing in Azure portal when viewing end-to-end transaction details; no test data displayed
rootCause: The response from the tested endpoint contains non-UTF8 characters. Non-UTF8 characters are not supported for viewing web test results in the Azure portal.
solution: Ensure the endpoint response uses only UTF-8 encoding. Remove or encode any non-UTF8 characters in the response body.

symptom: Availability test fails with 'This URL is not supported' error when testing internal or private IP addresses
rootCause: Availability tests only allow communicating over publicly available IP addresses and hostnames. Internal/private IPs that are not routable via the public internet are not supported.
solution: Use only public IP addresses in web tests. For internal servers behind firewalls, use the TrackAvailability API with custom code running on a test server inside the network, sending results to Application Insights.

symptom: DNS solution deployed to Log Analytics workspace successfully but DNS tables are not populated
rootCause: Table creation is not defined in the ARM template, or Windows DNS Events via AMA Data Connector is not installed/connected (no data ingestion)
solution: 1) Verify ARM template includes table definition; 2) Check Windows DNS Events via AMA Data Connector is installed and connected; 3) If table not auto-created, manually define it in ARM template

symptom: Error 'Cannot read properties of undefined' when creating a custom table in Azure Log Analytics
rootCause: Temporary glitch or misconfiguration in the JSON file used for table creation, or browser cache issues
solution: 1) Validate JSON file format with JSONLint; 2) Check ARM logs in Azure Monitor for errors; 3) Retry table creation using different browser or clear cache; 4) Collect HAR files and console logs for diagnosis

symptom: Error 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading length)' when creating custom log table via JSON file in Azure, occurs at final step despite valid JSON
rootCause: JSON structure issue or DCR configuration conflict — using an existing DCR that collects other data types causes conflicts; also requires at least Contributor role
solution: 1) Validate JSON format; 2) Create a NEW dedicated DCR for custom log table (don't reuse existing DCR); 3) Use PowerShell script per docs to create custom table; 4) Ensure Contributor role on workspace; 5) Refresh portal if issue persists

symptom: File upload errors when creating custom tables in Azure Log Analytics
rootCause: File format not supported by Azure for table creation, or file size exceeds Azure limits
solution: 1) Verify file is in supported format; 2) Check file size doesn't exceed limits, split large files if needed; 3) Upload via Azure portal Custom Logs section

symptom: Performance logs not populating in Azure Government when setting up VM insights alerts using Data Collection Rules (DCR)
rootCause: Alert scope defaults to DCR instead of Log Analytics workspace — DCR cannot access the required data tables directly
solution: Set the alert scope to the Log Analytics workspace associated with the DCR (not the DCR itself), then verify query runs successfully in the workspace

symptom: Unable to query logs in Azure Log Analytics due to log file configuration issues with custom tables
rootCause: Problem with the log file itself (integrity issues), not configuration settings
solution: 1) Verify log file integrity in Log Management section; 2) Ensure application team fixes log file issues; 3) Test log queries in query editor; 4) Monitor log performance with Azure Monitor charts

symptom: Cannot delete migrated custom log tables in Log Analytics
rootCause: Migrated tables may not be deletable through the portal UI; API permissions may be insufficient
solution: Use the Azure Log Analytics 'Tables - Delete' REST API to remove the migrated custom table; verify correct table name and workspace details; ensure necessary API permissions

symptom: MMA agent fails to connect to Log Analytics workspace with error 0x80090016 Keyset does not exist in Event ID 7022/3009
rootCause: Server was cloned from a golden image with MMA already installed and configured; certificates contain the original server name instead of the current hostname
solution: Stop MMA service → open MMC Certificates snap-in → export and delete existing MMA certificates → restart MMA service → new certificates auto-generated with correct hostname

symptom: Linux agent heartbeats missing in Log Analytics workspace
rootCause: Agent configured to report to a different workspace ID than expected
solution: Run `sudo sh /opt/microsoft/omsagent/bin/omsadmin.sh -l` to check workspace ID; compare with Azure portal workspace ID; reconfigure agent to point to correct workspace

symptom: Linux agent heartbeats missing — omsagent process not running
rootCause: The omsagent process failed to start or crashed
solution: Check with `ps -ef | grep oms`; if omsagent not running, start manually: `/opt/microsoft/omsagent/bin/service_control start`; verify output in omsagent.log

symptom: Linux agent heartbeats missing — SSL connection errors
rootCause: Problems with VM SSL setup: invalid root CA certificates or broken SSL connectivity
solution: Download Ruby SSL test script (`wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ruby/openssl/master/test/openssl/test_ssl.rb`); run as omsagent user; fix CA certificates and SSL connection if test fails

symptom: Linux agent generates heartbeats but 'failed to flush the buffer' / 'Net::OpenTimeout' errors in omsagent.log
rootCause: Network connectivity issues preventing heartbeat transmission to *.ods.opinsights.azure.com endpoint
solution: Run `/opt/microsoft/omsagent/bin/troubleshooter` option 2; verify endpoint connectivity to *.ods.opinsights.azure.com:443, *.oms.opinsights.azure.com:443; consult network team to restore access

symptom: No Linux heartbeats in workspace — KQL query `Heartbeat | where OSType == 'Linux'` returns no results for any agent
rootCause: General Log Analytics ingestion issues or Azure service outage affecting the region
solution: Check Azure service health for outage notifications; verify ingestion latency delays; wait for affected service/region to recover

symptom: Linux performance counters missing in Log Analytics workspace despite heartbeats working
rootCause: Agent not receiving current workspace configuration for performance counter collection; omsagent.conf lacks performance counter source entries
solution: Force config pull: `sudo -u omsagent python /opt/microsoft/omsconfig/Scripts/PerformRequiredConfigurationChecks.py`; or restart agent: `service_control stop` then `start`; verify performance counter entries appear in omsagent.log

symptom: Microsoft Monitoring Agent proxy settings are erased after connecting to SCOM Operations Manager management group — 'Use a proxy server' option cleared
rootCause: Operations Manager specifies a different proxy address that overrides MMA proxy settings; or SCOM Advisor Proxy Setting Rule has an override set to True for the agent
solution: Option A: Disable SCOM proxy server in Azure Log Analytics Settings Wizard then re-enter MMA proxy address; Option B: In SCOM console, delete the override of Advisor Proxy Setting Rule for the agent, then re-enter MMA proxy address

symptom: Deleted Azure Event Hub is automatically re-created by diagnostic settings even after removing the diagnostic setting
rootCause: Diagnostic settings auto-create event hubs when they receive 'not found' errors; diagnostic settings maintain a configuration cache for ~1 hour, so deleted settings continue sending data during that window
solution: Update diagnostic settings to stop sending to the event hub before deleting; if diagnostic settings already deleted, wait at least 1 hour for cache expiry before deleting the event hub

symptom: Customers receive a bill and are asking for more detail about Azure Log Analytics data usage than is available in their Azure bill.
rootCause: The information in the Log Analytics Usage table in the Azure Log Analytics blade cannot help them because the default retention period is 1 month and they need to see data from 2 or 3 month in the past.
solution:
Introduction
