    Customers occasionally create support requests with Azure billing or with Log Analytics when they get an unexpectedly high bill. The information that billing provides often does not have sufficient granularity to tell the customer exactly what has changed that caused the high bill. 
    This KB will describe queries that can be executed by the customer in their workspace to get detailed information about the data that has been ingested. 
      Background
    Documentation for Azure Log Analytics billing information is located at these links:
      Azure Log Analytics
      Security Center Pricing
      Insights and Analytics Pricing
      Automation & Control Pricing
    This document does not describe how actual dollar amounts that are on a customers bill are computed. There are many factors that may affect the actual charged amount such as Enterprise Agreements, promotional deals, etc.
    The document is designed to help explain to a customer where the data consumption is coming from so that they can understand changes in their environment that may have impacted the bill.
      Security Group
    Currently the data in the Usage tables which are accessible by the customer are retained for the customer set retention period. The default is 31 days for paid workspaces and 7 days for free workspaces.  There is an internal database, InMem, 
    which retains 90 days of data
    . This is normally sufficient to answer a customers question.
    To access the DB you must join the La Ingestion Telemetry, lainmemtel, security group to access the OperationInsights_InMem_PROD DB to execute the queries. Go to 
      https://idweb
     and search for lainmemtel 
    Security Group:
      Introduction
    Customer questions are typically in the form of My month X bill is higher than my month X-1 bill. What is the cause? or sometimes more specific, Starting some date my bill went from about $X/day to 3*$X/day. What is the cause?
    If the ca... [truncated, see KB]

symptom:
When the 
    Application Insights Log Analytics solution is added to a different workspace the user will get the following error:
    Application is linked to another workspace (workspace id: de38fc84-****-45d6-****-
    b4eb****bf7a). Applications may only be linked to one workspace at a time.
rootCause:
There is currently an outstanding bug. When a Log Analytics workspace is deleted that contained the Application Insights solution, Applications Insights is not completely removed. The effect of this is that if Application Insights is added to a different workspace the user will get the following error:
    Application is linked to another workspace (workspace id: de38fc84-****-45d6-****-
    b4eb****bf7a). Applications may only be linked to one workspace at a time.
    This error can be generated 2 ways:
    1) Workspace exists
    2) Workspace has been deleted. 
    Detailed procedures for both if these cases are described below
solution:
DETAILED PROCEDURE WHEN WORKSPACE EXISTS
    The workspace Id in the error message still exists and contains the Application Insights Solution.
    If the workspace exists have them delete the Application Insights solution manually using the Azure portal as described in this section.
    1)
    Open the workspace blade in the Azure portal
    2)
    Open the Application Insights solution blade
    3)
    Open the OMS portal using the link in the blade in step 2)
    4)
    Click the Application Insights tile
    5)
    Unselect all the applications being monitored by Application Insights
    6)
    Return to the home page in the OMS portal, the Application Insights tile should display Requires Configuration
    7)
    Return to the Azure portal in step 2)
    8)
    Click delete
      Detailed procedure when workspace is deleted
    To execute this procedure you need to have: 
    Access to the Orchestrator web site:  
      http://aoioneclickprod:82
     , Ramweb group 14048
      Create folder in 
        \
        \aoioneclickprod\OneClickDeployment
    Use this convention for the folder name:
    RFC958418_IcM12345678
     or 
    RFC958418_SR123556789012345
    Normally to execute a change like this an RFC request would need to be created and approved before it could be executed. Since this is recurring problem a permanent RFC, 958418, has been created to bypass the approval process and facilitate quick resolution to the customer issue.
      Copy from File Share
    Copy zip file from 
      https://microsoft-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/p/stevechi/EWwAlzGSDuhIi-FI1U878VwBTQp6UJsBmSE37QbP3eXeLw?e=oT6Uzf
    Unzip the file and add the bin folder, AppInsightsCmd.PROD.xml , AppInsightsCmdLauncher.ps1 to the folder created in 
      \\aoioneclickprod\OneClickDeployment
    The new folder in
      \
      \aoioneclickprod\OneClickDeployment
     should look like this:
      Edit the AppinsightsCmd.PROD.xml file
    This file is ca... [truncated, see KB]
